rewritten: the problem addressed is preventing meniscus overflow from a nozzle by curbing the rise in speed and flow rate during refilling using a straightforward design. the solution comprises an ink tank (32) for storing ink, a nozzle plate (104) with multiple hole openings for ink discharge, and a channel plate that features several pressurizing liquid chambers—each connected to a corresponding nozzle hole. additionally, a piezoelectric element is used to vary the pressure within these chambers, and a drive ic supplies the necessary drive signal to this element. a claim member incorporating a common liquid chamber that connects to several of the pressurizing liquid chambers is also included. finally, there is a negative pressure adjusting mechanism (piston 34) that regulates the negative pressure applied near the ink discharge opening of the nozzle hole according to the frequency of the drive signal.

raw: abstract historic cities have relics of the past in the form of artefacts, sites, stories or legends that affect the cultural and community life today. traditional character of a city is still attached to the activities of the population that keep local culture. the spiritual aspects existence of will be identified through the senses that represent the concepts of space are not only spatial course. demak city as one of the historic city and the development of civilization of islam in the archipelago became a topic choice trying will be appointed based on their spiritual vigor. understanding the spiritual symptoms related to aspects of urban space and place, author of better suited using a naturalistic approach to data and information from various sources and the respondent as the perpetrator of research. to the authors also studied using grounded theory method in trying to find a little a bright spot to understand the symbols hidden city unnoticed by the people who tend will be fascinated by the physical elements of space and architecture of the city. reflecting the town through the eyes of the actors who visit the sacred places can help researchers and urban planning to develop cities that still has local traditions with direction and purpose that does not abolish the heritage and culture of the people. likewise, the results of this study will be expected will be able to find parts of the knowledge related to the symbol of the city.
rewritten: historic cities are imbued with relics of the past—whether artifacts, historic sites, stories, or legends—that continue to shape their cultural and communal life today. the traditional character of a city remains rooted in the everyday acts of its residents, who preserve local culture. the existence of spiritual elements is perceived through sensory experiences, which reveal that the concept of space extends beyond its mere physical attributes. demak city, a historic urban center and a milestone in the development of islamic civilization in the archipelago, is chosen as the subject of this study for its vibrant spiritual essence. in examining the spiritual dimensions related to urban space and place, the author adopts a naturalistic approach, drawing on data and insights from multiple sources and participants. moreover, the use of the grounded theory method aims to uncover subtle city symbols that often go unnoticed by those who focus primarily on physical space and architecture. by interpreting the city through the perspectives of visitors to its sacred sites, this research could aid urban planners in developing cities that maintain their local traditions and cultural heritage. ultimately, the findings of this study are expected to enrich our understanding of the symbolic aspects that define the city.

raw: 2 eurailscout inspection & analysis b.v., torellstr. 1, 10243 berlin. germany summary fiber optic sensors are excellent tools to use for monitoring purposes on high voltage current collectors. because of their small cross section and electrical neutrality, they are easy to integrate into the current collector strip and are well specialized for detection of high-speed load events. also the conventional contact force measurement with four force sensors below the collector strips can be simplified by using fiber optic force and acceleration sensors.
rewritten: 2 eurailscout inspection & analysis b.v. torellstr. 1, 10243 berlin, germany summary: fiber optic sensors are highly effective for monitoring high voltage current collectors. their small size and electrical neutrality make them easy to integrate into the current collector strip, and they are particularly well-suited for detecting rapid load changes. additionally, the traditional method of measuring contact force using four sensors placed under the collector strips can be streamlined by substituting fiber optic force and acceleration sensors.

raw: the present invention relates to a broadband dual-polarized dipole antenna using multipath, and more specifically, to a broadband dual-polarized dipole antenna using multipath which may use both of a low frequency communications service and a high frequency communications services using a single antenna by forming a meander pattern on a radiation element of a dipole antenna. in particular, the present invention may use various frequency bands by adjusting a resonant frequency in the low frequency range according to a meandering times of a meander pattern formed on a radiation element and adjusting the resonant frequency in the high frequency range according to the length of the meander pattern. furthermore, the present invention may adjust the meandering times and meandering length of the meander pattern without an additional configuration such as a parasitic element or a parasitic patch to provide broadband characteristics, thereby easily manufacturing a dipole antenna and reducing manufacturing costs. therefore, reliability and competitiveness may be improved in a wireless communication antenna field, in particular, a wireless communication dipole antenna field as well as a base station antenna field using the same, a wireless communication field, and fields similar or associated therewith.
rewritten: the present invention concerns a broadband dual-polarized dipole antenna that employs multipath techniques. more specifically, it provides a design that supports both low and high frequency communications using a single antenna, achieved by incorporating a meander pattern on the radiation element of a dipole antenna. in particular, various frequency bands can be utilized by modifying the resonant frequency in the low frequency range through adjustments in the number of meander iterations, and by tuning the resonant frequency in the high frequency range via changes to the overall length of the meander pattern. moreover, this invention enables modifications to both the number of meander iterations and the total meander length without the need for extra configurations like parasitic elements or patches, thus facilitating broadband performance. as a result, the design simplifies dipole antenna manufacturing, reduces production costs, and can enhance the reliability and competitiveness in wireless communications, particularly in the fields of wireless dipole and base station antennas, as well as related applications.

raw: the utility model discloses a modified tailoring drying -machine, include base, casing, landing leg, motor and add the air heater, motor fixed mounting is equipped with the amortization cover at the downside of base, the outside cover of motor, the vertical pivot of fixedly connected with on the output shaft of motor, and the upper end of pivot is fixed with the horizontally stores pylon, installs pressure sensor in the bottom of casing, and the pilot lamp passes through the wire and the controller is connected with pressure sensor, sliding on the slide rail and being provided with the slider, be fixed with horizontally screw sleeve on the slider, screw sleeve female connection has the pin, the intercommunication has the air -supply line on adding the air heater. the utility model discloses the pivoted stores pylon that sets up makes the surface of clothing accept the hot -air on not equidirectional, and dry even, the clothing is provoked to the pin, makes insidely also can carry out the drying fast, improves drying effect, and whether pressure sensor can detect insidely has the clothing to drop, in time reminds people, guarantees that the clothing can be by the drying.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a modified tailoring drying machine that comprises a base, a casing, landing legs, a motor, and an additional air heater. the motor is firmly mounted on the base with an amortization cover attached to its underside, and it is enveloped by an outer cover. a vertical pivot is fixedly attached to the motor's output shaft, with a horizontal storing pylon secured to the top of this pivot. a pressure sensor is installed at the bottom of the casing, and a pilot lamp is wired to a controller that connects to this sensor. additionally, a slider is provided on a slide rail, and a horizontal screw sleeve is fixed onto the slider; a pin is inserted into the female connection of this screw sleeve, which is linked to the air-supply line of the air heater. this design enables the pivoted storing pylon to direct hot air onto different parts of the garment, ensuring an even drying process. the garment is drawn toward the pin, which promotes rapid interior drying and enhances the drying effect. moreover, if the pressure sensor detects that the garment has dropped, it promptly alerts the user, ensuring that the drying process can continue effectively.

raw: a multimedia message gateway flow control method and apparatus. a flow control sub-module (fcm) is added into a plurality of service processors of a multimedia message gateway, an activated afcm is selected according to a principle, and when the service processor receives a request submitted by an adjacent network element or a message needs to be forwarded to the adjacent network element, a limit application is sent to the afcm module, and the service message is determined to be received or transmitted according to the response returned by the afcm.
rewritten: this invention describes a flow control method and apparatus for a multimedia message gateway. it involves integrating a flow control sub-module (fcm) into several service processors within the gateway. an activated fcm is chosen based on specific criteria. when a service processor either receives a request from a neighboring network element or needs to forward a message to one, it sends a limitation request to the activated fcm. the decision to receive or transmit the service message is then made according to the response from the fcm.

raw: this paper presents a bidirectional ac-dc topology based on modular multilevel converter and employing the interleaving concept suitable for solid-state transformer applications. the main features of this topology are derived from the interleaving and the cascading converters concepts, as reduced conduction losses, passive elements operating with effective high frequency, reduced weight and volume, and reduced output high-frequency current ripple. the basic operation of the proposed topology as well as a pulse-width modulation scheme and proper control strategy are described. simulation results of the proposed converter operating with switching frequency of 2.5 khz, rated output power of 33.33 kw output voltage 800 v and fed from a 7.96 kv/ 60 hz ac grid are presented.
rewritten: this paper introduces a bidirectional ac-dc topology that utilizes a modular multilevel converter with an interleaving approach, making it well-suited for solid-state transformer applications. the topology benefits from both interleaving and cascading converter designs, resulting in reduced conduction losses, effective high-frequency operation of passive elements, lower weight and volume, and minimized high-frequency current ripple at the output. the document details the basic operation of the proposed topology, including a pulse-width modulation scheme and an appropriate control strategy. additionally, simulation outcomes are provided for a converter operating at a 2.5 khz switching frequency, with a rated output power of 33.33 kw, an output voltage of 800 v, and interfaced with a 7.96 kv/60 hz ac grid.

raw: the invention discloses a cation exchange chromatographic purification method an anti-tnf alpha-type monoclonal antibody. the method includes: adding urea and a betaine surfactant in the process of cex multistep elution to remove pollutants in a mixture containing the antibody and the pollutants to separate a polymer from the antibody. the method has good pollutant separation effect, the chromatographic process is simple in sample adjusting, drastic changes of ph value are avoided, and content of the polymer in a final purified product is lowered remarkably.
rewritten: the invention describes a cation exchange chromatographic purification technique for an anti-tnf alpha monoclonal antibody. this method involves adding urea and a betaine surfactant during the cation exchange multistep elution process to eliminate contaminants from a mixture containing the antibody and impurities, further separating a polymer from the antibody. it features effective pollutant removal, simplifies sample adjustment in the chromatographic process, avoids drastic ph fluctuations, and significantly reduces the polymer content in the final purified product.

raw: we theoretically investigate the application of the fringe-locking method (flm) in the dual-species quantum test of the weak equivalence principle (wep). with the flm, the measurement is performed invariably at the midfringe, and the extraction of the phase shift for atom interferometers is linearized. for the simultaneous interferometers, this linearization enables a good common-mode rejection of vibration noise, which is usually the main limit for high precision wep tests of dual-species kind. we note that this method also allows for an unbiased determination of the gravity accelerations difference, which meanwhile is readily to be implemented.
rewritten: we theoretically examine the use of the fringe-locking method (flm) for dual-species quantum tests of the weak equivalence principle (wep). with flm, measurements are always taken at the midfringe, which linearizes the phase shift extraction in atom interferometers. this linearization also enables excellent common-mode rejection of vibration noise in simultaneous interferometers—a factor that typically limits the precision of dual-species wep tests. moreover, the method allows for an unbiased determination of the difference in gravitational accelerations, and it can be implemented with relative ease.

raw: abstract we examine proton acceleration from spherical carbon-hydrogen targets irradiated by a relativistic laser pulse. particle-in-cell (pic) simulations are carried out in 2 and 3 dimensions (2d and 3d) to compare fast proton spectra. we find very different final kinetic energies in 2d and 3d simulations. we show that they are caused by the different coulomb fields in 2d and 3d. we propose a correction scheme for the proton energies to test this hypothesis. in the case of sub-focus diameter targets comparison of corrected 2d energies with 3d results show good agreement. this demonstrates that caution is required when modeling experiments with simulations of reduced dimensionality.
rewritten: abstract: this study investigates proton acceleration using spherical carbon–hydrogen targets exposed to a relativistic laser pulse. particle-in-cell simulations were conducted in both two and three dimensions to compare the spectra of fast protons. we observed significant discrepancies in the final kinetic energies between the 2d and 3d cases, attributed to variations in the coulomb fields inherent to each dimensionality. to examine this hypothesis, we introduced a correction scheme for the proton energy values. for targets with sub-focus diameters, the adjusted 2d energy results closely matched the 3d findings, underscoring the need for careful interpretation when using reduced-dimensionality simulations to model experiments.

raw: a novel set of multilevel basis functions is proposed in this paper to improve the system matrix conditioning of the radiation problem for line-fed patch antenna. the proposed basis functions are formulated in a wavelet-like manner. as a result, they possess spatial resolutions and mutual orthogonality which are significant for a well conditioning of the system matrix. compared with the conventional mr basis functions, the proposed basis functions are computational efficient without a need for the qausi-hemholtz decomposition or an interaction of the overall system matrix. meanwhile, by analyzing the diagonal dominance of the system matrix, an original explanation is presented for the well conditioning of the generic mr schemes. numerical examples have been given to demonstrate the validity and effectiveness of the proposed scheme.
rewritten: this paper introduces a new set of multilevel basis functions designed to enhance the conditioning of the system matrix for the radiation problem in line-fed patch antennas. formulated in a wavelet-like fashion, these functions offer both spatial resolution and mutual orthogonality—key factors in ensuring a well-conditioned system matrix. in contrast to conventional mr basis functions, the proposed functions are computationally efficient, eliminating the need for quasi-helmholtz decomposition or handling interactions across the entire system matrix. additionally, by examining the diagonal dominance of the system matrix, a novel explanation is provided for the robust conditioning observed in general mr schemes. numerical examples are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

raw: the invention relates to the field of travel navigation, and provides a method for dynamically planning a travel route. according to the technical scheme of the invention, a number of factors are comprehensively considered, and the target weight of the travel is adjusted based on user preferences. after that, a travel route is reasonably provided. the method comprises the steps of firstly, acquiring travel data; secondly, judging the time mode of the travel; if the travel is in the current time mode, directly extracting an optimal route for the current time point and the outputting the optimal route; if the travel is in the future definite time period mode, dividing the time period into multiple time points, sequentially extracting an optimal route for each time point, comparing optimal routes for all the time points and determining an optimal route and the time point for the travel. the above method is applicable to navigation systems.
rewritten: the invention pertains to travel navigation and outlines a method for dynamically planning a travel route. it takes into account multiple factors and adjusts the travel's target weight based on user preferences before providing a suitable travel route. the method involves the following steps: first, collecting travel data; second, determining the travel's time mode. if the travel is set for the current time, an optimal route for that moment is directly extracted and outputted. however, if the travel is scheduled for a future definite period, the period is divided into multiple time points. an optimal route is then sequentially extracted for each time point, and after comparing these, a single optimal route and its corresponding time point for travel are determined. this method is applicable to navigation systems.

rewritten: this utility model pertains to the field of power cords, and more specifically to a power cord designed to prevent static electricity and resist electromagnetic interference. the design includes a cable body and a plug that connects to the cable's tip conductor. at the opposite end, the cable is equipped with an anti-magnetic interference component. the plug itself comprises a hollow magnetic peg assembly, a cover, and positioning collets for the magnetic peg assembly. the cover houses an external handheld cover along with an electrode component related to the peg assembly. the collets contain at least two through-holes, with each through-hole having an electrode that is electrically connected to the cable body. additionally, an antistatic layer is applied to the surfaces of the cable body, the anti-magnetic interference component, and the handheld cover. overall, the utility model provides excellent electromagnetic interference suppression and antistatic functionality, along with the convenience of quick and efficient plug connection.

raw: structure-specific ligands are convenient tools for the recognition, targeting or probing of non-canonical dna structures. porphyrin derivatives exhibit a preference for interaction with g-quadruplex (g4) structures over canonical duplex dna and are able to cause photoinducible damage to nucleic acids. here, we show that zn(ii) 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(n-carboxymethyl-4-pyridinium)porphyrin ( znp1: ) interacts with different conformations of the telomeric sequence d(taggg(ttaggg)3) at submicromolar concentrations without any detectible disturbance of the particular fold. among different folds, potassium (3+1) hybrid g4-structure. reveal the highest affinity to znp1: the pattern of guanine oxidation is specific for each telomeric dna conformation and may serve as an additional tool for probing the g4 topology. the potassium (3+1) and parallel g4 conformations are more susceptible to light-induced oxidation than the sodium g4 conformation or double helix of the telomeric dna. the major products of the guanine modifications are spiroiminodihydantoin (sp) and 8-oxoguanine (8-oxog). znp1: -induced oxidation of guanines results in the structural rearrangement of parallel and (3+1) g4 conformations yielding an antiparallel-like g4 conformation. the mechanism of the observed light-induced conformational changes is discussed.
rewritten: structure-specific ligands serve as valuable tools for recognizing, targeting, and probing non-canonical dna structures. porphyrin derivatives, in particular, tend to interact preferentially with g-quadruplex (g4) structures over canonical duplex dna and can induce photoinduced damage to nucleic acids. in our work, we demonstrate that zn(ii) 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(n-carboxymethyl-4-pyridinium)porphyrin (znp1) interacts with various conformations of the telomeric sequence d(taggg(ttaggg)3) at submicromolar concentrations without noticeably disturbing their specific folding. notably, among the various folds examined, the potassium (3+1) hybrid g4 structure exhibits the highest affinity for znp1. moreover, the oxidation pattern of guanine appears to be unique for each telomeric dna conformation, suggesting it could be used as an additional means to probe g4 topology. the potassium (3+1) hybrid and parallel g4 conformations are found to be more susceptible to light-induced oxidation than the sodium g4 conformation or the telomeric dna double helix. the main products formed from guanine modifications are spiroiminodihydantoin (sp) and 8-oxoguanine (8-oxog). additionally, oxidation of guanines induced by znp1 leads to a structural rearrangement in both parallel and (3+1) g4 conformations, resulting in an antiparallel-like g4 structure. the mechanism underlying these light-induced conformational changes is also discussed.

raw: data oriented applications, usually written in a high-level, general-purpose programming language (such as java) interact with database through a coarse interface. informally, the text of a query is built on the application side (either via plain string concatenation or through an abstract notion of statement) and shipped to the database over the wire where it is executed. the results are then serialized and sent back to the "client-code" where they are translated in the language's native datatypes. this round trip is detrimental to performances but, worse, such a programming model prevents one from having richer queries, namely queries containing user-defined functions (that is functions defined by the programmer and used e.g. in the filter condition of a sql query). while some databases also possess a "server-side" language (e.g. pl/sql in oracle database), its integration with the very-optimized query execution engine is still minimal and queries containing (pl/sql) user-defined functions remain notoriously inefficient. in this setting, we reviewed existing language-integrated query frameworks, highlighting that existing database query languages (including sql) share high-level querying primitives (e.g., filtering, joins, aggregation) that can be represented by operators, but differ widely regarding the semantics of their expression language. in order to represent queries in an application language- and database-agnostic manner, we designed a small calculus, dubbed "qir" for query intermediate representation. qir contains expressions, corresponding to a small extension of the pure lambda-calculus, and operators to represent usual querying primitives. in the effort to send efficient queries to the database, we abstracted the idea of "good" query representations in a measure on qir terms. then, we designed an evaluation strategy rewriting qir query representations into "better" ones.
rewritten: data-oriented applications—typically developed in high-level, general-purpose programming languages like java—communicate with databases through a broad interface. in these applications, query text is usually constructed on the client side (either through simple string concatenation or by using an abstract statement construct) and then sent over the network to the database for execution. the resulting data is subsequently serialized and returned to the client code, where it is converted back into the language’s native data types. this round-trip process can harm performance, and more importantly, it restricts the ability to perform richer queries; for example, queries that include user-defined functions (functions created by the programmer and used, say, in a sql query’s filter condition) cannot be easily integrated. although some databases support server-side languages (such as oracle’s pl/sql), their integration with the highly optimized query execution engine remains limited, rendering queries that include pl/sql user-defined functions notoriously inefficient. in this context, we reviewed existing language-integrated query frameworks and noted that current database query languages—including sql—share high-level querying primitives (like filtering, joins, and aggregation) that can be represented as operators, even though their expression language semantics can vary significantly. to represent queries in a manner that is independent of both the application language and the database, we designed a concise calculus called "qir," short for query intermediate representation. qir comprises expressions based on a small extension of the pure lambda-calculus, along with operators for common querying functions. in our effort to optimize the queries sent to the database, we developed a method to measure the quality of qir terms, and we designed an evaluation strategy that rewrites qir query representations into improved forms.

raw: this research studies about the influence of transactional leadership style and organization culture to the performance of the employees at the technical implementation unit of east java provincial revenue office of east surabaya. the sample collection technique has been done by using saturated sampling. the population is all employees who work at technical implementation unit of east java provincial revenue office of east surabaya and the samples are 48 respondents. the data analysis technique has been done by using multiple linear regressions which is meant to calculate the amount of coefficient regressions in order to describe the amount of regression coefficient in order to describe the great influence of transactional leadership style and organization culture to the performance of employees at technical implementation unit of east java provincial revenue office of east surabaya. the result of multiple linear regressions test shows that the independent variable of transactional leadership style and organization culture has positive sign which means that the independent variable which has been applied in this research has unidirectional correlation with its dependent variable. meanwhile, the transactional leadership style is the variable which has dominant influence to the performance of the employees of the technical implementation unit of east java provincial revenue office of east surabaya. keywords: transactional leadership style, organization culture, and performance of employees.
rewritten: this study examines how transactional leadership style and organizational culture affect employee performance at the technical implementation unit of the east java provincial revenue office in east surabaya. a saturated sampling method was used, including all employees at the unit, resulting in a sample of 48 respondents. multiple linear regression analysis was performed to determine the regression coefficients that explain the impact of transactional leadership style and organizational culture on employee performance. the results indicate that both independent variables have a positive relationship with the dependent variable, with transactional leadership style showing a greater influence on employee performance at the unit. keywords: transactional leadership style, organizational culture, employee performance.

raw: valvular heart disease constitutes the majority of all causes of heart disease in pregnancy. in the presence of valvular heart disease, the necessary haemodynamic changes of pregnancy might cause heart failure, leading to severe maternal and fetal morbidity and even mortality. in lower-income countries, rheumatic heart disease remains one of the major causes of death related to pregnancy [6]. in low-income countries, rheumatic heart disease is found in 60% to 80% of the pregnant women with heart disease, and 10% to 30% have a congenital disorder including congenital valve disorders [4]. the most common valvular lesion of rheumatic heart disease is mitral stenosis. this valvular lesion can be the cause of extreme disability and even mortality during pregnancy due to an increase in the transvalvular gradient and a rise in left atrial pressure. the maternal mortality associated with mitral stenosis is stratified by new york heart association (nyha) classification: class i, 0.1%; class ii, 0.3%; class iii, 5.5%; and class iv, 6.0%. most patients are in class i or ii at presentation, but 12% to 25% of patients are in class iii or iv [14].
rewritten: valvular heart disease is the most common cause of heart disease in pregnancy. when this condition is present, the hemodynamic changes that occur during pregnancy can precipitate heart failure, resulting in significant maternal and fetal complications and even death. in lower-income countries, rheumatic heart disease is still a leading cause of pregnancy-related deaths. in these regions, 60% to 80% of pregnant women with heart disease suffer from rheumatic heart disease, while 10% to 30% have congenital heart disorders, including congenital valve defects. among the valvular lesions associated with rheumatic heart disease, mitral stenosis is the most prevalent. this condition can lead to severe disability or even death during pregnancy due to an increased transvalvular gradient and elevated left atrial pressure. maternal mortality rates for mitral stenosis vary according to the new york heart association (nyha) classification: 0.1% for class i, 0.3% for class ii, 5.5% for class iii, and 6.0% for class iv. although most patients present in nyha class i or ii, 12% to 25% are in class iii or iv.

raw: abstract chytridiomycosis is an emerging disease caused by the fungal pathogen batrachochytrium dendrobatidis (bd) that poses a serious threat to frog populations worldwide. several studies have shown that inoculation of bacterial species janthinobacterium lividum (jl) can mitigate the impact of the disease. however, there are many questions regarding this interaction. a mathematical model of a frog population infected with chytridiomycosis is developed to investigate how the inoculation of jl could reduce the impact of bd disease on frogs. the model also illustrates the important role of temperature in disease dynamics. the model simulation results suggest possible control strategies for jl to limit the impact of bd in various scenarios. however, a better knowledge of jl life cycle is needed to fully understand the interaction of jl, bd, temperature and frogs.
rewritten: chytridiomycosis is an emerging disease triggered by the fungal pathogen batrachochytrium dendrobatidis (bd), which severely threatens frog populations around the globe. research has indicated that the introduction of the bacterium janthinobacterium lividum (jl) can help mitigate the disease's effects. despite these findings, numerous questions about this interaction remain unanswered. to explore how jl inoculation may reduce the impact of bd on frogs, a mathematical model of a chytridiomycosis-infected frog population has been developed. this model also highlights temperature's significant influence on disease dynamics. simulation outcomes suggest potential strategies for using jl to curb the effects of bd in various scenarios. still, a deeper understanding of jl's life cycle is essential to fully grasp the interactions among jl, bd, temperature, and frogs.

raw: the utility model provides an unmanned aerial vehicle for power line prunes, the dish washer comprises a dish washer body, the organism top is equipped with spiral wing, the spiral wing of organism top drives through the drive arrangement who installs on the organism, it thoughtlessly controls the ware unit to be equipped with the receipt at bottom of the body, be equipped with camera device and mounting bracket at the underpart, the end connection of mounting bracket and connecting rod, the other end and the motor of connecting rod are connected, the tip of motor shaft is connected with laser generator in the motor, last at least one the laser cutting head that is equipped with of laser generator. the utility model provides a can prune the unmanned aerial vehicle of eminence branch.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an unmanned aerial vehicle designed for pruning power lines. the aerial vehicle comprises a main body whose top is equipped with spiral wings. these spiral wings are driven by a drive mechanism mounted on the body, which in turn controls a ware unit fitted with a receiver positioned at the bottom. additionally, a camera device and a mounting bracket are installed on the underside of the body. one end of the mounting bracket connects to a connecting rod, while the other end of the rod is attached to a motor. the tip of this motor’s shaft connects to a laser generator that is equipped with at least one laser cutting head. in summary, the utility model provides an unmanned aerial vehicle capable of pruning elevated branches on power lines.

raw: the present invention relates to a method for producing a filter including a polyvinylidene fluoride (pvdf) nanofiber on both sides of a base material. more specifically, the present invention relates to a filter produced by the following steps: electrospinning a high melting point polyvinylidene fluoride solution and a low melting point polyvinylidene fluoride solution on both sides of a base material; and laminating a high melting point polyvinylidene fluoride nanofiber non-woven fabric and a low melting point polyvinylidene fluoride nanofiber non-woven fabric, respectively. the present invention further relates to a production method thereof. according to the present invention, the low melting point polyvinylidene fluoride functions as an additive by undergoing a heat fusion process, and thus separation between the base material and the nanofiber non-woven fabric is avoided, without requiring the use of additional additives such as hotmelt.
rewritten: this invention describes a method for making a filter that features polyvinylidene fluoride (pvdf) nanofibers on both sides of a base material. in particular, the filter is produced by electrospinning a high melting point pvdf solution and a low melting point pvdf solution on opposite sides of the base material, followed by the lamination of the resulting high melting point pvdf nanofiber nonwoven fabric and low melting point pvdf nanofiber nonwoven fabric. furthermore, the invention provides a technique for its production. in this method, the low melting point pvdf acts as an additive through a heat fusion process, which prevents separation between the base material and the nanofiber nonwoven fabric without the need for additional additives such as hotmelt adhesives.

raw: the author provides an overview of heinz werner's life and contributions to the field of developmental psychology during the first half of the 20th century. she focuses on his early work in vienna and munich as well as his tenure at the psychological institute in hamburg, up through the time when he became a named professor in psychology at clark university. recognized as one of the founders of developmental psychology, heinz werner worked in the areas of perceptual development, comparative psychology, and symbol formation. versatile in rigorous experimental methodologies, and in observational and phenomenological methodologies, werner's approach to development stood in contrast to other approaches of development, both past and current. for werner, development was a heuristic, a way of looking at processes in a variety of domains, including ontogeny, phylogeny, microgenesis, biology, developmental psychopathology, neuropsychology, and comparative psychology. werner viewed development as proceeding from a state of relative globality and lack of differentiation to a state of increasing differentiation, articulation, and hierarchical integration, but he also stressed that individuals can function at different developmental levels under different times and conditions. werner's holistic, organismic, comparative, and contextual approach to development transcended interdisciplinary boundaries, allowing him to study the interrelatedness between thought, language, feeling, perception, and culture.
rewritten: the author outlines heinz werner's life and his impact on developmental psychology during the early 20th century. the focus is on his formative work in vienna and munich, his period at the psychological institute in hamburg, and culminates with his appointment as a distinguished professor of psychology at clark university. widely acknowledged as one of the pioneers in developmental psychology, werner made significant contributions to perceptual development, comparative psychology, and symbol formation. his expertise spanned both strict experimental techniques as well as observational and phenomenological methods, setting his developmental theories apart from other historical and contemporary approaches. for werner, development served as a heuristic—a lens for examining processes across various fields such as ontogeny, phylogeny, microgenesis, biology, developmental psychopathology, neuropsychology, and comparative psychology. he proposed that development moves from a state of general, undifferentiated unity towards one of increasing differentiation, articulation, and hierarchical organization, while also emphasizing that individuals may operate at different developmental stages depending on the circumstances. his holistic, organismic, comparative, and contextual approach transcended traditional disciplinary boundaries, facilitating an integrated study of how thought, language, emotion, perception, and culture interrelate.

raw: the article presents and analyzes the results of the empirical studies of the ideas about the family of the female students depending on the type of gender identity. it compiled the family portrait under the representations of the girls with the feminine and adroginnym types of gender identity. the findings could be useful in the practice of the psychological counseling, psychotherapy practice.
rewritten: this article presents and examines empirical findings on female students' perceptions of family according to their type of gender identity. it constructs a family portrait based on the perspectives of girls with both feminine and androgynous gender identities. the findings may prove useful in psychological counseling and psychotherapy practices.

raw: the existing distributed database is mainly based on the limited scale network environment. many systems implement algorithms are difficult to apply when it needs to be expanded or involved into the field of distributed query processing, distributed transaction processing and data replication. in this paper, one real-time transaction management architecture and its details are proposed to improve the efficacy of sql processing based on distributed database system. the seven primary functional models in the server cluster part and four functional models in the distributed database part are designed to enhance the query function such as insert or update in our network environment. the experiments and results proved that our method is more efficient than traditional methods.
rewritten: the current distributed database operates primarily within a network environment of limited scale. many systems implement algorithms that become challenging to apply when the system needs to scale up or integrate into domains such as distributed query processing, distributed transaction processing, and data replication. in this paper, a real-time transaction management architecture is presented along with its detailed components aimed at enhancing sql processing efficiency in a distributed database system. we designed seven core functional models for the server cluster and four functional models for the distributed database to improve query capabilities, such as insert and update operations, in our network environment. experimental results demonstrate that our approach is more efficient than traditional methods.

raw: using raising operators and geometric arguments, we establish formulas for the k-theory classes of degeneracy loci in classical types. we also find new determinantal and pfaffian expressions for classical cases considered by giambelli: the loci where a generic matrix drops rank, and where a generic symmetric or skew-symmetric matrix drops rank. in an appendix, we construct a k-theoretic euler class for even-rank vector bundles with quadratic form, refining the chow-theoretic class introduced by edidin and graham. we also establish a relation between top chern classes of maximal isotropic subbundles, which is used in proving the type d degeneracy locus formulas.
rewritten: by employing raising operators and geometric reasoning, we derive formulas for the k-theory classes of degeneracy loci in classical types. we also obtain new determinantal and pfaffian representations for classical cases originally examined by giambelli—specifically, the loci where a generic matrix, as well as a generic symmetric or skew-symmetric matrix, experiences a drop in rank. in an appendix, we construct a k-theoretic euler class for even-rank vector bundles equipped with a quadratic form, which refines the chow-theoretic class introduced by edidin and graham. additionally, we establish a relationship between the top chern classes of maximal isotropic subbundles, a connection that plays a crucial role in proving the type d degeneracy locus formulas.

raw: chemical and phase transformations in the course of synthesis of sodium titanate na2ti3o7 have been studied. prepared sodium titanates have been characterized by x-ray powder diffraction analysis, scaning electron microscopy, and impedance spectroscopy, their electrochemical testing has been conducted. mechanochemical interaction of titanium dioxide and sodium carbonate at the grinding stage has been revealed. the utilization of mesoporous titanium dioxide have been shown to provide a considerable decrease of final annealing temperature. thus obtained titanates exhibit high electrochemical capacity.
rewritten: phase and chemical changes during the synthesis of sodium titanate (na₂ti₃o₇) have been investigated. the produced sodium titanates were analyzed using x-ray powder diffraction, scanning electron microscopy, and impedance spectroscopy, and their electrochemical properties were evaluated. a mechanochemical reaction between titanium dioxide and sodium carbonate was observed during the grinding stage. additionally, the use of mesoporous titanium dioxide was found to significantly reduce the required final annealing temperature. the titanates obtained through this process exhibit high electrochemical capacity.

raw: objectivesthe aim of this study was to evaluate the ultrasonographic features for differentiation of follicular thyroid lesions.methodsultrasonographic features of surgically confirmed 56 follicular adenoma (fa), 22 follicular carcinoma (fc), and 100 nodular hyperplasia (nh) were evaluated using uni
rewritten: objective: this study aimed to assess the ultrasonographic characteristics that could help differentiate between follicular thyroid lesions. methods: the ultrasonographic features were analyzed using univariate analysis on surgically confirmed cases, which included 56 instances of follicular adenoma (fa), 22 cases of follicular carcinoma (fc), and 100 instances of nodular hyperplasia (nh).

raw: the invention provides a flocculation container stirring structure of a washing machine. a horizontally-arranged rotating shaft is put in a flocculation container body and is provided with a swing rod swinging around the same; the outside of the flocculation container is provided with a driving motor; the driving motor drives the swing rod to swing through an eccentric shaft. the invention also provides a washing machine with the flocculation container stirring structure. with the flocculation container stirring structure, washing water or rinsing water with dissolved flocculating agents in flocculation containers can be stirred, so that the flocculating agent dissolving rate and the flocculation reaction rate are increased. the flocculation container stirring structure is simple, is remarkable in effect and is suitable for popularization and application.
rewritten: the invention introduces a stirring mechanism for a washing machine's flocculation container. it features a horizontally arranged rotating shaft installed inside the container, equipped with a swing rod that pivots around it. on the exterior of the container, a driving motor is mounted. this motor, through an eccentric shaft, causes the swing rod to oscillate. additionally, the invention encompasses a washing machine incorporating this stirrer design. with the aid of this mechanism, flushing or rinsing water containing dissolved flocculating agents is effectively agitated in the flocculation container, thereby enhancing both the rate at which the agents dissolve and the speed of the flocculation reaction. the design is straightforward, highly effective, and well-suited for broad adoption.

raw: ours is the era of convergence in mobility, communication, computation, etc. which finally leads to a new class of machines hitherto thought impossible. these kinds of complexities raise very fundamental questions in modern science and technology from basic mathematical theory point of view. one of the issues, which we are interested in this work is nature of a communication process on an extremely volatile time-varying network where nodes can join or leave at its own and edges appear in a predictable or unpredictable time window as per mobility, communication and policy parameters. keeping these issues in consideration, we propose a simple framework to represent mobile networks dynamically in a spatio-temporal fashion and move towards a new model of communication process where edges and nodes can appear unpredictably with time which is most natural setting in communication networks. we illustrate some simple representations, algebraic structure and how it affects the connectivity of the network at various time instants. an illustrative example is also presented to outline the usage of this framework. future directions are outlined as analysis of system utility on dynamic networks under the given capacity constraint which will result in theorems stating the robustness, survivability and connectivity in the presence of volatile disruptions due to many factors affecting physical layer. these new representations will be explored in simulation and in real life experiments to ascertain their efficacy.
rewritten: our current era is marked by the convergence of mobility, communication, and computation, among other fields, which has given rise to a new class of machines once thought impossible. this convergence introduces complexities that prompt fundamental questions in modern science and technology, particularly from the perspective of basic mathematical theory. one issue we address in this work is the nature of communication in highly volatile, time-varying networks—networks where nodes can arbitrarily join or leave, and where edges may appear during either predictable or unpredictable time windows, influenced by factors such as mobility, communication, and policy parameters. considering these challenges, we propose a straightforward framework that dynamically represents mobile networks in a spatio-temporal context, moving toward a communication model in which nodes and edges can appear unpredictably over time—a setting that mirrors real-world networks. we provide examples of simple representations and algebraic structures, exploring how they influence network connectivity at various points in time. an illustrative example is also presented to demonstrate how this framework can be applied. looking ahead, we plan to analyze the system utility of dynamic networks under specific capacity constraints. this analysis will lead to the development of theorems that describe the network's robustness, survivability, and connectivity in the face of volatile disruptions driven by factors affecting the physical layer. ultimately, these new representations will be thoroughly evaluated through both simulations and real-life experiments to verify their effectiveness.

raw: ionic polysulfones have received widespread attention for their promising roles as ionic exchange membranes, antibacterial coatings, and dialysis membranes. therefore, the blends of the quaternized polysulfone with a tunable content of polyvinyl alcohol were obtained to create new materials that can modulate the membrane properties. the rheological response of quaternized polysulfone/polyvinyl alcohol in n-methyl-2-pyrrolidone blends to the structural peculiarity of polymers from the blend, composition of polymer mixtures, as well as the types of interactions was investigated. rheological behavior of the complex system, described by the non-linear flow curve, indicates the plasticizer effect of polyvinyl alcohol to quaternized polysulfone solutions, in order to facilitate the subsequently preparation of the bioactive membranes. additionally, the specific microarchitecture of the blends, determined using atomic force microscopy, represents an excellent porous membrane scaffold with expected future developments in the biomedical fields.
rewritten: ionic polysulfones have garnered significant attention due to their potential applications as ion exchange membranes, antibacterial coatings, and dialysis membranes. to enhance membrane properties, blends of quaternized polysulfone with adjustable amounts of polyvinyl alcohol were developed. the study examined how the rheological response of quaternized polysulfone/polyvinyl alcohol blends in n-methyl-2-pyrrolidone is influenced by the polymers' structural characteristics, the composition of the mixtures, and the types of interactions present. the non-linear flow behavior observed in the complex system reveals that polyvinyl alcohol acts as a plasticizer for quaternized polysulfone solutions, which facilitates the subsequent fabrication of bioactive membranes. moreover, the distinct microarchitecture of the blends, as revealed by atomic force microscopy, creates an excellent porous membrane scaffold with promising future applications in the biomedical field.

raw: a general understanding of traditional chinese philosophy may be achieved by a concise elaboration of its truth, goodness and beauty, which, in my view, are correlated with the integration of tian (heaven) and ren (man) and zhi (knowledge) and xing (practice), as well as qing (feeling) and jing (scenery) respectively.
rewritten: a broad grasp of traditional chinese philosophy can be attained by succinctly illustrating its essential elements of truth, goodness, and beauty. in my perspective, these qualities are respectively tied to the melding of tian (heaven) with ren (man), of zhi (knowledge) with xing (practice), and of qing (feeling) with jing (scenery).

raw: in this paper, by treating massive loop momenta to massless momenta in higher dimension, we are able to treat all-loop scattering equations as tree ones. as an application of the new aspect, we consider the chy-construction of bi-adjoint phi_3 theory. we present the explicit formula for two-loop planar integrands. we discuss carefully how to subtract various forward singularities in the construction. we count the number of terms obtained by our formula and by direct feynman diagram calculation and find the perfect match, thus provide a strong support for our results.
rewritten: in this paper, we show that by converting massive loop momenta into massless ones in a higher-dimensional setting, the all-loop scattering equations can be recast as tree-level equations. using this approach, we explore the chy formulation of the bi-adjoint φ₃ theory. we provide an explicit formula for the two-loop planar integrands and carefully explain how to remove various forward singularities in the process. finally, we compare the number of terms produced by our formula with those derived from direct feynman diagram calculations and find an exact match, which strongly supports our results.

raw: what factors explain the perception of the effectiveness of the probationary period rating? our main goal is to identify what factors explain this perception through a case study with technical administrative employees of the federal university of pernambuco. we test the hypothesis that contextual factors are more important than personal variables. methodologically, we adopted a multi-method approach, combining case study, non participative observation, document analysis and descriptive and multivariate statistics. data was gathered from a survey carried out with 550 participants and the answers were submitted to confirmatory factor analysis which identified four dimensions (effectiveness of the probationary period rating system, institutional support, management action and recognition of the probatory period as evaluation). then, we applied linear multiple regression to estimate the effect of these three different dimensions on the perception of the effectiveness of the probationary period rating system. the results support the research hypothesis since contextual factors exert greater impact than personal variables. moreover, institutional support was the dimension that yielded highest effect. by doing this study, we expect to contribute to encourage systematic reflection on the probationary rating system and to provide input for the elaboration of public policies particularly committed to improve bureaucratic quality in brazil.
rewritten: this study investigates which factors account for the perceived effectiveness of the probationary period rating. our primary objective is to understand these factors by conducting a case study involving technical administrative employees at the federal university of pernambuco. we explore the hypothesis that contextual factors have a greater influence on perceptions than personal variables. methodologically, we utilized a multi-method approach that combined a case study with non-participative observation, document analysis, and both descriptive and multivariate statistics. data were collected from a survey of 550 participants. the responses were then analyzed using confirmatory factor analysis, which revealed four dimensions: the effectiveness of the probationary period rating system, institutional support, management action, and the recognition of the probationary period as an evaluative stage. next, we applied linear multiple regression to estimate the impact of these dimensions on the perceived effectiveness of the probationary period rating system. the results confirm our hypothesis, showing that contextual factors have a stronger impact than personal variables—with institutional support being the most significant dimension. through this research, we aim to foster systematic reflection on the probationary rating system and provide valuable insights for developing public policies aimed at enhancing bureaucratic quality in brazil.

raw: the utility model discloses a two -way pivot of pitch arc rotary type, including rolling oil bearing, motor, shell fragment, breaker point, gyro wheel, micro -gap switch and jackshaft, first motor setting is inside the connecting piece, and just first motor level sets up in the top of fixed cover, and the both ends of first motor are rolled the oil bearing rotation with the first oil bearing of rolling, third respectively and are connected, the second motor is fixed to be set up in fixed cover, and the upper end of second motor is passed through the jackshaft and is connected with first motor, first shell fragment and second shell fragment all fixed cover are established on the jackshaft, the second rolls oil bearing, fourth and rolls oil bearing and the 5th roll oil bearing and use the jackshaft as the outside of angle setting such as axle center at the jackshaft, micro -gap switch's bottom is rotated with the gyro wheel and is connected. the utility model discloses can be used to realize the two -way rotation of robot head, hand, arm, leg, when saving the robot inner space, have guaranteed by 3 bearings head motion 's stability, realize that product permeability when accomplishing many complicated actions is also fine.
rewritten: the utility model describes a two-way pivot of the pitch arc rotary type that includes rolling oil bearings, motors, shell fragments, a breaker point, a gyro wheel, a micro-gap switch, and a jackshaft. in this design, a first motor is installed within a connecting piece and is positioned at the top inside a fixed cover. both of its ends are coupled with rolling oil bearings (referred to as the first and third bearings) that facilitate rotation. a second motor is fixed in the same cover, and its upper end passes through the jackshaft to connect with the first motor. two shell fragments (the first and second) are mounted on the jackshaft, and additional rolling oil bearings (the second, fourth, and fifth) are arranged around the jackshaft, acting as the outer angles set with the jackshaft's center as the reference. moreover, the bottom of the micro-gap switch rotates together with the gyro wheel and is connected accordingly. this utility model is intended to enable two-way rotation in robot components such as the head, hand, arm, or leg while conserving internal space. it ensures stability through the use of three bearings during head motion and maintains efficiency when executing various complex actions.

raw: the present research work with subject special examination to the account of the costs and expenses to determinate the sustenance of the result during 2014 of the company of metallic constructions in the city of riobamba; this allowed to determine the degree of the reasonableness of the balance sheet, as well as the income statement, helping a correct decision making in order to correct certain errors present within it. the methods used for this research were interviews, direct observations and the methodology of the audit through which the results were obtained that will be of benefit to the entity. the special examination carried out on the cost and expenses account is part of the income statement, which will constitute a support tool for the manager and employees of the company, under this analysis it is concluded that there are shortcomings, one of the company. at the end of this special review, new guidelines could be established to improve the processes and development of the entity, thus helping to apply goo and new methods of work.
rewritten: this research focuses on a special examination of the costs and expenses account to determine the sustainability of the company's 2014 results for a metallic constructions firm in riobamba. this analysis allowed for an evaluation of the reasonableness of the balance sheet and the income statement, thus supporting effective decision-making to correct certain identified errors. the study employed interviews, direct observations, and auditing methodologies to obtain results that will ultimately benefit the organization. the special examination of the cost and expense account, which forms part of the income statement, serves as a valuable tool for both management and employees. based on this analysis, various shortcomings in the company were identified. following this review, new guidelines could be developed to improve the entity's processes and overall development, fostering the adoption of sound and innovative work methods.

raw: the invention discloses a friction generator and a method for applying the same to generate power. the friction generator comprises an upper insulation frictional layer, a lower insulation frictional layer, a multi-layer electrode body and an electric power collecting circuit. the multi-layer electrode body comprises multiple electrode layers and an insulation dielectric layer between every two adjacent electrode layers, and is capable of reciprocating between the upper insulation frictional layer and the lower insulation frictional layer. the electric power collecting circuit connects two electrode layers in the multi-layer electrode body so as to output electric signals. the connected electrode layers are adjacent or not adjacent. according to the invention, quantity of collected electric charge can be increased; it is easy to regulate and control output voltage; and high-frequency electric signals and same-phase pulse can be easily provided.
rewritten: the invention discloses a friction generator and a method for using it to generate power. the friction generator includes an upper insulating friction layer, a lower insulating friction layer, a multi-layer electrode assembly, and an electric power collecting circuit. the multi-layer electrode assembly consists of several electrode layers separated by insulating dielectric layers between each pair of adjacent electrode layers, and it is capable of moving back and forth between the upper and lower insulating friction layers. the electric power collecting circuit connects two electrode layers within the multi-layer electrode assembly to output electric signals; these connected electrode layers may be adjacent or non-adjacent. according to the invention, this configuration increases the amount of collected electric charge, simplifies the regulation and control of the output voltage, and easily provides high-frequency electric signals and in-phase pulses.

rewritten: the utility model describes a suspension-type coin package press that rotates coins from both sides. it comprises a wallboard, a first conveyor belt, and a second conveyor belt. the first conveyor belt runs over a roller, while the second conveyor belt is mounted on the same side of the wallboard. one end of the first conveyor belt is partially wrapped by the second conveyor belt as it passes around the roller, and the opposing end of the first conveyor belt features a coin passageway. in this design, the second conveyor belt presses the coins from both sides at the end of the first conveyor belt. the coins are conveyed on both belts and, after being pressed on both sides, are flipped over during the process before being transferred to the coin slip-in bag. notably, both the first and second conveyor belts are installed on the same side of the wallboard, and the roller is fixed in a cantilever, unilateral manner. this configuration eliminates the need to dismantle the wallboard when replacing the conveyor belts, thereby saving time and labor.

raw: car door plant electronic component safety inspection frock, its structure is: including installing the rotatory frock on metal crate, rotatory frock is including the vertical support of installing on the chassis of metal crate both sides, and install between the support of both sides through the axis of rotation respectively the fixed frame both sides of rectangle, installs the runner coaxial with fixing the frame in the outside of one side support, rotates the runner and can drive fixed frame and rotate, install on fixed frame and be used for the response to wait to install the sensor of door plant and be used for pressing from both sides the pneumatic clamping mechanism who tightly waits to install the door plant. the utility model provides an automatic performance is strong and can reduce workman intensity of labour, improvement detection cycle and simple structure, convenient to use, the strong detection frock of stability.
rewritten: the car door plant electronic component safety inspection rig consists of several parts. it features a rotary assembly mounted on a metal frame, with vertical supports attached to both sides of the metal crate’s chassis. between these supports, a fixed rectangular frame is connected via a rotational axis on each side. additionally, a runner is installed coaxially with the fixed frame on the outside of one support; when the runner rotates, it drives the fixed frame to rotate as well. mounted on the fixed frame are a sensor interface for the door plant and a pneumatic clamping mechanism on both sides that firmly presses the door plant into position. this utility model provides robust automatic performance, reducing manual labor intensity, shortening the inspection cycle, and offering a simple, stable, and user-friendly structure.

raw: since first joining awwa in 1968, i have wondered why the journal is so theoretical and when, if ever, it will serve the needs and interests of the primary readership i.e., engineers. from my point of view, the only purpose currently served by the journal is as a medium of information exchange between members of the academic community and perhaps the research and development staffs of some large environmental firms. it is a vehicle for those who must publish or perish. as a diplomate in the academy of environmental engineers with 15 years of experience, i understand both the theoretical and practical aspects of environmental engineering. it is my firm belief that readers of the journal would be far better served by the publication of practical rather than theoretical articles.
rewritten: since i joined awwa in 1968, i have questioned why the journal remains so focused on theory and when—or if ever—it will address the needs and interests of its primary audience, namely engineers. in my opinion, the journal currently serves mainly as a platform for academic exchange, and perhaps for the r&d teams of large environmental firms, acting as a vehicle for those compelled to publish in order to advance. as a diplomate in the academy of environmental engineers with 15 years of experience, i understand both the theoretical and practical dimensions of environmental engineering. i strongly believe that the journal's readers would benefit much more from practical articles than from those that are purely theoretical.

raw: crowd counting is an important task in computer vision, which has many applications in video surveillance. although the regression-based framework has achieved great improvements for crowd counting, how to improve the discriminative power of image representation is still an open problem. conventional holistic features used in crowd counting often fail to capture semantic attributes and spatial cues of the image. in this paper, we propose integrating semantic information into learning locality-aware feature sets for accurate crowd counting. first, with the help of convolutional neural network (cnn), the original pixel space is mapped onto a dense attribute feature map, where each dimension of the pixel-wise feature indicates the probabilistic strength of a certain semantic class. then, locality-aware features (laf) built on the idea of spatial pyramids on neighboring patches are proposed to explore more spatial context and local information. finally, the traditional vlad encoding method is extended to a more generalized form in which diverse coefficient weights are taken into consideration. experimental results validate the effectiveness of our presented method.
rewritten: crowd counting plays a crucial role in computer vision, particularly in video surveillance applications. although regression-based approaches have made significant strides, enhancing the discriminative capability of image representations remains an unresolved challenge. traditional holistic features often used in crowd counting tend to overlook semantic attributes and spatial cues within images. in this work, we introduce a method that integrates semantic information into the learning of locality-aware feature sets, aiming for more precise crowd counting. first, a convolutional neural network (cnn) is employed to transform the original pixel space into a dense attribute feature map, where each pixel-wise feature dimension reflects the probability of a specific semantic class. next, we propose locality-aware features (laf), inspired by spatial pyramids constructed on adjacent patches, to capture richer spatial context and localized details. finally, we extend the classic vlad encoding technique into a more generalized version that incorporates diverse coefficient weights. experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed approach.
