rewritten: this note begins by identifying several mistakes in the aforementioned paper [1] and then provides a comprehensive proof of lemma 1-(i) from [1].

raw: english for specific purposes (esp), and english for academic purposes (eap) programs in english as a second language (esl), and english as a foreign language (efl) teaching-learning situations tend to rely on center-based teaching materials which do not reflect authentic language use by target informants, who are overwhelmingly from outer and expanding circle countries. this is particularly obvious when it comes to spoken academic discourse. this book explores such a teaching-learning situation in sri lanka, and through the analysis of a corpus of specialized spoken academic english, which is available in several modes, illustrates how the corpus and concordancing applications are used to decide on the lexis and structures appropriate for the teaching situation. the book is particularly suitable for esp, eap, esl and efl practitioners, material developers, teachers, students, and researchers in the fields of corpus linguistics, esl/efl, academic english, and new englishes.
rewritten: english for specific purposes (esp) and english for academic purposes (eap) programs, as well as english as a second language (esl) and english as a foreign language (efl) teaching contexts, often depend on centrally developed materials that fail to capture authentic language usage by native speakers—mostly from outer and expanding circle countries. this issue is especially apparent in spoken academic discourse. this book examines a similar teaching and learning environment in sri lanka. by analyzing a corpus of specialized spoken academic english available in various formats, the study demonstrates how corpus data and concordancing tools can help determine the appropriate vocabulary and structures for the specific educational context. the book is ideal for esp, eap, esl, and efl practitioners, material developers, educators, students, and scholars working in the fields of corpus linguistics, academic english, and new englishes.

raw: the paper deals with implementation of invertor q/u function in photovoltaic power plants (pvp) in lv distribution network (dn). discussed are individual modification and parameters of q/u characteristics of pvp invertors. based on result of simulations on existing lv distribution network in the dncalc software, the benefit and possible risks of this function are evaluated from the view-point of further integration of autonomously regulated pvp into low voltage dn.
rewritten: this paper focuses on implementing the inverter q/u function in photovoltaic power plants (pvps) within low voltage (lv) distribution networks. it examines individual modifications and the parameters of the q/u characteristics in pvp inverters. simulation results obtained from the dncalc software on an existing lv distribution network are used to assess both the advantages and potential risks of this function, particularly regarding the further integration of autonomously regulated pv systems into lv networks.

raw: background recently, isolated reports of pyoderma gangrenosum (pg) secondary to levamisole-contaminated cocaine have been described, with similar serologic findings to the vasculopathic presentation. objective we sought to describe clinical, histopathological, and serologic findings in 8 patients with pg associated with levamisole-contaminated cocaine. methods eight consecutive patients presenting with this disease spanning the period from 2011 to 2015 were included for the cohort. observed variables included: lesion distribution, morphology, serologic titers, and histopathologic evaluation for vasculitis and vasculopathy. results all patients reported cocaine exposure prior to the onset of lesions resembling pg. lesions appeared primarily on the upper (6 of 8 patients) and lower (all 8 patients) extremities. most patients demonstrated elevated titers for p-anca and antiphospholipid antibodies, and a diffuse dermal infiltrate dominated by neutrophils was seen in all biopsy specimens. lesions improved or remained stable with conservative management or short courses of steroids, and recurrence was only noted on re-exposure to adulterated cocaine. limitations the study is limited by sample size. conclusions pg may occur after exposure to levamisole-adulterated cocaine. clinical and histopathological findings resemble those seen in conventional forms of pg, whereas serologic findings mirror those seen in other levamisole-associated vasculopathic or vasculitic eruptions. cocaine avoidance represents a cornerstone of management in these patients.
rewritten: background: recently, there have been isolated cases of pyoderma gangrenosum (pg) arising secondary to cocaine contaminated with levamisole. these cases exhibit serologic findings similar to those observed in vasculopathic presentations. objective: we aimed to detail the clinical, histopathological, and serologic characteristics in eight patients diagnosed with pg linked to levamisole-contaminated cocaine. methods: the study included eight consecutive patients, seen between 2011 and 2015, who presented with this condition. we evaluated variables such as the distribution and morphology of lesions, serologic titers, and histopathological evidence of vasculitis and vasculopathy. results: every patient reported cocaine use prior to the development of lesions resembling pg. the lesions primarily appeared on the upper extremities (six of eight patients) and the lower extremities (all eight patients). most individuals also had elevated titers of p-anca and antiphospholipid antibodies, with all biopsy specimens showing a diffuse dermal infiltrate predominantly composed of neutrophils. lesions either improved or remained stable with conservative treatment or brief courses of steroids, and recurrence was only seen following re-exposure to adulterated cocaine. limitations: the primary limitation of this study is its small sample size. conclusions: pg may develop following exposure to levamisole-adulterated cocaine. while the clinical and histopathological features mirror those typically seen in conventional pg cases, the serologic profiles resemble those found in other levamisole-associated vasculopathic or vasculitic conditions. avoiding cocaine use is essential in managing these patients.

raw: the principal call of the constitutional period is either nationalism or social criticism, and the prevalent themes of the poems are freedom, nation, social criticism and etc. the first two of which appear as the basic foundations of the poem written in this period. the poets, in this particular era, laud the past rulers and the glories of culture under their mighty throne and the social justice, jubilations, and victories of these kings and knights once unprecedentedly powerful, nothing of which but the ruins remained for the people of this period. the poets aim at informing the mass of the glories of ancient persia and nurture in them the gust for freedom and nationalism.
rewritten: during the constitutional period, the primary focus was either nationalism or social criticism, with freedom, the nation, and social critique being recurring themes in the poetry. the first two of these themes form the core foundation of the poems from that era. poets of the time celebrated the former rulers, the cultural splendor displayed under their powerful reign, and the social justice, festivities, and triumphant successes of once-mighty kings and knights—legacies now reduced to ruins for later generations. their goal was to remind the masses of the ancient glories of persia while fostering a passion for freedom and nationalism.

raw: $\ell _1$ mean filtering is a conventional, optimization-based method to estimate the positions of jumps in a piecewise constant signal perturbed by additive noise. in this method, the $\ell _1$ norm penalizes sparsity of the first-order derivative of the signal. theoretical results, however, show that in some situations, which can occur frequently in practice, even when the jump amplitudes tend to $\infty$ , the conventional method identifies false change points. this issue, which is referred to as the stair-casing problem herein, restricts practical importance of $\ell _1$ mean filtering. in this paper, sparsity is penalized more tightly than the $\ell _1$ norm by exploiting a certain class of nonconvex functions, while the strict convexity of the consequent optimization problem is preserved. this results in a higher performance in detecting change points. to theoretically justify the performance improvements over $\ell _1$ mean filtering, deterministic and stochastic sufficient conditions for exact change point recovery are derived. in particular, theoretical results show that in the stair-casing problem, our approach might be able to exclude the false change points, while $\ell _1$ mean filtering may fail. a number of numerical simulations assist to show superiority of our method over $\ell _1$ mean filtering and another state-of-the-art algorithm that promotes sparsity tighter than the $\ell _1$ norm. specifically, it is shown that our approach can consistently detect change points when the jump amplitudes become sufficiently large, while the two other competitors cannot.
rewritten: ℓ₁ mean filtering is a traditional optimization approach used to determine the locations of jumps in a piecewise constant signal corrupted by additive noise. in this framework, the ℓ₁ norm is employed to impose a sparsity penalty on the signal’s first-order derivative. however, theoretical findings indicate that under certain practical conditions—even when the jump amplitudes grow arbitrarily large—the conventional method can mistakenly identify change points. this shortcoming, known as the stair-casing problem, limits the practical utility of ℓ₁ mean filtering. in our work, we address this issue by imposing a stricter sparsity penalty than that of the ℓ₁ norm, achieved through the use of a specific class of nonconvex functions, all while maintaining the strict convexity of the resulting optimization problem. this modification significantly enhances change point detection performance. to theoretically validate the improvements over ℓ₁ mean filtering, we derive both deterministic and stochastic sufficient conditions for exact change point recovery. notably, our results suggest that in cases affected by stair-casing, our approach can successfully eliminate false change points where traditional ℓ₁ mean filtering may not. furthermore, several numerical simulations demonstrate that our method outperforms not only ℓ₁ mean filtering but also another leading algorithm that enforces a stricter sparsity constraint than the ℓ₁ norm. specifically, the simulations reveal that our approach is capable of reliably detecting change points when the jump amplitudes are sufficiently large, a feat that the alternative methods fail to achieve.

raw: as the cloud computing technology develops during the last decade, outsourcing data to cloud service for storage becomes an attractive trend, which benefits in sparing efforts on heavy data maintenance and management. nevertheless, since the outsourced cloud storage is not fully trustworthy, it raises security concerns on how to realize data deduplication in cloud while achieving integrity auditing. in this work, we study the problem of integrity auditing and secure deduplication on cloud data. specifically, aiming at achieving both data integrity and deduplication in cloud, we propose two secure systems, namely seccloud and seccloud $^+$ . seccloud introduces an auditing entity with a maintenance of a mapreduce cloud, which helps clients generate data tags before uploading as well as audit the integrity of data having been stored in cloud. compared with previous work, the computation by user in seccloud is greatly reduced during the file uploading and auditing phases. seccloud $^+$ is designed motivated by the fact that customers always want to encrypt their data before uploading, and enables integrity auditing and secure deduplication on encrypted data.
rewritten: over the past decade, as cloud computing technology has advanced, outsourcing data storage to cloud services has become an attractive trend because it reduces the burden of heavy data maintenance and management. however, since outsourced cloud storage is not entirely trustworthy, concerns have arisen regarding how to achieve data deduplication in the cloud while ensuring integrity auditing. in this work, we investigate the challenges of integrity auditing and secure deduplication for cloud data. specifically, with the goal of maintaining both data integrity and deduplication, we introduce two secure systems named seccloud and seccloud⁺. seccloud utilizes an auditing entity that manages a mapreduce cloud, assisting clients in generating data tags prior to uploading and auditing the integrity of the stored data. compared to previous approaches, seccloud significantly reduces the computational load on users during file uploads and auditing. motivated by the fact that customers typically prefer to encrypt their data before uploading, seccloud⁺ enables integrity auditing and secure deduplication even on encrypted data.

raw: the edinburgh cognitive and behavioural als screen (ecas) has been developed to assess cognition and behaviour in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (als). cognitive impairments of als-specific and als-non-specific functions can be determined using cut-off scores based on performance of healthy subjects. however, detailed analyses show that older healthy subjects perform worse than younger ones, whereas highly-educated individuals perform better than those with lower education levels. as a consequence, this study presents new age and education matched cut-off scores for the revised german/swiss-german version of the ecas based on the performance of 86 healthy subjects.
rewritten: the edinburgh cognitive and behavioural als screen (ecas) was designed to evaluate cognitive and behavioral functions in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (als). using cut-off scores obtained from healthy subjects, the tool can identify deficits in both als-specific and non-als-specific cognitive areas. however, detailed findings indicate that healthy older adults tend to perform worse than younger ones, and individuals with higher education levels achieve better results than those with lower education levels. as a result, this study provides new cut-off scores, adjusted for age and education, for the updated german/swiss-german version of the ecas, based on data from 86 healthy participants.

raw: the present invention discloses a multipurpose polyurethane effective microorganism solidification carrier, which is prepared by mixing the following raw materials by weight: 15-30 parts of polyether polyol, 5-15 parts of polyester polyol, 20-35 parts of isocyanate, 0.31-0.40 part of a catalyst, 0.8-1.0 part of a foam stabilizer, 5-15 parts of a foaming agent, and 30-50 parts by dry weight of an effective microorganism culture medium. the carrier not only can carry high concentration content of effective microorganisms, but also has micropore and macropore crosslinking net structure and strong absorption and retention performance. the carrier combines large surface of micropore, permeability of large pore, hydrophilicity and strong adsorption in one, has a good affinity with microbes, high load capacity and stable structural performance. the product can be used in absorption of nitrogen and phosphorus pollutants and decomposition of organic matters in water, but also can be used as a lightweight versatile plant cultivation medium for cultivation of indoor plants and landscaping projects.
rewritten: the current invention describes a versatile polyurethane carrier for solidifying effective microorganisms. it is prepared by blending the following raw materials in the designated weight ratios: 15–30 parts polyether polyol, 5–15 parts polyester polyol, 20–35 parts isocyanate, 0.31–0.40 part catalyst, 0.8–1.0 part foam stabilizer, 5–15 parts foaming agent, and 30–50 parts (by dry weight) of an effective microorganism culture medium. this carrier not only supports a high concentration of active microorganisms but also features a crosslinked network structure with both micropores and macropores, providing excellent absorption and retention capabilities. combining a large micropore surface area with the permeability of macropores, along with hydrophilic and strong adsorption properties, it exhibits a high affinity for microbial life, robust load capacity, and stable structural performance. the product can be utilized for absorbing nitrogen and phosphorus pollutants and decomposing organic matter in water, and it also serves as a lightweight, multipurpose medium for cultivating indoor plants and for landscaping projects.

raw: henequen ( agave fourcroydes lem.) is a representative crop of the province of matanzas, cuba, which has been exploited extensively for the quality of its fibers. the present work aimed to characterize from the biochemical and histological point of view henequen plants cv. 'sac ki' in vitro acclimatized. the biochemical indicators analyzed were concentration of soluble phenols, total soluble carbohydrates, reducing sugars, soluble proteins and total chlorophylls, as well as the relative contents of flavonoids, terpins and anthocyanins. in addition, a histological analysis of the epidermis was performed and the results were compared with plants of one month in rooting culture medium. in vitro acclimatized plants were subsequently transferred to the ex vitro conditions. the contents of total soluble carbohydrates, reducing sugars and chlorophylls decreased, whereas the levels of soluble phenols, proteins and secondary metabolites were higher in relation to in vitro plants rooted for 30 days. the epidermis of the in vitro acclimatized plants showed a greater development of the stomatal apparatus that could positively affect the ex vitro acclimatization, where a survival rate of 87% and a reduction of the acclimatization time were obtained. keywords: chlorophyll, henequen, phenol, reducing sugar
rewritten: henequen (agave fourcroydes lem.) is a key crop in cuba’s matanzas province, renowned for the quality of its fibers. this study aimed to examine, from both biochemical and histological perspectives, the in vitro acclimatized henequen plants of the 'sac ki' cultivar. the biochemical parameters evaluated included the concentrations of soluble phenols, total soluble carbohydrates, reducing sugars, soluble proteins, and total chlorophylls, along with the relative amounts of flavonoids, terpenes, and anthocyanins. in addition, a histological study of the epidermis was conducted, and the results were compared with those obtained from plants grown for one month in a rooting culture medium. after in vitro acclimatization, the plants were transferred to ex vitro conditions. upon this transfer, the levels of total soluble carbohydrates, reducing sugars, and chlorophylls decreased, while the contents of soluble phenols, proteins, and secondary metabolites increased compared to the in vitro plants rooted for 30 days. furthermore, the epidermis of the acclimatized plants exhibited a more developed stomatal apparatus, which likely contributed to an 87% survival rate and a shorter acclimatization period under ex vitro conditions. keywords: chlorophyll, henequen, phenol, reducing sugar

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a manufacturing method of 2-pyrrolidone polymer capable of easily taking out 2-pyrrolidone polymer of high molecular weight from a reaction vessel with a high degree of freedom of shape.solution: in a manufacturing method of 2-pyrrolidone polymer, a mixture including 2-pyrrolidone and a basic polymerization catalyst is made to have contact with compressive fluid including a carbon dioxide to melt or dissolve the mixture, and then the 2-pyrrolidone is ring-opening polymerized.selected drawing: figure 3
rewritten: objective: develop a manufacturing method for 2‑pyrrolidone polymer that allows easy removal of high‑molecular‑weight polymer from a reaction vessel while providing significant flexibility in shape. in this method, a mixture of 2‑pyrrolidone and a basic polymerization catalyst is contacted with a compressive fluid that includes carbon dioxide to melt or dissolve the mixture, after which the 2‑pyrrolidone undergoes ring‑opening polymerization. (refer to figure 3.)

raw: the utility model discloses a prevent mine car that jolts, front end and rear end in the carriage are fixed respectively to carriage front bezel and carriage back plate, the upper portion right side fixedly connected with lamp plate of carriage front bezel, platform floor fixed connection is in the bottom in carriage, the bottom plate rotates with the carriage front bezel through the pivot to be connected, the left end fixedly connected with connecting rod of bottom plate, the center pin is through small -size connecting rod and bottom plate bottom middle part fixed connection, a connecting rod fixed right -hand member that sets up in the bottom plate bottom side, a connecting rod no. 2 and a connecting rod swing joint, the axletree passes through two fixed connection of the wheel connection pole and connecting rod, the wheel sets up the outside at the axletree, the axletree is through shock attenuation pull ring and well spindle connection, damping spring passes through the spring coupling pole and is connected with the bottom plate, little tire is disposed under the damping spring. this prevent mine car that jolts simple structure, convenient to use, the cost is low moreover, is fit for generally using.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a mine car designed to minimize jolting. in this design, the carriage’s front and rear ends are respectively fixed to the carriage front bezel and the carriage back plate. on the upper right side of the front bezel, a lamp plate is fixedly mounted. the bottom of the carriage features a platform floor that is firmly attached, and a bottom plate is rotatably connected to the front bezel via a pivot. on the left end of this bottom plate, a connecting rod is fixedly attached. additionally, a small connecting rod secures the middle bottom part of the bottom plate via a central pin. on the bottom side of the bottom plate, a right-hand member is fixed using another connecting rod. furthermore, a second connecting rod is swing-jointed with an additional connecting rod. the axletree is fixedly connected at two points: one with the wheel connection pole and another with a connecting rod, with the wheel mounted externally on the axletree. the axletree is also linked to a well spindle through a shock-absorbing pull ring. moreover, a damping spring, connected by a spring coupling pole to the bottom plate, supports a small tire located underneath it. this mine car is characterized by its simple structure, ease of use, low cost, and suitability for general applications.

raw: the invention relates to the technical field of glass substrate production, in particular to a method for repairing a crack of a glass substrate. the method includes the steps that firstly, the crack part of the surface of the glass substrate is coated with a bonding agent, and the bonding agent is made to permeate into the crack of the glass substrate; secondly, the part, coated with the bonding agent, of the glass substrate is subjected to curing treatment so that the parts, on the two sides of the crack, of the glass substrate can be bonded together; thirdly, a protecting layer is arranged at the crack part, treated in the second step, of the glass substrate. according to the method for repairing the crack of the glass substrate, the bonding agent is made to permeate into the crack through the capillary action to act together with small-power laser welding on the crack part of the glass substrate, the parts, at the two ends of the crack, of the ultra-thin glass substrate can be effectively bonded, and the roughness of the crack on the glass surface is greatly reduced, so that propagation of the crack is prevented, the utilization rate of the ultra-thin glass substrate is increased, and production cost is reduced.
rewritten: this invention belongs to the technical field of glass substrate production and, more specifically, relates to a method for repairing a crack in a glass substrate. the method comprises the following steps: 1. first, a bonding agent is applied to the cracked area of the glass substrate so that it penetrates into the crack. 2. second, the area where the bonding agent has been applied is subjected to a curing treatment, which bonds the two sides of the crack together. 3. third, a protective layer is placed over the treated crack area. by allowing the bonding agent to seep into the crack through capillary action and combining this process with low-power laser welding on the crack, the method effectively bonds the two ends of the ultra-thin glass substrate. this significantly reduces the roughness of the cracked surface, prevents the further propagation of the crack, increases the utilization rate of the ultra-thin glass substrate, and ultimately reduces production costs.

raw: this research presents a study of the human being from the perspective of christian anthropology, in which the relationship with god is essential for the achievement of every man, every woman and all mankind. the christian faith points out that the encounter between god and human happens unsurpassed way in jesus christ. it is through the central mystery of the son of the living god, in his incarnation, passion, death and resurrection, which reveals the divine plan for humanity. it is through him and the grace of the spirit, that the human being has the possibility of conformity to god and to share in his divine life, the ultimate goal of human life.
rewritten: this study examines the human being through the lens of christian anthropology, emphasizing that a relationship with god is fundamental for every person and all of humanity. according to the christian faith, the most unparalleled encounter between god and humanity occurs in jesus christ. it is through the profound mystery of the son of the living god—his incarnation, passion, death, and resurrection—that god's divine plan for humanity is revealed. through jesus and the enabling grace of the spirit, human beings are offered the chance to align themselves with god and partake in his divine life, which is considered the ultimate purpose of human existence.

raw: the meaning and attainment of influence, power, and success fre? quently provide conflicts and themes for fiction. in spite of the fact that female writers were necessarily concerned with these concepts, that readers of novels were predominantly women, and that the issues of the feminist controversy remained alive throughout the eighteenth century, critics have paid little attention to a central problem for the heroine of many early english novels by women: discovering appropriate and effec? tive ways to influence the men around her. in jane barker's love intrigues, the heroine galesia describes her girlhood experiences in order to divert a friend from the melancholy news of the war of the spanish succession:
rewritten: influence, power, and success often serve as sources of conflict and thematic material in fiction. even though female writers were naturally preoccupied with these ideas, with most novel readers being women and feminist issues remaining pertinent throughout the eighteenth century, critics have largely overlooked a key challenge faced by heroines in many early english novels by women: finding appropriate and effective ways to sway the men around them. in jane barker's love intrigues, the heroine, galesia, recalls her childhood experiences in an effort to distract a friend from the dismal news of the war of the spanish succession.

raw: the utility model provides an imitative ctenophore shape middle -size and small -size seawater desalination device in deep sea of six comb pieces, sets up six preliminary treatment and filters the comb pieces, and the preliminary treatment is filtered comb piece i and is allied oneself with the union coupling on a reverse osmosis pellicle subassembly section of thick bamboo through the pivot, and six preliminary treatment are filtered the comb pieces and are evenly arranged, install spring coupling between them respectively, constitute the resonator system from this, overall structure lays in three ten meter lower dark seabeds on the sea through the earth anchor, and under seawater pressure, the sea water allies oneself with the pipe through the pivot and gets into a reverse osmosis pellicle subassembly section of thick bamboo in getting into preliminary treatment filtration comb piece after its preliminary treatment is filtered, reverse osmosis pellicle subassembly section of thick bamboo emergence reverse osmosis output fresh water, and it gets into the water storage box through the force (forcing) pump pressurization, and the water storage box is exported water and is got into the sterilizer, and the sterilizer is through light water outlet pipe output fresh water, resonance takes place for the resonator system under of the springing up of sea water, shake off the preliminary treatment and filter the dirts such as marine alga on the comb piece, improves reverse osmosis efficiency simultaneously, and the dirt that comb piece and reverse osmosis membrane were filtered in the preliminary treatment is got rid of through the ultrasonic wave to the ultrasonic wave descaling ware.
rewritten: the utility model describes a seawater desalination device for deep-sea use that mimics a ctenophore’s shape and is available in medium and small sizes. the device features six comb-like components that serve as preliminary treatment and filtration units. in this design, each comb piece—such as comb piece i—is pivotally coupled via a union connector to a reverse osmosis membrane subassembly housed within a thick bamboo-like structure. these six treatment combs are arranged evenly and interconnected by spring couplings to form a resonator system. the entire assembly is anchored to the seabed at a depth of 30 meters using an earth anchor. under the pressure of the surrounding seawater, water is drawn through pipes via the pivot into the reverse osmosis subassembly after passing through the initial filtration by the comb pieces. fresh water is produced by the reverse osmosis process and is then forced by a pump into a storage tank. the stored water is subsequently transferred to a sterilizer and finally discharged through an outlet as purified fresh water. additionally, the resonator system—activated by the rising movement of seawater against the springs—facilitates the shaking off and removal of debris, such as marine algae, from the comb pieces. this action not only clears the combs and the reverse osmosis membrane of accumulated dirt but also enhances the overall efficiency of the reverse osmosis process by enabling subsequent cleaning through an ultrasonic descaling device.

raw: future internet-of-things (iot) will connect billions of small computing devices embedded in the environment and support their device-to-device (d2d) communication. powering this massive number of embedded devices is a key challenge of designing iot since batteries increase the devices' form factors and their recharging/replacement is difficult. to tackle this challenge, we propose a novel network architecture that integrates wireless power transfer and backscatter communication, called wirelessly powered backscatter communication (wp-bc) networks. in this architecture, power beacons (pbs) are deployed for wirelessly powering devices; their ad-hoc communication relies on backscattering and modulating incident continuous waves from pbs, which consumes orders-of-magnitude less power than traditional radios. thereby, the dense deployment of lowcomplexity pbs with high transmission power can power a largescale iot. in this paper, a wp-bc network is modeled as a random poisson cluster process in the horizontal plane where pbs are poisson distributed and active ad-hoc pairs of backscatter communication nodes with fixed separation distances form random clusters centered at pbs. furthermore, by harvesting energy from and backscattering radio frequency (rf) waves transmitted by pbs, the transmission power of each node depends on the distance from the associated pb. applying stochastic geometry, the network coverage probability and transmission capacity are derived and optimized as functions of the backscatter reflection coefficient and duty cycle as well as the pb density. the effects of the parameters on network performance are characterized.
rewritten: the future internet-of-things (iot) will interconnect billions of small computing devices embedded throughout our environments, enabling direct device-to-device (d2d) communications. one of the primary challenges in designing iot systems is powering such a vast number of devices, as traditional batteries tend to increase device size and are difficult to recharge or replace. to overcome this challenge, we propose an innovative network architecture that combines wireless power transfer with backscatter communication, termed wirelessly powered backscatter communication (wp-bc) networks. in this architecture, power beacons (pbs) are deployed to supply energy wirelessly to devices. these devices communicate by backscattering and modulating the continuous wave signals transmitted by the pbs—a process that consumes significantly less power than conventional radio communications. as a result, the deployment of numerous low-complexity pbs with high transmission power can effectively support a large-scale iot network. in our paper, we model the wp-bc network as a random poisson cluster process in the horizontal plane. here, pbs are distributed according to a poisson process, and active ad-hoc pairs of backscatter communication nodes—maintaining a fixed separation—form random clusters centered around the pbs. additionally, since each node harvests energy from and backscatters the radio frequency (rf) waves emitted by the pbs, its transmission power depends on its distance from the corresponding pb. applying stochastic geometry techniques, we derive and optimize both the network coverage probability and transmission capacity, taking into account parameters such as the backscatter reflection coefficient, duty cycle, and pb density. our analysis characterizes how these parameters influence overall network performance.

raw: maternal diet during pregnancy impacts foetal growth and development. in particular, dietary levels of methylating micronutrients (methionine, folate, choline, vitamins b6, and b12) interfere with the availability and allocation of methyl groups for methylation reactions, thereby influencing normal transcription. however, the currently recommended methylating micronutrient supplementation regimen is haphazard and arbitrary at best. to investigate the effects of a methylating micronutrient-rich maternal diet, pregnant pietrain sows were fed either a standard diet (con) or a diet supplemented with methionine, folate, choline, b6, b12, and zinc (met). foetal liver and muscle (m. longissimus dorsi) tissues were collected at 35, 63, and 91 days post-conception. transcriptional responses to diet were assessed in foetal liver. altered insulin-like growth factor (igf) signalling in transcriptome analyses prompted investigation of igf-2 and insulin-like growth factor binding proteins (igfbps) levels in muscle and liver. maternal diet enriched with methylating micronutrients was associated with increased foetal weight in late gestation. hepatic transcriptional patterns also revealed differences in vitamin b6 and folate metabolism between the two diets, suggesting that supplementation was effective. additionally, shifts in growth-supporting metabolic routes of the lipid and energy metabolism, including igf signalling, and of cell cycle-related pathways were found to occur in liver tissue in supplemented individuals. weight differences and modulated igf pathways were also reflected in the muscle content of igf-2 (increased in met) and igfbp-2 (decreased in met). maternal dietary challenges provoke stage-dependent and tissue-specific transcriptomic modulations in the liver pointing to molecular routes contributing to the organismal adaptation. subtle effects on late foetal growth are associated with changes in the igf signalling mainly in skeletal muscle tissue that is less resilient to dietary stimuli than liver.
rewritten: a maternal diet during pregnancy plays a crucial role in fetal growth and development. specifically, the levels of micronutrients involved in methylation—methionine, folate, choline, vitamins b6, and b12—affect the availability and distribution of methyl groups for methylation reactions, which in turn influence normal gene transcription. despite this, current guidelines for supplementing methylating micronutrients are rather inconsistent and arbitrary. to explore the effects of a maternal diet rich in these micronutrients, pregnant pietrain sows were assigned to one of two groups: one receiving a standard diet (con) and the other receiving a diet supplemented with methionine, folate, choline, vitamins b6 and b12, and zinc (met). researchers collected fetal liver and longissimus dorsi muscle samples at 35, 63, and 91 days post-conception. they examined the liver’s transcriptional response to the diet and, following the observation of altered insulin-like growth factor (igf) signaling in the transcriptome analysis, they also measured the levels of igf-2 and insulin-like growth factor binding proteins (igfbps) in both muscle and liver tissues. the findings revealed that a maternal diet enriched with methylating micronutrients was associated with increased fetal weight in late gestation. hepatic transcription patterns showed differences in vitamin b6 and folate metabolism between the two diets, suggesting effective supplementation. in addition, changes were observed in metabolic pathways related to lipid and energy metabolism, including igf signaling, as well as in cell cycle-related pathways in the liver of supplemented individuals. these alterations correlated with differences in muscle tissue, where igf-2 levels increased and igfbp-2 levels decreased in the supplemented group. overall, the study indicates that maternal dietary challenges trigger stage-dependent and tissue-specific changes in liver gene expression, highlighting molecular mechanisms that contribute to the organism’s adaptation. moreover, the subtle effects on late fetal growth appear to be mainly linked to modifications in igf signaling in skeletal muscle, which seems to be less resilient to dietary influences than the liver.

raw: background osteoporotic fractures impose significant morbidity, mortality and economic burden (1). earlier research showed low rates of secondary prevention for patients discharged from emergency department (ed) with a fracture (2). the fracture liaison service (fls) aimed to identify, review and then manage these patients following their discharge from the ed. objectives determine the economic benefits of a fls in a western australian hospital. methods patients aged >50yrs whom presented to the emergency department at sir charles gairdner hospital (scgh) after a fracture were invited to the fls. a retrospective control group from scgh and a prospective control group from fremantle hospital were the comparators. a health economic analysis from the payer9s perspective (wa health) examined recurrent fracture rates and quality of life (eq-5d). bottom-up costing included medications, investigations, gp visits and the cost of fracture determined by the literature or the ar-drg 2013/14 prices for hospital presentation and admission. the mean incremental cost effectiveness ratios were bootstrapped (5000 iterations). cost-effectiveness acceptability curves were generated and the willingness-to-pay was $50,000aud. results this fls reduced the rate of re-fracture compared to the retrospective cohort and other tertiary centre by 9.2 - 10.2%, equating to cost savings of approximately $750,168 - $810,400/1,000 patient-years in the first year compared to control cohorts. there was no difference in the qalys gained across groups over 12 months. the fls compared to scgh retrospective cohort had a mean incremental cost of $6,168 (95ci $133, $18,626) per 1% reduction in the 12 month recurrent fracture risk. the fls compared to other sites had a mean incremental cost of $6,782 (95ci -$2,562, $25,686) per 1% reduction in the 12 month recurrent fracture risk. the fls compared to scgh retrospective cohort had a mean incremental cost of $859 (95ci -$4,074, $4,864) per qaly gained at 12 months. the fls compared to other sites had a mean incremental cost of -$119 (95ci -$1,665, $700) per qaly gained at 12 months. figure 1 demonstrates that the fls is cost-effective in delivering a reduction in the fracture rate at 12 months. however, the qaly gained (eq-5d) was no different at 12 months between the study groups. conclusions this fls demonstrated to be effective in reducing rates or recurrent fracture(s) and resulted in significant cost effectiveness and cost-saving. references briggs am et al.(2015) hospitalisations, admission costs and re-fracture risk related to osteoporosis in western australia are substantial: a 10-year review. anz j public health. inderjeeth ca et al. (2010) a multimodal intervention to improve fragility fracture management in patients presenting to emergency departments. mja disclosure of interest none declared
rewritten: background: osteoporotic fractures create considerable health issues, including high levels of morbidity and mortality, as well as placing a heavy economic burden on healthcare systems (1). prior research revealed that patients discharged from the emergency department (ed) with a fracture experienced low rates of secondary prevention (2). in response, the fracture liaison service (fls) was established to identify, evaluate, and manage these patients once they left the ed. objective: the purpose of this study was to determine the economic benefits of implementing an fls at a hospital in western australia. methods: patients over the age of 50 who presented to the ed at sir charles gairdner hospital (scgh) with a fracture were invited to participate in the fls. their outcomes were compared with two control groups—a retrospective cohort from scgh and a prospective cohort from fremantle hospital. a health economic analysis was conducted from the payer’s perspective (wa health), focusing on recurrent fracture rates and quality of life as measured by the eq-5d. a bottom-up costing approach was used, which included expenses for medications, investigations, general practitioner visits, and fracture-related hospital costs based on either literature values or ar-drg 2013/14 pricing. mean incremental cost-effectiveness ratios (icers) were calculated using 5,000 bootstrap iterations, cost-effectiveness acceptability curves were produced, and a willingness-to-pay threshold of aud $50,000 was applied. results: the fls was associated with a 9.2% to 10.2% reduction in re-fracture rates compared to both the scgh retrospective cohort and the tertiary center control group, translating to cost savings of approximately aud $750,168 to $810,400 per 1,000 patient-years in the first year relative to controls. there was no difference in qalys gained (eq-5d) over a 12-month period across the groups. when compared with the scgh retrospective cohort, the fls had a mean incremental cost of aud $6,168 (95% ci: $133 to $18,626) per 1% reduction in the 12-month recurrent fracture risk. in comparison with the other sites, the mean incremental cost was aud $6,782 (95% ci: -$2,562 to $25,686) per 1% reduction in risk. regarding qalys, the fls had a mean incremental cost of aud $859 (95% ci: -$4,074 to $4,864) per qaly gained at 12 months versus the scgh retrospective cohort, and aud -$119 (95% ci: -$1,665 to $700) per qaly gained when compared to the other sites. figure 1 clearly demonstrates that the fls is a cost-effective method for reducing fracture rates at 12 months, despite no observed difference in qalys gained during that period. conclusions: this fls was effective in lowering rates of recurrent fractures and proved to be both cost-effective and cost-saving. references: • briggs am et al. (2015). "hospitalisations, admission costs and re-fracture risk related to osteoporosis in western australia are substantial: a 10-year review." anz j public health. • inderjeeth ca et al. (2010). "a multimodal intervention to improve fragility fracture management in patients presenting to emergency departments." mja. disclosure of interest: none declared.

raw: in this paper i explore the intersections of digital media, psychoanalysis, and the subject through reference to felix guattari's notion of the chaosmic spasm, which the italian philosopher franco berardi picks up in his work and most recently his book and. guattari's idea, which appears in his chaosmosis, designates the fatal acceleration of the media semio-universe towards spasm, or apocalyptic collapse, and the emergence of some new form of schizoid organisation. following an exploration of guattari's idea, and a broader discussion of its relationship to his wider thought and anti- oedipal collaborations with deleuze, i consider how bernard stiegler may be seen to provide a meta-historical account of guattari's concept of the spasm in his technics and time. according to stiegler's account, the hyper-acceleration of media forms results in a process of disorientation and de-subjectivization where the individual is destroyed because of their inability to situate themselves in social, symbolic, structures that have collapsed towards hyper-rational, meaningless forms. in lacanian terms, psychosis becomes a kind of generalised condition. in response to this generalisation of madness, stiegler imagines the need for a new politics of knowledge and grammar that is able to suture individuals back into wider social systems and provide the kind of secure, careful, space that d. w. winnicott writes about in his work on play. in order to unpack what this politics of culture and knowledge might comprise, i turn to guattari's work on schizoid subjectivity, or endless subjectivization without subjectivity, and berardi's contrast between financial semio-capitalism and poetry, in order to suggest an ethical mode of thought and cultural expression that might capture the political core of stiegler's vision and paint a picture of what human thought might look like on the other side of the new media apocalypse, the chaosmic spasm.
rewritten: this paper examines the intersections between digital media, psychoanalysis, and the subject by drawing on félix guattari's notion of the "chaosmic spasm"—a concept later taken up by the italian philosopher franco berardi in both his work and his most recent book. in guattari’s chaosmosis, the term refers to the fatal acceleration of the media semio-universe towards a state of spasm or apocalyptic collapse, which in turn gives rise to a new form of schizoid organization. after exploring guattari’s idea and discussing how it fits within his broader theoretical framework—as well as his anti-oedipal collaborations with deleuze—i turn to bernard stiegler’s work. stiegler is seen as offering a meta-historical account of guattari’s spasm concept through his exploration of technics and time. according to stiegler, the hyper-acceleration of media forms initiates a process of disorientation and de-subjectivization, effectively destroying the individual by stripping away their ability to locate themselves within social and symbolic structures that have collapsed into hyper-rational and meaningless forms. in lacanian terms, this makes psychosis resemble a generalized condition. in response to this widespread madness, stiegler envisions the need for a new politics of knowledge and grammar—one that can re-integrate individuals into larger social systems and create the kind of secure, nurturing environment that d. w. winnicott describes in his work on play. to further explore what this politics of culture and knowledge might look like, i consider guattari’s work on schizoid subjectivity—characterized as endless subjectivization without a stable subject—and berardi’s contrast between financial semio-capitalism and poetry. this analysis aims to propose an ethical mode of thought and cultural expression capable of capturing the political essence of stiegler’s vision, and to outline what human thought might become in the aftermath of the media apocalypse defined by the chaosmic spasm.

raw: it has been recently shown that ultrathin spiral metamaterials can support localized spoof plasmon modes whose resonant wavelength is much larger than the size of the structure. here, an analytical model is developed to describe the electromagnetic properties of the two-dimensional version of these devices: a perfect conducting wire corrugated by spiral grooves. the emergence of localized spoof plasmons in this geometry is quantitatively investigated. calculations show that these modes can be engineered through the spiral angle and the number of grooves. the theory also allows us to elucidate the contribution of magnetic and electric localized spoof plasmons to the optical response of these metamaterial devices. finally, experimental evidence of the existence of these modes in extremely thin textured copper disks is also presented.
rewritten: recent research has demonstrated that ultrathin spiral metamaterials can support localized spoof plasmon modes with resonant wavelengths far greater than the structure's dimensions. in this work, an analytical model is introduced to describe the electromagnetic behavior of a two-dimensional variant of these devices—a perfectly conducting wire featuring spiral grooves. the study quantitatively investigates the emergence of localized spoof plasmons in this configuration, with calculations indicating that their properties can be tuned by adjusting the spiral angle and the number of grooves. moreover, the theory clarifies the individual contributions of magnetic and electric localized spoof plasmons to the metamaterials’ optical response. finally, experimental evidence is provided to confirm the presence of these modes in extremely thin, textured copper disks.

raw: the utility model discloses a power distribution cabinet has solved small -size debris and has put into when the documents pouch both sides, is difficult for taking out, can't see whether the small -size debris of documents pouch both sides are deposited from the documents pouch, and its technical scheme main points are that in the middle of the bag body of documents pouch keep to the side department of boundary and respectively be equipped with a long waist hole in both sides, the utility model discloses a power distribution cabinet can make various paper and file easily get and put to whether survey has depositing of small -size debris.
rewritten: the utility model introduces a power distribution cabinet designed to resolve issues related to small debris and difficulty in retrieving items from dual-sided document pouches. previously, debris accumulation on either side of the pouch made it hard to remove documents and prevented users from seeing if debris had collected inside. the innovative solution involves aligning a long waist opening on each side of the pouch body, adjacent to the side boundaries. this design allows for easy access to various papers and files, regardless of any debris that may have accumulated.

raw: quantitative methods emphasize objective measurements and the statistical, mathematical, or numerical analysis of data collected through polls, questionnaires, and surveys, or by manipulating pre-existing statistical data using computational techniques.
rewritten: quantitative methods rely on objective measurements and involve the statistical, mathematical, or numerical analysis of data gathered through surveys, questionnaires, and polls, or through the manipulation of existing statistical data using computational techniques.

raw: regulator of g protein signaling (rgs) proteins modulate the complex signaling pathways elicited by g protein coupled receptor activation. recent studies have implicated rgs proteins in the development and progression of multiple cancers. provided herein are inhibitors of rgs17, compositions thereof and methods of treating diseases using the inhibitors.
rewritten: regulator of g protein signaling (rgs) proteins influence the complex pathways activated by g protein coupled receptors. recent research has connected rgs proteins to the onset and advancement of various cancers. here, inhibitors targeting rgs17 are provided, along with compositions containing these inhibitors and methods for treating diseases using them.

raw: the utility model discloses an amplification control ware circuit, including diode d1, resistance r1, triode vt1, electric capacity c1, triode vt2, inductance l and triode vt3, the diode d1 positive pole vcc that connects, triode vt1 base and resistance r1 are connected respectively to diode d1 negative pole, ground resistance r2 and triode vt2 base are connected respectively to the resistance r1 other end, triode vt1 projecting pole is connected to triode vt2 projecting pole, triode vt1 collecting electrode connecting resistance r3, the resistance r3 other end is connecting resistance r6 and inductance l respectively, the resistance r6 other end vcc that connects, the inductance l other end is connecting resistance r5 respectively, triode vt3 collecting electrode and electric capacity c2, output vo is connected to the electric capacity c2 other end. the utility model discloses amplification control ware circuit adds the totem -pote circuit of compriseing triode vt1 and vt2 at amplifying triode vt3 front end, the effectual enlarged efficiency that improves triode vt3, and circuit structure is very simple, and the cost is lower, and market competition is strong.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an amplification control circuit that comprises diode d1, resistor r1, triode vt1, capacitor c1, triode vt2, inductor l, and triode vt3. in this circuit, the positive terminal (vcc) of diode d1 is connected to the rest of the circuit, while the base of triode vt1 and resistor r1 are respectively connected to the negative terminal of diode d1 and ground. resistor r2 and the base of triode vt2 both connect to the opposite end of resistor r1. the anodes (plates) of triodes vt1 and vt2 are interconnected, and the collector of triode vt1 is connected through resistor r3; the other end of resistor r3 is linked to both resistor r6 and inductor l. resistor r6 then connects to vcc, and the other end of inductor l is connected to resistor r5. meanwhile, the collector of triode vt3 is connected to capacitor c2, with the output voltage vo taken from the opposite end of c2. in addition, the amplification control circuit incorporates a totem-pole network—including triodes vt1 and vt2—at the input stage of the amplifying triode vt3. this configuration effectively enhances the efficiency of triode vt3 while maintaining a simple circuit structure, reducing costs, and offering strong market competitiveness.

raw: background assessing and evaluating mental health status can provide educational planners valuable information to predict the quality of physicians' performance at work. these data can help physicians to practice in the most desired way. the study aimed to evaluate factors affecting psychological morbidity in iranian emergency medicine practitioners at educational hospitals of tehran. methods in this cross sectional study 204 participants (emergency medicine residents and specialists) from educational hospitals of tehran were recruited and their psychological morbidity was assessed by using a 28-question goldberg general health questionnaire (ghq-28). somatization, anxiety and sleep disorders, social dysfunction and depression were evaluated among practitioners and compared to demographic and job related variables. results two hundreds and four participants consisting of 146 (71.6%) males and 58 (28.4%) females were evaluated. of all participants, 55 (27%) were single and 149 (73%) were married. most of our participants (40.2%) were between 30-35 years old. by using ghq-28, 129 (63.2%) were recognized as normal and 75 (36.8%) suffered some mental health disorders. there was a significant gender difference between normal practitioners and practitioners with disorder (p=0.02) while marital status had no significant difference (p=0.2). only 19 (9.3%) declared having some major mental health issue in the previous month. conclusion females encountered more mental health disorders than male (p=0.02) and the most common disorder observed was somatization (p=0.006).
rewritten: background: assessing mental health status provides educational planners with crucial insights to predict how well physicians perform their duties. these insights, in turn, can guide physicians toward the most effective practices. this study sought to investigate the factors contributing to psychological morbidity among iranian emergency medicine practitioners at tehran’s educational hospitals. methods: in this cross-sectional study, 204 emergency medicine residents and specialists from tehran’s educational hospitals participated. researchers evaluated their psychological morbidity using the 28-item goldberg general health questionnaire (ghq-28), which measured somatization, anxiety and sleep disorders, social dysfunction, and depression. these outcomes were then compared with various demographic and job-related variables. results: among the 204 participants, 146 (71.6%) were male and 58 (28.4%) were female. of these, 55 (27%) were single and 149 (73%) were married, with the largest age group (40.2%) being between 30 and 35 years. based on the ghq-28 scores, 129 participants (63.2%) were considered normal, whereas 75 (36.8%) experienced some form of mental health disorder. a significant difference was found between genders regarding the prevalence of disorders (p = 0.02), although marital status did not show a significant impact (p = 0.2). additionally, only 19 participants (9.3%) reported having encountered a major mental health issue in the past month. conclusion: the findings indicate that female practitioners were more likely to encounter mental health disorders than their male counterparts (p = 0.02), with somatization emerging as the most common disorder among the group (p = 0.006).

raw: in this paper, we will explore and develop an embedded real time system and associated algorithms that enable an aggregation of limited resource, low-quality, projectionenabled mobile devices to collaboratively produce a higher quality video stream for a superior viewing experience. such a resource aggregation across multiple projector enabled devices can lead to a per unit resource savings while moving the cost to the aggregate. the pico-projectors that are embedded in mobile devices such as cell phones have a much lower resolution and brightness than standard projectors. tiling (putting the projection area of multiple projectors in a rectangular array overlapping them slightly around the boundary) and superimposing (putting the projection area of multiple projectors right on top of each other) multiple of such projectors, registered via automated registration through the cameras residing within those mobile devices, result in different ways of aggregating resources across these multiple devices. evaluation of our proof-of-concept system shows significant improvement for each mobile device in two primary factors of bandwidth usage and power consumption when using a collaborative federation of projection-embedded mobile devices. to the best of our knowledge, this is the first time aggregation of resources across a federation of low-cost, low power mobile devices can be achieved completely and automatically in real-time that can result in a viewing experience of as high as 4k (3840x2160) content with integrated four mobile devices playing 1080p content.
rewritten: this paper investigates and develops an embedded, real-time system along with the necessary algorithms to allow a group of resource-limited, lower-quality mobile devices equipped with projectors to work together and generate a high-quality video stream, resulting in an enhanced viewing experience. by aggregating resources from multiple projector-enabled devices, significant per-unit cost savings can be achieved, shifting the expense to the collective system. mobile devices' built-in pico-projectors generally offer considerably lower resolution and brightness compared to conventional projectors. techniques such as tiling (arranging the projection areas of multiple projectors into a rectangular grid with slight overlaps at the boundaries) and superimposing (overlaying the projection areas of several projectors) are employed, with automated registration via the devices’ onboard cameras ensuring alignment. our proof-of-concept evaluation demonstrates notable improvements for each mobile device regarding bandwidth usage and power consumption when operating within a collaborative network of projection-embedded mobile devices. to our knowledge, this represents the first instance where resources are aggregated completely and automatically in real time from a network of low-cost, low-power mobile devices to achieve a viewing experience as high as 4k (3840x2160), even when four mobile devices are each presenting 1080p content.

raw: we consider hybrid systems with lti continuous dynamics under collaborative control, that is, for which some events and inputs are controlled solely by a human operator and other events and inputs are controlled by the automation. the user-interface is a device through which the output of the system can be observed, and inputs from the human can be initiated. we model the user as an observer with additional requirements beyond a standard (automated) observer. we state conditions for user-observability and user-predictability to evaluate whether a given user-interface provides the user with adequate information to complete a desired task. we apply these conditions to two examples in aircraft flight management systems.
rewritten: we analyze hybrid systems with lti continuous dynamics that operate under collaborative control—where certain events and inputs are exclusively managed by a human operator, and others by automation. the user interface serves as the medium for both viewing the system's output and initiating human inputs. in our model, the user functions as an observer who has additional requirements beyond those of a standard (automated) observer. we introduce criteria for user-observability and user-predictability to determine if a given user interface supplies sufficient information for the user to accomplish a specific task. these criteria are then applied to two case studies in aircraft flight management systems.

raw: the present invention relates to a refrigerator with a defrosting device. the refrigerator with a defrosting device comprises: a refrigerator main body; an evaporator provided to the refrigerator main body; a defrost heater provided to a lower side of the evaporator to generate heat when power is applied to the defrost heater; and a heat pipe of which one side is disposed to transmit the heat to one side of the defrost heater and the other side is extended upward in the vertical direction of the evaporator to transmit the heat to the evaporator. therefore, the present invention can reduce defrost time and power consumption.
rewritten: this invention relates to a refrigerator equipped with a defrosting mechanism. the refrigerator includes a main body, an evaporator housed within the main body, a defrost heater positioned on the lower side of the evaporator that emits heat when energized, and a heat pipe. one end of this heat pipe is arranged to transfer heat to a side of the defrost heater, while its other end extends upward in alignment with the evaporator to deliver heat to it. as a result, this invention is capable of reducing both defrost time and power consumption.

raw: erythema elevatum diutinum (eed) is a rare form of vasculitis characterized clinically by red-violet brown papules, plaques, and nodules mainly involving the extensor surfaces; histologically by leukocytoclastic vasculitis in early lesions, and fibrosis and cholesterolosis in late lesions. eed has been associated with many systemic disorders including infections, autoimmune disorders, and both benign and malignant hematological disorders. as it is a rare form of vasculitis and only 250 cases reported till date, we report a case of eed in association with iga monoclonal gammopathy with partial response to dapsone treatment.
rewritten: erythema elevatum diutinum (eed) is an uncommon type of vasculitis that typically presents with red, violet, or brown papules, plaques, and nodules, most often on the extensor surfaces. in its early stages, biopsies show leukocytoclastic vasculitis, while later lesions reveal fibrosis and cholesterol deposition. eed has been linked to several systemic conditions, including infections, autoimmune diseases, and various hematologic disorders, both benign and malignant. due to its rarity—only about 250 cases have been documented—we present a case of eed associated with iga monoclonal gammopathy, which showed a partial response to dapsone treatment.

raw: the utility model discloses a commercial vehicle hill start auxiliary system air -operated controller solenoid valve. including one or two tee bend normally closed valve (100) and one diaphragm valve (200), air inlet (3) of two tee bend normally closed valve (100) are used for communicating with each other with gas holder (1), its gas outlet (12) are used for communicating with each other with brake chamber (20), its gas vent (19) are used for communicating with each other with the atmosphere, its valve pocket (18) communicate with each other through last valve port (8) and gas outlet (12), communicate with each other through lower valve port (10) and gas vent (19), diaphragm valve (200) include guide's air chamber (7) and admit air diaphragm (4), guide's air chamber (7) communicate with each other through orifice (11) and valve pocket (18), admit air diaphragm (4) with guide's air chamber (7) and air inlet (3) isolation, when the diaphragm that admits air (4) is in the closed condition, can make air inlet (3) and gas outlet 12 communicate with each other.
rewritten: the utility model relates to a hill start auxiliary system for commercial vehicles that uses an air-operated controller solenoid valve. the system comprises one or two tee-bend normally closed valves (100) and one diaphragm valve (200). the air inlet (3) of the tee-bend normally closed valve (100) communicates with the gas holder (1), while its gas outlet (12) connects to the brake chamber (20), and the gas vent (19) is linked to the atmosphere. the valve pocket (18) connects with the gas outlet (12) via a last valve port (8) and with the gas vent (19) via a lower valve port (10). the diaphragm valve (200) includes a guide’s air chamber (7) and an admit-air diaphragm (4). the guide’s air chamber (7) communicates with the valve pocket (18) through an orifice (11), and the admit-air diaphragm (4) is isolated from both the guide’s air chamber (7) and the air inlet (3). when the admit-air diaphragm (4) is in the closed condition, this arrangement allows communication between the air inlet (3) and the gas outlet (12).

raw: in the last 15 years, financial markets experienced a kind of tsunami effect, as the risk first disappeared from the markets like water from the oceans only to return with a vengeance at the start of the crisis in the form of a destructive tidal wave. such developments were accompanied and fostered by swings in the approach to banking regulation. in the pre-crisis decades politicians and policy-makers adopted too optimistic view of the self-regulation, market discipline and risk management, and, in effect, regulatory framework got too lenient. after the crisic, the corrective and effectively opposite stance has been adopted. both basel iii rules and further initiatives should contribute positively to the stability of financial systems. the experience obtained thus far supports the view that the banks that are better capitalised and have higher loss absorbency have a capacity to weather the adverse shocks coming from gobal financial environment much better.
rewritten: over the past 15 years, financial markets have undergone a tsunami-like event: risk seemed to vanish as if swallowed by the ocean, only to return forcefully at the beginning of the crisis like a crushing tidal wave. this phenomenon coincided with frequent shifts in banking regulation approaches. in the decades preceding the crisis, politicians and policy-makers were overly optimistic about self-regulation, market discipline, and risk management, resulting in a regulatory framework that was too lenient. following the crisis, authorities adopted a corrective, essentially opposite, stance. both basel iii regulations and subsequent initiatives are expected to contribute to a more stable financial system. experience so far indicates that better-capitalized banks with more robust loss absorption capabilities are significantly more resilient against adverse shocks from the global financial landscape.

raw: the invention relates to a well mouth device with two purposes of self-seepage type groundwater recharging and pumping, and belongs to the technical field of water retention and crossing with water. the well mouth device comprises a recharging well lid and a pumping device. the well mouth device is characterized in that the recharging well lid is composed of a well opening head cover, a pumping device fixing ring beam and a geotextile inverted filter layer. the overall well lid is shaped like a hollow-core bottomless cylinder; a circular orifice surrounded by the pumping device fixing ring beam is formed in the middle of the top face of the upper portion of the well mouth head cover; circular water inlets are formed in the top face and the side face of the upper portion of the well mouth head cover respectively; the lower portion of the well mouth head cover adopts a watertight body; the geotextile inverted filter layer is arranged on the outer side of the well mouth and is consistent with the shape of the well mouth. the pumping device is composed of a water suction pump, a pump water pipe, a pump support and a pump valve. the water suction pump usually adopts a submersible pump; the pump support is a steel frame and is fixed on the pumping device fixing ring beam. by means of the well mouth device, organic combination of groundwater recharging and pumping is achieved, and the well mouth device has the advantages of being large in quantity of water recharge and capable of filtering particles in water, preventing silt, resisting rush and preventing low water level sewage recharging.
rewritten: the invention pertains to a well-mouth device designed for both self-seepage groundwater recharging and pumping, placing it within the technical realm of water retention and cross-flow. the device consists of a recharging well lid and a pumping component. notably, the recharging well lid is made up of a well opening head cover, a ring beam that secures the pumping device, and a geotextile inverted filter layer. the entire well lid is constructed as a hollow, bottomless cylinder; on the upper section of the head cover, it features a centrally located circular opening encircled by the ring beam, along with circular water inlets on both its top and side surfaces. the lower section of the head cover is built as a watertight body, and the geotextile inverted filter layer, which matches the shape of the well mouth, is set on its exterior. the pumping component includes a water suction pump—typically a submersible pump—a pump water pipe, a steel frame support fixed to the ring beam, and a pump valve. this integrated well-mouth device enables a seamless combination of groundwater recharging and pumping, offering benefits such as high recharge capacity, effective particle filtration to prevent silt, resistance to surge effects, and avoidance of low water level sewage during recharge.

raw: the invention discloses a linear engineering three-dimensional geological modeling method. three-dimensional geological modeling methods and technologies are immature due to engineering geological condition complexity and cognition limitation. present three-dimensional geological modeling business software is mainly for the industries such as oil reservoirs and mines, and is seldom based on linear engineering characteristics; in addition, the workload of man-machine interaction is greater and can not meet large scale engineering application requirements. based on the characteristics of linear engineering prospective design, the method mainly involves a vertical section segment, a horizontal-vertical section segment and a special geological structure (a fault, etc), and includes first setting an interest region entity, establishing a geological interface model, and diving into geologic bodies through boolean operation, thereby avoiding possible topological problems by employing surface closed body formation; besides, the model is an entity, is in favor of late stage simulation, design and engineering workload calculation, has the characteristics of small man-machine interaction workload and strong model controllability, and can meet large scale application demands.
rewritten: the invention introduces a linear engineering three-dimensional geological modeling method. due to the complex nature of engineering geological conditions and limitations in understanding, current three-dimensional geological modeling technologies are still in their infancy. existing three-dimensional geological modeling software tends to be tailored for industries such as oil reservoirs and mining, rarely addressing the specific needs of linear engineering; furthermore, the significant man-machine interaction required makes them unsuitable for large-scale engineering projects. this method, designed with the characteristics of linear engineering in mind, primarily deals with a vertical section, a horizontal-vertical section, and specialized geological structures (like faults). it begins by defining an area of interest, then establishes a geological interface model, and divides geologic bodies using boolean operations—this process minimizes potential topological issues by forming closed surfaces. additionally, because the resulting model is entity-based, it supports subsequent simulations, designs, and calculations of engineering workload, while reducing the need for extensive man-machine interaction and offering high model controllability, making it well-suited for large-scale applications.

raw: the utility model relates to a mould positioning mechanism, including base plate and the positioning mechanism of symmetry setting in the base plate both sides, positioning mechanism includes first locating plate, second locating plate, pivot, connecting block, slide cylinder, compresses tightly cylinder and clamp plate, first locating plate and second locating plate link to each other, just all can revolute the axle and rotate, the junction of first locating plate and second locating plate links to each other with the connecting block, the connecting block links to each other with slide cylinder, it is equipped with the recess to be located first locating plate and second locating plate one side on the base plate respectively, be equipped with in the recess and compress tightly the cylinder, it has set firmly the clamp plate to compress tightly the cylinder upper end for it is tight with first locating plate and second location plate holder, the utility model discloses its simple structure, convenient to use can be rapidly effectually advances line location to some anomalous moulds, and need not carry out frequent change positioning mechanism, has satisfied the processing demand of actual production, has higher practical value.
rewritten: the utility model pertains to a mold positioning mechanism that consists of a base plate and a positioning mechanism that is symmetrically arranged on both sides of the base plate. this positioning mechanism comprises a first locating plate, a second locating plate, a pivot, a connecting block, a slide cylinder, a clamping cylinder, and a clamp plate. the first and second locating plates are interconnected, allowing them to rotate around an axle. the connecting block connects to the junction of the first and second locating plates and further links to the slide cylinder. on one side of the base plate, there are recesses designed to house the first and second locating plates respectively. additionally, each recess is equipped with a clamping cylinder. a clamp plate is firmly attached to the upper end of the clamping cylinder to secure the first and second locating plates together. this utility model is characterized by its simple structure and ease of use, enabling rapid and effective positioning of irregular molds without the need for frequent adjustments. it meets the processing requirements of actual production and possesses significant practical value.

raw: the invention discloses a one-piece four-gun charging pile comprising a cabinet. the upper ends of the front and back sides of the cabinet are each symmetrically provided with two operation displays. indicator lamps are arranged above the operation displays. a charging card swiping area is arranged below each operation display. an emergency stop button is arranged between the two displays on the front side of the cabinet, and an emergency stop button is arranged between the two displays on the back side of the cabinet. the lower ends of the left and right sides of the cabinet are each connected with two charging cables, the front end of each charging cable is equipped with a charging gun, and each charging gun is equipped with a handle. the upper ends of the left and right sides of the cabinet are each provided with two charging gun placing mouths, and the charging guns are placed in the charging gun placing mouths. the charging pile is reasonable in structure design, is provided with four charging guns, can charge four electric vehicles at the same time, realizes charging of four electric vehicles through one machine, has a quick charging mode and a slow charging mode, can be used to perform quick charging through one gun and perform uniform charging through multiple guns, and meets the using demand. the floor area of the charging pile is saved, the number of charging guns is increased, and waiting is avoided.
