rewritten: the invention describes a one-piece charging pile with four charging guns, all housed in a single cabinet. at the top of both the front and back sides of the cabinet, two operation displays are symmetrically arranged, with indicator lamps positioned above them. directly below each display is an area for swiping a charging card. between the two displays on both the front and back sides, there is an emergency stop button. at the lower ends of the cabinet’s left and right sides, two charging cables are connected; each cable ends at a charging gun that comes with a handle. additionally, the upper ends of these sides feature two designated slots where the charging guns are stored when not in use. this innovative design efficiently integrates four charging guns into one unit, allowing simultaneous charging of four electric vehicles. it offers both fast and slow charging modes—enabling quick charging through a single gun as well as balanced, simultaneous charging via multiple guns—to meet various user needs. as a result, the charging pile minimizes floor space usage while increasing the number of available charging points and reducing waiting times.

raw: the paper aims to contribute to studies that complicate the analysis of transitions between education and work. thus, are analyzed devices including socio-educational and job seeking to intervene in the subjectivity of people who participate in them, after improving opportunities for socio-labor inclusion. two cases are presented, with particular features: the youth program for more and better jobs and a vocational training center, both based in two municipalities of argentina. the cases are approached from a double perspective: institutional through the regulations governing them and the voices of their managers, and the speech of participants subjects themselves.
rewritten: this paper seeks to add to research that deepens the analysis of the transition from education to work. it examines mechanisms such as socio-educational programs and job-seeking initiatives aimed at influencing the subjectivity of participants, ultimately enhancing their socio-labor inclusion opportunities. two distinct cases are discussed: a youth program focused on creating more and improved job opportunities, and a vocational training center, both situated in different municipalities in argentina. these cases are explored from two perspectives: the institutional view, which considers the regulatory framework and insights from their managers, and the personal perspective, which brings in the narratives of the participants themselves.

raw: in a number of ecg systems the acceptable trace is still selected manually. an able technician decides whether a trace is good enough for final selection. this process at times can be error prone. in case of telemedicine, sometimes automatic detection of the acceptable trace is necessary, as there may not be available personnel to select an acceptable trace manually. thus an algorithm for detecting acceptable trace automatically can be a simple yet very important aspect of an ecg system in certain conditions. in this paper a method is proposed to detect an ecg acceptable trace automatically. information on amplitude and position of qrs complexes are acquired by applying pan-tompkins algorithm and based on those a decision making algorithm is developed which compares the normalized amplitude and position index of the qrs complexes with the raw ecg signal. the analysis leads to rejecting ecg signals which deviate from isoelectric line and accepts an ecg signal for tracing only when it is free of any such deviation.
rewritten: in many ecg systems, the acceptable trace is still selected manually by a skilled technician who judges if a tracing is suitable for final analysis, a process that can sometimes be prone to error. in telemedicine situations, automatic detection of an acceptable trace is sometimes required because available personnel may not be present. therefore, an algorithm that can automatically detect an acceptable trace can be a simple yet crucial component of an ecg system under specific conditions. in this paper, we propose a method to automate the detection of an acceptable ecg trace. the method involves using the pan-tompkins algorithm to extract information regarding the amplitude and position of the qrs complexes. a decision-making algorithm is then developed, which compares the normalized amplitude and position index of these qrs complexes with the raw ecg signal. this analysis results in the rejection of ecg signals that deviate from the isoelectric line, accepting an ecg tracing only when it is free from such deviations.

raw: we propose a method for adding visual details to fluid animation while reducing noisy appearances. in grid-based fluid simulations, an issue is that while highly detailed fluids with small eddies can be obtained by increasing the number of grid cells, it costs much more computational time. to address this, various methods for adding details (or up-scaling resolutions) have been proposed. those methods can generate fine animations quickly by adding high-frequency noises or external forces to coarse simulation results. however, those methods typically generate tiny eddies on a whole surface of fluid and the result appears too noisy. in this paper, we consider the distribution of kinetic energy in the spatial frequency domain and then apply it to two existing methods for adding details. by using our method, noises or external forces can be added to the appropriate positions of fluids and consequently natural-looking details can be achieved.
rewritten: we introduce an approach to enhance visual details in fluid animations while minimizing noisy effects. in grid-based fluid simulations, a common challenge is that increasing the grid resolution to capture small eddies and fine details significantly raises computational costs. to address this, several methods have been developed to upscale resolution or add details by injecting high-frequency noise or external forces into coarse simulation data, producing detailed animations rapidly. however, these techniques often result in the formation of tiny eddies across the entire fluid surface, making the overall appearance excessively noisy. in our work, we analyze the distribution of kinetic energy in the spatial frequency domain and apply this understanding to two existing detail-enhancement methods. as a result, our method enables the addition of noise or external forces to the appropriate locations within the fluid, leading to more naturally looking details.

raw: the invention discloses a labor-saving trolley which comprises a trolley frame assembly and a wheel assembly. the trolley frame assembly comprises a carrying platform and a hand-pushing frame. the hand-pushing frame is fixedly arranged on the carrying platform. the labor-saving trolley is characterized by further comprising a transmission assembly. the wheel assembly comprises two driving wheels and two driven wheels. the transmission assembly comprises a gearbox, a vertical transmission component, a horizontal transmission component and a rocker component. the rocker component is used for inputting power which is then amplified after passing through the gearbox, the vertical transmission component and the horizontal transmission component, labor is saved when the trolley is used, and the labor-saving trolley can conveniently climb; and labor is greatly saved, and work efficiency is improved.
rewritten: the invention presents a labor-saving trolley that consists of a trolley frame assembly and a wheel assembly. the trolley frame assembly includes a carrying platform and a fixed hand-pushing frame mounted on the platform. additionally, the trolley features a transmission assembly. the wheel assembly is made up of two driving wheels and two driven wheels. the transmission assembly comprises a gearbox, a vertical transmission component, a horizontal transmission component, and a rocker component. the rocker component serves as the power input, which is then amplified through the gearbox and the vertical and horizontal transmission components. this design reduces the physical effort required during use, allows the trolley to climb with ease, and overall enhances work efficiency.

raw: the utility model provides a portable medical orthopedics driving system battery case, including battery case (1), battery interface seat, its characterized in that: still include flip (2), be equipped with switching device (3) on this flip (2). switching device 3 is last including driver plate, reset spring, and this switching device is the concave surface, driver plate, spring are placed in the concave surface. the utility model is suitable for a surgery electrical driven system.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a portable battery case for a medical orthopedics driving system. the battery case comprises a battery interface seat and a flip cover (2) to which a switching device (3) is attached. the switching device (3) includes a driver plate, a reset spring, and a concave surface that houses both the driver plate and the spring. this design is particularly applicable to electrically driven surgical systems.

raw: a natural vegetable fiber towel is produced from, by weight, 25-45 parts of cotton fibers, 0.3-0.6 parts of an antioxidant, 4-7.2 parts of polysulfonamide, 5-10 parts of corn protein fibers, 4-6 parts of a wheat straw pulp, 2-4 parts of an expanding agent, 2-7 parts of glycerin, 5-9 parts of isopropanol, 6-11 parts of methylene blue, 3-6 parts of zncl, 4-7 parts of starch, 2-4 parts of a mold release agent, 8-13 parts of a silane coupling agent, 2-6 parts of an organic pigment or a food pigment, and 4-8 parts of spun silk. the natural vegetable fiber towel has the advantages of very good antibiosis effect, very good elasticity, high tensile strength, improvement of the softness of fibers, and strong deformation recovery capability.
rewritten: a natural vegetable fiber towel is manufactured using, by weight, 25–45 parts of cotton fibers; 0.3–0.6 parts of an antioxidant; 4–7.2 parts of polysulfonamide; 5–10 parts of corn protein fibers; 4–6 parts of wheat straw pulp; 2–4 parts of an expanding agent; 2–7 parts of glycerin; 5–9 parts of isopropanol; 6–11 parts of methylene blue; 3–6 parts of zncl; 4–7 parts of starch; 2–4 parts of a mold release agent; 8–13 parts of a silane coupling agent; 2–6 parts of an organic or food pigment; and 4–8 parts of spun silk. this towel offers significant benefits, including excellent antibacterial properties, great elasticity, high tensile strength, improved fiber softness, and remarkable deformation recovery.

raw: the giant congenital lobar emphysema is a rare malformation infant pathology. the authors report a similar case which is distinguished by its segmental location even rare with its compressive character in which segmentectomy was successful performed to lift emergency distress in a developing country.
rewritten: giant congenital lobar emphysema is a rare congenital malformation seen in infants. the authors describe a similar case that, despite its uncommon segmental location and compressive characteristics, was successfully managed with a segmentectomy to urgently relieve respiratory distress in a developing country.

raw: teaching public internatio al law in united states univ rsities is heavily influe ced by six factors: the disti ctive status of the law schools in academia, the perceived power of he nited states in foreign affairs, the co plexity of its foreign affairs process, the philosophical traditio s of pragmat sm and legal realism, the p pulari y of moot court competiti n, and, above all, the rol of published coursebooks in legal education. the result, generally speaking, is that public int rnational aw is taught primaily in the professional schools s a broadly-defined curriculum t at tends to h ghlight the interplay between national a d international decisionmaking, to rely on problem-solving techniques of instruction, and to focus on daily assignme ts of aterial draw from any of only a handful of significant coursebooks. necessarily, then, the following comme tary will devote substantial attention to these books and rather less attention to s b-
rewritten: teaching public international law at united states universities is significantly shaped by six factors: the unique standing of law schools in academia, the perceived influence of the united states in global affairs, the complexity of its foreign policy processes, the philosophical traditions of pragmatism and legal realism, the popularity of moot court competitions, and, most notably, the impact of published coursebooks in legal education. overall, public international law is predominantly taught in professional schools through a broadly defined curriculum that emphasizes the interaction between national and international decision-making, relies on problem-solving instructional techniques, and centers on daily assignments drawn from only a select few influential coursebooks. consequently, this commentary will concentrate extensively on these books while providing relatively less discussion on other topics.

raw: freshwater triclads, viz. dendrocoelum lacteum (mull.), bdellocephala punctata (dallas), planaria torva m. schultze, phagocata vitta (duges), crenobia alpina (dana) or dugesia tigrina (girard), a recently discovered american species, although only small numbers of some species, particularly planaria torva have been examined. earlier workers (see keiser 1921 for references) have recorded it from dendrocoelum lacteum and planaria torva but it is evident that the epizoite thrives mainly on hosts of the genus polycelis. the biology and especially the population ecology of urceolaria mitra have been described in some detail (reynoldson 1950, 1951, 1955) and it is clear that the ciliate- utilizes the host-produced water currents for feeding purposes and does no direct harm to the host. the life-cycle is straightforward, the epizoite remaining the greater part of its life on the host where it grows and multiplies. however, a small proportion of the population is continually leaving the host and these urceolaria reattach after a few hours' free life, usually to another host. in this way urceolaria infests the host's progeny. when polycelis tenuis and p. nigra occur together in a pond the former always has a markedly higher population of urceolaria. the explanation of this disparity in terms of population dynamics is the main problem tackled here. it can be said at the outset that careful examination of specific characters such as the number of denticles in the skeletal ring, number of striae per denticle, etc. (faure-fremiet 1943) showed that there were no anatomical differences between the urceolaria on the two hosts. three explanations with different biological emphases seem possible. first, the two epizoic populations may represent biological races and exhibit rigorous host specificity. second, host specificity may not be absolute and some exchange may occur but nevertheless urceolaria may be able to thrive better on one host i j.a.e.
rewritten: freshwater triclads—such as dendrocoelum lacteum (müller), bdellocephala punctata (dallas), planaria torva m. schultze, phagocata vitta (dugès), crenobia alpina (dana), and dugesia tigrina (girard), along with a recently discovered american species (although only a few specimens, particularly of planaria torva, have been studied)—have been noted by earlier researchers (see keiser 1921 for references) to host this organism in dendrocoelum lacteum and planaria torva. however, it is now clear that the epizoite mainly favors hosts of the genus polycelis. the biology and, in particular, the population ecology of urceolaria mitra have been detailed extensively (reynoldson 1950, 1951, 1955). the ciliate uses the water currents generated by its host for feeding and does not directly harm the host. its life cycle is straightforward: the epizoite spends the majority of its life on the host, where it grows and reproduces, yet a small portion of the population continuously departs. these individuals, after several hours of free life, reattach—usually to a different host—thereby eventually infesting the host’s offspring. moreover, when polycelis tenuis and polycelis nigra are found together in a pond, the former consistently hosts a significantly larger population of urceolaria. explaining this disparity in population dynamics is the main focus of this study. initial examinations of specific anatomical features—such as the number of denticles in the skeletal ring and the number of striae per denticle (faure-fremiet 1943)—have revealed no anatomical differences between the urceolaria found on the two hosts. three potential explanations, each emphasizing different biological aspects, are considered. first, the two epizoic populations might represent distinct biological races that exhibit strict host specificity. second, host specificity may not be absolute, and some exchange could occur, even though urceolaria still appears to thrive better on one particular host.

raw: the etiology of peripheral precocious puberty(ppp) is complex and varied, which is mainly categorized into either genetic or acquired disorders.albright-mccune syndrome and familial male-limited precocious puberty are the 2 most important genetic ppps.the purposes of therapy are to halt pubertal development and restore sex steroids to prepubertal values and delayed rate of skeletal maturation in order to maximize height potential.the several major categories of clinical drugs for ppp include anti estrogen/androgen, estrogen receptor blocker, aromatase inhibitors and cytochrome p450 inhibitors and so on. key words: peripheral precocious puberty; treatment; mccune-albright syndrome; familial male-limited precocious puberty
rewritten: the causes of peripheral precocious puberty (ppp) are diverse and complex, generally stemming from either genetic or acquired disorders. the two most significant genetic forms of ppp are albright-mccune syndrome and familial male-limited precocious puberty. treatment aims to halt the progression of puberty, reduce sex steroid levels to a prepubertal state, and slow skeletal maturation to help maximize adult height. the main categories of drugs used in managing ppp include anti-estrogen/androgen agents, estrogen receptor blockers, aromatase inhibitors, and cytochrome p450 inhibitors, among others. key words: peripheral precocious puberty; treatment; mccune-albright syndrome; familial male-limited precocious puberty.

raw: the structure of bacterial biofilms depends on environmental conditions, such as availability of nutrients, during biofilm formation. in turn, variations in biofilm structure in part reflect differences in bacterial motility during early biofilm formation. pseudomonas aeruginosa deprived of nutrients remain dispersed on a surface, whereas cells supplemented with additional nutrients cluster and form microcolonies. at the single-cell scale, how bacteria modify their motility to favour distinct life cycle outcomes remains poorly understood. high-throughput algorithms were used to track thousands of p. aeruginosa moving using type-iv pili (tfp) on surfaces in varying nutrient conditions and hence identify four distinct motility types. a minimal stochastic model was used to reproduce the tfp-driven motility types. we report that p. aeruginosa cells differently deploy tfp to alter the distribution of motility types under different nutrient conditions. bacteria preferentially crawl unidirectionally under nutrient-limited conditions, but preferentially stall under nutrient-supplemented conditions. motility types correlate with subcellular localisation of fimx, a protein required for tfp assembly and implicated in environmental response. the subcellular distribution of fimx is asymmetric for unidirectional crawling, consistent with tfp assembled primarily at the leading pole, whereas for non-translational types fimx expression is symmetric or non-existent. these results are consistent with a minimal stochastic model that reproduces the motility types from the subcellular average concentration and asymmetry of fimx. these findings reveal that p. aeruginosa deploy tfp symmetrically or asymmetrically to modulate motility behaviours in different nutrient conditions and thereby form biofilms only where nutrients are sufficient, which greatly enhances their competitive capacity in diverse environments.
rewritten: the architecture of bacterial biofilms is influenced by environmental factors—especially nutrient availability—during their formation. in turn, differences in biofilm structure partly mirror variations in bacterial motility during the early stages of development. for instance, pseudomonas aeruginosa lacking nutrients stays spread out over a surface, while cells with extra nutrients tend to cluster and form microcolonies. at the level of single cells, it is still not well understood how bacteria adjust their movement to produce different life cycle outcomes. researchers used high-throughput algorithms to track thousands of p. aeruginosa cells moving on surfaces with type-iv pili (tfp) under various nutrient conditions, which allowed them to identify four distinct motility behaviors. a simple stochastic model was then employed to replicate these tfp-driven movement types. the study found that p. aeruginosa cells use tfp in different ways to change the distribution of motility types depending on nutrient availability. under nutrient-limited conditions, bacteria preferentially crawl in one direction, while under nutrient-rich conditions, they tend to remain stationary. these motility patterns correlate with the subcellular localization of fimx, a protein essential for tfp assembly and involved in environmental responses. when cells exhibit unidirectional crawling, fimx is asymmetrically located, mainly at the leading pole, but for non-translational movements, fimx is either symmetrically distributed or absent. these observations align with the stochastic model that links the average concentration and asymmetry of fimx at the subcellular level to the motility types. overall, the findings indicate that p. aeruginosa modulates tfp deployment—either symmetrically or asymmetrically—to control its motility in response to nutrient conditions, thereby forming biofilms only where nutrients are sufficient, which enhances its competitive abilities in various environments.

raw: auxetic materials are solids that exhibit negative poisson's ratio. due to its uniqueness, it is expected that auxetic solids and structures manifest certain mechanical responses that are distinct from conventional materials. so far classical plate theory (cpt) has been used to investigate the behavior of thin auxetic plates and, for thick auxetic plates, the first-order shear deformation theory (fsdt) has been recently employed. the third-order shear deformation theory (tsdt) is adopted herein as it employs a realistic transverse shear stress and strain distribution across the plate cross section, and therefore removes the need for a correction factor. the results obtained herein reveal that as a plate material's poisson's ratio becomes more negative, the plate deflection characteristic mimics that of (a) reduced plate thickness, (b) reduced plate aspect ratio, and (c) reduced load waviness. the results of this investigation support (i) the use of auxetic materials in applications where there is a need to reduce the extent of transverse shear deformation, and (ii) the use of the simpler cpt instead of any shear deformation plate theory if the poisson's ratio of the plate material is sufficiently negative.
rewritten: auxetic materials are solids that exhibit a negative poisson's ratio. because of this unique property, auxetic solids and structures are expected to show mechanical responses that differ from those of conventional materials. to date, classical plate theory (cpt) has been used to study thin auxetic plates, while the first-order shear deformation theory (fsdt) has more recently been applied to thick auxetic plates. in this work, the third-order shear deformation theory (tsdt) is used because it provides a realistic distribution of transverse shear stress and strain across the plate's cross-section, eliminating the need for any correction factor. the findings indicate that as a plate material’s poisson's ratio becomes more negative, the plate's deflection behavior increasingly resembles that of a plate with (a) reduced thickness, (b) a lower aspect ratio, and (c) less load waviness. consequently, this investigation supports (i) the use of auxetic materials in applications that require minimized transverse shear deformation and (ii) the choice of the simpler classical plate theory over more complex shear deformation theories when the material's poisson's ratio is sufficiently negative.

raw: parents of infants in the neonatal intensive care unit may often find themselves seeking healthcare information from online and social media sources. social media applications are available to healthcare consumers and their families, as well as healthcare providers, in a variety of formats. information that parents gather on their own, and information that is explained by providers, is then used when parents make healthcare decisions regarding their infants. parents also seek support from peers and family while making healthcare decisions. the combination of knowledge obtained and social support given may empower the parent to feel more confident in their decision making. healthcare professionals can guide parents to credible resources. the exchange of information between providers and parents can occur using a variety of communication methods. misperceptions can be corrected, support given, open sharing of information occurs, and parent empowerment may result.
rewritten: parents of infants in the neonatal intensive care unit often turn to the internet and social media for healthcare information. these platforms are accessible not only to families and patients but also to healthcare providers, offering content in various formats. both self-sourced information and explanations provided by professionals serve as the basis for parents' healthcare decisions regarding their infants. additionally, parents frequently seek guidance and emotional support from family and peers during this process. when combined, the knowledge acquired and the support received can empower parents, increasing their confidence in decision-making. healthcare professionals can play a vital role by directing parents to trustworthy resources, and various communication methods facilitate the exchange of information between providers and parents. this open dialogue allows for the correction of misconceptions, the provision of support, and ultimately, enhances parental empowerment.

raw: sylvia wynter has often stated, quoting aime cesaire, that all her life she has been writing a single poem. indeed, poetic may be the most precise term to describe her life and work, the two having become virtually indistinguishable. being born in cuba, where her parents had migrated as labourers, inserted wynter into a dynamic of the western world system that she would later decipher in her scholarly work the flow of labour and capital and the realisation of the basic standard of living of third world peoples. her subsequent education as a british colonial subject in jamaica also became a significant stimulus for her thought. here, the anti-colonial reversal of taught values raised fundamental questions about the nature of history and the discourses by which societies produce and maintain order (see keith walker for detailed discussion). a series of scholarships later allowed wynter to pursue undergraduate and postgraduate studies in english and the renaissance/golden age literature of spain at king's college, london university as well as the university of madrid. as patricia fox notes, this training formed "the bedrock of [wynter's] musings on the transitional moment from the medieval to the modern." with studies behind her, she embarked on her first career: to be a writer. like most of her counterparts in the group of london-based caribbean writers, wynter was determined not only to reconfigure the stereotype of the "backward" and "primitive native" projected on all non-white colonised peoples, but also to challenge a specific and unique
rewritten: sylvia wynter has frequently remarked—citing aimé césaire—that throughout her life she has been composing one continuous poem. in fact, describing her life and work as poetic captures their inherent unity, making them nearly indistinguishable from one another. born in cuba to parents who had immigrated as laborers, wynter was thrust into the heart of the western world system—a system she would later analyze in her academic research on the flow of labor and capital, as well as the ways in which the basic standard of living is achieved for people in the third world. her education in jamaica, under british colonial rule, further energized her intellectual pursuits. there, the anti-colonial challenge to traditional values prompted her to question the nature of history and the societal discourses that enforce and sustain order (refer to keith walker for an in-depth discussion). later, a sequence of scholarships enabled wynter to undertake undergraduate and postgraduate studies in english, along with the renaissance and golden age literature of spain, at king’s college, university of london, and at the university of madrid. as patricia fox observes, this academic background laid “the bedrock of [wynter’s] musings on the transitional moment from the medieval to the modern.” with her academic studies completed, wynter embarked on her first career: that of a writer. like many of her fellow london-based caribbean authors, she was committed not only to dismantling the stereotype of the “backward” and “primitive native” imposed on all non-white, colonized peoples, but also to challenging a uniquely specific set of assumptions.

raw: reading strategy interventions relying upon peer tutoring are a common way to foster poor readers' comprehension skills. those interventions are based on the assumption that tutees benefit from the (higher) reading skills of their tutors. however, this interpersonal effect has not yet been tested explicitly because the effectiveness of peer tutoring is commonly analyzed separately for tutees and tutors or with aggregated data. the present study illustrates how interpersonal and individual effects of a peer-tutored reading strategy intervention can be estimated using dyads as units of analysis in structural equation modeling. to demonstrate the application of the model, data from an experimental intervention study comparing the effects of a peer-tutored reading strategy training and a control condition in grade 4 (n = 68 dyads) were analyzed.
rewritten: reading strategy interventions based on peer tutoring are frequently used to enhance the comprehension skills of struggling readers. these interventions rely on the premise that tutees gain from the higher reading abilities of their tutors. however, this interpersonal effect has not been directly tested because most studies analyze the effectiveness of peer tutoring by examining tutees and tutors separately or by using aggregated data. in this study, we demonstrate how both interpersonal and individual effects of a peer-tutored reading strategy intervention can be estimated by treating dyads as the unit of analysis within a structural equation modeling framework. to illustrate the model's application, we analyzed data from an experimental intervention study with fourth-grade students (n = 68 dyads) comparing a peer-tutored reading strategy training with a control condition.

raw: the utility model provides an end down, it includes first fin, second fin and third fin, and one end and the middle part of third fin of first fin are connected, and the first medial surface of the extending direction of first fin and third fin belongs to the first angle of plane one -tenth, the one end and the middle part of third fin of second fin are connected, and the second medial surface of the extending direction of second fin and third fin place plane becomes the second angle, the lateral surface of third fin is in on the same horizontal plane. the utility model provides a zip fastener. the utility model discloses a third fin of end down and corresponding zip fastener through setting up the lateral surface and being in same horizontal plane can better of the preventing be being withheld the in -process down with onlying and is being cracked, and is descending whole outward appearance only better.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an “end down” component that comprises a first fin, a second fin, and a third fin. in this design, one end and the middle section of the third fin are connected to the first fin, and the primary medial surface along the extending direction of the first and third fins forms a first angle relative to a designated plane. similarly, one end and the middle section of the third fin are connected to the second fin, with the corresponding medial surface along the extending direction of the second and third fins forming a second angle on the same plane. moreover, the lateral surface of the third fin is arranged in a horizontal plane. the utility model also provides a zip fastener. by incorporating a third fin with a lateral surface positioned on the same horizontal plane, the design helps prevent issues such as unwanted holding or cracking during the descent process, and it improves the overall external appearance when descending.

raw: the invention relates to a traction driving isochronism speed governor and a multiple-driving-shaft transmission adopting the same. the multiple-driving-shaft transmission comprises a speed governor chamber and a variable-speed gear chamber. according to the invention, the power of an engine is transmitted to an output set of the transmission in a traction driving manner by the isochronism speed governor in the speed governor chamber, a plurality of roller sets are arranged between an input part and an output part of the speed governor in a planetary manner and the output part pushes against the roller sets to move axially so as to be in contact with the input part, therefore, the effect of transmission of power is realized; and meanwhile, the shaft of all driving sets in the variable-speed gear chamber are arranged in parallel at unequal shaft intervals around the shaft of the output set and by taking the shaft of the output set as a center, so that number of gears of the transmission can be increased in limited space. through adopting the traction driving isochronism speed governor and implementing the technical means of arranging shafts of all driving sets, the problems of low transmission efficiency of a transmission, clutch abrasion and larger occupied area during speed change gear increase in the prior art are improved.
rewritten: the invention discloses a traction driving isochronism speed governor and a multi-driving-shaft transmission that incorporates it. this transmission features a speed governor chamber and a variable-speed gear chamber. in the system, the engine’s power is transferred to an output set using the traction drive provided by the isochronism speed governor within the speed governor chamber. in this chamber, multiple sets of rollers are arranged in a planetary configuration between an input part and an output part; the output part applies force against these rollers, causing them to move axially into contact with the input part, thus enabling power transmission. additionally, in the variable-speed gear chamber, the shafts of all driving sets are arranged in parallel at irregular intervals around the output set’s shaft, using this shaft as the center. this layout allows for an increased number of gears within a limited space. by using the traction driving isochronism speed governor and the method of arranging the driving set shafts, the invention resolves issues found in previous designs—namely, the low transmission efficiency, clutch wear, and larger space requirements during gear changes.

raw: the present invention relates to a composite beam structure having a reinforcement structure according to the size of moment generation, and a construction method using same, the composite beam structure comprising a column formed of flanges and a web, an upper flange, a lower flange spaced apart in parallel to the upper flange, and the web connecting the upper flange and the lower flange, and the composite beam structure comprising a beam horizontally installed in between the column, a joining part having a flat plate shape so as to be surface-joined to the flange of the column, and a box-shaped reinforcement part connected with the joining part and having an opening part formed on the upper part thereof so as to enable concrete to be poured therein, and the composite beam structure comprising a connecting bracket installed between the column and the beam, a coupling means for combining the joining part and the joining surface of the column in order to join, through rigid joint, the joining part and the flange of the column, and concrete poured in predetermined thicknesses into the reinforcement part.
rewritten: the present invention concerns a composite beam system that features a reinforcement structure designed according to the size of moment generation and includes a construction method using the same. the composite beam system consists of a column made up of a web that connects an upper flange and a lower flange (which are arranged in parallel), and a beam that is installed horizontally between such columns. it also comprises a joining element with a flat-plate shape that is surface-attached to the column’s flange, and a box-shaped reinforcement component connected to the joining element and having an upper opening that allows concrete to be poured inside. additionally, the system includes a connecting bracket positioned between the column and the beam, along with a coupling mechanism that unites the joining element with the column’s flange via a rigid joint, and concrete is then poured into the reinforcement component at predetermined thicknesses.

raw: attractiveness of a face plays an important role in many social endeavors. it influences careers like digital entertainment, modeling and acting, as well as person's career prospect, financial status, and personal relationships. computational approaches to exploring the nature and components of face attractiveness have been proposed, and have become an emerging topic in facial analysis research. integrating techniques from image processing, computer vision and machine learning, this subarea aims to develop computational methods to quantify and investigate the attractiveness of a face. this paper summarizes the most recent advances in four related aspects of face attractiveness: (a) facial attractiveness prediction, (b) facial attractiveness enhancement, (c) lateral facial attractiveness and (d) 3d facial attractiveness. the motivations, innovative techniques, and significant results are summarized and discussed. the open problems in these areas and directions for future work are also briefly stated.
rewritten: the appeal of a face significantly impacts many social spheres. it not only affects careers in areas such as digital entertainment, modeling, and acting but also influences an individual’s career opportunities, economic standing, and personal relationships. researchers have proposed computational methods to delve into the nature and factors of facial attractiveness, making it a rising focus in facial analysis studies. by combining image processing, computer vision, and machine learning, this particular field strives to create objective methods for evaluating and understanding facial appeal. this paper reviews the latest developments in four key areas related to facial attractiveness: (a) predicting facial attractiveness, (b) enhancing facial attractiveness, (c) lateral facial attractiveness, and (d) 3d facial attractiveness. it outlines the motivations, innovative approaches, and noteworthy findings in these domains, and briefly discusses the current challenges and possible directions for future research.

raw: the invention discloses an imaging lens, an iris imaging module and an iris identification device, and belongs to the field of biological identification. from front to back of a light incidence direction, the imaging lens sequentially comprises: a first lens, wherein the first lens is a double-convex lens with positive power, the front surface is a convex surface, and the back surface is a convex surface; a second lens, wherein the second lens is a double-concave lens with negative power, the front surface is a concave surface, and the back surface is a concave surface; a third lens, wherein the third is a convex-concave lens with positive power, the front surface is a convex surface, and the back surface is a concave surface; a fourth lens, wherein the fourth lens is a double-convex lens with positive power, the front surface is a convex surface, and the back surface is a convex surface; and a fifth lens, wherein the fifth lens is a convex-concave lens with negative power, the front surface is a convex surface, and the back surface is a concave surface. the third lens and the fourth lens are glued together. the imaging lens provided by the invention is good in imaging quality, wide in collection range, convenient to use and simple in structure.
rewritten: the invention relates to the field of biological identification and discloses an imaging lens, an iris imaging module, and an iris identification device. in the light incidence direction (from front to back), the imaging lens is composed sequentially of the following elements: • a first lens: a double-convex lens with positive power, having both a convex front surface and a convex back surface. • a second lens: a double-concave lens with negative power, featuring a concave front surface and a concave back surface. • a third lens: a convex-concave lens with positive power, with its front surface convex and its back surface concave. • a fourth lens: a double-convex lens with positive power, with both the front and back surfaces being convex. • a fifth lens: a convex-concave lens with negative power, characterized by a convex front surface and a concave back surface. additionally, the third and fourth lenses are bonded together. this imaging lens design offers excellent imaging quality, a broad collection range, ease of use, and a simple structure.

raw: background/aims: clinically, there is lack of predictors for diabetic nephropathy (dn) in diabetes mellitus (dm) without microalbuminuria, macroalbuminuria or ret
rewritten: background/aims: clinically, there are currently no established predictors for diabetic nephropathy (dn) in patients with diabetes mellitus (dm) who do not present with microalbuminuria, macroalbuminuria, or retinopathy.

raw: in this brief, a new modulation scheme called sample-and-hold asynchronous sigma-delta modulation (sh-asdm), the extension of the classical asynchronous sigma-delta scheme (asdm), is introduced. the sh-asdm is aimed to provide an instantaneous time encoding instead of the mean value encoding realized in the asdm. this eliminates the effect of the dynamic range reduction of output pulses lengths, which appears for high-frequency input signals in asdm. sh-asdm outperforms the asdm by about 3 db in the signal-to-noise ratio quantization characteristics, in respect to the worst case input signal. the new analytic model of asdm is also proposed, which decomposes this type of time-encoding into a linear filtering, sampling, and nonlinear sample transformation.
rewritten: this brief introduces a new modulation scheme—sample-and-hold asynchronous sigma-delta modulation (sh-asdm)—which extends the classical asynchronous sigma-delta modulation (asdm). unlike the asdm that uses mean value encoding, the sh-asdm provides instantaneous time encoding. this approach eliminates the reduction in output pulse dynamic range observed with high-frequency input signals in the asdm. in the worst-case scenario, the sh-asdm demonstrates approximately a 3 db improvement in signal-to-noise ratio quantization performance compared to the asdm. additionally, a new analytical model for the asdm is presented that breaks its time-encoding process into three stages: linear filtering, sampling, and a nonlinear sample transformation.

raw: curvature sensing is an intensity-based technique for wavefront reconstruction using two defocused images located on the opposite sides of the focal plane. it requires either one detector placed at two consecutive axial locations or a dual path with a pair of detectors from which the sensor signal is obtained. the method yields sensitivity comparable to that of the hartmann test in the adjustment and evaluation of ground-based optical telescopes. we introduce the analytical framework underlying the function of a curvature sensor which operates from a single defocused image. a series of twin images is computed from the propagation law of the mutual intensity along the optical axis. the polynomial decomposition of the wavefront allows retrieval of zernike coefficients by means of the standard least-squares algorithm. the paper concludes with a review of image sampling requirements and a discussion on the signal-to-noise ratio.
rewritten: curvature sensing is an intensity-based method for reconstructing the wavefront using two defocused images taken from opposite sides of the focal plane. it can be implemented either with one detector positioned at two consecutive axial locations or with a dual-path system employing a pair of detectors to acquire the sensor signal. this technique offers sensitivity on par with the hartmann test, which is commonly used for adjusting and evaluating ground-based optical telescopes. in this work, we outline the analytical framework that explains how a curvature sensor operates using a single defocused image. a series of paired images is generated based on the propagation law of mutual intensity along the optical axis. by decomposing the wavefront using polynomials, the zernike coefficients can be obtained via a standard least-squares algorithm. the paper concludes with a review of image sampling requirements and a discussion of the signal-to-noise ratio.

raw: the complete mitochondrial nucleotide sequence of the bama miniature pig was sequenced in this study. this sequence is homologous to that of the other vertebrate mitochondrial genome. the mitochondrial genome of the bama miniature pig is 16,711 bp and contains 37 genes including 22 trna genes, 13 protein-coding genes (pcgs), 2 ribosomal rna genes and 1 control region (d-loop). the 13 pcgs initiated with atn as start codon and terminated with taa, ta or t as stop codon. the dna sequence in the bama miniature pig d-loop region contains the repeat motif (-tacacgtgcg-) and 5'nucleotide of the first repeat is at the position 796 bp.
rewritten: in this study, researchers sequenced the complete mitochondrial dna of the bama miniature pig. its sequence is homologous to those found in other vertebrate mitochondrial genomes. the mitochondrial genome, measuring 16,711 base pairs, comprises 37 genes: 22 trna genes, 13 protein-coding genes (pcgs), 2 ribosomal rna genes, and 1 control region (d-loop). each of the 13 pcgs begins with an atn start codon and concludes with taa, ta, or t as the stop codon. additionally, the d-loop region contains a repeat motif (-tacacgtgcg-), with the first repeat starting at nucleotide position 796.

raw: familial mediterranean fever (fmf) is the most common mendelian autoinflammatory disease, characterized by uncontrolled activation of the innate immune system that manifests as recurrent brief fever and polyserositis (e.g., peritonitis, pleuritic, and arthritis). fmf is caused by autosomal recessive mutations of the mediterranean fever gene, mefv which encodes the pyrin protein. although fmf predominantly affects people from mediterranean and middle eastern ethnic origins, 3 cases of fmf have been reported in korea since 2012. we report another case of fmf in korea in which the patient presented with a month-long fever without serositis. after treatment with colchicine was initiated, the patient's symptoms quickly subsided. the response to colchicine was helpful for diagnosis. we compare the fmf genotypes in korea with in other countries. studying fmf cases in korea will help establish the best mefv exons to use for screening and diagnosis of korean fmf.
rewritten: familial mediterranean fever (fmf) is the most common mendelian autoinflammatory disorder, marked by the uncontrolled activation of the innate immune system. this results in recurrent, short-lived fevers and polyserositis, such as peritonitis, pleurisy, and arthritis. fmf is caused by autosomal recessive mutations in the mefv gene, which encodes the pyrin protein. although fmf primarily affects individuals of mediterranean and middle eastern descent, three cases have been reported in korea since 2012. in this report, we detail an additional korean case where the patient experienced a month-long fever without evidence of serositis. the patient’s symptoms quickly subsided after starting colchicine, and the favorable response to this treatment helped in making the diagnosis. we also compare the fmf genotypes found in korea with those identified in other countries. studying fmf cases in korea will assist in establishing the optimal mefv exons for screening and diagnosing fmf in the korean population.

raw: slot optical waveguides gained considerable attention since their invention. in an soi slot optical waveguide, the slot region is guarded by two high index slabs. the schematic structure results in considerable optical power confinement inside the slot region due to electric field discontinuity at the boundary between slot region and the slab region. hence it is pertinent to mention repeatedly that in an soi slot optical waveguide, the slot is defined only by the presence of slabs on both sides. where the refractive index contrast ratio between slot region and slab region will have a direct impact on the power confined inside the slot region. in this work, we have used the most basic structure of soi slot optical waveguide with air cladding and its impact on solar power concentration for the conventional photo voltaic solar cells.
rewritten: slot optical waveguides have attracted significant interest since their development. in an soi slot optical waveguide, two high-index slabs flank the slot region. this configuration leads to a strong confinement of optical power within the slot, owing to the discontinuity of the electric field at the interface between the slot and the slabs. it is important to note that, in an soi slot optical waveguide, the slot is characterized solely by the presence of these adjacent slabs, and the refractive index contrast between the slot and the slabs directly affects the degree of power confinement in the slot. in this study, we employ the simplest soi slot optical waveguide structure with air cladding to examine its influence on concentrating solar power for conventional photovoltaic cells.

raw: the invention discloses a metal composition detection and transmission system which can avoid interference to signals under complicated measurement conditions and is low in setting and maintaining cost. the system comprises a measuring gun, wherein one end of the measuring gun is connected with a probe, the other end of the measuring gun is detachably connected with a data acquisition module, the data acquisition module receives an analog signal sent by the probe, converts the analog signal into a digital signal and outwards transmits the digital signal, and the wirelessly transmitted digital signal is received by a data receiving device. the data acquisition module is driven by an independent power supply battery module without an external power supply device, thus, the overall detection device is not required to be connected with the outside through a wire or a data wire, the data signal is converted from the analog signal into the digital signals in the data acquisition module and is sent, influence of a cable to the signal accuracy can be reduced, and the measurement result is more accurate. similarly, the given metal composition detection and transmission method can realize the same technical effect.
rewritten: this invention presents a metal composition detection and transmission system designed to minimize signal interference even in complex measurement environments while keeping installation and maintenance costs low. the system includes a measuring gun with a probe attached at one end and a data acquisition module detachably connected at the other. the probe sends an analog signal to the data acquisition module, which then converts the signal into digital format for transmission. a data receiving device wirelessly receives this digital signal. the data acquisition module is powered by a dedicated battery, eliminating the need for an external power supply. as a result, the overall detection device does not require physical cables or wired connections, reducing the impact of cabling on signal accuracy and ensuring more precise measurements. additionally, the metal composition detection and transmission method described achieves the same technical effects.

raw: the utility model discloses an automatic high -voltage board of dehumidification, the intelligent cabinet temperature adjusting device comprises a cabinet body, the right side of the cabinet body is equipped with the air intake, the air intake outside is equipped with from hanging down formula shutter air port, the left side of the cabinet body is equipped with the air outlet, the inboard of air outlet is equipped with fan assembly, the outside of air outlet is equipped with heat radiation grille, heat radiation grille's the outside also is equipped with from hanging down formula shutter air port, the inside top of the cabinet body is equipped with temperature -sensing ware and humidity inductor, the inside lower extreme of the cabinet body is equipped with the switch board, be equipped with temperature controller and unlimited sending?module on the switch board, the upper end of the cabinet body is equipped with alarm device, the internal subordinate end of cabinet still is equipped with bamboo carbon box, the inside of the cabinet body is equipped with the workstation that is used for installing high voltage circuit breaker, the inside of the cabinet body is equipped with the slide rail, the outside of slide rail is fixed is equipped with the chain, the workstation is installed on the slide rail, be provided with the motor on the workstation, the motor through retarder connection have with chain complex gear. the utility model discloses simple structure, dehumidification are effectual, and dustproof heat dispersion is strong, and the security is high.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an automatic high-voltage dehumidification board. the intelligent cabinet temperature regulation device includes a cabinet body with an air intake located on its right side. a hanging formula shutter air port is installed on the exterior of this air intake. on the left side of the cabinet body is an air outlet, which houses a fan assembly internally; a heat radiation grille is mounted externally on the air outlet, and it too has a hanging formula shutter air port attached to its outside. near the top interior of the cabinet are installed temperature-sensing equipment and a humidity sensor, while a switch board is located at the bottom of the cabinet. this switch board is equipped with a temperature controller and an unlimited sending module. additionally, an alarm device is mounted at the upper end of the cabinet, and a bamboo carbon box is positioned at the lower subordinate end. inside the cabinet, a workstation designed for installing a high-voltage circuit breaker is mounted on a slide rail, which is fitted externally with a fixed chain. the workstation itself carries a motor that is connected through a retarder to a chain complex gear. overall, the utility model features a simple structure, effective dehumidification, strong dustproof heat dispersion, and high security.

raw: lipids when exogenously applied are known to cause various changes in ros levels produced within plants. they can either be beneficial to the plant when not stimulating the overproduction of ros thus resulting in improved germination and development or on the contrary, increasing the level of ros produced, causing oxidative stress and thus leading to cell death of the plant. in this study, we report that a saturated fatty acid known as ma increased the germination percentages of brassica napus l. seedlings when applied at a low concentration. when applied at higher concentrations, it was shown that elevated levels of ros within the seedlings occurred therefore leading to a decrease in germination percentage as well as stunting of seedling growth. physiological experiments such as biomass and cell death determination were conducted to further elucidate the effects of ma on the seedlings. biochemical assays were performed to determine the oxidative state of specific ros such as superoxide (o2-) and hydrogen peroxide (h2o2).
rewritten: when applied from an external source, lipids can induce various changes in the production of reactive oxygen species (ros) in plants. depending on their effects, these compounds may either benefit the plant by not overproducing ros—thereby enhancing germination and development—or they may trigger an increase in ros levels, which leads to oxidative stress and subsequent cell death. in this study, we demonstrate that a saturated fatty acid, referred to as ma, boosted the germination rate of brassica napus l. seedlings when used at a low concentration. however, at higher concentrations, ma elevated ros levels in the seedlings, resulting in reduced germination rates and stunted seedling growth. additionally, physiological experiments, including biomass measurement and cell death assays, were conducted to further understand ma’s impact on the seedlings. biochemical tests were also carried out to assess the oxidative status of specific ros, such as superoxide (o2-) and hydrogen peroxide (h2o2).

raw: the invention discloses a movable type transfer assembly used for a stamping mechanical hand. the movable type transfer assembly comprises an engine base (1), wherein a lifting type load-bearing supporting post (2) is arranged on the engine base (1); a transverse long housing device (3) is arranged at the upper end of the lifting type load-bearing supporting post (2); a movable rail device (4), a telescopic type sliding device (5), a rotary plate (6) and a concave grabbing device (7) are arranged on a housing; a master control device (8) and a display device (9) are arranged above the supporting post; during operation, a to-be-stamped part is subjected to translation and rotary conveying operation after overall adjustment is carried out according to the height of a stamping machine and a control console; and a prompting device (10) is arranged at the right side of the master control device (8), and the prompting device is used for sending corresponding prompting information after the to-be-stamped part is transferred. the movable type transfer assembly is simple and direct in appearance structural design, not only can effectively ensure that workpieces are transferred to the positions through one step to quicken the stamping process, but also can carry out manual intelligent control, so that the operation is simple.
rewritten: the invention discloses a movable type transfer assembly designed for a stamping mechanical hand. the assembly includes an engine base (1) with a lifting, load-bearing support post (2) mounted on it. at the upper end of this post, a transverse long housing unit (3) is provided, within which a movable rail device (4), a telescopic sliding device (5), a rotary plate (6), and a concave grabbing device (7) are arranged. additionally, a master control device (8) and a display device (9) are located above the support post. in operation, the part to be stamped is translated and rotated after adjusting its height in accordance with the stamping machine and control console. a prompting device (10), positioned to the right of the master control device (8), sends the corresponding prompts once the part is transferred. the design of the movable type transfer assembly is straightforward and efficient, not only ensuring that parts are transferred accurately and quickly in one step to speed up the stamping process, but also enabling manual intelligent control for simplified operation.

raw: the purpose of this research is to study technical and policy improvements of carrier billing services, which are a developing and growing industry, by adopting a meta-analysis method. implications were derived from studying 15 years-worth of academic research papers (from 2001 to 2015), by analyzing the trends over a total of 109 such research papers on carrier billing services. first, looking at the characteristics of the main studies of the papers from 2008 to 2009, it papers appeared that dealt with preventing fraudulent transactions and preserving consumer protection. privacy, policy proposals and studies for new systems were researched from 2010-2012. service, success factors, damage prevention systems research, law, and institutions were discussed in 2013-2015 mainly. second, the research showed that the number of carrier billing service studies increased due to social issues(telecommunications billing services deregulation, the emergence smartphones, fraud). third, in sectorial research results, technology was the focus area of 47 papers (43.1%), the service sector was the focus of 39 pieces (35.8%) and law and policy improvement accounted for 23 pieces (21.1%). the study of technology and service areas was the most active and according to changes in the environment, law and policy improvement were second to most active. this study will contribute to improving relevant institutions and research directions about study trends in carrier billing services.
rewritten: this research aims to examine technical and policy advancements in carrier billing services—a fast-growing industry—through a meta-analysis approach. it derives insights from analyzing trends in 109 academic papers on carrier billing, spanning 15 years (2001 to 2015). initially, when reviewing major studies spanning 2008 to 2009, the focus was primarily on fraud prevention and safeguarding consumer protection. then, between 2010 and 2012, research shifted towards privacy, policy proposals, and investigations into new systems. from 2013 to 2015, discussions centered on service quality, success factors, damage prevention mechanisms, as well as legal and institutional developments. further analysis revealed that the rise in carrier billing service studies correlated with pressing social issues such as the deregulation of telecommunications billing services, the advent of smartphones, and increased fraudulent activity. in terms of sector-specific research, 47 papers (43.1%) concentrated on technology, 39 (35.8%) on the service sector, and 23 (21.1%) on law and policy improvements. the technology and service areas were most actively studied, with law and policy adjustments following closely as they evolved alongside environmental changes. overall, this study seeks to guide improvements in related institutions and future research directions by highlighting trends in the field of carrier billing services.

raw: the subciliary incision is the most commonly used approach for lower eyelid blepharoplasty because of the advantages like ease of combining with other periocular and midface rejuvenation techniques. however, the most common reported major complication is lower lid malposition. in this article we described our subciliary dissection technique to protect the motor branches of tarsal orbicularis oculi to avoid from ectropion.
rewritten: the subciliary incision remains the most popular method for lower eyelid blepharoplasty because it easily integrates with other periocular and midface rejuvenation procedures. however, the most frequently reported major complication is lower lid malposition. in this article, we outline our subciliary dissection technique, which is specifically designed to protect the motor branches of the tarsal orbicularis oculi and thereby prevent ectropion.

raw: the utility model discloses an emergent communication network twine of emergent intelligent wrist -watch and applied this wrist -watch, emergent intelligent wrist -watch are equipped with host system, touch screen, are used for to fix a position module of satellite position, the sensor interface circuit of the wrist -watch person of wearing position, the power module who is used for inserting zigbee communication module, drive circuit, the warning module of emergent communication network twine and is used for being emergent intelligent wrist -watch power supply, emergent communication network twine be that the above -mentioned intelligent wrist -watch of meeting an urgent need passes through more than two zigbee communication module constitutes wireless mesh network or wireless sensor network. wrecked user can be to emergent communication network twine broadcasting sos packet through emergent intelligent wrist -watch for thereby other emergent intelligent wrist -watch users also can receive the user that the sos packet goes on crying for help to experience predicament, demand and implement to rescue under the environment of nos operator network. the utility model has the advantages of can be applicable to the no network environment of operator, communication transmission wide range, fast, be applicable to emergency rescue communication.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an emergency communication network system built from intelligent wristwatches. each emergency intelligent wristwatch is equipped with a host system, a touchscreen, a satellite positioning module for location tracking, a sensor interface circuit to detect when it is worn, a power module, a zigbee communication module, a drive circuit, and an emergency warning module—all powered by the wristwatch’s power supply. when more than two of these wristwatches are connected via their zigbee modules, they form a wireless mesh network or wireless sensor network. in an emergency, a distressed user can broadcast an sos packet via their wristwatch, allowing other nearby wristwatch users to receive the distress signal and initiate rescue measures, even in the absence of a traditional operator network. this utility model offers the advantages of working in network-free environments, providing wide-ranging and fast communication that is highly suitable for emergency rescue situations.

raw: a particular three-dimensional integrated circuit stack includes a first die including a first bonding interface and a first plurality of interconnect layers arranged according to a first manhattan wiring scheme. the three-dimensional integrated circuit stack also includes a second die including a second bonding interface and a second plurality of interconnect layers arranged according to a second manhattan wiring scheme. the first die and the second die stacked with the first bonding interface coupled to the second bonding interface such that the first manhattan wiring scheme and the second manhattan wiring scheme are non-manhattan with respect to each other.
rewritten: a three-dimensional integrated circuit stack comprises a first die that has a first bonding interface along with multiple interconnect layers set up in a first manhattan wiring pattern. it also includes a second die which features a second bonding interface and several interconnect layers arranged according to a second manhattan wiring pattern. the two dies are stacked so that the first bonding interface connects to the second bonding interface, with the two manhattan wiring patterns oriented non-manhattan relative to each other.

raw: bacterial species belonging to the genus burkholderia have been repeatedly reported to be associated with fungi but the extent and specificity of these associations in soils remain undetermined. to assess whether associations between burkholderia and fungi are widespread in soils, we performed a co-occurrence analysis in an intercontinental soil sample collection. this revealed that burkholderia significantly co-occurred with a wide range of fungi. to analyse the molecular basis of the interaction, we selected two model fungi frequently co-occurring with burkholderia, alternaria alternata and fusarium solani, and analysed the proteome changes caused by cultivation with either fungus in the widespread soil inhabitant b. glathei, whose genome we sequenced. co-cultivation with both fungi led to very similar changes in the b. glathei proteome. our results indicate that b. glathei significantly benefits from the interaction, which is exemplified by a lower abundance of several starvation factors that were highly expressed in pure culture. however, co-cultivation also gave rise to stress factors, as indicated by the increased expression of multidrug efflux pumps and proteins involved in oxidative stress response. our data suggest that the ability of burkholderia to establish a close association with fungi mainly lies in the capacities to utilize fungal-secreted metabolites and to overcome fungal defense mechanisms. this work indicates that beneficial interactions with fungi might contribute to the survival strategy of burkholderia species in environments with sub-optimal conditions, including acidic soils.
rewritten: bacterial species of the burkholderia genus have often been found in association with fungi, yet the extent and specificity of these interactions in soil environments remain unclear. to determine whether such associations are common across soils, we conducted a co-occurrence analysis using a soil sample collection from several continents. our findings revealed that burkholderia significantly co-occurs with a varied range of fungi. to delve into the molecular mechanisms underlying this interaction, we chose two model fungi—alternaria alternata and fusarium solani—that frequently appear with burkholderia, and we examined the changes in the proteome of b. glathei, a common soil inhabitant whose genome we had sequenced, following cultivation with each fungus. the co-cultivation with both fungi led to similar proteomic alterations in b. glathei. our results suggest that b. glathei benefits substantially from these interactions, as indicated by the reduced levels of several starvation-related factors that were highly expressed when grown alone. however, the interactions also triggered stress responses, evident from the increased expression of multidrug efflux pumps and proteins linked to oxidative stress defense. overall, our data imply that burkholderia’s ability to closely associate with fungi largely depends on its capacity to utilize metabolites secreted by the fungi and to counteract fungal defense mechanisms. this study suggests that such beneficial interactions with fungi may enhance the survival of burkholderia species in harsh environments, including acidic soils.

raw: the invention discloses a graphene printing ink and a preparation method thereof. the graphene printing ink comprises the following raw materials in percentage by weight: 0.01 to 40% of graphene, 5 to 50% of oligomer, 20 to 80% of monomer, 0.1 to 25% of photoinitiator, and 0 to 20% of auxiliary agent; wherein the oligomer is polyester acrylate, and the monomer is (meth)acrylate. due to the graphene component, the cured coating has an excellent electro-conductive performance; based on the properties of polyester acrylate, the coating has excellent characteristics such as excellent flexibility, good caking property, weatherability, performance on resisting yellowing, high refractive index, and the like, and thus the graphene printing ink has a good using property. furthermore, by controlling the ratio of monomers to oligomer, the rubber system has a proper viscosity so as to ensure that graphene is well dispersed in the system.
rewritten: this invention relates to a graphene printing ink and its method of preparation. the ink consists of raw materials in the following weight percentages: graphene 0.01–40%, oligomer 5–50%, monomer 20–80%, photoinitiator 0.1–25%, and an auxiliary agent 0–20%. in this formulation, the oligomer used is polyester acrylate, and the monomer is a (meth)acrylate. the inclusion of graphene results in a cured coating with outstanding electrical conductivity. moreover, the intrinsic properties of polyester acrylate impart the coating with excellent flexibility, strong adhesion, weather resistance, resistance to yellowing, and a high refractive index, ensuring the printing ink performs reliably. additionally, by precisely managing the ratio of monomers to oligomer, the rubber system attains an optimal viscosity that guarantees uniform dispersion of graphene throughout the formulation.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide an operation device for an opening/closing body that ensures safety of residents regardless of a product size of the opening/closing body.solution: an operation device for an opening/closing body includes an upper case 140 having an upper pulley 141 around which a lower edge of a driving cord 120 is wound, a lower case 150 having a lower pulley 151 around which an upper edge of an operation cord 110 is wound, and engaging means 160 for connecting the upper case 140 and the lower case 150 by engaging the cases. when a load is exerted on either side of the operation cord 110 that hangs in a loop form from the lower case 150, rotation of the lower pulley 151 is conveyed to the upper pulley 141. when a load is exerted on both sides of the operation cord 110 that hangs in a loop form from the lower case 150, the engaging means 160 is disengaged, separating the upper case 140 from the lower case 150. the configuration eliminates a need to strictly set engaging force of a connecting member that performs a fail-safe function, in a way that suits a product size.selected drawing: figure 2
rewritten: challenge addressed: to develop an operating mechanism for an opening/closing component that guarantees resident safety regardless of the component's size. proposed solution: the device comprises three main parts: • an upper case (140) that houses an upper pulley (141) around which the lower end of a driving cord (120) is wound. • a lower case (150) featuring a lower pulley (151) around which the upper end of an operation cord (110) is wound. • engaging means (160) that secure the upper case (140) to the lower case (150) by interlocking them. when a force is applied to one side of the looped operation cord (110) hanging from the lower case (150), the rotation of the lower pulley (151) is transferred to the upper pulley (141). conversely, if forces are exerted simultaneously on both sides of the loop, the engaging means (160) disengage, resulting in the separation of the upper case (140) from the lower case (150). this arrangement removes the need for precisely setting the engagement force of a connecting member that performs a fail-safe function, making it adaptable to various product sizes. refer to figure 2 for the selected drawing.

raw: the utility model relates to a generation of random number device. the device includes: adjustable delay module for postpone first clock signal, generate n the 2nd clock signal, adjust the 2nd individual clock signal's of n delay under control signal's control, storage module, including n level memory cell, n level memory cell includes the 1st grade of memory cell, intermediate stage memory cell and n level memory cell, synchronous logic modules for synchronization is carried out in output to n level memory cell, postpone the control logic module for according to n level memory cell's stable output, generate control signal, true random number is generated and exported. the utility model discloses be used for improving the output emergence metastable possibility of trigger, realize generating true random number according to the metastable state of trigger output, and need not adopt special clock to produce the complexity of asynchronous input signal, reduction chip.
rewritten: the utility model describes a random number generation device comprising the following components: • an adjustable delay module that delays the initial clock signal, generates a second clock signal (n times), and adjusts the delay of each of these n second clock signals under the control of a control signal. • a storage module that consists of memory cells arranged in n levels. among these, the memory cells include a first-level memory cell, an intermediate memory cell, and an n-level memory cell. • synchronous logic modules that synchronize the outputs to the n-level memory cells. • a control logic module that, based on the stable output of the n-level memory cells, produces a control signal. when the control signal is generated, a true random number is produced and output. the utility model reveals that by relying on the metastable state of the trigger output, it improves the likelihood of metastability in the trigger for generating true random numbers. this approach avoids the need for a specialized clock or complex asynchronous input signals, thereby reducing the circuit complexity.
