raw: in recent years, knowledge about metabolite changes which are characteristic for the physiologic state of cancer cells has been acquired by liquid chromatography coupled to mass spectrometry. distinct molecularly characterized breast cancer cell lines provide an unbiased and standardized in vitro tumor model reflecting the heterogeneity of the disease. tandem mass spectrometry is a widely applied analytical platform and highly sensitive technique for analysis of complex biological samples. endo- and exometabolite analysis of the breast cancer cell lines mda-mb-231, -453 and bt-474 as well as the breast epithelial cell line mcf-10a has been performed using two different analytical platforms: uplc-esi-q-tof based on a scheduled precursor list has been applied for highlighting of significant differences between cell lines and hplc-esi-qqq using multiple reaction monitoring has been utilized for a targeted approach focusing on rna metabolism and interconnected pathways, respectively. statistical analysis enabled a clear discrimination of the breast epithelial from the breast cancer cell lines. as an effect of oxidative stress, a decreased gsh/gssg ratio has been detected in breast cancer cell lines. the triple negative breast cancer cell line mda-mb-231 showed an elevation in nicotinamide, 1-ribosyl-nicotinamide and nad+ reflecting the increased energy demand in triple negative breast cancer, which has a more aggressive clinical course than other forms of breast cancer. obtained distinct metabolite pattern could be correlated with distinct molecular characteristics of breast cancer cells. results and methodology of this preliminary in vitro study could be transferred to in vivo studies with breast cancer patients.
rewritten: recent advancements in liquid chromatography coupled with mass spectrometry have enhanced our understanding of metabolite changes that reflect the physiological state of cancer cells. well-characterized molecular breast cancer cell lines serve as unbiased, standardized in vitro tumor models that mirror the heterogeneity of the disease. tandem mass spectrometry, recognized as a highly sensitive analytical technique, is commonly applied to analyze complex biological samples. both endo- and exometabolite profiles of the breast cancer cell lines mda-mb-231, -453, and bt-474, along with the breast epithelial cell line mcf-10a, were examined using two distinct analytical platforms. a uplc-esi-q-tof system, employing a scheduled precursor list, was used to highlight significant differences among the cell lines, while an hplc-esi-qqq system, utilizing multiple reaction monitoring, was employed for a targeted analysis focusing on rna metabolism and its interconnected pathways. statistical analysis clearly distinguished the breast epithelial cell line from the breast cancer cell lines. additionally, due to oxidative stress, a reduced gsh/gssg ratio was observed in the breast cancer cell lines. the triple-negative breast cancer cell line mda-mb-231 exhibited increased levels of nicotinamide, 1-ribosyl-nicotinamide, and nad+, reflecting the higher energy demand typically associated with triple-negative breast cancer, which tends to have a more aggressive clinical course compared to other breast cancer types. the distinct metabolite patterns obtained were correlated with specific molecular characteristics of the breast cancer cells, and the findings and methodology of this preliminary in vitro study could potentially be applied to in vivo studies involving breast cancer patients.

rewritten: the utility model discloses an automatic assembly system for a pearl cage, comprising a board with a fixture mounted on it for centrally gripping a holder. one side of the fixture features an inclined rail for transporting billets, while the opposite side is equipped with a mechanism for unloading finished products. additionally, there are two feed mechanisms located beneath the fixture for loading the holder material, and a billet pressing mechanism mounted on the top of the fixture. the fixture is further connected to push-pull systems on both sides, which facilitate the efficient loosening and pressing of components. overall, the disclosed system is a fully automatic assembly apparatus for a pearl cage that offers fast production speeds, high efficiency, excellent product uniformity, and a low rate of defective products.

raw: literature is considered as the expression of people's thoughts, feelings and imaginations in beautiful language. literature has great function in developing human's feelings, ideas, and interests. literary works could be in the form poem, novel, drama or film. this study analyzed a film because film can be enjoyed audio visually. a "freedom writers" film is chosen to be analyzed because it is based on life which is showed by the main character eva benitez. in order to analyze "freedom writers" film, there are some purposes of this study. the purposes of this study were to explain the violence faced by eva, to find out the causes of the violence and also to discover eva overcome the violence as a student at woodrow wilson high school on richard lagravenese's film "freedom writers". this analysis was focused on violence faced by eva as a student at woodrow wilson high school on richard lagravenese's film "freedom writers". the research applied descriptive qualitative design by collecting the data and reviewing literature which are relevant to the topic to prove research data. the technique used in this research is library research; meanwhile, the data were taken from the film itself. the approach used in this research was liberal humanism approach which focused on analyzing the main character as a student in this film. the finding of this analysis found the violence faced by eva as a student, they are divided into separated section depending on races, insulting by other race in school, and being hit by her classmate. there were three causes of violence faced by eva: not willing to contact with other races, a black man want to snatch eva's territory, and behaving as a dominant student. then, it is followed by eva overcome the violence as a student: being helped by her teacher, following her heart to say the truth, and stopped by the ringing bell.
rewritten: literature is often seen as the artistic expression of human thoughts, emotions, and imagination through beautifully crafted language. it plays a crucial role in shaping our feelings, ideas, and interests, and can take many forms including poetry, novels, dramas, and films. this study focuses on analyzing a film—the "freedom writers"—because films provide an audio-visual experience that can bring stories to life. the film was selected for analysis as it is based on real-life events depicted through the experiences of its main character, eva benitez. the study has several objectives: to explain the violence that eva faces, to identify the causes of this violence, and to examine how eva overcomes such challenges during her time as a student at woodrow wilson high school, as portrayed in richard lagravenese's film "freedom writers." the analysis concentrates on the different forms of violence experienced by eva at school. a descriptive qualitative research design was used, which involved gathering data and reviewing relevant literature to support the findings. the primary research method was library research, supplemented by data extracted directly from the film. adopting a liberal humanism approach, the study focuses on analyzing the main character’s experiences as a student. the findings reveal that eva encounters various forms of violence, including racial segregation, racial insults by peers, and physical assault by a classmate. three main causes of the violence were identified: a reluctance to interact with other races, the interference of a black man attempting to take over eva's territory, and another student’s dominant behavior. eva ultimately overcomes these challenges through the support of her teacher, by staying true to herself and speaking the truth, and by the intervention marked by the ringing of the school bell.

raw: the effects of ecg on follicular growth, ovulation, and pregnancy per artificial insemination (p/ai) in multiparous and primiparous bos indicus beef cows submitted to timed artificial insemination (tai) were evaluated in three experiments. in experiments 1 (follicular responses; n = 64), 2 (follicular growth and ovulation rate; n = 662), and 3 (p/ai; n = 2092), cows submitted to tai were assigned to receive one of two treatments on day 8 of the synchronization protocol: control (no additional treatment) or ecg (300-iu of ecg intramuscularly). in experiment 1, largest follicle (lf) diameter on day 8 (p = 0.56) and the interval from progesterone (p4)-device removal to ovulation (p = 0.79) did not differ between treatments. however, the maximum diameter of the lf (p = 0.05) and ovulation rate (p = 0.03) were greater in cows that received ecg. in experiment 2, the diameter of the lf on day 10, follicular growth, and ovulation rate were greater in ecg-treated cows (p < 0.01). however, cl diameter was similar between treatments (p = 0.11). in experiment 3, there was a treatment-by-parity interaction (p = 0.003) on p/ai, such that treatment with ecg was more effective in primiparous cows. in conclusion, ecg treatment resulted in increased final follicular growth, ovulation rate, and fertility in b indicus cows submitted to tai protocols, especially in primiparous cows.
rewritten: three experiments evaluated how ecg treatment affected follicular growth, ovulation, and pregnancy per artificial insemination (p/ai) in multiparous and primiparous bos indicus beef cows undergoing timed artificial insemination (tai). in all three studies—experiment 1 (examining follicular responses with 64 cows), experiment 2 (assessing follicular growth and ovulation rate with 662 cows), and experiment 3 (evaluating p/ai with 2092 cows)—the cows were assigned on day 8 of the synchronization protocol to either a control group (receiving no additional treatment) or to an ecg group (given 300 iu of ecg intramuscularly). in experiment 1, there was no difference between the groups in the diameter of the largest follicle on day 8 (p = 0.56) or in the interval from progesterone device removal to ovulation (p = 0.79). however, cows treated with ecg had a larger maximum diameter of the largest follicle (p = 0.05) and a higher ovulation rate (p = 0.03). in experiment 2, ecg-treated cows showed a greater diameter of the largest follicle on day 10, increased overall follicular growth, and a higher ovulation rate (all p < 0.01), although the corpus luteum diameter did not differ between treatments (p = 0.11). experiment 3 revealed a significant treatment-by-parity interaction (p = 0.003) for p/ai, indicating that ecg treatment was more effective in primiparous cows. in summary, administering ecg increased final follicular growth, ovulation rate, and overall fertility in bos indicus cows undergoing tai protocols, with a particularly strong effect in primiparous cows.

raw: we examined the interaction in face-to-face selling situation. in particular, we examined how customer's nonverbal behaviors were related to their preference in a natural environment. we found that customers' body posture could be a good predictor about their preference. we also found that estimation on customer's preference could be achieved from two nonverbal behaviors better than single behavior. we discuss the present contributions toward constructing the efficient agent systems.
rewritten: we analyzed interactions within face-to-face sales scenarios. specifically, we investigated how customers’ nonverbal cues in a natural setting relate to their preferences. our findings suggest that customers’ body posture is a strong indicator of their preferences. moreover, we discovered that using two nonverbal behaviors to make predictions is more effective than relying on a single behavior. we conclude by discussing how these insights contribute to the development of more efficient agent systems.

raw: a thermally stable 2d array of spheres and their morphology control become important for the fabrication of novel nanostructures. here, a simple method is presented for fabrication of large-area and well-ordered arrays of carbonized polystyrene (ps) hollow spheres with a controlled (close-packed or non-close-packed hexagonal) morphology, prepared by combining the self-assembly of ps-grafted silica nanoparticles, etching, electron irradiation, and subsequent thermal annealing. fine control in the 2d or 3d nanostructure of carbon materials can open up new opportunities for high-performance nanoscale applications that require an efficient fabrication method for preparation of the porous carbon array.
rewritten: a thermally robust, two-dimensional array of spheres and the ability to control their morphology are key for developing new nanostructures. here, a straightforward method is introduced to fabricate large-area, highly ordered arrays of carbonized polystyrene (ps) hollow spheres with adjustable configurations—either close-packed or non-close-packed hexagonal. this approach combines the self-assembly of ps-grafted silica nanoparticles with etching, electron irradiation, and subsequent thermal annealing. precise control of the carbon material’s two- or three-dimensional nanostructure can thus open up new opportunities for advanced nanoscale applications that demand a highly efficient fabrication process for porous carbon arrays.

raw: new integrative approaches are needed to harness the potential of rapidly growing datasets of protein expression and microbial community composition in colorectal cancer. chemical and thermodynamic models offer theoretical tools to describe populations of biomacromolecules and their relative potential for formation in different microenvironmental conditions. the average oxidation state of carbon (z c) can be calculated as an elemental ratio from the chemical formulas of proteins, and water demand per residue ([formula: see text]) is computed by writing the overall formation reactions of proteins from basis species. using results reported in proteomic studies of clinical samples, many datasets exhibit higher mean z c or [formula: see text] of proteins in carcinoma or adenoma compared to normal tissue. in contrast, average protein compositions in bacterial genomes often have lower z c for bacteria enriched in fecal samples from cancer patients compared to healthy donors. in thermodynamic calculations, the potential for formation of the cancer-related proteins is energetically favored by changes in the chemical activity of h2o and fugacity of o2 that reflect the compositional differences. the compositional analysis suggests that a systematic change in chemical composition is an essential feature of cancer proteomes, and the thermodynamic descriptions show that the observed proteomic transformations in host tissue could be promoted by relatively high microenvironmental oxidation and hydration states.
rewritten: innovative, integrated methods are required to fully leverage the vast and rapidly expanding datasets on protein expression and microbial community composition in colorectal cancer. chemical and thermodynamic models provide theoretical frameworks for characterizing populations of biomacromolecules and assessing their likelihood of formation under varying microenvironmental conditions. in particular, the average oxidation state of carbon (zc) is determined using the elemental ratios derived from protein chemical formulas, while the water demand per residue is calculated by formulating the overall formation reactions of proteins from their basis species. data from proteomic studies indicate that many datasets show higher mean zc or water demand values in carcinoma or adenoma tissues compared to normal tissues. conversely, proteins encoded in bacterial genomes typically exhibit a lower zc in bacteria that are more prevalent in fecal samples from cancer patients than in those from healthy donors. thermodynamic calculations reveal that the formation of cancer-associated proteins is energetically favored by alterations in the chemical activity of h2o and the fugacity of o2, which mirror these compositional differences. this compositional analysis implies that systematic shifts in chemical makeup are a defining characteristic of cancer proteomes, and the corresponding thermodynamic descriptions suggest that the observed proteomic changes in host tissue may be driven by relatively higher oxidation and hydration states in the microenvironment.

raw: the green chemistry revolution is providing an enormous number of challenges to those who practice chemistry in industry in education and research. with these challenges however, there are an equal number of opportunities to discover and apply new chemistry, to improve the economics of chemical manufacturing. green chemistry is the philosophy and study of design of product or substances that will not involve materials harmful to the environment. it is a modern science of chemistry that deals with the application of environmentally friendly chemical compounds in the various areas of our life such as industrial uses and many other. this area of chemistry had been developed by the need to avoid chemical hazards that organic and inorganic compounds had on the body of humans and animals. chemistry play a pivotal role in determining the quality of modern life. the chemicals industry and other related industries supply us with huge variety of essential products, from plastics to pharmaceuticals. however, these industries have the potential to seriously damage our environment. green chemistry therefore serves to promote the design and efficient use of environmentally benign chemicals and chemical processes.
rewritten: the green chemistry revolution presents many challenges to professionals in industry, education, and research. yet, these challenges also bring equal opportunities to discover and implement innovative chemistry while enhancing the cost-effectiveness of chemical production. green chemistry is the philosophy and practice focused on creating products or substances without using materials that harm the environment. this modern field of chemistry is concerned with the use of eco-friendly chemical compounds across various sectors, including industrial applications. its development emerged from the need to minimize the hazardous effects that both organic and inorganic compounds can have on humans and animals. chemistry plays a crucial role in shaping the quality of modern life by providing essential products—from plastics to pharmaceuticals—through the chemical industry and related fields, which, however, also have the potential to cause serious environmental damage. consequently, green chemistry promotes the design and efficient use of chemicals and processes that are environmentally benign.

raw: the utility model provides an electron parking system, includes piece in turning mold, piece in turning mold includes shell, control circuit board and hinders the fuel tap, wherein control circuit board with hinder the fuel tap and all pacify casedly, the lower part both sides of shell are equipped with the input tube respectively and connect and connect with the output tube, the input tube connects and connects through hindering the fuel tap intercommunication with the output tube, should hinder the fuel tap and comprise with solenoid system moving valve body, wherein makes the interior central iron core of case connection solenoid of moving valve body, solenoid connects control circuit board, control circuit board connects the connector socket, is equipped with four interfaces on this connector socket, this electron parking system provides brake pressure by brake master cylinder, treats that the brake fluid through carrying out the intercepting to it behind this device, makes brake force exert pressure all the time in the brake shoe to the vehicle is static in addition to form great frictional force, can apply parking braking automatically in the flame -out back of engine, and the parking is convenient, reliable, but to?prevent?accidental's release is worth wideling popularize.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an electronic parking system that includes a turning component composed of a casing, a control circuit board, and a fuel-blocking element. both the control circuit board and the fuel-blocking element are securely housed within the casing. on the lower sides of the casing, there are input tubes that connect to corresponding output tubes, and these tubes are linked via the fuel-blocking element. this element comprises a solenoid-operated moving valve, whose central iron core is positioned inside the casing and connected to the solenoid. the solenoid, in turn, is connected to the control circuit board, which is also linked to a connector socket containing four interfaces. in operation, the electronic parking system receives brake pressure from the brake master cylinder by intercepting the brake fluid before it reaches the braking mechanism. this arrangement ensures that a constant braking pressure is exerted on the brake shoes, generating significant friction that holds the vehicle stationary. the system automatically applies the parking brake upon engine shutdown, offering a convenient, reliable, and secure means of preventing accidental release, making it ideal for widespread adoption.

raw: semiconductor nanocrystals offer an enormous diversity of potential device applications, based on their size-tunable photoluminescence, high optical stability and 'bottom-up' chemical approaches to self-assembly. however, the promise of such applications can be seriously limited by photoluminescence intermittency in nanocrystal emission, that is, 'blinking', arising from the escape of either one or both of the photoexcited carriers to the nanocrystal surface. in the first scenario, the remaining nanocrystal charge quenches photoluminescence via non-radiative auger recombination, whereas for the other, the exciton is thought to be intercepted before thermalization and does not contribute to the photoluminescence. this review summarizes the current understanding of the mechanisms responsible for nanocrystal blinking kinetics as well as core-shell engineering efforts to control such phenomena. in particular, 'softening' of the core-shell confinement potential strongly suppresses non-radiative auger processes in charged nanocrystals, with successful non-blinking implementations demonstrated in cdse-cds core-thick-shell nanocrystals and their modifications.
rewritten: semiconductor nanocrystals present a wide range of potential device applications due to their size-dependent photoluminescence, high optical stability, and the utilization of 'bottom-up' chemical self-assembly methods. however, the realization of these applications can be compromised by the photoluminescence intermittency (or 'blinking') observed in nanocrystal emissions. this blinking occurs when one or both photoexcited carriers escape to the nanocrystal surface. in one case, the remaining charge within the nanocrystal quenches the photoluminescence through non-radiative auger recombination, while in the other case, the exciton is intercepted before it can thermalize, thereby not contributing to the photoluminescence. this review provides an overview of the current understanding of the mechanisms behind the blinking kinetics in nanocrystals, as well as the core-shell engineering strategies developed to manage this behavior. notably, by 'softening' the core-shell confinement potential, non-radiative auger processes in charged nanocrystals are considerably suppressed, a result that has been demonstrated in cdse-cds core-thick-shell nanocrystals and their modified forms.

raw: an efficient muck conveying system for temporary switching comprises an underground screw conveyor, a muck bucket and a conveyor arranged between the muck outlet of the screw conveyor and the muck bucket. the front end of the conveyor is a muck inlet end located below the screw conveyor and detachably connected with the screw conveyor, the tail end of the conveyor is a muck outlet end located on the muck bucket, and the muck outlet end of the conveyor is higher than the muck inlet end of the conveyor. by the efficient muck conveying system, the problems that the muck bucket is small in size and needs to be lifted for many times, construction efficiency is low, and muck discharge quantity cannot be controlled easily are solved.
rewritten: the temporary switching system for efficient muck conveying includes an underground screw conveyor, a muck bucket, and a connecting conveyor positioned between the screw conveyor’s muck outlet and the muck bucket. the conveyor’s front end, which serves as the muck inlet, is located beneath the screw conveyor and is detachably connected to it, while the rear, or muck outlet, end is mounted on the muck bucket and is positioned higher than the inlet. this innovative system overcomes the drawbacks of using a small muck bucket that requires multiple lifts, improves construction efficiency, and allows for easier control over the discharge quantity of the muck.
