rewritten: the utility model discloses a fixed mechanism for installing a hard disk. this mechanism includes a casing, at least one door closure actuation mechanism, and an opening on the front side of the casing where the hard disk is inserted and where the door closure pivots. the actuation mechanism is located at the rear side of the casing and comprises a fixed base, a pivot mounted on the fixed base, a guiding board, and a torsional spring. the torsional spring urges the guiding board to rotate away from the pivot toward the casing. when a hard disk is inserted into the casing, its support contacts the guiding board. as a result, the guiding board drives a lateral movement of the hard disk from the rear side of the casing, ensuring that the hard disk's rear-end pin correctly aligns with the corresponding jack on a connector.

raw: the leading explanation for the origin of galactic cosmic rays is particle acceleration at the shocks surrounding young supernova remnants (snrs), although crucial aspects of the acceleration process are unclear. the similar collisionless plasma shocks frequently encountered by spacecraft in the solar wind are generally far weaker (lower mach number) than these snr shocks. however, the cassini spacecraft has shown that the shock standing in the solar wind sunward of saturn (saturn's bow shock) can occasionally reach this high-mach number astrophysical regime. in this regime cassini has provided the first in situ evidence for electron acceleration under quasi-parallel upstream magnetic conditions. here we present the full picture of suprathermal electrons at saturn's bow shock revealed by cassini. the downstream thermal electron distribution is resolved in all data taken by the low-energy electron detector (caps-els, 18 kev) measured a suprathermal electron signature at 31 of 508 crossings, where typically only the lowest energy channels (<100 kev) were above background. we show that these results are consistent with theory in which the "injection" of thermal electrons into an acceleration process involves interaction with whistler waves at the shock front, and becomes possible for all upstream magnetic field orientations at high mach numbers like those of the strong shocks around young snrs. a future dedicated study will analyze the rare crossings with evidence for relativistic electrons (up to ~1 mev).
rewritten: the predominant explanation for the origin of galactic cosmic rays is that particles get accelerated at the shock fronts of young supernova remnants (snrs), even though key details of this process remain uncertain. in contrast, spacecraft data show that collisionless plasma shocks in the solar wind are typically much weaker (i.e., they have lower mach numbers) than those found in snrs. nevertheless, measurements from the cassini spacecraft have demonstrated that saturn's bow shock—the shock formed in the solar wind on the sunward side of saturn—can sometimes attain these high-mach number conditions. under such high-mach regimes, cassini provided the first on-site evidence of electron acceleration occurring with quasi-parallel upstream magnetic fields. here, we offer a comprehensive account of suprathermal electrons at saturn’s bow shock as revealed by cassini. in all observations made with the low-energy electron detector (caps-els, covering energies around 18 kev), a suprathermal electron signal was detected in 31 out of 508 shock crossings, with typically only the lowest energy channels (below 100 kev) showing measurable intensities above the background. our findings align with theoretical models in which the “injection” of thermal electrons into an acceleration process is facilitated by their interaction with whistler waves at the shock front. this mechanism becomes viable for all upstream magnetic field orientations when the mach numbers are as high as those seen in the powerful shocks around young snrs. a forthcoming study will focus on the rare events that exhibit signs of relativistic electrons, with energies reaching up to approximately 1 mev.

raw: we study the production of lighter fragments and associated phenomena within the quantum molecular dynamics (qmd) model. the minimum spanning tree (mst) method is used to identify the pre-clusters. the final stable fragments were identified by imposing binding energy criteria on the fragments formed using the mst method. the effect of different binding energy criteria was investigated by employing various liquid-drop-based binding energy formulae. though light clusters show significant effect of different binding energies, their associated phenomenon, i.e. entropy production is insensitive towards different binding energy criteria.
rewritten: we investigate the formation of lighter fragments and related phenomena using the quantum molecular dynamics (qmd) model. pre-clusters are identified with the minimum spanning tree (mst) method, and final stable fragments are determined by applying binding energy criteria to these mst-formed fragments. we explored the influence of various binding energy criteria by utilizing different liquid-drop-inspired binding energy formulae. although the lighter clusters are notably affected by the choice of binding energy, the associated phenomenon, namely entropy production, remains largely insensitive to these variations.

raw: the present invention provides a dispersion of inorganic particles which combines excellent dispersion stability with a high viscosity and can be burned at relatively low temperatures. the dispersion of inorganic particles is characterized by including: inorganic particles, at least some of the surface thereof having a c6 or lower short-chain amine; a polymeric dispersant comprising a polymer of a comb-shaped structure that has one or more pigment-philic groups in the main chain and/or a plurality of side chains thereof and has a plurality of side chains each constituting a salvation moiety, or a polymer that has, in the main chain, a plurality of pigment-philic moieties each constituted of a pigment-philic group, or a linear polymer that has, at one terminal of the main chain, a pigment-philic moiety constituted of a pigment-philic group; and a dispersion medium. the dispersion is further characterized in that the dispersion medium contains a protective dispersant having an acid value, the acid value of the protective dispersant being 1-200 and the addition amount of the protective dispersant being 0.1-10 wt% based on the inorganic particles on a solid basis.
rewritten: the invention relates to a dispersion of inorganic particles that exhibits excellent dispersion stability, high viscosity, and the ability to be burned at relatively low temperatures. this dispersion comprises inorganic particles, where at least a portion of their surface is modified with a short-chain amine containing six or fewer carbon atoms. additionally, it contains a polymeric dispersant, which can be one of the following: a polymer with a comb-like structure that has one or more pigment-attracting groups in its main chain and/or on several of its side chains—with each side chain acting as a solvation moiety; a polymer whose main chain includes multiple pigment-attracting moieties, each made up of a pigment-attracting group; or a linear polymer that carries a pigment-attracting moiety at one end of its main chain. the dispersion also includes a dispersion medium that is further characterized by the presence of a protective dispersant. this protective dispersant has an acid value between 1 and 200, and it is added in an amount corresponding to 0.1–10 wt% of the solid inorganic particles.

raw: we investigated the prevalence of sensitization to the cat lipocalin fel d 7 among 140 cat-sensitized swedish patients and elucidated its allergenic activity and cross-reactivity with the dog lipocalin can f 1. sixty-five of 140 patients had ige to rfel d 7 whereof 60 also had ige to rcan f 1. a moderate correlation between ige levels to rfel d 7 and rcan f 1 was found. rfel d 7 activated basophils in vitro and inhibited ige binding to rcan f 1 in 4 of 13 patients, whereas rcan f 1 inhibited ige binding to rfel d 7 in 7 of 13 patients. fel d 7 and can f 1 showed high similarities in protein structure and epitopes in common were found using cross-reactive antisera. fel d 7 is a common allergen in a swedish cat-sensitized population that cross-reacts with can f 1, and may contribute to symptoms in cat- but also in dog-allergic patients.
rewritten: we examined the prevalence of sensitization to the cat lipocalin fel d 7 in 140 swedish patients known to be cat-allergic, and we explored its allergenic properties along with its cross-reactivity with the dog lipocalin can f 1. out of the 140 patients, 65 had detectable ige specific to rfel d 7; notably, 60 of these patients also exhibited ige against rcan f 1. a moderate correlation was observed between ige levels to rfel d 7 and rcan f 1. in vitro studies showed that rfel d 7 activated basophils and, in 4 out of 13 patients, inhibited ige binding to rcan f 1, whereas rcan f 1 blocked ige binding to rfel d 7 in 7 out of 13 patients. structural analysis revealed significant similarities between fel d 7 and can f 1, and they were found to share common epitopes as demonstrated by cross-reactive antisera. overall, fel d 7 emerges as a prevalent allergen in this swedish cat-allergic population, exhibiting cross-reactivity with can f 1, which could contribute to allergic symptoms in patients sensitive to both cats and dogs.

raw: in this study, global positioning system satellite transmitters were attached to three mallards (anas platyrhynchos) wintering in south korea to track their migration routes, stopover sites, breeding sites, and migration patterns. we successfully tracked only one mallard (no. 108917) from november 15, 2011, to november 29, 2013, and determined separate migration routes in two cases of spring migration and one case of fall migration. the mallard repeatedly migrated to the same final destination, even though the travel path varied. we identified six stopover sites: hunhe river, liaohe river, yinma river, yalu river, songjeon bay, and dahuofang reservoir in china and south korea. the wintering sites of two migration cases were discovered to be identical (gokgyo river in asan, south korea). the terminal sites, which were presumed to be breeding grounds, were the same in both cases (hinggan league in inner mongolia autonomous region, china). on the basis of the migration routes identified in this study, we suggest that future efforts to control highly pathogenic avian influenza (hpai) should not only include avian influenza surveillance but also implement flyway-based strategies, with regard to all countries affected by potential hpai outbreaks.
rewritten: in this study, we attached global positioning system satellite transmitters to three mallards (anas platyrhynchos) wintering in south korea to monitor their migration routes, stopover locations, breeding grounds, and overall migration patterns. however, we were only able to successfully track one mallard (no. 108917) from november 15, 2011, to november 29, 2013. analysis of the data revealed distinct migration routes during two spring migrations and one fall migration, with the mallard consistently arriving at the same final destination despite variations in its travel paths. we identified six stopover sites located at the hunhe river, liaohe river, yinma river, yalu river, songjeon bay, and dahuofang reservoir in china and south korea. additionally, the wintering sites for two of the migration cases were found to be the same, namely the gokgyo river in asan, south korea, while the terminal sites—assumed to be the breeding grounds—were consistently in the hinggan league in the inner mongolia autonomous region of china. based on these migration routes, we recommend that future measures to control highly pathogenic avian influenza (hpai) should incorporate not only avian influenza surveillance but also flyway-based strategies that consider all countries potentially affected by hpai outbreaks.

raw: background: pharmacogenetic markers of antipsychotic response have the potential to reduce trial and error prescribing, leading to better outcomes. glp1r genetic variation has previously shown pgx potential. this study was intended to identify testable glp1r pgx markers and replicate their impact across multiple cohorts and studies. methods and findings: this study assigned haplotypes and biomarker status to subjects treated with olanzapine (n=172) or risperidone (n=181) in two independent cohorts of the clinical trials of antipsychotic intervention effectiveness (catie) and an additional independent clinical study of schizophrenia and bipolar disorder at vanderbilit university (id nct00179062). in each cohort subjects were assigned biomarker positive or negative status glr1p antipsychotic response predictor (garp 1 and 3 respectively) and evaluated for change in positive and negative syndrome scale from baseline. garp 1 status predicted greater symptom reduction in olanzapine-treated subjects in both catie cohorts (p=0.007 and 0.02, combined p=0.0004) and vanderbilt (p=0.03). garp 3 status predicted poorer symptom reduction in risperidone-treated subjects in the combined catie cohort (p=0.01) and vanderbilt (p=0.01). conclusions: in this study, we refined previous results that identified two pgx markers (garp 1 and garp 3) that predicted response to olanzapine and risperidone in the catie trial. we then replicated these findings in an independent clinical trial pgx. these pgx markers offer physicians a potential tool for more effective antipsychotic selection.
rewritten: background: pharmacogenetic markers related to antipsychotic response could minimize trial-and-error prescribing and lead to improved patient outcomes. earlier research indicated that genetic variation in glp1r might have pharmacogenetic potential. this study aimed to pinpoint testable glp1r pharmacogenetic markers and validate their effects across several cohorts and studies. methods and findings: participants from two independent groups—those treated with olanzapine (n=172) or risperidone (n=181) in the clinical trials of antipsychotic intervention effectiveness (catie) and an additional study at vanderbilt university (id nct00179062) involving individuals with schizophrenia and bipolar disorder—were analyzed. in each group, subjects were classified as biomarker positive or negative based on the glp1r antipsychotic response predictor (garp 1 and 3, respectively) and monitored for changes in positive and negative syndrome scale (panss) scores from baseline. results showed that garp 1 status was associated with a greater reduction in symptoms among olanzapine-treated subjects in both catie cohorts (p=0.007 and p=0.02, with a combined p=0.0004) and at vanderbilt (p=0.03). conversely, garp 3 status predicted a less favorable symptom reduction in risperidone-treated subjects, with significant findings in the combined catie cohort (p=0.01) and vanderbilt (p=0.01). conclusions: this study refines earlier findings by confirming that two pharmacogenetic markers, garp 1 and garp 3, predict responses to olanzapine and risperidone, respectively, as initially identified in the catie trial. these results were further replicated in an independent clinical trial, suggesting that these pharmacogenetic markers could serve as valuable tools for clinicians to select the most effective antipsychotic treatment.

raw: water resources have increasingly come under pressure of late due to competing and ever increasing demands from different sectors. what is often said that future wars will be fought for water, seems prophetic, given the depleting water resource endowment both in quality and quantity. that the groundwater resource in the country is the most stressed in the world, drives us to think of alternatives to water management. as we all know, understanding of technical aspects of water resources is essential to appreciate the limits to exploitation and technological options for judicious use of the resource. [nabard knowledge series-1].
rewritten: lately, water resources have been under mounting pressure as various sectors compete for an ever-growing share. the oft-repeated warning—that future wars could be fought over water—appears increasingly accurate given the declining quality and quantity of our available water. recognizing that our country's groundwater is among the most stressed in the world compels us to consider alternative water management strategies. it is clear that a solid understanding of the technical aspects of water resources is key to determining both the limitations on their use and the technological options for their sustainable management.

raw: the invention discloses a two-dimensional expandable chip type active array antenna. a multifunctional panel comprises a power supply wave control circuit layer, a low-frequency distribution network layer, a first isolation layer, a correction network layer, a second isolation layer, a power dividing and synthesizing network layer and a blind matching interface layer which are sequentially and vertically connected. four-channel tr assemblies with 2*2 arrays are used as plane sub-arrays of tile type tr assemblies, and array face expansion is conducted according to the even number times of 2*2 sub-array modules. the multifunctional panel is connected with the tr assemblies through radio frequency blind matching connectors and low-frequency blind matching connectors respectively. an antenna array face comprises an smp blind matching connector, an antenna radiation layer and a thermal control and supporting layer. the antenna radiation layer is connected to the thermal control and supporting layer. the smp blind matching connector is connected with the antenna radiation layer and the four-channel tr assemblies respectively. the defects that in the prior art, because the number of layers is too large, the profile height and the mass surface density are difficult to decrease and because the interlayer vertical interconnection span is large, efficiency and the integration level are difficult to increase are overcome.
rewritten: the invention presents a two-dimensional, expandable chip-type active array antenna. it features a multifunctional panel that is vertically stacked from top to bottom with the following layers in order: a power supply wave control circuit layer, a low-frequency distribution network layer, a first isolation layer, a correction network layer, a second isolation layer, a power dividing and synthesizing network layer, and finally a blind matching interface layer. as part of its design, four-channel tr assemblies, each comprising a 2×2 array, serve as the planar sub-arrays for the tile-type tr assemblies. the expansion of the antenna face is achieved by combining an even number of these 2×2 sub-array modules. the multifunctional panel is linked to the tr assemblies via radio frequency blind matching connectors and low-frequency blind matching connectors. additionally, the antenna array face includes an smp blind matching connector, an antenna radiation layer, and a thermal control and supporting layer, with the antenna radiation layer directly connected to the thermal layer. the smp blind matching connector is coupled with both the antenna radiation layer and the four-channel tr assemblies. this design overcomes previous limitations, such as excessive layer count leading to high profile height and large mass surface density, as well as issues with inefficient and less integrated interlayer vertical interconnections due to extensive spans.

raw: we study the dynamics of a quantum heavy particle undergoing a repulsive interaction with a light one. the main motivation is the detailed description of the loss of coherence induced on a quantum system (in our model, the heavy particle) by the interaction with the environment (the light particle). the content of the paper is analytical and numerical. concerning the analytical contribution, we show that an approximate description of the dynamics of the heavy particle can be carried out in two steps: first comes the interaction, then the free evolution. in particular, all effects of the interaction can be embodied in the action of a collision operator that acts on the initial state of the heavy particle. with respect to previous analytical results on the same topics, we turn our focus from the m{\o}ller wave operator to the full scattering operator, whose analysis proves to be simpler. concerning the numerical contribution, we exploit the previous analysis to construct an efficient numerical scheme that turns the original, multiscale, two-body problem in two one-body problems which can be solved separately. this leads to a considerable gain in simulation time. we present and interpret some simulations carried out on specific one-dimensional systems by using the new scheme. finally, we propose a new description of the mechanism of decoherence induced by scattering: decoherence is produced by an interference-free bump which arises from the initial state of the heavy particle immediately after the collision. we support such a picture by numerical evidence as well as by an approximation theorem.
rewritten: in this work, we investigate the dynamics of a quantum heavy particle that experiences a repulsive interaction with a light one. our main motivation is to provide a detailed description of how coherence is lost in a quantum system (represented in our model by the heavy particle) due to its interaction with the environment (represented by the light particle). the paper combines both analytical and numerical approaches. on the analytical side, we demonstrate that the dynamics of the heavy particle can be approximately described in two steps: first, the interaction occurs, followed by free evolution. more specifically, the effects of the interaction can be encapsulated by a collision operator acting on the heavy particle's initial state. unlike previous work that emphasized the m{\o}ller wave operator, our focus is on the full scattering operator, which simplifies the analysis. on the numerical side, we leverage the analytical results to develop an efficient computational method that transforms the original multiscale, two-body problem into two separate one-body problems. this transformation significantly reduces the simulation time. we detail and interpret some simulations performed on particular one-dimensional systems using this new scheme. lastly, we introduce a novel interpretation of the decoherence mechanism induced by scattering. according to our picture, decoherence arises from an interference-free bump that originates from the heavy particle’s initial state immediately after the collision. we substantiate this interpretation with both numerical evidence and an approximation theorem.

raw: in the present invention, provided is a tip protector for a retractable fishing rod. a tip protector according to an embodiment of the present invention comprises: a tube-shaped body; a rib integrated in the inner surface of the tube-shaped body; a slit punched through the tube-shaped body at the front of the rib. the front end of the fishing rod is inserted into the tube-shaped body through an insertion hole in the rear end of the tube-shaped body. the rib includes a guide passage groove which allows a part of a guide of the fishing rod to pass but makes the other part thereof caught thereby. the guide passage groove extends in a length direction of the tube-shaped body and has a width tapered toward the front end of the tube-shaped body. the slit has a width, which is greater than the width of the front end of the guide passage groove.
rewritten: the present invention provides a tip protector designed for a retractable fishing rod. in one embodiment, the tip protector consists of a tube-shaped body, a rib incorporated along the interior surface of this body, and a slit located at the front of the rib that is punched through the tube. the front end of the fishing rod is inserted into the tube-shaped body via an insertion hole positioned at its rear end. the rib features a guide passage groove that allows part of the fishing rod's guide to pass through while catching the remainder. this groove runs longitudinally along the tube and gradually tapers in width toward the front end. additionally, the slit is manufactured with a width that exceeds the width of the front end of the guide passage groove.

raw: the invention discloses a cooling device of oil filtering machine. the cooling device comprises a cooling box and a water pump. the water pump is arranged on one side of the top of the cooling box. the other side of the top of the cooling box is provided with a water return pipe mouthpiece and a water filling port. a steam joint is arranged on a middle-upper position of one side of the cooling box. the bottom of one side of the cooling box is provided with a condensed water outlet, the water return pipe mouthpiece is connected to a rubber pipe, and the a steel ball valve is arranged in the condensed water outlet. the steam rising principle is utilized, when steam enters the cooling device, under a natural cooling state, water is injected into the cooling box to accelerate the cooling speed; and the water pump can pump out excess water, and pump the excess water into the water return pipe. the whole equipment is well sealed, the vaporized water out of the equipment will not be increased, and the work environment is improved.
rewritten: the invention relates to a cooling system designed for an oil filtering machine. this system consists of a cooling chamber and a water pump. the water pump is installed on one side near the top of the cooling chamber, while the opposite side of the top is equipped with both a water return pipe outlet and a water filling port. a steam joint is positioned at the middle-upper part of one side of the chamber. additionally, there is a condensed water outlet at the bottom of one side, where the water return pipe outlet is connected to a rubber hose, and a steel ball valve is installed at the condensed water outlet. utilizing the natural rising behavior of steam, the system allows water to be injected into the cooling chamber as steam enters, thereby speeding up the cooling process under natural cooling conditions. if there is an excess of water, the water pump is used to expel it and to circulate it back into the water return pipe. the entire setup is tightly sealed to prevent any increase in vaporized water escaping from the equipment, which in turn enhances the work environment.

raw: the initial promising prospect of autologous bone marrow-derived stem cell therapy in the setting of cardiovascular diseases has been overshadowed by functional shortcomings of the stem cell product. as powerful epigenetic regulators of (stem) cell function, micrornas are valuable targets for novel therapeutic strategies. indeed, modulation of specific mirna expression could contribute to improved therapeutic efficacy of stem cell therapy. first, this review elaborates on the functional relevance of mirna dysregulation in bone marrow-derived progenitor cells in different cardiovascular diseases. next, we provide a comprehensive overview of the current evidence on the effect of specific mirna modulation in several types of progenitor cells on cardiac and/or vascular regeneration. by elaborating on the cardioprotective regulation of progenitor cells on cardiac mirnas, more insight in the underlying mechanisms of stem cell therapy is provided. finally, some considerations are made regarding the potential of circulating mirnas as regulators of the mirna signature of progenitor cells in cardiovascular diseases.
rewritten: the early promise of autologous bone marrow-derived stem cell therapy for cardiovascular diseases has been diminished by the functional limitations of the stem cell products. recognized as potent epigenetic regulators of (stem) cell function, micrornas present attractive targets for new therapeutic approaches. in fact, modifying the expression of certain micrornas could potentially enhance the effectiveness of stem cell therapy. this review first discusses the importance of microrna dysregulation in bone marrow-derived progenitor cells across various cardiovascular diseases. it then offers a detailed overview of current findings on how modulating specific micrornas in different progenitor cell types affects cardiac and/or vascular regeneration. by examining how progenitor cells regulate cardiac micrornas in a cardioprotective manner, the review sheds light on the underlying mechanisms of stem cell therapy. the discussion concludes with considerations regarding the role of circulating micrornas in shaping the microrna profile of progenitor cells in cardiovascular diseases.

raw: the utility model discloses a feed mixing machine, for horizontal spiral mixer, including the organism, helical agitator and commentaries on classics movable fluted disc, it is outside at the organism to change the movable fluted disc setting, and is connected with helical agitator, the feed inlet is installed on organism upper portion, and the discharge gate is installed to the lower part of organism, the feed inlet sub -unit connection has unloading buffering platform, and organism internally mounted has liquid to add the pipe, organism externally mounted has the liquid loading hopper, is equipped with flow controller between liquid loading hopper and the organism, and the baffle is installed with organism contacting area to the discharge gate, organism top discharge mouth position is equipped with the spout that is used for the baffle roll -off. the setting of liquid loading hopper and liquid filling tube can make the joining that liquid?material is more accurate among this feed mixing machine, and mixed more even, improve the productivity effect and the product quality of fodder, and the discharge gate is more durable, and sealing performance is good, prevents lou material.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a feed mixing machine designed for a horizontal spiral mixer. the machine comprises a main body, a helical agitator, and a movable fluted disc. the movable fluted disc is externally adjustable and is connected to the helical agitator. a feed inlet is located at the upper part of the main body, while a discharge gate is positioned at its lower part. additionally, the feed inlet sub-unit is equipped with an unloading buffering platform. inside the main body, a liquid addition pipe is installed, and externally, there is a liquid loading hopper. a flow controller is arranged between the liquid loading hopper and the main body. in the area where the main body meets the discharge gate, a baffle is installed, and a spout is provided at the top discharge opening for the removal of the baffle. the combination of the liquid loading hopper and the liquid filling tube allows for more precise integration of liquid materials in the feed mixing machine, ensuring a more uniform mix, which in turn improves both productivity and the quality of the fodder product. the discharge gate is designed to be more durable and offers excellent sealing performance to prevent material leakage.

raw: the utility model discloses a multi -functional children's toys baby with music broadcast is just easily accomodate, including doll body and low limbs, the internally mounted of doll body has circuit board and slide rail, the head is installed through rotary device in the top of doll body, the arm is all installed through first hinge means in the left and right sides of doll body, the below at the doll body is installed to low limbs, install pa -system, led lamp and function adjustment key on the front surface of doll body. the utility model discloses all set doll's four limbs to collapsible and mobilizable four limbs and head through the hinge means to guarantee that accomodating of toy is comparatively convenient, the functions that have music broadcast and simple english teaching that increase can help children's study more when children's amusement to can cultivate children's interest in learning.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a multifunctional baby doll toy featuring music broadcast capabilities and easy adjustability. the doll comprises a body and lower limbs, with a circuit board and slide rail mounted inside the body. the head is attached to the top of the body via a rotary device, while both arms are fixed to the left and right sides using hinge mechanisms. the lower limbs are installed at the bottom of the doll’s body, and a pa system, led lamp, and function adjustment key are placed on the front surface. the design allows all four limbs and the head to collapse and move via hinge mechanisms, ensuring convenient adjustability. additionally, the integrated music broadcast and simple english teaching functions not only enhance playtime enjoyment but also help cultivate children's interest in learning.

raw: the internet, and specifically social media, has unquestionably changed the way entrepreneurs do business. in many ways it has disrupted the traditional model of business, allowing companies to connect immediately with their consumers, become more responsive to opportunities and threats in the market, and drive and grow business. it has become a way of networking by allowing people to develop and grow their business networks faster and more efficiently online. while the traditional model of networking- going to events, passing out cards, and hoping to make that vital connection -still works, social media is now a critical element of networking that cannot be ignored. social media platforms such as facebook, linkedin, youtube, twitter, and instagram have taken the traditional networking model one step further, allowing savvy participants to connect to, literally, hundreds or thousands of contacts in a relatively short amount of time. social media also allows companies to continually interact with their contacts to develop long-standing relationships. while nothing beats face-to-face, personal interaction, especially in business, social media has elevated this model, increasing the likelihood that you are meeting people who are interested and engaged in what you are talking about.
rewritten: the internet—especially social media—has undeniably transformed how entrepreneurs operate. it has upended traditional business models by enabling companies to instantly connect with their customers, respond more quickly to market opportunities and challenges, and foster business growth. social media has revolutionized networking too, offering a faster and more efficient means to build and expand business connections online. while conventional networking methods like attending events, handing out business cards, and hoping for that key introduction still hold value, social media has become an indispensable part of the networking toolkit. platforms like facebook, linkedin, youtube, twitter, and instagram enhance traditional networking by allowing individuals to reach hundreds or even thousands of contacts in a short time. additionally, they enable companies to maintain ongoing communication with their contacts, fostering long-lasting relationships. although nothing can fully replace face-to-face interactions, especially in business, social media has elevated the networking experience by increasing the chances of engaging with individuals who are genuinely interested in what you have to say.

raw: with four children with autism we evaluated a refinement to time-based reinforcement designed to reduce response persistence when we simultaneously introduced time-based reinforcement and extinction. we further evaluated whether this refinement mitigated response recurrence when all reinforcer deliveries ceased during an extinction-only disruptor phase. the refinement involved increasing the saliency of the contingency change from contingent reinforcement (during baseline) to time-based reinforcement by delivering different colored reinforcers during time-based reinforcement. behavioral momentum theory predicts that increasing the discriminability of the change from variable-interval to variable-time reinforcement should lead to faster reductions in responding. we present data on four participants, three of whom displayed response patterns consistent with the predictions of behavioral momentum theory during time-based reinforcement. however, the participants showed more varied patterns of recurrent behavior during extinction. we discuss these results within a translational research framework focusing on strategies used to mitigate treatment relapse for severe destructive behavior, as time-based reinforcement is one of the most commonly prescribed interventions for destructive behavior displayed by individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
rewritten: we studied a modification of time-based reinforcement with four autistic children to lower the persistence of responding when extinction and time-based reinforcement were applied simultaneously. we also examined whether this modification reduced the recurrence of responses when all reinforcer deliveries stopped during a phase with only extinction. the modification involved making the contingency change—from contingent reinforcement during baseline to time-based reinforcement—more noticeable by using differently colored reinforcers during time-based reinforcement. according to behavioral momentum theory, enhancing the discriminability of the shift from variable-interval to variable-time reinforcement should lead to more rapid decreases in responding. data from four participants are presented; three showed response patterns during time-based reinforcement that aligned with behavioral momentum predictions, while their behavior during extinction revealed more varied recurrence patterns. these results are discussed within a translational research framework that focuses on strategies to reduce treatment relapse for severe destructive behavior, noting that time-based reinforcement is one of the most widely used interventions for destructive behavior in individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

raw: the utility model relates to a split formula resistance, including a resistor housing, first resistor core, the 2nd resistor housing, second resistor core, first resistor core is installed a resistor housing's inside, the second resistor core is installed the 2nd resistor housing's inside, just can dismantle continuously through the resistance pole between a resistor housing and the 2nd resistor housing. the utility model has the advantages of simple structure and reasonable design, can change the size of resistance, application scope is wide, and adopts zinc oxide ceramics linear resistance core and carbon film casing, can reduce cost, and convenient to use.
rewritten: this utility model pertains to a segmented resistor design that includes a resistor housing, a first resistor core, a second resistor housing, and a second resistor core. the first resistor core is positioned inside the resistor housing, while the second resistor core is positioned inside the second resistor housing. the two parts can be continuously adjusted by disassembling the connection via the resistance pole that links the resistor housing and the second resistor housing. this design boasts a simple structure and a well-thought-out layout, allowing for adjustments in resistance value and offering wide application potential. moreover, by utilizing a zinc oxide ceramics linear resistance core paired with a carbon film casing, the design is cost-effective and user-friendly.

raw: an embodiment of the utility model discloses a continuous automatic production line of dual component electron liquid silicon rubber for it is low to solve current electron liquid silicon rubber production efficiency, and the unstable problem of product quality. the embodiment of the utility model provides an in a continuous automatic production line of dual component electron liquid silicon rubber include: the side feeding machine, first screw rod host computer, the height glues the material cooler and dual component matrix storage tank loops through the pipe connection, the export of first powder metering tank, second powder metering tank and liquid material metering tank is all through the entry intercommunication of pipeline with the side feeding machine, the export of first component auxiliary agent metering tank and dual component matrix storage tank all communicates through pipeline and the first static entry that mixes the machine, and the first static export that mixes the machine is passed through the pipeline and is connected with first component split charging equipment, the export of second component auxiliary agent metering tank and dual component matrix storage tank all communicates through the static entry that mixes the machine of pipeline and second, and the static export that mixes the machine of second is passed through the pipeline and is connected with second component split charging equipment.
rewritten: one embodiment of the utility model discloses a continuous automatic production line for dual-component electron liquid silicone rubber, which addresses the challenges of low production efficiency and unstable product quality in current manufacturing processes. this production line includes a side feeding machine, a primary screw rod host computer, a material cooler (referred to as the "height glue"), and dual-component matrix storage tanks that are interconnected in a loop via pipelines. in addition, the outputs from the first powder metering tank, second powder metering tank, and liquid material metering tank are connected to the side feeding machine through pipelines. the outputs from the first component auxiliary agent metering tank and the dual-component matrix storage tank communicate via a pipeline with the first static inlet of the mixing machine; from the first static outlet of the mixing machine, another pipeline connects to the first component split charging equipment. similarly, the outputs of the second component auxiliary agent metering tank and the dual-component matrix storage tank are linked through a pipeline to the second static inlet of the mixing machine, and from the second static outlet, a pipeline connects to the second component split charging equipment.

raw: the utility model discloses an install skew monitoring devices's of light solar -thermal power generation system, include light and heat receiving arrangement, be used for with the sunlight reflection extremely reflection light condensation device and photic component on the light and heat receiving arrangement, the photic component is close to light and heat receiving arrangement and sets up for response illumination intensity, it still includes: radiating component, radiating component with the non - sensitive side of photic component is connected for dispel the heat to the photic component. non - sensitive side through at the photic component sets up radiating component, when the skew light and heat receiving arrangement of light shines the photic component, the heat absorption of photic component heaies up, but the heat that produces can conduct rapidly to the framework that dispels the heat, scatter and disappear to external environment through radiating component, form the protection to the photic component, avoided the photic member for a long time under the high temperature work still can cause the device ageing problem that seriously shortens its life rapidly, the temperature of also having avoided the photic component endures the limit above the temperature, the problem that leads to the damage of photic component.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an installation skew monitoring device for a light solar-thermal power generation system. the system comprises a light and heat receiving assembly equipped with both a sunlight-reflecting light condensation device and a photic component. the photic component is mounted close to the light and heat receiving assembly to respond to illumination intensity. in addition, the device includes a radiating component connected to the non-sensitive side of the photic component for heat dissipation. when light shines on the photic component via the light and heat receiving assembly, the photic component absorbs heat and its temperature increases. however, the heat is quickly conducted away into a heat-dispelling framework and then dispersed into the external environment by the radiating component. this effective heat dissipation protects the photic component by preventing prolonged exposure to high temperatures, which could otherwise cause accelerated aging and damage by exceeding its operational heat limits.

raw: we are happy to welcome you to the 18th international acm sigaccess conference on computers and accessibility (assets 2016). the assets conference is the premier computing research conference exploring the design, evaluation, and use of computing and information technologies to benefit people with disabilities and older adults. in our 18th year, the assets conference will continue its tradition of presenting innovative research on mainstream and specialized assistive technologies, accessible computing, and assistive applications of computer, network, and information technologies. for the main (technical paper) track of the conference, the call for papers attracted submissions from across the world: austria (1), brazil (7), canada (4), china (1), denmark (1), ecuador (1), finland (1), france (1), germany (3), india (1), japan (3), mexico (1), new zealand (1), pakistan (1), portugal (2), singapore (2), the united kingdom (8), and the united states (56). overall, we received 95 submissions from which we selected 24 for inclusion in the program, for an acceptance rate of 25%. acceptance decisions were made by consensus of the program committee nmembers, who discussed the papers online. the program committee consisted of 56 senior members of our research community who, along with 6 advanced doctoral students, produced a total of 288 reviews. we would like to thank them for their thorough reviews and thoughtful discussion and consideration of the submissions. in addition to the main technical paper track of the conference, our program committee also reviewed 10 experience reports (4 accepted), 58 posters submissions (29 accepted), and 15 demo submissions (7 accepted). in addition, members of the program committee and other senior researchers helped to review submissions for the student research competition and doctoral consortium events. the assets 2016 program is organized as follows: on monday (day 1), the program starts with a keynote presentation by dr. richard ladner who is a professor from the university of washington, and his keynote is entitled "accessibility is becoming mainstream." professor ladner is the recipient of the 2016 sigaccess award for outstanding contribution, an award that recognizes individuals who have made significant and lasting contributions to the development of computing technologies that improve the accessibility of media and services to people with disabilities. following the keynote, we will have a series of technical paper sessions organized around deaf and hard of hearing users, users with developmental disabilities, and tactile information for blind users. the poster sessions during the day will feature poster and demo papers, along with student research competition and sigaccess travel scholarship recipients. the day will end with a reception at the conference hotel. on tuesday (day 2), the program will continue with sessions on big data and blind users, rehabilitation and clinical technologies, and communication and aging. during the morning, we will also have a special session for the text entry challenge, featuring research in the area of text-entry technologies; this session is in memory of torsten felzer, a researcher in our community who passed away earlier this year. we will also hear short talks from the finalists in the acm student research competition. the poster sessions on this day will include poster and demo papers, along with posters on the topic of text-entry and posters from our doctoral consortium participants. the day will conclude with a special reception at the national automobile museum. on wednesday (day 3), the program will conclude with technical sessions on users with visual impairments, haptic and audio feedback for blind users, social issues and assistive technology, and accessibility education. after announcing the best paper and best student paper awards at the closing session, we will then introduce next year's chairs to tell you about assets 2017.

raw: the invention discloses an e-cigarette flavor and a preparation method thereof. the e-cigarette flavor is a natural e-cigarette flavor prepared with a biological fermentation method in combination with a dry distillation flavor preparation technology. the preparation method comprises the steps as follows: different fruits and vegetables are mixed in proportion and fermented with yeast, and an enzyme extract solution is prepared. meanwhile, fermentation residues are treated with the dry distillation technology and fractions with the strong curing aroma are obtained. fresh fruits produce abundant secondary metabolites and a large number of aromatic substances with the fruit flavor after microbial fermentation. fermented residues of fruits contain rich aroma-producing precursors such as fructose, protein and the like. a series of small-molecular aroma substances are produced during heating and dry distillation. the e-cigarette liquid flavor having the natural fruit aroma and the curing aroma can be obtained after these aroma components are mixed in a proper proportion.
rewritten: this invention introduces an e-cigarette flavor and its production method. the flavor is naturally derived using a biological fermentation approach combined with a dry distillation flavor preparation process. the production method involves several key steps: firstly, various fruits and vegetables are mixed in specified proportions and fermented with yeast to create an enzyme extract solution. at the same time, the fermentation residue is processed using dry distillation, yielding fractions with a pronounced curing aroma. fresh fruits generate an abundance of secondary metabolites and a wide range of aromatic compounds through microbial fermentation, while the fermented fruit residues are rich in aroma precursors such as fructose and proteins. these precursors transform into a range of small-molecule aroma substances during the heating and dry distillation process. finally, by blending these aroma components in suitable proportions, an e-cigarette liquid flavor that embodies both natural fruit and curing aromas is produced.

raw: the invention aims to provides an alkaline-lipase-producing aspergillus niger mutant strain which is prepared by the following steps: transforming an alkaline lipase gene into an aspergillus niger host strain to construct an aspergillus niger engineering strain capable of efficiently expressing alkaline lipase; and knocking out the amylase gene of the host strain by gene knock-out means, and screening to obtain the mutant strain with obviously enhanced alkaline lipase yield. the collection number of the mutant aspergillus niger strain is cctcc no:m2015761. the alkaline lipase activity of the mutant strain aspergillus niger su-12 reaches 15766 u/ml, and the protein expression level is 5.4 g/l, which are respectively enhanced by 56.3% and 18.9% as compared with the strain before mutation, thereby being beneficial to wide application of the enzyme.
rewritten: the invention provides an alkaline-lipase-producing mutant strain of aspergillus niger. this strain is developed through a two-step process. first, an alkaline lipase gene is introduced into an aspergillus niger host strain, resulting in an engineered strain that can efficiently express alkaline lipase. then, the host strain’s amylase gene is knocked out using gene knockout techniques, and the strain with a significantly increased alkaline lipase yield is isolated. this mutant strain has the collection number cctcc no.: m2015761. its alkaline lipase activity reaches 15,766 u/ml, and the protein expression level is 5.4 g/l—improvements of 56.3% and 18.9%, respectively, compared to the original strain—making the enzyme highly suitable for broad applications.

raw: abstract introduction identification is one of the challenging aspects of forensic sciences. despite the numerous anthropological researches on the scapula, there is a notable lack of information concerning the scapular growth. aim of the work aim of the work was to analyze the growth of the scapula by means of thirteen measurements commonly used for assessment among a sample of young aged egyptian population, in order to evaluate their significance and capacity for age and sex determination during bone development using reconstructed ct images. subjects and methods the study was conducted on 162 egyptian patients (83 males and 79 females), ranging from birth to 25 years of age, referred to the radiodiagnosis and intervention department, faculty of medicine, alexandria university for multidetector computed tomography (mdct) of the chest. thirteen measurements were selected on the scapula and were subjected to statistical analysis. results a significant positive correlation was detected between the measured scapular variables and the age. significant sexual dimorphism was identified in four of the scapular measurements in the youngest age group. however, significant differences between the sexes appeared after the cessation of growth in girls, where a large number of variables (the scapular length, breadth, maximum length of the spine, supra and infra scapular height, maximum length of the glenoid mass, thickness of the lateral border, as well as the glenoid, supra-infrascapular indices) were sexually dimorphic. conclusions the results achieved from the current study are useful tools in the diagnosis of age and sex in both forensic and bio-archeological identification procedures; however, further studies are strongly suggested.
rewritten: abstract introduction – identifying individuals is one of the most challenging aspects of forensic science. although extensive anthropological studies have focused on the scapula, there is still limited information available on scapular growth. aim of the work – the objective of this study was to examine scapular growth using thirteen standard measurements among a sample of young egyptian individuals. the goal was to assess the significance of these measurements and their potential for determining age and sex during bone development, utilizing reconstructed ct images. subjects and methods – the study involved 162 egyptian patients (83 males and 79 females) ranging in age from birth to 25 years. all participants underwent multidetector computed tomography (mdct) of the chest at the radiodiagnosis and intervention department, faculty of medicine, alexandria university. thirteen scapular measurements were taken and analyzed statistically. results – a significant positive correlation was observed between the scapular measurements and age. in the youngest age group, four scapular measurements already demonstrated significant sexual dimorphism. after girls' growth had ceased, additional significant sex differences were noted in several variables, including scapular length, breadth, maximum length of the spine, supra- and infra-scapular height, maximum length of the glenoid mass, thickness of the lateral border, as well as the glenoid and supra-infrascapular indices. conclusions – the findings of this study provide valuable tools for age and sex diagnosis in forensic and bioarchaeological investigations. nonetheless, further research in this area is strongly recommended.

raw: assessing hurricane landfall risk is of immense public utility, yet extant methods of diagnosing annual tropical cyclone (tc) activity demonstrate no skill in diagnosing u.s. hurricane landfalls. atlantic tc count itself has limited skill, explaining less than 20% of interannual variance in landfall incidence. using extended landfall activity and reanalysis data sets, we employed empirical poisson modeling to produce a landfall diagnostic index (ldi), incorporating spatially and temporally averaged upper level divergence, relative sea surface temperature, meridional wind, and zonal shear vorticity. ldi captures 31% of interannual variability of u.s. hurricane landfalls and offers physical insight into why indices that successfully capture tc activity fail to diagnose landfalls: there is inherent tension between conditions likely to steer hurricanes toward the u.s. and conditions favorable for tc development. given this tension, attempting to diagnose, predict, or understand tc count is inadequate for quantifying societal impacts due to landfalling hurricanes.
rewritten: evaluating the risk of hurricane landfalls is extremely valuable for the public, yet current approaches for assessing annual tropical cyclone (tc) activity are ineffective at predicting u.s. hurricane strikes. in fact, the total number of atlantic tcs explains less than 20% of the year-to-year variability in landfall occurrences. by using extended datasets on landfall activity and atmospheric reanalyses, we applied empirical poisson modeling to create a landfall diagnostic index (ldi). this index integrates spatially and temporally averaged measures of upper-level divergence, relative sea surface temperature, meridional wind, and zonal shear vorticity. the ldi captures 31% of the interannual variability in u.s. hurricane landfalls and provides physical insight into why indices that are successful in capturing overall tc activity fail to diagnose landfalls: there is an inherent conflict between the atmospheric conditions that tend to direct hurricanes toward the u.s. and those that promote tc formation. as a result, relying solely on tc counts is inadequate for accurately predicting, assessing, or understanding the societal impacts of hurricanes making landfall.

raw: power system operators commonly rely on a security-constrained unit commitment (scuc) to schedule available generation resources for satisfying the forecasted load and ensuring system security in response to limited components unavailability. although widely used and proved viable, the scuc problem cannot guarantee a useful solution in response to extreme events with potentially multiple outages. this paper proposes and formulates an event-driven scuc (e-scuc) model which ensures a resilient supply of loads in case of multiple component outages. the new n-1-m reliability criterion, in which m is the number of components on outage due to the extreme event, is proposed and integrated with the model. a probabilistic estimation model of possible component outages is further investigated. the e-scuc problem is formulated using mixed-integer linear programming (milp). merits and effectiveness of the proposed models are demonstrated using the standard ieee 30-bus system. comparing the results of the e-scuc with the scuc demonstrates the resiliency benefits of the proposed model as it ensures a considerably lower load curtailment at the expense of nominal cost increase.
rewritten: power system operators typically use a security-constrained unit commitment (scuc) process to schedule available generation resources, meeting the forecasted load while maintaining system security despite limited component outages. although this method is widely accepted and proven effective, the scuc approach may not yield a viable solution when facing extreme events involving multiple outages. this paper introduces an event-driven scuc (e-scuc) model designed to ensure load supply resilience during multiple component outages. the model incorporates a new n-1-m reliability criterion—where m denotes the number of components that fail during an extreme event—and also explores a probabilistic model for estimating potential component outages. the e-scuc problem is formulated as a mixed-integer linear programming (milp) issue. its advantages and effectiveness are demonstrated using the standard ieee 30-bus system. the results show that compared to the traditional scuc, the proposed e-scuc model significantly reduces load curtailment, albeit with a slight increase in overall cost.

raw: the invention provides a portable human body signal monitoring apparatus and a human body signal monitoring method. the portable human body signal monitoring apparatus comprises a mater monitoring assembly and a slave monitoring assembly. the master monitoring assembly comprises a first monitoring electrode, a first electrode connection portion for acquiring monitoring signals of the first monitoring electrode, a signal processing circuit board and a first power supply part for supplying power to the signal processing circuit board, wherein the first electrode connection portion is connected with the signal processing circuit board. the slave monitoring assembly comprises a second monitoring electrode, a second electrode connection portion for acquiring monitoring signals of the second monitoring electrode, and a second power supply part. a connection cable is used for connecting the master monitoring assembly with the slave monitoring assembly so as to connect the first power supply part with the second power supply part and connect the second monitoring electrode with the signal processing circuit board, and the signal processing circuit processes and/or transmits the monitoring signals of the first monitoring electrode and the monitoring signals of the second monitoring electrode so as to continuously perform human body signal monitoring.
rewritten: the invention introduces a portable apparatus for monitoring human body signals as well as a method for carrying out such monitoring. the apparatus includes a master monitoring assembly and a slave monitoring assembly. the master monitoring assembly features a first monitoring electrode, a first electrode connection portion that gathers signals from this electrode, a signal processing circuit board, and a first power supply unit that powers the circuit board. notably, the first electrode connection portion is linked to the signal processing circuit board. the slave monitoring assembly consists of a second monitoring electrode, a second electrode connection portion that collects signals from this electrode, and a second power supply unit. a connecting cable is used to join the master and slave monitoring assemblies, linking the first power supply to the second and connecting the second monitoring electrode to the signal processing circuit board. the signal processing circuit then processes and/or transmits the signals gathered from both the first and second monitoring electrodes, thereby enabling continuous monitoring of human body signals.

raw: the thermoreflectance-based techniques time- and frequency-domain thermoreflectance (tdtr and fdtr, respectively) have emerged as robust platforms to measure the thermophysical properties of a wide array of systems on varying length scales. routine in the implementation of these techniques is the application of a thin metal film on the surface of the sample of interest to serve as an opto-thermal transducer ensuring the measured modulated reflectivity is dominated by the change in thermoreflectance of the sample. here, we outline a method to directly measure the thermal conductivities of bulk materials without using a metal transducer layer using a standard tdtr/fdtr experiment. a major key in this approach is the use of a thermal model with z-dependent heat source when the optical penetration depth is comparable to the beam sizes and measuring the fdtr response at a long delay time to minimize non-thermoreflectivity contributions to the modulated reflectance signals (such as free carrier excitations). using this approach, we demonstrate the ability to measure the thermal conductivity on three semiconductors, intrinsic si (100), gaas (100), and insb (100), the results of which are validated with fdtr measurements on the same wafers with aluminum transducers. we outline the major sources of uncertainty in this approach, including frequency dependent heating and precise knowledge of the pump and probe spot sizes. as a result, we discuss appropriate pump-frequency ranges in which to implement this tdtr/fdtr approach and present a procedure to measure the effective spot sizes by fitting the fdtr data of an 80 nm al/sio2 sample at a time delay in which the spot size sensitivity dominates an fdtr measurement over the substrate thermal properties. our method provides a more convenient way to directly measure the thermal conductivities of semiconductors.
rewritten: thermoreflectance-based techniques—specifically time-domain thermoreflectance (tdtr) and frequency-domain thermoreflectance (fdtr)—have become reliable platforms for assessing the thermophysical properties of diverse systems across various length scales. in standard practice with these methods, a thin metal film is applied to the sample’s surface to act as an opto-thermal transducer, ensuring that the measured modulated reflectivity is primarily due to the sample’s thermoreflectance change. here, we describe a method that directly measures the thermal conductivity of bulk materials without the need for a metal transducer layer, utilizing a conventional tdtr/fdtr experiment. a critical aspect of this approach is the implementation of a thermal model that incorporates a z-dependent heat source when the optical penetration depth is similar in scale to the beam sizes, as well as taking fdtr measurements at a long delay time to reduce the contribution of non-thermoreflective effects (such as free carrier excitations) in the modulated reflectance signals. with this method, we demonstrate the capability to measure the thermal conductivity of three semiconductors—intrinsic si (100), gaas (100), and insb (100)—with the results validated by fdtr measurements on corresponding wafers that included aluminum transducers. we also discuss the primary sources of uncertainty inherent within this approach—specifically frequency-dependent heating and the need for precise determination of pump and probe spot sizes. consequently, we outline the pump-frequency ranges suitable for implementing this tdtr/fdtr method and present a procedure to determine the effective spot sizes by fitting fdtr data from an 80 nm al/sio2 sample, selecting a time delay where the measurement is more sensitive to the spot size than to the substrate’s thermal properties. this method offers a more streamlined approach for directly measuring the thermal conductivities of semiconductors.

raw: this article focuses on the mode-switch control of a new type of full hybrid electric vehicle with a torque-limiting clutch and an overrunning clutch. the working mode regions of hybrid electric ve...
rewritten: this article examines the mode-switching control strategy for a new full hybrid electric vehicle that is equipped with both a torque-limiting clutch and an overrunning clutch. it also discusses the different operational mode regions of hybrid electric vehicles...

raw: aim to investigate and compare the histological findings of patients with diabetes, hypothyroidism and idiopathic carpal tunnel syndrome (cts). material and methods subsynovial connective tissue samples of 51 idiopathic cts patients (group 1), 58 diabetic cts patients (group 2) and 16 hypothyroid cts patients (group 3) were evaluated in this study. the histopathological examination parameters were; number of fibroblasts, size of collagen fibers, vascular changes (vascular proliferation, intimal thickening and changes of vessel structures), edema and inflammatory infiltration. results in the majority of patients in all 3 groups, non-inflammatory fibrosis was observed in subsynovial connective tissue. number of fibroblasts, collagen fiber diameter and lengths were statistically different in diabetic cts patients (group 2) when compared with other groups. considering the overall results, neovascularization in subsynovial connective tissue was observed significantly more intensely in diabetic cts patients (group 2) and severe edema was found in hypothyroid cts patients (group 3). conclusion increased pressure in the carpal tunnel may be a result of reduction of the space or volume increase of the tunnel contents secondary to fibrosis or edema. it may be helpful for the physicians to better understand the causes of this entrapment neuropathy, and these etiological factors should be taken into consideration during the preoperative evaluation of the patients.
rewritten: this study set out to examine and compare the histological characteristics observed in patients with diabetes, hypothyroidism, and idiopathic carpal tunnel syndrome (cts). the investigation involved analyzing subsynovial connective tissue samples from three groups: 51 idiopathic cts patients (group 1), 58 diabetic cts patients (group 2), and 16 hypothyroid cts patients (group 3). the parameters evaluated during the histopathological examination included the number of fibroblasts, the size and length of collagen fibers, vascular alterations (such as vascular proliferation, intimal thickening, and changes in vessel structure), edema, and the presence of inflammatory infiltrates. the results indicated that non-inflammatory fibrosis in the subsynovial connective tissue was prevalent across the majority of patients in all three groups. however, diabetic cts patients (group 2) showed statistically significant differences with regard to the number of fibroblasts, as well as the diameter and length of collagen fibers, when compared with the other groups. additionally, intense neovascularization in the subsynovial connective tissue was significantly more pronounced in group 2, while severe edema was predominantly noted in hypothyroid cts patients (group 3). the study concludes that the increased pressure seen in the carpal tunnel may arise either from a decrease in available space or from an increase in the volume of the tunnel contents due to fibrosis or edema. recognizing these etiological factors could aid physicians in better understanding the mechanisms behind this entrapment neuropathy and should be factored into the preoperative assessment of patients.

raw: abstract the mechanisms of three cascade reactions between n-methylindole and sulfonylazides via huisgen cycloaddition were investigated using density functional theory, and the polarized continuum model (pcm) was applied to simulate the solvent effects. fukui functions and dual descriptor have been employed to assess the atomic reactivity. with respect to these three reactions, each has two possible reaction pathways (i and ii). the calculated results show that singlet o2 plays a crucial role in the oxidative dehydrogenation process in the first reaction between 2-imine indole and p-tosylazide (tsn3), when the reaction occurs without o2 but with n2, two pathways via the processes of breaking two covalent bonds, followed by 1,2-h shift and n2 removal (or by n2 removal and 1,2-h shift) exist. water molecule plays an important role in the h-shift process in the third reaction of the methyl substituted n-methylindole with tsn3. our results demonstrated that these reactions can take place at certain condition, in good agreement with the experimental observation. the understanding of the competitive pathways for huisgen cycloaddition of n-methylindole and sulfonylazides can provide valuable insights into related reactions.
rewritten: the study investigated the mechanisms of three cascade reactions between n-methylindole and sulfonylazides through huisgen cycloaddition, using density functional theory along with the polarized continuum model (pcm) to account for solvent effects. fukui functions and the dual descriptor were applied to evaluate atomic reactivity. for each of the three reactions, two reaction pathways (labeled i and ii) were considered. the calculations revealed that in the first reaction between 2-imine indole and p-tosylazide (tsn3), singlet o2 is essential for the oxidative dehydrogenation process. in contrast, under an inert atmosphere with n2 instead of o2, two different pathways emerge, involving the cleavage of two covalent bonds followed by either a 1,2-h shift and subsequent n2 removal or the reverse order. additionally, water molecules are found to play a key role in facilitating the h-shift process in the third reaction involving the methyl-substituted n-methylindole with tsn3. these findings corroborate experimental observations, suggesting that the reactions proceed under specific conditions. understanding these competitive mechanisms in the huisgen cycloaddition of n-methylindole and sulfonylazides offers valuable insights into related chemical processes.

raw: a vehicle radar system and a method for operating a vehicle radar system are disclosed. a vehicle radar system includes a first transmitting antenna configured to transmit a first radar signal toward a target, a second transmitting antenna configured to transmit a second radar signal having the same waveform as the first radar signal toward the target, and a first controller coupled to the first transmitting antenna. the first controller is configured to set a first blanking time that comprises a time of an inflection point of the first radar signal based on a delay time between a transmission time of the first radar signal and a transmission time of the second radar signal.
rewritten: a vehicle radar system and a method for operating such a system are disclosed. the vehicle radar system comprises a first transmitting antenna that sends a first radar signal toward a target, a second transmitting antenna that sends a second radar signal—with the same waveform as the first signal—toward the target, and a first controller connected to the first transmitting antenna. the first controller is programmed to establish a first blanking time, which includes the time corresponding to an inflection point of the first radar signal, based on the delay between the transmission times of the first and second radar signals.
