raw: continued growth in the commercial aerospace industry makes aircraft manufacturers look for low maintenance, fuel efficient airplanes and aircraft engines are an important factor in the maintenance and fuel costs. the drive for operating cost reduction leads to innovative engine architectures that can place very different requirements on supplier provided components. the suppliers will then need to understand and predict consequences on their components and sub systems following the new system architectures proposed. insights they get about the consequences of engine architecture changes help them to be prepared for future developments. this research presents two aspects of the behaviour of aero engine static components from a supplier: (1) the behaviour of the component as a whole in the system and (2) the internal organisation of the component so that the behaviour required of it is achieved by the system. system in this context refers to the aero engine. the component is integrated in that multiple functions are satisfied by one single structure. studies in the first aspect provide a measure of the sensitivity of the whole engine to component designs. the studies also present an initial framework to perform such system-component interaction investigations. studies in the second aspect provide a way to organise functions and means for an integrated architecture product so that the internal organisation is better understood and can be subjected to evaluations. preliminary results show that the influence of the supplier component on system level operations cannot be neglected. the framework to evaluate system level effects of supplier component design needs further refinement with better fidelity models at all levels considered. with respect to the internal organisation of components, a method now exists to isolate, organise, represent and analyse design information of integrated components, taking into consideration different manufacturing options. continued studies will focus on functional and physical domain decomposition of the component reliably and objectively so that alternative product architectures for the component can be evaluated and appropriate design and manufacturing decisions can be made.
rewritten: the continued expansion of the commercial aerospace sector is driving aircraft manufacturers to seek airplanes that require less maintenance and offer better fuel efficiency. since aircraft engines play a major role in overall maintenance and fuel costs, there is a push to reduce operating expenses through innovative engine architectures. these new designs impose varied demands on components supplied by external vendors, who must then predict how their parts and subsystems will be affected. understanding the implications of changes in engine architecture enables suppliers to prepare for future developments. this research examines two aspects of how static aero engine components—viewed from a supplier’s perspective—behave. first, it considers the component’s performance as an integral part of the overall system. second, it looks at the internal organization of the component, ensuring that the system achieves the required functionality. here, the term “system” refers to the aero engine, where a single structure fulfills multiple functions. the first set of studies provides a measure of the engine’s sensitivity to different component designs and introduces an initial framework for analyzing system-component interactions. the second set offers a method to organize, represent, and analyze the design information of integrated components, taking into account various manufacturing options, thus enhancing understanding of the internal organization for evaluation purposes. preliminary results indicate that a supplier’s component can significantly influence system-level operations. the framework used to assess these effects needs further refinement, with higher-fidelity models at all levels of analysis. regarding internal organization, a new method allows for the isolation, organization, representation, and analysis of design information for integrated components, including different manufacturing alternatives. future work will focus on reliably and objectively breaking down both the functional and physical domains of the component, which will enable the evaluation of alternative product architectures and support well-informed design and manufacturing decisions.

raw: the unintended consequences of gene targeting in mouse models have not been thoroughly studied and a more systematic analysis is needed to understand the frequency and characteristics of off-target effects. using rna-seq, we evaluated targeted and neighboring gene expression in tissues from 44 homozygous mutants compared with c57bl/6n control mice. two allele types were evaluated: 15 targeted trap mutations (trap); and 29 deletion alleles (del), usually a deletion between the translational start and the 3' utr. both targeting strategies insert a bacterial beta-galactosidase reporter (lacz) and a neomycin resistance selection cassette. evaluating transcription of genes in +/- 500 kb of flanking dna around the targeted gene, we found up-regulated genes more frequently around del compared with trap alleles, however the frequency of alleles with local down-regulated genes flanking del and trap targets was similar. down-regulated genes around both del and trap targets were found at a higher frequency than expected from a genome-wide survey. however, only around del targets were up-regulated genes found with a significantly higher frequency compared with genome-wide sampling. transcriptome analysis confirms targeting in 97% of del alleles, but in only 47% of trap alleles probably due to non-functional splice variants, and some splicing around the gene trap. local effects on gene expression are likely due to a number of factors including compensatory regulation, loss or disruption of intragenic regulatory elements, the exogenous promoter in the neo selection cassette, removal of insulating dna in the del mutants, and local silencing due to disruption of normal chromatin organization or presence of exogenous dna. an understanding of local position effects is important for understanding and interpreting any phenotype attributed to targeted gene mutations, or to spontaneous indels.
rewritten: the unintended effects of gene targeting in mouse models have not been fully explored, and a more systematic study is needed to determine the frequency and nature of off-target events. using rna sequencing, we compared the expression of targeted and nearby genes in tissues from 44 homozygous mutant mice against c57bl/6n controls. two types of alleles were examined: 15 gene trap mutations ("trap") and 29 deletion alleles ("del"), with the deletion alleles generally involving removal from the translation start site to the 3' utr. both approaches introduce a bacterial beta-galactosidase reporter (lacz) along with a neomycin resistance selection cassette. when assessing transcription of genes within ±500 kb of the targeted gene, we observed that upregulation was more common around deletion alleles compared to gene trap alleles, although the frequency of local downregulation was similar for both mutant types. in fact, downregulated genes flanking both del and trap targets appeared more often than what would be expected from a genome-wide survey. however, only deletion alleles exhibited a significantly higher frequency of upregulated genes compared to genome-wide sampling. additionally, transcriptome analysis confirmed successful targeting in 97% of the deletion alleles but only in 47% of the gene trap alleles—a discrepancy likely due to the appearance of non-functional splice variants and some splicing events occurring around the gene trap. the local effects on gene expression are probably the result of several factors, including compensatory regulation, the loss or disruption of intragenic regulatory elements, the influence of the exogenous promoter in the neomycin cassette, the removal of insulating dna in the deletion mutants, and local silencing caused by changes in chromatin organization or the presence of foreign dna. understanding these local positional effects is essential for accurately interpreting any phenotype attributed to targeted gene mutations or spontaneous indels.

raw: the utility model discloses a filter circuit of radiation dose detector, the first resistance of signal input part through establishing ties, the positive going input end that a preceding stage filter and a capacitance connection were put to first fortune, the preceding stage filter includes two parallelly connected electric capacity branch roads, second electric capacity and third electric capacity that first electric capacity route is established ties are formed, fourth electric capacity and the 5th electric capacity that a second electric capacity route is established ties are formed, be connected to between fourth electric capacity and the 5th electric capacity through first inductance between second electric capacity and the third electric capacity, through the 6th capacity ground between fourth electric capacity and the 5th electric capacity, the output of putting through second ohmic connection to first fortune between preceding stage filter and the first electric capacity, the positive going input end that first fortune was put is through the 3rd grounding, the reverse input end that first fortune was put passes through fourth grounding. the utility model discloses the not enough of prior art can be improved, the interference killing feature of detector has been improved.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a filter circuit for a radiation dose detector. in this design, a first resistor is connected at the signal input section, and the positive input terminal is connected—via a capacitor—to what is referred to as the first node. this circuit includes a preceding-stage filter that features two parallel capacitor branches. in one branch, a second capacitor and a third capacitor are connected in parallel; in the other branch, a fourth capacitor and a fifth capacitor are connected in parallel. an inductor is connected between the branch containing the fourth and fifth capacitors and the branch containing the second and third capacitors, while a sixth capacitor is connected between the fourth and fifth capacitors and ground. the output from the preceding-stage filter is then connected through a second resistor to the first node. additionally, the positive input of the first node is connected to ground via a third grounding point, while the negative input is connected to ground through a fourth grounding point. this utility model improves on shortcomings found in the prior art by enhancing the detector’s ability to suppress interference.

raw: an optical product can reproduce a first 3d image of at least part of a first 3d object and a second 3d image of at least part of a second 3d object. the optical product comprises a surface configured, when illuminated, to reproduce by reflected or transmitted light, the first 3d image without reproducing the second 3d image at a first angle of view, and the second 3d image without reproducing the first 3d image at a second angle of view. each portion of first portions can correspond to a point on a surface of the first 3d object, and comprise first non-holographic features configured to produce at least part of the first 3d image. each portion of second portions can correspond to a point on a surface of the second 3d object, and comprise second non- holographic features configured to produce at least part of the second 3d image.
rewritten: an optical product is capable of reproducing a first three-dimensional (3d) image of at least part of a first 3d object, as well as a second 3d image of at least part of a second 3d object. this product features a surface that, when illuminated, is designed to display the first 3d image using reflected or transmitted light without showing the second image from a first viewing angle, and to display the second 3d image without showing the first image from a different (second) viewing angle. in this design, each segment of the first image correlates with a point on the surface of the first 3d object and includes first non-holographic features that generate at least a portion of the first 3d image. similarly, each segment of the second image corresponds to a point on the surface of the second 3d object and features second non-holographic elements that generate at least a portion of the second 3d image.

raw: the invention relates to the socket peripheral technical field, and especially relates to a hanging vertical type power strip hanger comprising a hanging piece, a support piece and a pedestal piece; the hanging piece is connected with the support piece; the support piece is connected with the pedestal piece; the hanging piece comprises a hanging body and a suspending piece arranged on the hanging body; the suspending piece is arranged on one end, far away from the support piece, of the hanging body, and comprises a suspending portion used for suspending the power strip; the support piece comprises a rod body connected with the hanging body; the pedestal piece comprises a pedestal body connected with one end, far away from the suspending piece, of the rod body; the hanging vertical type power strip hanger arranges the suspending piece on the hanging piece so as to hang the power strip; an opening direction of the jacks on the power strip and the gravity direction are staggered, thus preventing dusts in the air from entering the power strip through the jacks, and improving power consumption safety.
rewritten: the invention is in the field of socket peripherals and specifically relates to a hanging vertical power strip hanger that consists of a hanging piece, a support piece, and a pedestal piece. the hanging piece is connected to the support piece, which in turn connects to the pedestal piece. more specifically, the hanging piece includes a hanging body that features a suspending piece at the end farthest from the support piece. this suspending piece includes a portion specifically designed for hanging the power strip. meanwhile, the support piece comprises a rod body that connects with the hanging body, and the pedestal piece comprises a pedestal body connected to the end of the rod body that is farthest from the suspending piece. by positioning the suspending piece on the hanging piece, the design allows the power strip to hang, and the staggered orientation between the opening direction of its jacks and the direction of gravity prevents airborne dust from entering the jacks, thereby enhancing power consumption safety.

raw: true teacher, the substance is a true example of a teacher, tireless , hard work, struggle and sacrifice for the success of his students. teachers have a minimum three main tasks, namely: (1) professional duties, (2) human duty, and (3) civic duties. furthermore, with regard to who the true teacher? there are five important things attached as a true teacher, namely: (1) the teachers are second parents, (2) the teacher is a motivator, (3) the teacher is the adventurous, (4) teacher, liberator and fighters, and (5) teacher, personal prophetic spirit. to be able to be a true teacher, five elements must be attached, namely: (1) responsibility , not a privilege, (2) the sacrifice, not the facility, (3) work hard , not relaxed; (4) authority, not authoritarian, and (5) modeling. true teacher is fully teachers in goodness, not tempted by the glitter of the world. if teachers are tempted by the glitter of the world, they will ruin their students and even society. hope all the children of this nation remains a good teacher or professional teachers who meet the competency of personality, pedagogic, social, and professional as well as the holy prophet (siddeeq, trustworthy, sermons, and fathonah). they eventually bring success for the glory of indonesian education.
rewritten: a true teacher embodies unwavering dedication—displaying tireless effort, perseverance, and sacrifice to ensure the success of their students. teachers hold at least three core responsibilities: professional duties, human obligations, and civic roles. moreover, what defines a true teacher can be seen in five key characteristics: acting as a second parent, serving as a motivator, embracing adventurousness, being a liberator and fighter, and exhibiting a personal prophetic spirit. to truly become a teacher, one must cultivate five essential qualities: embracing responsibility rather than seeking privilege, choosing sacrifice over ease, committing to hard work instead of complacency, exercising authority without authoritarianism, and serving as a positive role model. a true teacher remains devoted to goodness and resists the temptations of worldly allure. should teachers fall prey to such distractions, they risk undermining not only their students but also society at large. it is our hope that every child of this nation grows up to be a good teacher or professional educator—one who meets the standards of personality, pedagogy, social engagement, and professionalism, along with the noble attributes of the holy prophet (integrity, trustworthiness, eloquence, and wisdom)—ultimately contributing to the success and honor of indonesian education.

raw: leptin is an adipokine that regulates various metabolism, but its association with secondary hyperparathyroidism (shpt), a clinical manifestation of chronic kidney disease-mineral and bone disorder (ckd-mbd), remains obscure. parathyroidectomy (ptx) is recommended for severe shpt patients. here, the associations between circulating leptin and clinical characteristics in ckd patients were investigated. effects of ptx on leptin production were analyzed in vivo and in vitro. controls and ckd patients had approximate serum leptin levels in that a larger proportion of ckd patients with body mass index (bmi) <23 kg/m2. serum leptin was related to anemia, albumin, and bone metabolism disorders in ckd patients. lower intact parathyroid hormone (pth) was related with higher leptin in ptx patients group. severe shpt inhibited uremia-enhanced leptin production in 3t3-l1 adipocytes, which was attenuated after ptx. high levels of pth were found to reduce akt phosphorylation and leptin production in vitro but high levels of calcium and phosphorus were not. successful ptx was found to improve anemia and malnutrition in severe shpt patients, and this was correlated with increased circulating leptin levels via up-regulated akt signaling in adipocytes. these findings indicated the therapeutic potential of leptin and related target pathway for improving survival and quality of life in ckd.
rewritten: leptin is an adipokine involved in regulating various metabolic processes, yet its link to secondary hyperparathyroidism (shpt)—a clinical manifestation of chronic kidney disease-mineral and bone disorder (ckd-mbd)—remains unclear. parathyroidectomy (ptx) is the recommended treatment for patients with severe shpt. in this study, we examined the relationships between circulating leptin levels and clinical characteristics in ckd patients, and we analyzed the effects of ptx on leptin production both in vivo and in vitro. the serum leptin levels of the control group and ckd patients were similar overall, although a larger proportion of ckd patients had a body mass index (bmi) below 23 kg/m². in ckd patients, serum leptin was associated with anemia, albumin levels, and disorders in bone metabolism. in the ptx patient group, lower intact parathyroid hormone (pth) levels were linked to higher leptin levels. severe shpt was found to suppress the uremia-induced enhancement of leptin production in 3t3-l1 adipocytes, an effect that was mitigated after ptx. in vitro experiments demonstrated that high levels of pth, but not elevated calcium or phosphorus, reduced akt phosphorylation and leptin production. moreover, successful ptx was shown to improve anemia and malnutrition in patients with severe shpt, which was correlated with increased circulating leptin levels via upregulated akt signaling in adipocytes. these findings suggest that leptin and the pathways associated with its regulation may offer therapeutic potential for improving survival and quality of life in ckd patients.

raw: private tutoring in malaysia is still quite young when compared to other countries in the world. parents often find it quite difficult in finding the right tutors for their children in terms of cost and location wise. our objectives served to create another cost-flexible alternative to finding private tutor that is closer, safer and more secure. the proposed product was introduced as tutor society, a crowdsourcing platform that offers services for private tutoring via crowd-sourcing private tutors available in the localized area. the proposed system was successfully developed albeit being the first version and the business modal was also developed and refined to suit other competitors and malaysian structure. several similar platform was found to exist however; the market is still big and therefore there are still opportunities to be taken. further improvement was required to improve the interface of the system to make it more user friendly and strengthen tutor society branding. more aggressive and proactive marketing are also needed to ensure audiences are aware of our presences.
rewritten: private tutoring in malaysia is relatively new compared to other countries. many parents struggle to find suitable tutors in their neighborhoods that meet both budget and location preferences. our goal was to introduce a cost-effective alternative for accessing local, secure, and trustworthy private tutoring options. this led to the launch of tutor society, a crowdsourcing platform that connects families with private tutors available in nearby areas. despite being the first version, the system was successfully developed, and our business model has since been refined to address the competition and suit malaysia's market structure. although similar platforms exist, the market remains large and offers ample opportunities. to further enhance our service, improvements are needed to create a more user-friendly interface and strengthen the tutor society brand, along with more aggressive and proactive marketing efforts to increase public awareness of our presence.

raw: the utility model discloses a drilling machine for building, which comprises a bod, the lower extreme of organism is equipped with the handle, the front end of organism is equipped with quick fixed adjusting device, quick fixed adjusting device includes fixed loop and swivel becket, the fixed loop is fixed and is cup jointed the front end at the organism, be equipped with the ball in the fixed loop, and the lower extreme of ball is equipped with the elastic component, the swivel becket has been cup jointed in the outside of fixed loop, the inboard of swivel becket is equipped with the annular that matches with the ball, and the outside of swivel becket is equipped with the connecting rod, the bracing piece is installed through the fastener to the connecting rod. the utility model discloses a quick fixed adjusting device's fixed loop and swivel becket cooperation are used, have realized the quick fixed of organism and quick the dismantlement, and the relative position who adjusts the bracing piece simultaneously confirms the utility model discloses an angle is gone into in the cutting, effectually so firmly prevents the skew with organism relatively fixed, has improved the success rate of punching.
rewritten: this utility model discloses a construction drilling machine that includes a body. at the lower end of the body, a handle is provided, while the front end features a quick-fix adjustment device. this device comprises a fixed loop and a swivel becket. the fixed loop is securely attached to the front end of the body and houses a ball, whose lower end is fitted with an elastic component. the swivel becket is hinged to the outer side of the fixed loop; inside the swivel becket is an annular part that matches the ball, and on its outer side, a connecting rod is attached. a bracing piece is then fastened to the connecting rod using a fastener. this design utilizes the cooperative function of the fixed loop and the swivel becket within the quick-fix adjustment device to achieve rapid positioning and disassembly of the body. moreover, the position of the bracing piece can be adjusted simultaneously, allowing the drilling machine to enter at an angle and effectively preventing any misalignment between itself and the body, thereby improving the success rate of punching.

raw: the invention provides a golf ball impact point calculation device and method. the device comprises a map database, a logging module, a processing module, a navigation module and a display module. the method comprises the steps that an electronic map is stored in the map database; the logging module records the acting force of a golf club on a golf ball, duration of the acting force, and the initial direction of the golf ball when the golf ball disengages from the golf ball club; the processing module calculates the movement distance of the golf ball according to the acting force, the duration, the initial direction and the mass of the golf ball, and calculates the impact point of the golf ball on the electronic map; the navigation module positions the golf ball club and generates a finding route with the position of the golf ball club as the starting point and the ball impact point as the final point; the display module displays the ball impact point and the finding route on the electronic map.
rewritten: the invention relates to a device and method for determining the impact point of a golf ball. the device comprises a map database, a logging module, a processing module, a navigation module, and a display module. the method includes the following steps: first, an electronic map is stored in the map database; next, the logging module records parameters such as the force exerted by the golf club on the ball, the duration of this force, and the ball’s initial direction upon separation from the club; then, the processing module calculates the ball's travel distance using the recorded force, duration, initial direction, and the mass of the ball, subsequently determining its impact point on the electronic map; following this, the navigation module locates the golf club’s position and creates a route that starts at the club and ends at the ball's impact point; finally, the display module presents the impact point and the calculated route on the electronic map.

raw: the invention provides an index plate assembly for grinding of a six-cylinder crankshaft rod journal. the index plate assembly comprises a crankshaft headstock flange, wherein a first indexing and positioning hole, a second indexing and positioning hole and a third indexing and positioning hole are uniformly distributed in the circumference of the crankshaft headstock flange; a guide channel is arranged below the crankshaft headstock flange, an end cap is arranged at the front end of the guide channel, a compression spring and a separation pin boss are arranged in the guide channel, a pin head is arranged at the front end of the separation pin boss, the compression spring presses and touches the separation pin boss till the pin head extends outwards to the end cap, and a poking pin is arranged in the vertical direction of the separation pin boss; a positioning bent plate is arranged on the crankshaft headstock flange at one side of the guide channel, a separation handle is horizontally arranged above the guide channel, the top of the separation handle is movably connected to the positioning bent plate by a pivot bolt, and the middle of the separation handle is connected with the poking pin; a strip-shaped hole is formed in the guide channel at the combining part of the poking pin and the separation handle; and the first indexing and positioning hole, the second indexing and positioning hole and the third indexing and positioning hole are provided with a common indexing inserted pin which can realize no-clearance match, and the indexing inserted pin is provided with an indexing handle. the index plate assembly is high in grinding precision and lowers the production cost.
rewritten: the invention discloses an indexing plate assembly designed for the grinding process of a six-cylinder crankshaft's rod journal. this assembly features a crankshaft headstock flange with three equally spaced indexing and positioning holes around its circumference. beneath the headstock flange, a guide channel is provided, ending at its front with an end cap. within this guide channel, a compression spring and a separation pin boss are installed, with a pin head positioned at the front of the separation pin boss. the compression spring forces the separation pin boss so that its pin head protrudes beyond the end cap, while a poking pin is vertically arranged relative to the separation pin boss. additionally, a positioning bent plate is fixed on one side of the guide channel on the crankshaft headstock flange, and a separation handle is mounted horizontally above the guide channel. this handle is pivotably linked to the positioning bent plate via a pivot bolt at its top, with its center connected to the poking pin. a strip-shaped hole is cut into the guide channel at the junction where the poking pin meets the separation handle. moreover, all three indexing and positioning holes share a common indexing inserted pin that ensures a tight, no-clearance fit; this pin is further equipped with an indexing handle. overall, the indexing plate assembly offers high grinding precision while significantly reducing production costs.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide an interconnection system capable of avoiding power purchase or suppressing purchase power/power purchase time by suppressing sudden variation of incoming point power.solution: the interconnection system includes: a pv power generation system 100 and a plurality of nas battery systems 200a-200c which are connected in common to a power incoming point of a grid; and a grid stabilization device 360 which performs charge/discharge control of the nas battery systems. the nas battery systems 200a-200c include automatic control mode machines which are operated in an automatic control mode, and machines which are operated in any one of a charge reset mode, a manual charge more and a manual discharge mode. if an incoming point power prediction total value is equal to or smaller than a charge-time incoming point power setting value, a charge/discharge power command value of the automatic control mode machine is increased in a discharge direction, thereby suppressing the sudden variation of the incoming point power caused by sudden reduction of solar radiation or the like. thus, the power purchase is avoided or the purchase power/power purchase time is suppressed.selected drawing: figure 1
rewritten: challenge addressed: develop an interconnection system that mitigates the need to purchase power or reduces the duration/quantity of power purchases by curtailing abrupt changes in power at the connection point. proposed solution: the system consists of a pv power generation unit (100), several nas battery systems (200a-200c) that are jointly connected to the grid's power input, and a grid stabilization device (360) that manages the charge/discharge operations of the nas battery systems. the nas battery systems (200a-200c) are equipped with both an automatic control mode and other modes including charge reset, manual charge increase, and manual discharge. when the expected total power at the input remains at or below a predefined charge-time threshold, the system increases the discharge command from the automatic mode machine. this adjustment helps counter sudden drops in incoming power—caused, for example, by abrupt decreases in solar radiation—thereby minimizing the need to purchase additional power and reducing the duration or quantity of power procurement. reference diagram: figure 1

raw: carbon fiber laminate composites are increasingly employed for aerospace structures as they offer advantages, such as a good strength to weight ratio. however, impact during the operation and servicing of the aircraft can lead to barely visible and difficult to detect damage. depending on the severity of the impact, fiber and matrix breakage or delaminations can occur, reducing the load carrying capacity of the structure. efficient nondestructive testing and structural health monitoring of composite panels can be achieved using guided ultrasonic waves propagating along the structure. the scattering of the a0 lamb wave mode at delaminations was investigated using a full three-dimensional (3d) finite element (fe) analysis. the influence of the delamination geometry (size and depth) was systematically evaluated. in addition to the depth dependency, a significant influence of the delamination width due to sideways reflection of the guided waves within the delamination area was found. mixed-mode defects were simulated using a combined model of delamination with localized material degradation. the guided wave scattering at cross-ply composite plates with impact damage was measured experimentally using a non-contact laser interferometer. good agreement between experiments and fe predictions using the mixed-mode model for an approximation of the impact damage was found.
rewritten: carbon fiber laminate composites are being increasingly used in aerospace structures because they provide a favorable strength-to-weight ratio. however, impacts during aircraft operation and maintenance can cause damage that is barely visible and hard to detect. depending on the impact’s severity, fiber and matrix breakage or delaminations may occur, thereby reducing the structure's capacity to carry loads. efficient non-destructive testing and structural health monitoring of composite panels can be accomplished by using guided ultrasonic waves that travel along the structure. in this study, the scattering of the a0 lamb wave mode at delaminations was analyzed through a full three-dimensional (3d) finite element (fe) approach. the analysis systematically explored how the size and depth of the delamination affect the wave scattering. not only did the depth play a role, but the width of the delamination was also found to have a significant impact due to the lateral reflection of the guided waves within the delaminated area. mixed-mode defects were simulated using a combined model that incorporated both delamination and localized material degradation. furthermore, guided wave scattering in cross-ply composite plates with impact damage was experimentally measured using a non-contact laser interferometer, and the results showed good agreement with the fe predictions based on the mixed-mode model for approximating the damage from impact.

raw: the invention discloses a method and apparatus for making a call via a bluetooth car phone, and belongs to the technical field of automotive electronics. the method comprises the steps of receiving a communication event; detecting whether an object is on a first seat in a vehicle, the first seat being the co-pilot seat or a back seat in the vehicle; if an object is on the first seat, detecting whether the object is a person; if an object is on the first seat and the object is a person, making a call with an opposite terminal of the communication event through a driver's phone in the vehicle; if no object is on the first seat or if an object is on the first seat but the object is not a person, making a call with the opposite terminal of the communication event through a car speaker in the vehicle. the apparatus comprises a receiving module, a first detection module, a second detection module, a first call module and a second call module. according to the invention, the content of the call will not be leaked, and no security risk will exist.
rewritten: the invention relates to a method and apparatus for making calls via a bluetooth car phone, belonging to the field of automotive electronics. the method includes the following steps: 1. receiving a communication event. 2. detecting whether an object is present on a designated seat inside the vehicle, where the designated seat could be either the co-pilot seat or a back seat. 3. if an object is detected on the designated seat, determining whether the object is a person. 4. if an object is detected on the designated seat and identified as a person, placing the call to the communication event’s opposite terminal using the driver's phone installed in the vehicle. 5. if no object is detected on the designated seat, or if the object is not a person, placing the call to the communication event’s opposite terminal through the vehicle's car speaker. the apparatus consists of a receiving module, a first detection module, a second detection module, a first call module, and a second call module. according to the invention, this approach ensures that the contents of the call remain secure and free from any security risks.

raw: the utility model discloses a heat recovery power generation system of steerable temperature and flow mainly contains the thermoelectric conversion equipment that is become by evaporimeter, condenser, group pu, turbine, generating set, the aqua storage tank of connecting thermoelectric conversion equipment, connects a heating device and the 2nd heating device of aqua storage tank. wherein, a heating device, the 2nd heating device and aqua storage tank are equipped with the flow respectively and take into account the temperature and listen the piece, and the 2nd heating device is equipped with the heater. a heating device, the 2nd heating device are used for for the liquid of the suitable temperature of aqua storage tank supply, and the aqua storage tank is used for the liquid for thermoelectric conversion supply of equipment constant temperature and constant voltage, and partial liquid is retrieved by the aqua storage tank again to make thermoelectric conversion equipment reach stable power generator effect, and then improve the spoilage of electricity generation efficiency and reduction equipment.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a heat recovery power generation system with adjustable temperature and flow. this system chiefly comprises thermoelectric conversion equipment—which includes an evaporator, a condenser, a group pu, a turbine, a generating set, and an aqua storage tank that connects to the thermoelectric conversion equipment—and it is linked to both a primary heating device and a secondary heating device attached to the aqua storage tank. in this design, the primary heating device, the secondary heating device, and the aqua storage tank each incorporate their own fluid control mechanisms that monitor temperature and other key parameters, with the secondary heating device also featuring an additional heater. the primary and secondary heating units serve to provide the aqua storage tank with liquid at an optimal temperature, while the tank itself supplies the thermoelectric conversion equipment with liquid at a constant temperature and voltage. moreover, part of the liquid is recirculated from the aqua storage tank to ensure that the thermoelectric conversion equipment achieves stable power generation, thereby enhancing overall power generation efficiency and reducing equipment degradation.

raw: this study investigates what the key factors are on word segmentation on l2(english) learning: phonetic or lexical? previous researches on the second language learners' abilities to segment l2 speech in terms of phonetic cues focused on the presence or absence of allophonic cues. for example, presence or absence of aspiration or glottalization determines the location of word boundaries. not taking into consideration other potential cues such as lexical information or word frequency, it is not clear which cues of linguistic subparts play a main role in word segmentation on english learners. to control potential lexical influences, sequences of non-words are examined with strong phonetic cues across word boundaries. if listeners rely on phonetic cues in segmenting words, non-word sequences should be perceived as real word sequences are perceived. we compare the perception rate of non-words with that of real words. it is hypothesized that different perception rates may be resulted from cues other than phonetic ones. 40 korean college learners of english participated in an english perception task for the strong phonetic cue of aspiration in both real and non-words. the results show that learners' perception was better on real words than on non-words, suggesting lexical information is contributing to english word segmentation on l2 learning.
rewritten: this study aims to determine the primary influences on word segmentation in l2 (english) learning—are phonetic or lexical factors more critical? previous research on second language learners' abilities to segment l2 speech has focused on phonetic cues, specifically the presence or absence of allophonic features such as aspiration or glottalization, which can mark word boundaries. however, by not considering other potential cues, like lexical information or word frequency, it remains unclear which linguistic components play a more dominant role in word segmentation for english learners. to isolate the effect of phonetic cues from lexical influences, the study examines sequences of non-words that include strong phonetic cues at their boundaries. the rationale is that if listeners rely solely on phonetic cues, they should perceive non-word sequences similarly to real word sequences. therefore, the study compares the perception rates of non-words with those of real words, hypothesizing that any differences in perception may result from cues beyond the phonetic ones. forty korean college learners of english participated in a perception task focusing on the strong phonetic cue of aspiration, which was embedded in both real words and non-words. the results indicated that the learners had better perception accuracy with real words than with non-words, suggesting that lexical information indeed plays a role in english word segmentation for l2 learners.

raw: as an important ecological factor, the illumination plays an important role in the quality of the flue cured tobacco seeding; in the experiment, led light source is used to conduct the short- distance irradiation and conducted the irradiation on the tobacco seeding point by point with different wavelengths at different positions and spaces, for the flue-cured tobacco seedling with intensive cultivation at low position, it is needed to fill the light, and to adjust the light environment for the growth of flue-cured tobacco seedlings in the process of greenhouse seedling. the research on the photosynthetic productivity, photosynthetic characteristics of tobacco seedlings and the measures to fill light in different light environments has an impact on the quality of the flue-cured tobacco floating seedling sprout and the growth of tobacco seedling.
rewritten: illumination, as a key ecological factor, significantly influences the quality of flue-cured tobacco seedlings. in this experiment, an led light source was employed to deliver short-range irradiation on the tobacco seedlings, targeting them point by point with various wavelengths at different positions and distances. for flue-cured tobacco seedlings cultivated intensively at lower positions, it is essential to supplement the available light and adjust the lighting environment during greenhouse seedling production. studying the photosynthetic productivity and characteristics of the tobacco seedlings, along with the use of supplemental lighting under diverse light conditions, affects the quality of the flue-cured tobacco floating seedling sprouts and the overall growth of the tobacco plants.

raw: in recent decades, the simultaneous design of a product and its supply chain has become a trend in the manufacturing industry. this approach, requiring massive data sharing that allows different designers to collaborate in order to choose the best product design along with its corresponding supply chain, taking into account cost, quality, and time. to get a successful collaboration between designers, a model of multi agent system is presented. the model is based on a framework containing two different levels: product design level and supply chain design level. this architecture aims to have a better cooperation between designers. since the presented agents are autonomous and have the power to decide about product design choices and supply chain configuration in a simultaneous way, we present an example based on a multi-objective approach to select from several product redesigns and among several suppliers, the best redesign accompanied by the best suppliers that optimize supply, transportation, storage and quality of components. a case study is presented in the end to have a better insight of the proposed model.
rewritten: over the past few decades, designing a product alongside its supply chain has become increasingly popular in the manufacturing industry. this integrated approach involves extensive data sharing, enabling various designers to work together to select the optimal product design and its corresponding supply chain by considering factors such as cost, quality, and time. to facilitate effective collaboration among designers, a multi-agent system model is introduced. this model is structured around a framework with two distinct levels: one for product design and another for supply chain design. the goal of this structure is to enhance cooperation between the designers. because the agents in the system are autonomous and capable of making concurrent decisions regarding product design and supply chain configuration, an example using a multi-objective approach is provided. this example demonstrates how to choose, from multiple product redesign options and suppliers, the best redesign paired with the optimal suppliers to improve supply, transportation, storage, and component quality. a case study is presented at the end to offer further insight into the proposed model.

raw: introduction: eclampsia is a preventable and treatable cause of maternal morbidity and mortality with poor feto-maternal outcome in developing countries. despite development in the level of health education expertise in human resources and institutional obstetric care in our country, the delay in early recognition of the problem, transportation to proper health facility and getting proper expert care are major hurdles to reduce complications. therefore we decided to study feto-maternal outcomes in our setting.methods: a retrospective cross-sectional hospital based study carried out in nobel medical college, biratnagar, from 17th june 2014 to 16th june 2015. details and data obtained from medical record section were analysed. all patients with eclampsia were included and fetomaternal outcomes measured in terms of complications. simple descriptive statistical method was applied for analysis.results: among 8,066 deliveries, 112 had eclampsia with incidence of 13.8/1000 deliveries. majority (41%) were of <19 years of age. above 90% were unbooked. aoubt 63.4% were primiparas and 83% had antepartum eclampsia. eclamptic fits were more common (41.6%) in 37-40 weeks period of gestation. fits to delivery interval was more than six hours in 89.1% women and 69.3% women underwent caesarean delivery. about 18.9% women developed eclampsia related complications. common causes of maternal deaths (5.36%) were pulmonary edema, aspiration pneumonia, cerebrovascular accidents and hellp syndrome. perinatal death was nine percent.conclusions: although the obstetric care facilities are improving with time, the feto-maternal outcomes are still poor in our country. therefore early recognition and proper management are vital to tackle this challenge. keywords: eclampsia; fetomaternal outcomes; retrospective analysis. | pubmed
rewritten: introduction: eclampsia remains a preventable and treatable condition that significantly contributes to maternal morbidity and mortality, leading to unfavorable feto-maternal outcomes in developing nations. despite improvements in health education, human resource expertise, and institutional obstetric services in our country, delays in early detection, transportation to appropriate medical facilities, and accessing expert care continue to be major challenges in reducing complications. this prompted us to examine the feto-maternal outcomes in our own setting. methods: we conducted a retrospective, cross-sectional, hospital-based study at nobel medical college in biratnagar, covering the period from 17 june 2014 to 16 june 2015. data were collected from the medical records section, including all patients diagnosed with eclampsia, and outcomes were assessed based on the complications observed. a simple descriptive statistical method was used for data analysis. results: out of 8,066 deliveries, 112 cases involved eclampsia, resulting in an incidence rate of 13.8 per 1000 deliveries. a significant portion (41%) of these patients were under 19 years old, and more than 90% had not received prior antenatal care. approximately 63.4% were first-time mothers, and 83% experienced antepartum eclampsia. eclamptic seizures were most common during the 37-40 week gestational period, accounting for 41.6% of cases. in 89.1% of the women, the interval between the seizure and delivery exceeded six hours, and 69.3% underwent caesarean section. nearly 18.9% of the women developed complications related to eclampsia. maternal deaths (5.36%) were primarily due to pulmonary edema, aspiration pneumonia, cerebrovascular accidents, and hellp syndrome, while the perinatal mortality rate was 9%. conclusions: even though obstetric care facilities are gradually improving in our country, the feto-maternal outcomes remain unsatisfactory. early detection and proper management are crucial to overcoming this challenge. keywords: eclampsia; feto-maternal outcomes; retrospective analysis.

rewritten: this guide is created to offer both undergraduate and graduate students prompt access to ust libraries' primary accounting e-resources, including the fasb codification.

raw: the invention relates to a multi-channel reservoir physical model of heavy oil thermal production well to be constructed. according to the model, a filling channel group which covers three penetration rate ranges of high, middle and low levels and is filled with oil-water with different degrees is prepared by stratum sand, crude oil and stratum water, or simulation stratum sand, simulation crude oil and simulation stratum water. by use of the model, in site channel sealing and water plugging by plugging agent injecting, solidification and plugging effects in a construction well of heavy oil thermal production are accurately simulated. thus, simulation test of channel sealing and water plugging in heavy oil thermal production well is carried out, plugging agent property is regulated and optimized, and construction control data of in site channel sealing and water plugging in heavy oil well is tested and acquired to provide guidance for compiling construction designs of channel sealing and water plugging, to increase success rate of channel sealing and water plugging construction and to increase production. by applying the physical model and corresponding test method in guiding selective channel sealing and water plugging in site of heavy oil thermal production well, great economic benefit and social benefit will be brought about and the application aspect is quite broad.
rewritten: this invention pertains to a physical model with multiple channels that represents the heavy oil thermal production well reservoir to be constructed. in the model, a group of filling channels is created covering three penetration rate ranges—high, medium, and low—each filled with oil-water mixtures of varying degrees using formation sand, crude oil, and formation water, or their simulated equivalents. using this model, the in-situ channel sealing and water plugging of heavy oil thermal production wells are accurately simulated by injecting plugging agents to achieve solidification and plug formation. as a result, simulation tests for channel sealing and water plugging in heavy oil thermal production wells are conducted, which enable the properties of the plugging agent to be adjusted and optimized. this testing provides the construction control data required for in-situ channel sealing and water plugging in heavy oil wells, offering guidance for developing construction designs, improving the success rate of these operations, and boosting production. by employing this physical model and its corresponding test method for guiding selective channel sealing and water plugging in heavy oil thermal production wells, significant economic and social benefits can be achieved, and the potential applications are extensive.

raw: autoconfiguration in mobile ad-hoc networks is a challenging mechanism in a potentially hostile environment. autoconfiguration schemes are prone to various types of security threats that interfere or even paralyze the mechanism. in this paper an autoconfiguration scheme that overcomes the security threats has been proposed. the protocol uses a grid based hierarchical architecture to resolve the address duplication detection. specialized agent nodes with high confidence score are dynamically selected for the duplication detection process. the selected agents use digital signatures for authentication and also a hashing scheme to check the tampered messages during communication. the proposed protocol has been simulated and compared with three other schemes. the results show that even with added security features the protocol has fairly good protocol overhead, address acquisition delay and packet losses when compared to the similar schemes. the analyses also conclude that protocol provides high security in unsafe environments.
rewritten: in mobile ad-hoc networks, autoconfiguration is a difficult task, particularly in potentially hostile settings. such schemes are vulnerable to various security threats that can either disrupt or completely incapacitate the process. this paper introduces an autoconfiguration strategy designed to address these security issues effectively. the protocol utilizes a grid-based hierarchical structure to manage the detection of duplicate addresses. it dynamically selects specialized agent nodes with high confidence scores to carry out the duplication detection process. these agents authenticate messages using digital signatures and employ a hashing method to verify that messages have not been tampered with during transmission. simulations of the proposed protocol, compared against three other schemes, indicate that—even with its additional security measures—the protocol maintains competitive protocol overhead, address acquisition delays, and packet loss rates. the analysis also confirms that the protocol delivers robust security even in unsafe environments.

raw: the utility model discloses a blood sugar detects lock, including a lock body and be located the lock core subassembly of lock body, set up on one side of this lock body flutedly, be provided with blood glucose meter in this recess. the utility model discloses blood sugar detects lock combines together the domestic blood glucose meter of tradition with the lock body of lock, utilize the showy and essential characteristic of opening the door in lock body position, deposit blood glucose meter and remind the user to detect blood sugar, thereby avoid the user to forget use blood glucose meter or can't find domestic blood glucose meter and just give up the blood sugar tests scheduling problem, more importantly, can take notes the historical data that the patient detected blood sugar at every turn, and can, the detection indicate patient blood sugar level normal or on the high side on the low side when finishing, help better grasp self situation of patient is so that make prevention and treatment.
rewritten: this utility model discloses a blood sugar detection lock that comprises a lock body with an integrated lock core subassembly. one side of the lock body features a recessed area where a blood glucose meter is installed. the design combines a traditional domestic blood glucose meter with a lock body by employing the characteristic of the lock's door-opening mechanism. when the door is opened, the blood glucose meter is revealed, reminding the user to perform a blood sugar test. this ensures that the user doesn't forget to use it or misplace the meter, thus avoiding missed tests. furthermore, the device records historical data from each blood sugar test and indicates whether the patient's blood sugar level is normal, high, or low, thereby helping the patient better understand their condition for effective prevention and treatment.

raw: cancer cachexia remains a leading cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide, despite extensive research and clinical trials. the prominent clinical feature of cancer cachexia is the continuous loss of skeletal muscle that cannot be fully reversed by conventional nutritional support, and that leads to progressive functional impairment. the mechanism underlying muscle loss in patients with cachexia is poorly understood. the present study analyzed 21 cancer patients with or without cachexia, and demonstrated that mitofusin-2 (mfn2) was downregulated in the rectus abdominis of patients with cachexia, which was associated with body weight loss. in vitro cell experiments indicated that loss of mfn2 was associated with atrophy of the c2c12 mouse myoblast cell line. furthermore, in vivo animal experiments demonstrated that cachexia decreased gastrocnemius muscle mass and mfn2 expression, and overexpression of mfn2 in gastrocnemius muscle was able to partially attenuate cachexia-induced gastrocnemius muscle loss. the results of the present study suggested that mfn2 is involved in cachexia-induced muscle loss and may serve as a potential target for therapy of cachexia.
rewritten: cancer cachexia remains a major contributor to illness and death worldwide, despite significant research efforts and numerous clinical trials. one of its key clinical signs is the persistent loss of skeletal muscle, which cannot be entirely reversed through standard nutritional support and eventually leads to a decline in physical function. the underlying mechanisms of muscle loss in cachexia patients are not well understood. in this study, 21 cancer patients, both with and without cachexia, were analyzed. the findings revealed that in cachexia patients, mitofusin-2 (mfn2) levels in the rectus abdominis were reduced, correlating with weight loss. in vitro experiments using the c2c12 mouse myoblast cell line further indicated that reduced mfn2 levels were linked to cell atrophy. additionally, in vivo animal studies showed that cachexia led to a decrease in both gastrocnemius muscle mass and mfn2 expression, while overexpressing mfn2 in the gastrocnemius partially mitigated the muscle loss caused by cachexia. these results suggest that mfn2 plays a role in cachexia-induced muscle wasting and could serve as a potential therapeutic target for this condition.

raw: in this paper, we propose a measurement-based approach to the real-time security-constrained economic dispatch (sced). the real-time sced is a widely used market scheduling tool that seeks to economically balance electricity supply and demand and provide locational marginal prices (lmps), while ensuring system reliability standards are met. to capture network flows and security considerations, the conventional sced formulation relies on sensitivities that are typically computed from a linearized power flow model, which is vulnerable to phenomena such as undetected topology changes, changes in the system operating point, and the existence of incorrect model data. our approach to the formulation of the sced problem utilizes power system sensitivities estimated from phasor measurement unit (pmu) measurements. the resulting measurement-based real-time sced is robust against the aforementioned phenomena. moreover, the dispatch instructions and lmps calculated with the proposed measurement-based sced accurately reflect real-time system conditions and security needs. we illustrate the strengths of the proposed approach via several case studies.
rewritten: in this paper, we introduce a measurement-based technique for real-time security-constrained economic dispatch (sced). real-time sced is a market scheduling tool that economically balances electricity supply and demand, produces locational marginal prices (lmps), and ensures that system reliability standards are upheld. typically, the conventional sced formulation addresses network flows and security by using sensitivities derived from a linearized power flow model—a method that can be compromised by issues such as undetected topology changes, shifts in the system operating point, and inaccuracies in the model data. in contrast, our method estimates power system sensitivities directly from phasor measurement unit (pmu) data, rendering the resulting real-time sced robust against these challenges. moreover, the dispatch instructions and lmps produced by our measurement-based approach accurately mirror the system’s real-time conditions and security requirements. we demonstrate the advantages of our method through several case studies.

raw: the utility model discloses a formula of taking a breath air conditioner, including fan, evaporimeter, empty gas shift device, air detector, air conditioner casing, program controller, compressor, condenser, return air portion and air supply portion, empty gas shift unit bit is among the air conditioner casing, and the inside of empty gas shift device is equipped with the ventilation governor motor, among the air detector was located sky gas shift device, the lateral part of empty gas shift device was equipped with the evaporimeter, and the lateral part of evaporimeter is equipped with the fan, and the lateral part of fan is equipped with program controller, and among the compressor was located the air conditioner casing, the compressor lateral part was equipped with the condenser. this kind of formula of taking a breath air conditioner can be applied to two kind indoor and outdoor occasions, and air inlet portion and air -out portion can change according to the installation occasion, the utility model discloses an integral mounting means, simple structure is convenient, also can normally install and use in some the glass curtain wall buildings that can not use the off -premises station. do not occupy the interior space, noiselessness when outdoor installation.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a design for a breathing air conditioner comprising a fan, an evaporator, an empty gas shift device, an air sensor, an air conditioner casing, a program controller, a compressor, a condenser, a return air section, and an air supply section. the empty gas shift unit is housed within the air conditioner casing and contains a ventilation governor motor. the air sensor includes a sky gas shift component, the evaporator is mounted on the side of the empty gas shift device, and the fan is installed on the lateral side of the evaporator. the program controller is positioned on the side of the fan, while the compressor is placed within the air conditioner casing with its lateral side equipped with the condenser. this breathing air conditioner design can be applied in both indoor and outdoor settings, with the air inlet and outlet sections adjustable according to the installation requirements. the utility model also presents an integrated mounting method that is simple, convenient, and suitable for installation in glass curtain wall buildings where outdoor units cannot be used. additionally, it does not take up interior space and operates quietly when installed outdoors.

raw: a discount group buying with store in school system and a method thereof are provided. commodities with discount are ordered by an ordering application which is installed in a handheld device. then, order forms are integrated by an assignation server and provided to a store client. commodities are ordered according to the order forms are integrated through the store client. therefore, the efficiency of buying commodities with large quantity or group buying conveniently nay be achieved.
rewritten: a discount group buying system with an in-school store and its method are provided. in this system, an ordering application installed on a handheld device is used to order discounted commodities. the order forms generated by the application are then consolidated by an assignation server and forwarded to a store client, which processes the orders based on the integrated forms. as a result, the system facilitates efficient bulk or group buying of commodities.

raw: abstract in general, the pipelines within a nuclear power plant facility may experience high temperatures up to several hundred degrees. thus it is absolutely vital to monitor these pipes to prevent leakage of radioactive substances which may lead to a catastrophic outcome of the surrounding environment. over the years, one of the structural health monitoring technique known as the electromechanical impedance (emi) technique has been of great interests in various fields including civil infrastructures, mechanical and aerospace structures. although it has one of the best advantages to be able for a single piezoelectric transducer to act as a sensor and an actuator, simultaneously, its low curie temperature makes it difficult for the emi technique to be conducted at high temperature environment. to overcome this problem, this study shows a method to avoid attaching the piezoelectric transducer directly onto the target structure using a metal wire for damage detection at high temperature. by shifting the frequency to compensate the signature changes subjected to the variations in temperature, the experimental results indicate that damage identification is more successful above 200 o c, making the metal wire method suitable for the emi technique at high temperature environment.
rewritten: in nuclear power plant facilities, pipelines can reach temperatures of several hundred degrees. thus, monitoring these pipes is critical to prevent radioactive leaks that could have catastrophic environmental consequences. over time, the electromechanical impedance (emi) technique—a structural health monitoring method—has garnered significant interest in fields such as civil, mechanical, and aerospace engineering. although one notable advantage of the emi method is that a single piezoelectric transducer can serve as both sensor and actuator, its low curie temperature poses challenges when used in high-temperature environments. to overcome this, the current study presents an approach that avoids directly attaching the piezoelectric transducer to the target structure. instead, a metal wire is used for damage detection at elevated temperatures. by shifting the frequency to compensate for signature changes due to temperature variations, the experimental results demonstrate that damage identification is more effective above 200°c, making the metal wire method a suitable adaptation of the emi technique for high-temperature settings.

raw: natural killer (nk) cells are important effector cells in hepatitis c virus (hcv) infection, a virus that chronically infects around 2.5% of the world population and is a major cause of liver disease and hepatocellular carcinoma. the exact mechanisms, however, through which nk cells are activated in response to hcv remain elusive. using the well-established hcv replicon cell-culture model we show that after co- culture of hcv replicon-carrying hepatocytes with peripheral blood mononuclear cells (pbmcs), nk cells increase expression of the high-affinity il-2 receptor chain cd25, proliferate rapidly and produce ifn-gamma. activation of nk cells was dependent on il-2, most likely produced by t cells and on cell-cell contact mediated signals from monocytes. monocytes from replicon-carrying co-cultures showed increased expression of ox40l, a member of the tumor necrosis factor family and concurrently its receptor ox40 was increased on nk cells. blocking of ox40l in those co-cultures, as well as depletion of cd14+ monocytes abrogated the virus-induced activation and effector functions of nk cells. together, our data reveals a novel mechanism of monocyte mediated nk cell activation against virus-infected cells involving the ox40/ox40l axis with potential relevance for therapeutic intervention by e.g. agonistic antibodies against ox40, which are already tested in cancer therapy.
rewritten: natural killer (nk) cells serve as crucial effector cells during hepatitis c virus (hcv) infection—a virus that persistently infects roughly 2.5% of the world’s population and is a leading cause of liver disease and hepatocellular carcinoma. however, the specific mechanisms by which nk cells are activated in response to hcv remain unclear. using a well-established hcv replicon cell-culture model, we found that when hepatocytes carrying the hcv replicon were co-cultured with peripheral blood mononuclear cells (pbmcs), nk cells significantly increased the expression of the high-affinity il-2 receptor chain cd25, underwent rapid proliferation, and produced ifn-gamma. this activation was dependent on il-2, likely supplied by t cells, as well as on cell-to-cell interactions involving signals from monocytes. notably, monocytes from the replicon-carrying co-cultures showed enhanced expression of ox40l—a member of the tumor necrosis factor family—while concurrently, the ox40 receptor was upregulated on nk cells. blocking ox40l in these co-cultures or depleting cd14+ monocytes completely inhibited the virus-induced activation and effector functions of the nk cells. overall, our findings reveal a novel monocyte-mediated mechanism of nk cell activation against virus-infected cells involving the ox40/ox40l axis, which could be therapeutically targeted, for example, with agonistic antibodies against ox40 that are already being tested in cancer therapy.

raw: the invention relates to a genetic nramp1 diagnosis kit which is closely related to j-subtype avian leukosis resistance, and a use method of the genetic nramp1 diagnosis kit, and belongs to the technical field of molecular biology. the kit comprises an upper/lower stream primer mixture of nramp1 gene pcr amplification, restrictive incision enzyme tscai and rtaq enzyme. the use method comprises the following steps: detecting the gene type of an nramp1 gene and a segment of a pcr amplification locus by using an rflp method, and performing enzyme digestion reaction on the incision enzyme tscai. nramp1 exon c125t mutation has influence on the resistance of j-subtype avian leukosis, an individual carrying t-allele belongs to a j-subtype avian leukosis resistant group, and particularly a cock carrying the t-allele has relatively high resistance on j-subtype avian leukosis.
rewritten: this invention pertains to a genetic nramp1 diagnostic kit that is closely linked to resistance against j-subtype avian leukosis, as well as to a method for using this kit. it falls within the field of molecular biology. the kit includes a combination of upper and lower primers for pcr amplification of the nramp1 gene, the tscai restriction enzyme, and the rtaq enzyme. the usage method involves the following steps: first, using an rflp technique to detect the genotype of the nramp1 gene and a segment of its pcr amplification region, then carrying out an enzymatic digestion with the tscai enzyme. the c125t mutation in the nramp1 exon affects resistance to j-subtype avian leukosis; individuals carrying the t allele are classified as resistant to this subtype, with roosters carrying the t allele showing notably higher resistance.

raw: the invention provides a method for no-perception authentication free internet access of a fat wifi ap and a thin wifi ap, comprising steps: 1, building a connection between an app and the fat wifi ap; 2, sending a webpage access request to the fat wifi ap by the app, judging whether the app is authenticated, if so, ending after accessing a webpage; if not, redirecting the app to a portal page, and after redirecting successfully, acquiring an authentication parameter from the portal page by the app; 3, initiating an authentication request to a wifi portal, performing authentication according to the authentication parameter, and returning back a gateway token; 4, sending a gateway opening request and the gateway token to the fat wifi ap, and after receiving the gateway opening request, verifying the gateway token by the fat wifi ap. the method provided by the invention uses a new authentication mode of a client, and can simultaneously support two types of ap devices, and thus, selection range of devices is expanded.
rewritten: the invention discloses a method for providing internet access without perceptible authentication for both a fat wifi access point (ap) and a thin wifi ap. the method comprises the following steps: 1. establishing a connection between an application and the fat wifi ap. 2. the application sends a webpage access request to the fat wifi ap. the system then checks if the application is authenticated. if it is authenticated, webpage access is granted and the process concludes; if not, the application is redirected to a portal page. once the redirection is successful, the application obtains an authentication parameter from the portal page. 3. the application initiates an authentication request to a wifi portal, where authentication is carried out based on the previously obtained parameter, resulting in a gateway token being issued. 4. the application sends a gateway-opening request together with the gateway token to the fat wifi ap. upon receiving the request, the fat wifi ap verifies the gateway token. this method employs a novel client authentication mode that supports both types of ap devices simultaneously, thereby expanding the range of selectable devices.

raw: the invention discloses an environment-friendly adhesive for wickerwork. the adhesive is prepared from the following raw materials in parts by weight: 20-25 parts of polyvinyl alcohol, 2-3 parts of shellac, 5-8 parts of gum arabic, 6-8 parts of triethanolamine, 3-4 parts of modified soybean protein, 1-2 parts of rosin, 2-4 parts of dimethylol urea and 3-4 parts of ricinoleic acid. compared with the existing products, the adhesive disclosed by the invention belongs to a water-soluble adhesive, has the advantages of simple required raw materials, no formaldehyde, high safety, environment friendliness, high storage stability and quick strength establishment, and can be widely used in wickerwork industry. the initial adhesive strength can reach 1.1-1.8 mpa, and the adhesion strength can reach 0.35-0.8 mpa.
rewritten: the invention presents an eco-friendly adhesive designed for wickerwork applications. it is formulated from raw materials combined in parts by weight as follows: 20–25 parts polyvinyl alcohol, 2–3 parts shellac, 5–8 parts gum arabic, 6–8 parts triethanolamine, 3–4 parts modified soybean protein, 1–2 parts rosin, 2–4 parts dimethylol urea, and 3–4 parts ricinoleic acid. unlike existing products, this water-soluble adhesive utilizes simple raw materials, is completely free of formaldehyde, boasts high safety and environmental benefits, demonstrates excellent storage stability, and develops strength quickly. its initial adhesive strength can reach between 1.1 and 1.8 mpa, while the final adhesion strength ranges from 0.35 to 0.8 mpa.

raw: microrna (mirna) analysis in a single cell is extremely important because it allows deep understanding of the exact correlation between the mirnas and cell functions. herein, we wish to report a highly sensitive and precisely quantitative assay for mirna detection based on ligation-based droplet digital polymerase chain reaction (ddpcr), which permits the quantitation of mirna in a single cell. in this ligation-based ddpcr assay, two target-specific oligonucleotide probes can be simply designed to be complementary to the half-sequence of the target mirna, respectively, which avoids the sophisticated design of reverse transcription and provides high specificity to discriminate a single-base difference among mirnas with simple operations. after the mirna-templated ligation, the ddpcr partitions individual ligated products into a water-in-oil droplet and digitally counts the fluorescence-positive and negative droplets after pcr amplification for quantification of the target molecules, which possesses the pow...
rewritten: analyzing microrna (mirna) in a single cell is crucial because it provides deep insight into the precise relationship between mirnas and cell functions. in this report, we introduce a highly sensitive and quantitatively precise assay for detecting mirna, based on a ligation-based droplet digital polymerase chain reaction (ddpcr) method that enables mirna quantification at the single-cell level. in this assay, two target-specific oligonucleotide probes are designed to each complement half of the target mirna sequence. this approach eliminates the need for the complex design required for reverse transcription and ensures high specificity, even discerning single-base differences among mirnas with straightforward operations. after ligation guided by the mirna template, the ddpcr method partitions the individual ligated products into water-in-oil droplets and then digitally counts the fluorescent-positive and negative droplets following pcr amplification, providing a powerful means to quantify the target molecules.

raw: monitoring output variation in, for example, a nuclear power plant so as to allow performance of maintenance, replacement etc. of individual instruments in consideration of cumulative fatigue due to variation of generator output with respect to the respective instrument structures. when a change of generator output in a nuclear power plant is requested, a cumulative fatigue factor due to output variation is calculated with respect to, for example, a reactor pressure vessel (1, fig. 1) and a turbine (3) as respective component instruments of the nuclear power plant by a cumulative fatigue factor calculation unit (22, fig. 3), and whether the calculated cumulative fatigue factor exceeds a set value or not is determined by a fatigue accumulation state determination unit (23). when the calculated cumulative fatigue factor exceeds the set value, a fatigue accumulation alarm generation unit (19) informs the possibility of damaging an instrument structure with an alarm.
