rewritten: this study involves the design and optimization of a honeycomb energy absorber utilizing a genetic algorithm. the primary goal is to absorb nearly all of the impact energy, while also minimizing the shock force. the first section focuses on a parametric analysis of the crashworthiness behavior of the honeycomb structure. the findings from this analysis are then applied in the second section to optimize the shock absorber design. in this phase, the dynamic response of an aluminum honeycomb structure is simulated using the ls-dyna finite element code. parametric studies are conducted to determine how various model parameters affect the crashworthiness characteristics of the honeycomb structure. to lower computational costs, a repeatable model of a “y” cross-section column is simulated numerically. the study explores how variations in material properties such as young’s modulus, yield stress, and tangent modulus, as well as geometrical properties like cell size and foil thickness, along with impact velocity, influence the deformation behavior of the structure. a total of 25 different geometries with a constant height but varying cell sizes and thicknesses are examined, focusing on how thickness and cell size affect energy absorption properties. the results indicate that crashworthiness parameters, including mean and peak stress, are primarily influenced by cell size and thickness, whereas the friction coefficient and young’s modulus play a lesser role. additionally, any change in the absorber’s geometry has a more pronounced effect on the mean collapse stress compared to the peak stress, and changes in thickness are more impactful than alterations in cell size.

raw: the invention discloses an energy-saving window made of a composite material. the energy-saving window made of the composite material comprises aerogel glass components, a window frame and window sash frames, wherein each aerogel glass component comprises a plurality of flat glass layers and aerogel layers, each window sash frame is a square frame formed by connecting a plurality of first composite section rods, and the window frame is a square frame formed by connecting a plurality of second composite section rods. each first composite section rod comprises a first outer aluminum alloy section plate, a first inner aluminum alloy section plate and a first straw fiber resin composite rod, and each second composite section rod comprises a second outer aluminum alloy section plate, a second inner aluminum alloy section plate and a second straw fiber resin composite rod. the energy-saving window made of the composite material has the advantages that heat-insulating and sound-insulating properties are improved, a heat transfer coefficient is decreased, waterproofing and air tightness are guaranteed through the design of sealing strips, and integral heat-insulating and energy-saving effects are improved.
rewritten: this invention introduces an energy-efficient window constructed from a composite material. the window comprises aerogel glass components, a window frame, and window sash frames. each aerogel glass component includes several layers of flat glass and aerogel layers. the window sash frames are designed as square frames made by connecting a series of first composite section rods, while the window frame is also a square frame, assembled from several second composite section rods. each first composite section rod consists of a first outer aluminum alloy section plate, a first inner aluminum alloy section plate, and a first straw fiber resin composite rod. similarly, each second composite section rod comprises a second outer aluminum alloy section plate, a second inner aluminum alloy section plate, and a second straw fiber resin composite rod. the energy-efficient window boasts enhanced thermal and acoustic insulation, a reduced heat transfer coefficient, and improved waterproofing and airtightness through the use of sealing strips, ultimately resulting in better overall insulation and energy-saving performance.

raw: this paper presents a rotary power scavenging unit comprised of two systems of flexible beams connected by two masses which are joined by means of a spring, considering a pzt (qp16n, mide corporation) piezoelectric sheet mounted on one of the beams. the energy harvesting (eh) system is mounted rigidly on a rotating hub. the gravitational force on the masses causes sustained oscillatory motion in the flexible beams as long as there is rotary motion. the intention is to use the eh system in the wireless autonomous monitoring of wind turbines under different wind conditions. specifically, the development is oriented to monitor the dynamic state of the blades of a wind generator of 30 kw which rotates between 50 and 150 rpm. the paper shows a complete set of experimental results on three devices, modifying the amount of beams in the frame supporting the system. the results show an acceptable sustained voltage generation for the expected range, in the three proposed cases. therefore, it is possible to use this system for generating energy in a low-frequency rotating environment. as an alternative, the system can be easily adapted to include an array of piezoelectric sheets to each of the beams, to provide more power generation.
rewritten: this paper introduces a rotary power scavenging unit that consists of two flexible-beam systems linked by two masses connected via a spring, featuring a piezoelectric sheet (pzt, model qp16n from mide corporation) mounted on one of the beams. the energy harvesting system is securely attached to a rotating hub, and the gravitational force acting on the masses initiates continuous oscillations in the flexible beams as long as rotation persists. the aim is to implement this energy harvesting unit for wireless autonomous monitoring of wind turbines across various wind conditions. in particular, the design is targeted at monitoring the dynamic behavior of a 30 kw wind generator's blades, which rotate between 50 and 150 rpm. the paper presents comprehensive experimental results from three devices, each with a different number of beams in the support frame. the findings demonstrate that a satisfactory and consistent voltage is generated within the expected range for all three configurations. consequently, this system is a viable option for energy generation in low-frequency rotating environments. additionally, the design can be readily modified to incorporate an array of piezoelectric sheets on each beam, thereby enhancing power generation.

raw: abstract the security and privacy issues of android system have attracted a lot of attention from both industry and academia in recent years. static detection is one typical method to analyze malicious code. however, existing single static detection method can introduce high false alarm rate and is only appropriate for a limited scope. in this paper, we propose an integrated static detection framework, which consists of four layers of filtering mechanisms, that is, the message digest (md5) values, the combination of malicious permissions, the dangerous permissions, and the dangerous intention, respectively. an intuitive threat-degree model is proposed especially on dangerous permissions detection. furthermore, we implement a prototype system ase and validate its feasibility, performance and scalability. a comprehensive evaluation shows that the proposed framework has obvious advantages especially in efficiency, granularity, layers, and correctness.
rewritten: in recent years, the android system’s security and privacy issues have garnered significant attention from both industry and academia. one common approach to analyzing malicious code is static detection. however, traditional standalone static detection methods often suffer from high false alarm rates and are only applicable within a limited scope. in this paper, we introduce an integrated static detection framework that employs four sequential filtering layers: by message digest (md5) values, by combined malicious permissions, by dangerous permissions, and by dangerous intent. we also present an intuitive threat-level model specifically for detecting dangerous permissions. moreover, we have implemented a prototype system named ase and validated its feasibility, performance, and scalability. our comprehensive evaluation demonstrates that the proposed framework offers clear advantages in efficiency, granularity, multi-layered analysis, and accuracy.

raw: chagas disease is a significant health problem in latin america and the available treatments have significant issues in terms of toxicity and efficacy. there is thus an urgent need to develop new treatments either via a repurposing strategy or through the development of new chemical entities. a key first step is the identification of compounds with anti-trypanosoma cruzi activity from compound libraries. here we describe a hit discovery screening cascade designed to specifically identify hits that have the appropriate anti-parasitic properties to warrant further development. the cascade consists of a primary imaging-based assay followed by newly developed and appropriately scaled secondary assays to predict the cidality and rate-of-kill of the compounds. finally, we incorporated a cytochrome p450 cyp51 biochemical assay to remove compounds that owe their phenotypic response to inhibition of this enzyme. we report the use of the cascade in profiling two small libraries containing clinically tested compounds and identify clemastine, azelastine, ifenprodil, ziprasidone and clofibrate as molecules having appropriate profiles. analysis of clinical derived pharmacokinetic and toxicity data indicates that none of these are appropriate for repurposing but they may represent suitable start points for further optimisation for the treatment of chagas disease.
rewritten: chagas disease remains a major health challenge in latin america, and current treatments face significant issues concerning both toxicity and effectiveness. therefore, there is an urgent need to develop new therapies, whether through repurposing existing drugs or discovering new chemical entities. a critical initial step involves screening compound libraries to identify those with anti-trypanosoma cruzi activity. here, we describe a multi-step screening cascade designed to pinpoint candidates with suitable anti-parasitic properties for further development. this cascade begins with a primary, imaging-based assay, which is followed by newly developed, appropriately scaled secondary assays that assess both the cidality and rate-of-kill of the compounds. additionally, a cytochrome p450 cyp51 biochemical assay is included to exclude compounds whose observed activity is solely due to inhibition of this enzyme. using this approach to profile two small libraries of clinically tested compounds, we identified clemastine, azelastine, ifenprodil, ziprasidone, and clofibrate as molecules exhibiting favorable profiles. however, an analysis of clinical pharmacokinetic and toxicity data suggests that while none of these compounds are immediately suitable for repurposing, they could serve as promising starting points for further optimization in the treatment of chagas disease.

raw: the invention belongs to the technical field of communication, and particularly relates to a skin surface condition detecting method. the method comprises the steps that the relation between capacitor discharge time and a count pulse value is calculated through a capacitive measuring circuit based on voltage/time conversion; the equivalent resistance and equivalent capacitance of the skin surface condition are obtained through reverse-reasoning calculation according to the time period; the moisture content and oil content of the skin surface are calculated according to the equivalent resistance and the equivalent capacitance. according to the skin surface condition detecting method, a detected capacitor can be measured only by being charged and discharged one time through the capacitive measuring circuit based on v/t conversion, and the measuring method is easy and convenient to implement, reliable and high in measuring precision.
rewritten: the present invention pertains to the communication technology sector and specifically addresses a method for detecting skin surface conditions. the method includes the following steps: first, it calculates the relationship between the capacitor’s discharge time and a count pulse value using a capacitive measurement circuit based on voltage-to-time conversion. next, it determines the equivalent resistance and equivalent capacitance of the skin surface condition through reverse-calculation according to the measured time period. finally, it computes the skin’s moisture and oil content based on these determined equivalent resistance and capacitance values. this skin surface condition detection method requires only a single charge and discharge cycle of the capacitor via the voltage-to-time conversion based circuit, making it an easy-to-implement, reliable, and accurate measurement technique.

raw: the invention provides a mechanism for disassembling and assembling a grass trimming head of a grass trimmer and a blade of a brush cutter without tools. the mechanism comprises a working rod, a motor shell installed on the working rod, a motor installed in the motor shell, a rotor in the motor, a shaft in the rotor, an upper end cover at the upper end of the motor shell, a rigid cover, a cap and a protection cover which are installed at the lower end of the motor shell or a rigid cover and a protection cover which are installed at the lower end of the motor shell, the grass trimming head installed on the shaft or the blade installed on the shaft, and a nut for fixing the grass trimming head or the blade. the mechanism is characterized in that a pressing and braking column is arranged on the upper end cover, and a pressing and braking mechanism which can downwards press the pressing and braking column and press and brake the rotor so that the rotor can not rotate when the motor is in the non-working state is arranged on the pressing and braking column, the upper end cover and the rotor. by means of the mechanism, when the grass trimming head of the grass trimmer and the blade of the brush cutter are disassembled and assembled and the motor is in the non-working state, the rotor can be pressed and braked only by downwards pressing the pressing and braking column without other tools, and operation is convenient and safe; in addition, the mechanism can be easily improved on the existing grass trimmer and the existing brush cutter, and is low in manufacturing cost.
rewritten: the invention introduces a tool-free mechanism for disassembling and assembling a grass trimmer’s grass trimming head and a brush cutter’s blade. the mechanism consists of a working rod; a motor shell mounted on the working rod; a motor housed within the motor shell; a rotor inside the motor; a shaft within the rotor; an upper end cover attached to the top of the motor shell; and, at the lower end of the motor shell, either a combination of a rigid cover, a cap, and a protection cover or just a rigid cover and a protection cover. either a grass trimming head or a blade is attached to the shaft, and a nut is used to secure them. the key feature of the mechanism is that a pressing and braking column is built into the upper end cover. additionally, a pressing and braking mechanism is integrated with the pressing and braking column, the upper end cover, and the rotor. this mechanism allows the rotor to be pressed and braked—thus preventing it from rotating—when the motor is in a non-working state, simply by pushing the pressing and braking column downward. by using this mechanism, the grass trimming head of a grass trimmer and the blade of a brush cutter can be disassembled and assembled safely and conveniently without using additional tools when the motor is off. furthermore, the design makes it easy to retrofit existing grass trimmers and brush cutters, while keeping the manufacturing cost low.

raw: the article has the objective of analysing the profile of the brazilian tax system from the point of view of the incidence of the taxes upon society. the objective is to shed a preliminary light upon the weight of the taxes and contributions in regard to consumption, spending and patrimony in the organization of the brazilian tax system and to start a discussion about the revenue of state, the goal is to contribute to a more profound discussion about the public finances of the nation, appointing alternatives to the adoption of the restrictive policies of the economic activity and which causes a regressive movement of the civilians rights granted by the constitution.
rewritten: this article aims to analyze the brazilian tax system by examining how taxes impact society. it seeks to provide an initial insight into the burden of taxes and contributions related to consumption, expenditures, and assets within the structure of brazil’s tax framework. furthermore, the article intends to spark a discussion regarding state revenue, ultimately contributing to a deeper conversation on the nation’s public finances. it also proposes alternatives to restrictive economic policies that lead to inequitable impacts on the constitutional rights of citizens.

raw: the formation of an influential diaspora from among the learned and profes sional classes remains one of the most remarkable, and least acknowledged, pat terns of irish migration in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries. a wave of professional migration followed the expansion of irish higher education with the opening of the queen's colleges in 1849, and middle-class graduates? many of whom were catholic?used ireland's position within the empire to forge careers and to escape the social and economic confines of their time and place. taken as a group, the intellectual and professional diaspora?comprising individuals with some university education, a degree, or professional qualifica tion?succeeded in attaining influential positions in the colonial services. this group also played a significant and often disproportionate role in the growth of the knowledgeable professions and english language print culture throughout the empire.1 that irish writers played a prominent role in the country's diaspora is not surprising, given ireland's tradition of literary excellence. numerous recent studies have begun to examine the work of irish journalists, travel writers, and newspaper editors in relation to the expansion of print culture in north amer ica, southern africa and australia.2 the impact of this literary diaspora on the british empire was ore diffuse and widespread.
rewritten: one of the most noteworthy—and often overlooked—examples of irish migration in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries was the emergence of a prominent diaspora from among the country's educated and professional classes. with the expansion of irish higher education marked by the opening of queen’s colleges in 1849, many middle-class graduates (predominantly catholic) leveraged ireland’s imperial connections to build careers and escape the strict social and economic boundaries of their home environment. as a group, these intellectual and professional emigrants—individuals holding university degrees or professional qualifications—were successful in securing influential roles within the colonial services. they also played a crucial, if sometimes disproportionate, part in the development of the learned professions and the growth of english-language print culture throughout the empire. given ireland’s longstanding literary excellence, it is no surprise that irish writers formed a key part of this diaspora. in recent studies, scholars have increasingly explored the contributions of irish journalists, travel writers, and newspaper editors to the spread of print culture in regions such as north america, southern africa, and australia. overall, the impact of this literary diaspora on the british empire was both multifaceted and far-reaching.

raw: the role of trees and forests as a critical component of the biosphere and critical zone, and of the earth system more generally, is widely appreciated. less known and acknowledged are the geomorphological functions of tree roots, although their importance has been widely referred to in soil studies, paleopedology, and paleobotany. tree roots and their impact on weathering processes and soil production were incorporated in the devonian plant hypothesis and tree root casts served as a key evidence of recognition of past soils in geology, sedimentology, and paleopedology. however, knowledge of biomechanical and biochemical weathering induced by vascular plant roots (mainly trees) has been rarely utilized in geomorphic studies. biogeomorphic and pedologic studies in recent decades have highlighted the importance of tree uprooting, in which roots play a primary role, in soil development, regolith disturbance and bedrock mining. other important functions of roots were also recognized, e.g., soil displacement by growing roots, infilling of stump holes and root cavities, root groove development, direct and indirect effects taking place in the rhizosphere and mycorrhizosphere (mainly biochemical weathering of minerals, support by microbial communities and symbiotic fungi), and changes in porosity, permeability, and hydrology of soils in the root zone. however, further studies are urgently needed because many aspects of biochemical and biomechanical weathering are not well understood. this is especially true with respect to taxa-specific impacts. variations in root architectures, edaphic settings, ecological relationships, and geographic ranges result in substantially different biogeomorphic impacts of different tree species. additionally, the same species in different environmental settings may have different effects.
rewritten: trees and forests are widely recognized as vital elements of the biosphere, the critical zone, and the earth system as a whole. however, the geomorphological functions of tree roots are less well-known, despite being frequently mentioned in soil research, paleopedology, and paleobotany. for example, tree roots and their effects on weathering processes and soil creation were integral to the devonian plant hypothesis, with casts of tree roots providing key evidence for the existence of ancient soils in geology, sedimentology, and paleopedology. yet, the role of vascular plant roots—primarily those of trees—in inducing both biomechanical and biochemical weathering has seldom been incorporated into geomorphic studies. in recent decades, biogeomorphic and pedologic research has underscored the importance of tree uprooting—in which roots play a central role—in driving soil development, disturbing regolith, and contributing to bedrock breakdown. additional significant functions of roots have also been observed; these include soil displacement by growing roots, the filling of stump holes and root cavities, the formation of root grooves, and both direct and indirect effects within the rhizosphere and mycorrhizosphere (such as the biochemical weathering of minerals, support from microbial communities and symbiotic fungi), along with changes in soil porosity, permeability, and hydrology in the root zone. nonetheless, further studies are urgently needed as many aspects of biochemical and biomechanical weathering remain poorly understood. this gap is particularly apparent regarding species-specific impacts, since variations in root structures, soil environments, ecological interactions, and geographic distributions lead to notably different biogeomorphic effects among tree species. moreover, the same species may exhibit different effects depending on the environmental context.

raw: abstract background despite its dramatic improvement, china's tuberculosis burden is still high according to the 2015 who tb report. evidence show that experienced physicians do better in reducing delay diagnosis and patient mortality and are more likely to follow the treatment guidelines. however, in china, few studies have focused on physicians specialised in tuberculosis; and these few studies were mainly centred on the prevalence and risk factors among them. we assessed the occupational challenges of these physicians in china. methods we conducted 14 focus group discussions in yichang city of the hubei province and in hanzhong city of the shaanxi province, china between may 5, 2013, and aug 11, 2015. we purposively sampled 169 physicians, 92 from yichang, to ensure representation and geographical diversity. health administrators, doctors and nurses, who are currently responsible for the tuberculosis care, were included. interviewers used open-ended discussion guides concerning occupational risk, workload, work-reward, and challenges encountered. data were transcribed verbatim and were coded, sorted, and classified by the thematic framework using nvivo 11.0. ethical permission was obtained from the institutional ethics committee of chinese center for disease control and prevention (cdc). findings 169 (100%) physicians specialised in tuberculosis considered that they are working in high-risk departments bearing heavy workloads with insufficient compensation. the amount of workload given to the physicians seems to have increased. the integrated approach shifted the clinical tuberculosis care from the cdcs to general hospitals, resulting in hospitals having to do excessive things including detection, diagnosis, and treatment and case management. to facilitate patients' compliance, all of the physicians have also been responsible for the hand out of subsidy and reimbursement. due to inflexible information system, physicians have to handle patients' information by handwriting all the details on paper. the disunity between the three basic insurances seems to add to this additional workload. 160 (95%) seemed to be discontent with their compensation; they did not feel their reward reflects their increased workload, or sometimes it is even lower than what it was before, resulting in abhorrent attitude. 101 (60%) physicians still worried about occupational exposure and external prejudice, which reflects the high-risk work. interpretation to our knowledge, this is the first study focusing on tuberculosis physicians and their benefits. these findings should help policy makers and perfect the tuberculosis control. funding natural science foundation of china (71203068 and 71573095) and the china national health and family planning commission and the gates foundation tb project (51914). the funders had no role in the study.
rewritten: despite china's marked progress, tuberculosis remains a significant challenge, as highlighted by the 2015 who tb report. studies indicate that experienced physicians are more effective at minimizing diagnostic delays, reducing patient mortality, and adhering to treatment guidelines. however, in china, research specifically examining tuberculosis specialists is scarce, and when available, such studies predominantly address the prevalence and risk factors rather than their occupational experiences. our study aimed to evaluate the work-related challenges faced by these physicians in china. methods: between may 5, 2013, and august 11, 2015, we conducted 14 focus group discussions in yichang city (hubei province) and hanzhong city (shaanxi province). a purposive sample of 169 physicians (92 from yichang) was selected to ensure a diverse geographical representation. participants included health administrators, doctors, and nurses currently involved in tb care. interviewers employed open-ended discussion guides covering topics such as occupational risk, workload, work-reward issues, and encountered challenges. all discussions were transcribed verbatim and analyzed using thematic coding with nvivo 11.0. ethical approval was granted by the institutional ethics committee of the chinese center for disease control and prevention (cdc). findings: all 169 tb-specialized physicians reported working in high-risk departments with excessive workloads and inadequate compensation. many observed an increase in workload, particularly following the integrated approach that transferred clinical tuberculosis care from the cdcs to general hospitals. this shift has led hospitals to take on numerous tasks including detection, diagnosis, treatment, and case management. additionally, physicians are now tasked with distributing subsidies and reimbursement to patients to ensure compliance. the inflexibility of the information systems forces them to document patient details manually. furthermore, the lack of coordination among the three basic insurance systems contributes to their extra workload. approximately 95% (160) of the participants expressed dissatisfaction with their compensation, feeling that it does not adequately reflect their increased workload—in some instances, it is even lower than before—resulting in negative sentiments. moreover, 60% (101) of the physicians remain concerned about occupational exposure and external stigma, underscoring the risks associated with their work. interpretation: to our knowledge, this is the first study that specifically examines the benefits and challenges faced by tuberculosis physicians. these findings are intended to inform policymakers and support improvements in tb control strategies. funding: this study was supported by the natural science foundation of china (grants 71203068 and 71573095), as well as funding from the china national health and family planning commission, and the gates foundation tb project (grant 51914). the funders had no involvement in the study's design, execution, or reporting.

raw: abstract the comparative studies were performed in order to assess the most suitable matrix polymer for ascorbic acid (aa). the antioxidant (0.5, 1.0, 2.0% w/w) was included into sorbitol-plasticized emulsion films based on different biopolymers: carboxymethyl cellulose (cmc), oxidized potato starch (ops), soy protein isolate (spi), and gelatin (gel). aa reduced the mechanical strength of the films in a concentration dependent manner. gel and cmc produced films with higher mechanical strength and stiffness compared to the plant storage polymers. the moisturizing action of aa inhibited plasticizer recrystallization in the protein-based films. it was found, that non-enzymatic browning of the films prepared at lower ph values (ops and gel) was slower than those of casted at higher ph (cmc and spi). spi films displayed the most intensive browning development. the highest aa retention was achieved in ops films, while the lowest in gel films. the aa stability increased with increasing aa concentration. a significant correlation existed between the antioxidant activity of films and the concentration of aa released into aqueous food simulant. cmc films, due to rapid dissolution, tended to have the highest ferric reducing antioxidant power (frap) and antiradical activity. in turn, water-swollen gel films had the lowest antioxidant activity. prolonged testing in an emulsified linoleic acid model system revealed that at higher aa incorporation levels the films did not differ in their anti-lipid peroxidation capacity.
rewritten: a comparative study was conducted to identify the most suitable polymer matrix for incorporating ascorbic acid (aa). the antioxidant (at concentrations of 0.5, 1.0, and 2.0% w/w) was incorporated in sorbitol-plasticized emulsion films made from various biopolymers: carboxymethyl cellulose (cmc), oxidized potato starch (ops), soy protein isolate (spi), and gelatin (gel). it was observed that aa reduced the films’ mechanical strength in a concentration-dependent manner. films made from gel and cmc exhibited greater mechanical strength and stiffness compared to those derived from plant storage polymers. additionally, aa’s moisturizing effect helped prevent the recrystallization of the plasticizer in the protein-based films. the study also found that non-enzymatic browning occurred more slowly in films prepared at lower ph values (ops and gel) than in those cast at higher ph (cmc and spi), with spi films showing the most pronounced browning. the highest aa retention was observed in ops films, while gel films retained the least amount of aa, and aa stability improved with increased aa concentration. there was a significant correlation between the films’ antioxidant activity and the concentration of aa released into an aqueous food simulant. cmc films, which dissolved quickly, tended to display the highest ferric reducing antioxidant power (frap) and antiradical activity, whereas water-swollen gel films exhibited the lowest antioxidant activity. however, extended testing in an emulsified linoleic acid model system showed that at higher levels of aa incorporation, the films performed similarly in terms of anti-lipid peroxidation capacity.

raw: the utility model discloses a waste water tank stirring formula fertilizer compression drainage device, including the support frame, be provided with the water collector on the support frame, the water collector bottom is provided with the delivery port, is provided with the compression bucket on the water collector, is provided with a plurality of outer rounds on the lateral wall of compression bucket, and there is the extrusion cake compression bucket top through the support mounting, and the extrusion cake is the vertical motion from top to bottom, the delivery port is connected with the waste water tank, and the waste water tank top is provided with the waste water tank apron, and the waste water tank has inside and outside two -layer box, and for still having stretched into the puddler in vacuum gap, the waste water tank between the two -layer box, the utility model discloses installation extrusion cake above compressing the bucket comes the moisture among the extrusion materials by extrudeing the cake, makes its outer round from compressing on the bucket discharge, the waste water tank is for vacuous double -layer design and add and to have the waste water tank apron, has effectually avoided the inside and outside temperature transmission of waste water tank, and ph value back is adjusted to waste water, can be for the raw materials of other reactions, so install the puddler in the waste water tank, convenient to use just saves time.
rewritten: this utility model discloses a drainage device for fertilizer processing that compresses material and stirs wastewater in a tank. the device comprises a support frame on which a water collector is mounted. the water collector features a delivery port at its bottom and supports a compression bucket. the lateral wall of the compression bucket is equipped with several external rollers. additionally, an extrusion mechanism is affixed to the top of the compression bucket via the support frame; this mechanism moves vertically from top to bottom to squeeze moisture out of the extruded material. the extracted moisture is discharged through the delivery port, which is connected to a wastewater tank. the wastewater tank is designed with a top apron and a double-layer construction consisting of inner and outer boxes, between which a vacuum gap is maintained. a mixer (or puddler) is installed within this gap. the compression process in the bucket effectively separates moisture from the material, discharging it through the rollers. furthermore, the vacuum-insulated, double-layer design of the wastewater tank with its apron ensures minimal heat transfer between the interior and exterior, thereby allowing for adjustment of the wastewater’s ph. this treated wastewater can then be used as a raw material in other chemical reactions. overall, the integration of the mixer within the wastewater tank enhances operation efficiency and saves time.

raw: the ncip addon streamlines processing of both lending and borrowing interlibrary loan requests automatically using existing illiad work flows with alma. circulation desk staff no longer need to toggle between two systems to manage current loans and view items on hold. patrons need only view their account in primo to see all of their loans or outstanding requests. lending staff no longer need to update requests to shipped in illiad and then check the item out in alma, eliminating the need for pseudopatrons. everybody wins!
rewritten: the ncip addon automates the processing of both lending and borrowing interlibrary loan requests by using existing illiad workflows with alma. this means that circulation desk staff no longer have to switch between two systems to manage loans and view items on hold. patrons can simply check their account in primo to see all their current loans and outstanding requests. additionally, lending staff are freed from having to update a request to "shipped" in illiad and then check the item out in alma—eliminating the need for pseudopatrons. in short, it's a win for everyone!

raw: this work presents the results of spectral analysis of biopolymer materials that are implemented in medical sphere. polymer samples containing polycaprolactone and iron oxides of different valence were used in the studies. raman spectroscopy method was used as a main control method. relative content of iron and polycaprolactone in studied materials was assessed using ratio of rs intensities values at 604 cm -1 and 1726 cm -1 wavenumbers to intensity value of 1440 cm -1 line.
rewritten: this study presents the outcomes of a spectral analysis performed on biopolymer materials used in the medical field. the research utilized polymer samples made from polycaprolactone combined with iron oxides of varying valence states. raman spectroscopy served as the primary control method. the relative amounts of iron and polycaprolactone in the materials were determined by comparing the intensity ratios of the raman signals at 604 cm⁻¹ and 1726 cm⁻¹ to the intensity at the 1440 cm⁻¹ line.

raw: to understand the mechanisms those are involved in the appearance of foetal heart rate decelerations (fhr) after the combined epidural analgesia in labour. observational study done at university hospital for 86-term singleton pregnant women with spontaneous labour. serial bedside measurement of the main cardiac maternal parameters with uscom technique; stroke volume (sv), heart rate (hr), cardiac output (co) and total vascular resistances (tvr) inputting systolic and diastolic blood pressure before combined epidural analgesia and after 5', 10', 15' and 20 min. fhr was continuously recorded though cardiotocography before and after the procedure. correlation between the appearance of foetal heart rate decelerations and the modification of maternal haemodynamic parameters. fourteen out of 86 foetuses showed decelerations after the combined spino epidural procedure. no decelerations occurred in the women with low tvr ( 1200 dyne/s/cm(-5)). soon after the epidural procedure, the absence of increase in sv and co was observed in these women. no variations in systolic and diastolic blood pressure values were found. the level of tvr before combined epidural analgesia in labour may indicate the risk of fhr abnormalities after the procedure. low tvr (<1000 dyne/s/cm(-5)) showed a reduced risk of fhr abnormalities. fhr decelerations seem to occur in women without the ability to upregulate sv and co in response to the initial effects of analgesia.
rewritten: this study aims to clarify the mechanisms that contribute to the development of fetal heart rate (fhr) decelerations following combined epidural analgesia during labor. conducted at a university hospital, the observational study involved 86 term singleton pregnant women who entered labor spontaneously. researchers performed repeated bedside assessments of the primary maternal cardiac parameters—namely stroke volume (sv), heart rate (hr), cardiac output (co), and total vascular resistance (tvr)—using the uscom technique. measurements, which also incorporated systolic and diastolic blood pressure, were taken before the administration of epidural analgesia and then at 5, 10, 15, and 20 minutes afterward. continuous fetal heart rate monitoring via cardiotocography was performed both before and after the procedure. the study examined the relationship between the onset of fetal heart rate decelerations and changes in maternal hemodynamic parameters. fhr decelerations were observed in 14 out of the 86 fetuses following the combined spinal-epidural procedure. notably, no decelerations were detected in women with low tvr values (less than 1200 dyne/s/cm⁻⁵). it was found that, immediately after the epidural procedure, these women did not exhibit an increase in stroke volume and cardiac output, even though there were no significant changes in systolic and diastolic blood pressure. the findings suggest that the pre-procedure level of tvr during labor may serve as an indicator for the risk of fhr abnormalities post-procedure. specifically, a low tvr (under 1000 dyne/s/cm⁻⁵) is linked to a reduced risk of such abnormalities. moreover, it appears that fhr decelerations predominantly occur in women who are unable to increase their sv and co in response to the initial effects of analgesia.

rewritten: using photoluminescence (pl) probes in immunodiagnostic procedures requires the specific and accurate labeling of biomarkers, along with tags whose pl signal is clearly distinguishable from the autofluorescence of cells in clinical specimens. nanoprobes composed of core/shell quantum dots (qds)—particularly cdse/zns types—and single-domain antibodies (sdabs) that exhibit pl when excited with multiphoton techniques, satisfy both of these criteria. these diagnostic nanoprobes are…

raw: we report the case of a 63-year-old woman who required emergent intubation after a choking episode at home. it resulted in a 5-cm tear in the membranous trachea. she was treated by placement of a temporary tracheal stent, which was successfully removed 3 months later.
rewritten: we present the case of a 63-year-old woman who needed emergency intubation after experiencing a choking incident at home. the event caused a 5-cm tear in the membranous portion of her trachea. she was treated by placing a temporary tracheal stent, which was successfully removed three months later.

raw: the addition of synthetic colors in foods in an attempt to attract the attention of consumers, not a ban, but if the dye used is a dye that is unusual for the food, it is harmful to health. one source of natural pigments that can be used is the red dragon fruit skin. these skins contain anthocyanins potential as a dye. in this reasearch, the observed red dragon fruit skin moisture content and size of the powder. it also conducted a stirring speed variation during the extraction process. while variable temperature, time, and solvent refers to the optimum conditions of research that has been done. analysis of the results was conducted to determine ph, anthocyanin concentration and yield results. based on the results obtained, the smaller the water content contained in the skin, giving absorbance values are higher. this shows that extractable anthocyanins levels are also higher. the lowest levels of the skin can be achieved in this study was 6%. the smaller the grain size, the higher the anthocyanin content. the smallest grain size achieved was 100 mesh. the highest mixing speed in this study was 300 rpm, the higher the stirring speed, the higher the anthocyanin content fetched. organoleptic tests were conducted to study the effect of anthocyanins on the quality of the food shows that the addition of anthocyanins provide the appropriate color and do not significantly affect flavor and aroma. so it can be used as a natural dye for food.
rewritten: synthetic colors are added to foods to capture consumers' attention; while they are not banned, using a dye that is atypical for a particular food can be harmful to health. one natural source of pigments is the red dragon fruit skin, which contains anthocyanins that serve as a potential dye. in this research, the moisture content of red dragon fruit skins and the size of the resulting powder were examined, along with variations in stirring speed during the extraction process. other factors such as temperature, time, and solvent were adjusted to determine the optimal conditions. the analysis focused on ph levels, anthocyanin concentration, and yield. results showed that lower moisture content in the skin led to higher absorbance values, indicating increased extractable anthocyanins; the minimum moisture level achieved in this study was 6%. moreover, smaller powder particle sizes—down to 100 mesh—resulted in greater anthocyanin content. similarly, higher stirring speeds, up to 300 rpm, were associated with improved anthocyanin extraction. organoleptic tests confirmed that adding anthocyanins produced an appealing color without significantly affecting the food's flavor or aroma, making them a viable natural dye for food applications.

rewritten: the utility model pertains to an innovative electrical apparatus connecting plate socket. it consists of a connecting plate with a connecting terminal installed at one end. surrounding the connecting terminal, spliced eyes are provided at both ends. the rear end of the connecting terminal is fitted with a socket fixing base mounted onto the connecting plate, into which a socket is installed. both ends of the socket are equipped with plug terminals that insert into the spliced eyes. the utility model features a simple structure that allows the socket to be quickly and easily connected to the terminal on the connecting plate, provides ease of maintenance, and ensures high safety.

raw: strontium cerate (srceo3) is a well known proton conductor. in applications, it is frequently doped with yttrium to increase stability and promote ionic conductivity; however, the microscopic effects of doping and native defects are not fully understood. we use first-principles hybrid density functional calculations to study the atomic and electronic structure of impurities and defects and their impact on proton conduction. interactions of hydrogen with cation vacancies are found to negatively impact proton conductivity. we also investigate band alignments as well as the alignment of the thermodynamic charge-state transition levels of hydrogen. comparison with baceo3 allows us to establish trends across the perovskite cerates.
rewritten: strontium cerate (srceo₃) is a well-known proton conductor. in practical uses, it is often doped with yttrium to enhance its stability and boost ionic conductivity; however, the detailed microscopic effects of such doping and the presence of native defects remain only partially understood. in this work, we employ first-principles hybrid density functional calculations to explore the atomic and electronic structures of impurities and defects, along with how they affect proton conduction. our findings indicate that the interaction between hydrogen and cation vacancies can detrimentally impact proton conductivity. we further examine both the band alignments and the thermodynamic charge-state transition levels of hydrogen. by comparing these results with those obtained for baceo₃, we are able to discern trends across the perovskite cerates.

raw: phosphoinositide 3-kinase (pi3k) is a promising anti-cancer target, because various mutations and amplifications are observed in human tumors isolated from cancer patients. our dihydropyrrolopyrimidine derivative with a phenylurea moiety showed strong pi3k enzyme inhibitory activity, but its pharmacokinetic property was poor because of lack of solubility. herein, we report how we improved the solubility of our pi3k inhibitors by introducing a solubilizing group and ortho substituents to break molecular planarity.
rewritten: phosphoinositide 3-kinase (pi3k) is considered a promising target for cancer therapy, as various mutations and amplifications have been identified in tumors from cancer patients. our dihydropyrrolopyrimidine derivative, which includes a phenylurea group, exhibited strong inhibitory activity against the pi3k enzyme. however, its poor solubility led to suboptimal pharmacokinetic properties. in this report, we describe how we enhanced the solubility of our pi3k inhibitors by incorporating a solubilizing group and adding ortho substituents to disrupt the molecule’s planarity.

raw: the invention discloses a liriomyza preventing fertilizer. the fertilizer comprises main components of, by weight, 90-100 parts of original fertilizers, 15-25 parts of potassium carbonate, 10-20 parts of chelating agents, 5-10 parts of compound sodium nitrophenolate, 5-10 parts of calcium superphosphate, 5-10 parts of corn cervi pantotrichum, 10-22 parts of bignoniad, 10-20 parts of radix lithospermi, 5-10 parts of lignum apuilariae resinatum, 20-30 parts of kaolin and 5-10 parts of ammonium salt. in this way, the liriomyza preventing fertilizer is capable of preventing damage from liriomyzas and guaranteeing the plant stability.
rewritten: the invention reveals a fertilizer formulation designed to prevent liriomyza infestations. this fertilizer consists of the following major components by weight: 90–100 parts original fertilizers, 15–25 parts potassium carbonate, 10–20 parts chelating agents, 5–10 parts compound sodium nitrophenolate, 5–10 parts calcium superphosphate, 5–10 parts corn cervi pantotrichum, 10–22 parts bignoniad, 10–20 parts radix lithospermi, 5–10 parts lignum apuilariae resinatum, 20–30 parts kaolin, and 5–10 parts ammonium salt. as a result, this liriomyza-preventing fertilizer not only combats damage caused by liriomyzas but also ensures the stability of the plants.

raw: the utility model relates to a purifier suitable for fine particle thing is got rid of. including the purifier casing, the wind gap is gone into in clean wind export, and the purifier casing is inside including dust collecting chamber, gaseous cleaning channel and plenum chamber, inside polygon whirlwind of arranging of cleaning channel, the sub - top gas outlet of whirlwind is connected with the plenum chamber, and the bottom goes out the dirt mouth and is connected with the dust collection box, be provided with little fine needle thorn form coating on the inside shells inner wall of gaseous cleaning channel, the part or whole is packed in dust collecting chamber, the plenum chamber has the filler, purifier is one -level or multilevel hierarchy. because the improvement of structure makes the inside flow field of this purifier distribute can be optimized, the impurity that contains in the fluid will be isolated, improves to micron order separation of impurities efficiency to realize that the fluidic effectively purifies, and simple structure, processing is convenient, low cost, and can the energy saving not produce extra purifying waste water, to the diameter 0.5 5 the separation efficiency of mu m's impurity can reach more than 97%.
rewritten: this utility model discloses a purifier designed to remove fine particles. the apparatus comprises a purifier casing that features a wind gap for the discharge of clean air and internally houses a dust collecting chamber, a gaseous cleaning channel, and a plenum chamber. within the casing, the cleaning channel is arranged in a polygonal cyclone pattern, wherein the cyclone’s sub-top gas outlet connects to the plenum chamber, and the bottom dust outlet connects to a dust collection box. the inner wall of the gaseous cleaning channel is coated with a fine, needle-like layer, which may partially or wholly extend into the dust collecting chamber. the plenum chamber is also filled with a filler. the design allows for a single-level or multi-level structure. due to the improved structural design, the internal flow field of the purifier is optimized, enabling the isolation of impurities suspended in the fluid and significantly enhancing the separation efficiency for micron-sized particles. as a result, the device effectively purifies the fluid. additionally, the purifier features a simple and convenient structure that is cost-effective and energy-saving, without producing additional wastewater. the separation efficiency for particles with diameters between 0.5 and 5 micrometers can reach over 97%.

raw: background: dengue infections can result in a wide spectrum of disease severity ranging from an influenza-like illness (dengue fever; df) to the life-threatening dengue hemorrhagic fever (dhf)/dengue shock syndrome (dss). the study was aimed to compare the clinical profile of all patients diagnosed with dengue viral infection at mamc. methods: this retrospective study included 188 patients infected with dengue virus, age 6 years to 70 years. laboratory and haematological data were included. results: peak of infection occurred in october 2015 and least number of cases were recorded in december 2015. common clinical symptoms were fever, and abdominal pain. common haematological abnormalities were thrombocytopenia and leucopoenia. all patients survived. there was no case of dengue hemorrhagic fever or dengue shock syndrome. conclusions: significant differences in the clinical profile is possibly because of infection with different serotypes of dengue virus (denv), concurrent/sequential infection of more than one serotype, and differences in host immune responses associated with host genetic variations.
rewritten: background: dengue infections present with a range of severity, from a mild, influenza-like illness (dengue fever; df) to the more severe dengue hemorrhagic fever (dhf) and dengue shock syndrome (dss). this study aimed to compare the clinical profiles of all patients diagnosed with dengue viral infection at mamc. methods: in this retrospective study, 188 patients aged between 6 and 70 years, all infected with the dengue virus, were analyzed. both laboratory results and hematological data were reviewed. results: the highest number of infections was recorded in october 2015, with the fewest cases in december 2015. the most common symptoms were fever and abdominal pain, while the primary hematological abnormalities observed were thrombocytopenia and leukopenia. notably, all patients survived, and there were no cases of dhf or dss. conclusions: the observed variations in clinical profiles may be attributed to infections caused by different dengue virus serotypes, simultaneous or sequential infections with multiple serotypes, and variations in the host’s immune response due to genetic differences.

raw: the invention discloses a method and system for analyzing a domain name, evaluating and feeding back data quality and optimizing data. the method comprises the following steps that: (1), an analyzer on a client side initiates a negotiation request to a dns server; and the negotiation request contains a plurality of domain names to be evaluated; (2), the dns server returns dns data of a set domain name to the analyzer while receiving the negotiation request of the analyzer; (3), the analyzer analyzes the dns application server corresponding to each set domain name from the received dns domain name data, initiates a service request to the corresponding dns application server according to the historical usage mode of the set domain name, and obtains quality feedback data of a service; (4), the analyzer generates a evaluation report according to the quality feedback data, and sends the evaluation report to the dns server; and (5), the dns server optimizes the dns data according to the evaluation report and customizes the dns data for the client side. by means of the method and the system disclosed by the invention, the data analysis quality of the domain name server and the service quality thereof can be increased.
rewritten: the invention introduces a system and method for analyzing domain names, assessing data quality, providing feedback, and optimizing data. the method comprises the following steps: 1. an analyzer on the client side initiates a negotiation request to a dns server that includes multiple domain names to be evaluated. 2. upon receiving the request, the dns server returns the dns data associated with a specific set of domain names to the analyzer. 3. the analyzer examines the dns application server corresponding to each provided domain name based on the received dns data, sends a service request to the relevant dns application server in line with the historical usage pattern of that domain name, and collects quality feedback on the service. 4. using the quality feedback, the analyzer creates an evaluation report which is then sent back to the dns server. 5. finally, the dns server uses the evaluation report to optimize the dns data and customize the dns service for the client. by employing this method and system, the analysis quality of the dns and the overall service quality can be significantly improved.

raw: locomotor activities are part and parcel of daily human life. during walking or running, feet are subjected to high plantar pressure, leading sometimes to limb problems, pain, or foot ulceration. a current objective in foot plantar pressure measurements is developing sensors that are small in size, lightweight, and energy efficient, while enabling high mobility, particularly for wearable applications. moreover, improvements in spatial resolution, accuracy, and sensitivity are of interest. sensors with improved sensing techniques can be applied to a variety of research problems: diagnosing limb problems, footwear design, or injury prevention. this paper reviews commercially available sensors used in foot plantar pressure measurements and proposes the utilization of pressure sensors based on the mems (microelectromechanical systems) technique. pressure sensors based on this technique have the capacity to measure pressure with high accuracy and linearity up to high pressure levels. moreover, being small in size, they are highly suitable for this type of measurement. we present two mems sensor models and study their suitability for the intended purpose by performing several experiments. preliminary results indicate that the sensors are indeed suitable for measuring foot plantar pressure. importantly, by measuring pressure continuously, they can also be utilized for body balance measurements.
rewritten: human locomotor activities are an inherent part of daily life. when we walk or run, our feet endure substantial plantar pressure, which can sometimes lead to limb issues, pain, or even foot ulceration. consequently, a significant goal in foot plantar pressure measurement research is to develop sensors that are compact, lightweight, energy efficient, and capable of high mobility—especially for wearable applications—while also improving spatial resolution, accuracy, and sensitivity. advanced sensing techniques can be employed to tackle various research challenges, including diagnosing limb problems, enhancing footwear design, and preventing injuries. this paper reviews the commercially available sensors used to measure foot plantar pressure and suggests the adoption of pressure sensors based on microelectromechanical systems (mems). these mems-based sensors offer the ability to accurately and linearly measure pressure even at high values, and their small size makes them particularly well-suited for such measurements. we introduce two mems sensor models and assess their suitability for this purpose through a series of experiments. early results show that these sensors are indeed effective for measuring foot plantar pressure and, with continuous pressure monitoring, can also be utilized for evaluating body balance.

raw: the utility model discloses a self -propelled anticorrosive tub of beveling machine, including the guide rail, install self -propelled dolly and limit stop on the guide rail, install brace table and servo motor on the sweep of self -propelled dolly, servo motor's output shaft passes through the belt to be connected with the gyro wheel of self -propelled dolly, is provided with the backup pad on the brace table, is provided with the protection casing in the backup pad, is provided with grinding motor on the brace table, is fixed with the brush of polishing on the grinding motor output shaft. the utility model discloses only by common null such as i -steel, channel -section steel, in the aspect of simple structure, the manufacturing, with low costs, be applicable to medium and minor enterprise and use, do benefit to the popularization. through the reciprocating motion of the self -propelled dolly by a small margin of servo motor drives, realize processings of polishing and pass through to the pipe end only needing the operation of 1 people at the brush of polishing of grinding motor output through installing, efficiency is higher, and greatly the degree has saved labour cost.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a self-propelled, anticorrosive beveled machine that comprises a guide rail with an installed self-propelled dolly equipped with a limit stop. on the dolly’s sweep, a brace table and a servo motor are mounted. the output shaft of the servo motor is connected via a belt to the dolly’s gyro wheel. attached to the brace table is a backup pad that houses a protection casing, and a grinding motor is also fixed on the brace table. a polishing brush is mounted on the output shaft of the grinding motor. constructed from common materials such as i-beam steel or channel-section steel, the design is simple and cost-effective, making it ideal for medium and small enterprises and conducive to widespread adoption. the device uses a reciprocating motion of the self-propelled dolly, driven over a small margin by the servo motor, to perform both polishing and pipe-end processing. this setup allows a single operator to efficiently manage the polishing brush at the grinding motor’s output, significantly reducing labor costs.

raw: the disk diffusion test (kirby-bauer method) and antimicrobial gradient (etest, biomerieux, durham, nc) are two methods used for susceptibility testing. the kirby-bauer method uses antibiotic-containing disks on agar plates inoculated with the bacteria of interest[1][1]; it is a qualitative method
rewritten: two methods used for susceptibility testing are the disk diffusion test (kirby-bauer method) and the antimicrobial gradient method (etest, biomérieux, durham, nc). in the kirby-bauer method, antibiotic-impregnated disks are placed on agar plates that have been inoculated with the bacteria of interest, and this approach yields qualitative results.

raw: the aim of this study is to synthesize the existing literature on hospital hygiene and robotic animals to provide researchers and professionals that use robotic animals in this environment with tools and guidelines. the conducted literature review leads to the conclusion that cleaning procedures reported in the included studies and hygienic guidelines cannot be applied to currently available robotic animals due to excessive use of water, disinfectants and high temperatures. this study consequently raises questions about the application of these robotic animals in more vulnerable populations and adds to the understanding of the required conditions for robotic animals if being developed to be used in healthcare institutions.
rewritten: this study seeks to compile and integrate existing literature on hospital hygiene and robotic animals to equip researchers and professionals using these devices with practical tools and guidelines. the literature review indicates that the cleaning methods and hygienic protocols described in the studies are not suitable for current robotic animal models because they involve excessive water use, strong disinfectants, and high temperatures. as a result, the study questions the suitability of these robotic animals for use with vulnerable populations and enhances our understanding of the necessary conditions for their development and application in healthcare settings.

rewritten: the utility model falls within the field of filtering materials and discloses an environmentally friendly anti-static felt. this felt comprises a base cloth and a layer of dacron chemical fiber that is attached to one side of the base cloth. on the surface of the dacron layer that faces the wind, a plurality of independent polymer anti-static layers are uniformly distributed. in this design, these evenly arranged polymer layers enhance the anti-static properties, while their independent structure improves the gas permeability of the felt, thereby increasing its overall filtration efficiency.

raw: we study collective dynamics of a small-sized chain of the unidirectionally coupled phase-locked loop. the conditions for the synchronous-regime existence are found, the asynchronous selfoscillation regimes and the transitions among them are studied, and the property of inheriting the structure of the parameter space of the chain when a new element is added to it is established.
rewritten: this work examines the collective dynamics of a small chain of unidirectionally coupled phase-locked loops. it identifies the conditions required for a synchronous regime to exist, investigates asynchronous self-oscillation regimes along with transitions between them, and demonstrates that when a new element is added, the chain’s parameter space structure is preserved.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a cultivation method and a chemical fertilizer capable of improving acidity of fruits while suppressing reduction of the fruit yields.solution: a cultivation method of the present invention is a cultivation method of fruit vegetables having a fertilizer supply step for supplying a fertilizer, wherein the average potassium content rate in the fertilizer supplied in the fertilizer supply step is increased after taking root. the ratio of the average potassium content after taking root to the average potassium content up to taking root in the fertilizer supplied in the fertilizer supply step is preferably 1.5 or more and 3 or less. the average potassium content of the fertilizer supplied until taking root in the fertilizer supply step is preferably 10 me/l or more and 18 me/l or less, and the average potassium content of the fertilizer supplied after taking root is preferably 20 me/l or more and 45 me/l or less. in the fertilizer supplied in the fertilizer supply step, the average nitrogen component content after the early stage of fruit enlargement may be made lower than the average nitrogen component content up to the early stage of fruit enlargement.selected drawing: figure 6
rewritten: problem to be solved: develop a cultivation technique and a chemical fertilizer formulation that enhance fruit acidity while preventing a decline in fruit yield. solution: this invention relates to a cultivation method for fruit vegetables that includes a fertilizer application stage. in this stage, the fertilizer's average potassium content is increased after the plants have established roots. specifically, the ratio of the average potassium content after rooting to that before rooting is preferably between 1.5 and 3. additionally, the average potassium content in the fertilizer applied before rooting should ideally be between 10 me/l and 18 me/l, while that applied after rooting should be between 20 me/l and 45 me/l. moreover, in the fertilizer applied during this stage, the average nitrogen content after the initial phase of fruit enlargement may be set lower than the average nitrogen content before that phase. selected drawing: figure 6.

raw: a convertible container assembly comprising a convertible container body. the container body includes at least one angular conversion demarcation, defining an angular conversion section, and, optionally, at least one storage conversion demarcation, defining a storage conversion section. the angular conversion section includes a portion of a sidewall of the container body and a bottom portion of the container body. the angular conversion section toggles between an expanded configuration having a common horizontal upper rim and a compressed configuration creating a new container body support surface, angling the upper rim. the depth conversion section toggles between an expanded configuration as a container and a compressed configuration collapsing the container for storage. the conversion section can employ a single section or a series of sections, wherein the series of sections enable an accordion styled formation when collapsed.
rewritten: a convertible container assembly comprises a container body that is designed to change configuration. the body features an angular conversion demarcation that defines an angular conversion section, and it may also include a storage conversion demarcation that defines a storage conversion section. the angular conversion section incorporates a part of the container’s sidewall along with a portion of its bottom. in its expanded state, this section forms a common horizontal upper rim; when compressed, it creates a new support surface by angling the upper rim. similarly, the depth (or storage) conversion section can switch between an expanded configuration, functioning as a container, and a compressed configuration that collapses the container for storage. this conversion feature can be implemented as a single section or as a series of sections, with the latter allowing for an accordion-like collapse.

raw: the invention provides an l-shaped sub-array utilization method, wherein the l-shaped sub-array is provided with four antenna units. the l-shaped sub-array utilization method comprises the following steps of 1), establishing a square antenna sub-array module; 2) establishing a module equipment database; 3), splicing for forming an objective antenna array; and 4) defining the phase center of a single l-shaped sub-array. compared with the prior art, the l-shaped sub-array utilization method is advantageous in that the boundary rule of the square antenna sub-array module which is composed of four l-shaped sub-arrays facilitates mounting of various planar arrays and realizes easy engineering realization; combination of ten splicing methods of the square antenna sub-array module and the selection mode for the phase center of the l-shaped sub-array can obtain an array antenna phase center distribution with high randomness, thereby greatly weakening an array antenna radiation pattern grating lobe; and the square antenna sub-array module has a small scale and can be conveniently assembled for forming the planar arrays in different forms, thereby realizing non-single array surface form and wide application range.
rewritten: the invention introduces an l-shaped sub-array utilization method, where each l-shaped sub-array includes four antenna units. this method involves the following steps: 1. creating a square antenna sub-array module. 2. developing a module equipment database. 3. splicing the modules to form a target antenna array. 4. defining the phase center of an individual l-shaped sub-array. compared to previous methods, this approach offers several advantages. first, the square antenna sub-array module, composed of four l-shaped sub-arrays, adheres to a clear boundary rule that simplifies the mounting of various planar arrays and facilitates straightforward engineering implementation. second, by combining ten splicing techniques for the square antenna sub-array module with a robust selection method for the phase center of the l-shaped sub-array, the resulting array exhibits a phase center distribution with high randomness, significantly reducing the grating lobes in the antenna radiation pattern. finally, the compact size of the square antenna sub-array module makes it easy to assemble in various configurations to form different types of planar arrays, supporting diverse surface forms and a broad range of applications.

raw: case description: a 77 year-old male presented with acute-onset, sharp, shooting pain in his left posterior occiput. the pain attacks lasted seconds and occurred multiple times daily without an obvious trigger. there were no associated infectious or neurological symptoms. palpation just posterior to the mastoid process triggered a sharp pain that radiated up to the top of the head. a head ct showed age-appropriate small vessel disease. a lesser occipital nerve block with 1ml 0.5[percnt] bupivacaine and 40mg methylprednisolone was performed for suspected lesser occipital neuralgia, which resulted in complete pain relief. two weeks later, he denied occipital pain, but developed gradual onset bilateral fronto-temporal headache, jaw tightness, horizontal diplopia, as well as low-grade fevers. his left temporal scalp was tender, and left temporal artery pulse was absent. ophthalmic examination showed abduction deficit of the left eye causing horizontal diplopia upon left gaze, consistent with left 6th nerve palsy. both optic discs were normal on dilated eye exam, but automated perimetry showed a right partial inferior arcuate defect, concordant with superior-temporal retinal nerve fiber layer thinning on oct, suggestive of posterior ischemic optic neuropathy. esr was elevated at 80 mm/h. the patient was started on prednisone 60mg daily for giant cell arteritis. ultrasound evaluation of his head and neck showed significant focal stenosis and perivascular halos in multiple facial arteries consistent with giant cell arteritis. one month later, the patient had complete resolution of his symptoms and the esr decreased to 7 mm/h. giant cell arteritis can affect any vessel in the scalp therefore initial symptoms are not confined to the temples or jaw. this presentation showed lesser occipital neuralgia caused by left occipital arteritis as the presenting symptom of giant cell arteritis, and responded to localized steroid injection by decreasing arterial inflammation. disclosure: dr. marshall has nothing to disclose. dr. vardeh has nothing to disclose.
rewritten: case description: a 77-year-old man experienced a sudden onset of sharp, shooting pain in the left back of his head. the brief pain attacks, each lasting only seconds, occurred multiple times daily without any clear trigger and without accompanying infectious or neurological symptoms. on examination, palpating the area just behind his mastoid process provoked a sharp pain that spread upward toward the crown of his head. a head ct scan revealed small vessel disease consistent with his age. with a suspected diagnosis of lesser occipital neuralgia, a nerve block using 1 ml of 0.5% bupivacaine combined with 40 mg of methylprednisolone was administered, which completely relieved his pain. however, two weeks later, while he no longer reported occipital pain, he developed a gradually worsening bilateral fronto-temporal headache, jaw tightness, horizontal double vision, and low-grade fevers. his left temple was notably tender, and his left temporal artery pulse was absent. ophthalmologic examination revealed an abduction deficit in the left eye, resulting in horizontal diplopia during left gaze, findings that were consistent with a left sixth cranial nerve palsy. although both optic discs appeared normal on dilated exam, automated perimetry detected a right-sided partial inferior arcuate defect that correlated with superior-temporal retinal nerve fiber layer thinning on oct—suggestive of posterior ischemic optic neuropathy. an elevated esr of 80 mm/h further supported an inflammatory process. given these findings, the patient was started on prednisone 60 mg daily for giant cell arteritis. ultrasound imaging of the head and neck showed significant, focal stenosis and perivascular halos in several facial arteries, confirming the diagnosis. one month after starting treatment, his symptoms had completely resolved and his esr had dropped to 7 mm/h. giant cell arteritis can involve any scalp vessel, so early symptoms may not be limited to the temples or jaw. in this case, the patient’s initial presentation of lesser occipital neuralgia, stemming from left occipital arteritis, ultimately revealed underlying giant cell arteritis. his response to a localized steroid injection underscored the role of arterial inflammation in his symptoms. disclosure: dr. marshall has nothing to disclose. dr. vardeh has nothing to disclose.

raw: nicotine is the most abundant alkaloid in tobacco accounting for 95% of the alkaloid content. there are also several minor tobacco alkaloids; among these are nornicotine, anatabine, and anabasine. we developed and applied a 96 well plate-based capillary lc-tandem mass spectrometry method for the analysis of nornicotine, anatabine, and anabasine in urine. the method was validated with regard to accuracy and precision. anabasine was quantifiable to low levels with a limit of quantitation (loq) of 0.2 ng/ml even when nicotine, which is isobaric, is present at concentrations >2500-fold higher than anabasine. this attribute of the method is important since anatabine and anabasine in urine have been proposed as biomarkers of tobacco use for individuals using nicotine replacement therapies. in the present study, we analyzed the three minor tobacco alkaloids in urine from 827 smokers with a wide range of tobacco exposures. nornicotine (loq 0.6 ng/ml) was detected in all samples, and anatabine (loq, 0.15 ng/ml) an...
rewritten: nicotine is the primary alkaloid present in tobacco, making up 95% of its alkaloid content. tobacco also contains several minor alkaloids, including nornicotine, anatabine, and anabasine. we developed and implemented a method using a 96-well plate-based capillary lc-tandem mass spectrometry approach to analyze nornicotine, anatabine, and anabasine in urine. this method was rigorously validated for both accuracy and precision. notably, anabasine could be quantified at very low levels, with a limit of quantitation (loq) of 0.2 ng/ml, even in the presence of nicotine—an isobaric compound—at concentrations more than 2,500 times higher than that of anabasine. this capability is significant because anatabine and anabasine in urine have been suggested as biomarkers of tobacco use in individuals utilizing nicotine replacement therapies. in this study, we measured the three minor tobacco alkaloids in the urine of 827 smokers who exhibited a wide range of tobacco exposure levels. nornicotine, with an loq of 0.6 ng/ml, was detected in every sample, while anatabine (loq 0.15 ng/ml) and anabasine were analyzed similarly.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a portable storage device capable of allowing a user to check remaining capacity by shaking the portable storage device while eliminating erroneous operation like pushing a remaining capacity button.solution: a controller 120 controls a vibration detection unit 114 to detect vibrations. an remaining capacity detection unit 113 notifies a detection result of remaining capacity in a storage unit 112 to a notification part (notification sound storage 115 and sound emitting section 116). with this, the remaining capacity of the storage unit 112 can be checked by shaking a usb memory 100 as the portable storage device.selected drawing: figure 3
