rewritten: challenge: develop a portable storage device that lets users check remaining capacity simply by shaking it, while preventing accidental activations such as unintended presses of a capacity check button. solution: a controller (120) manages a vibration detection unit (114) to sense shaking. meanwhile, a remaining capacity detection unit (113) relays the current storage capacity of a storage unit (112) to a notification module, which includes both a notification sound storage (115) and a sound emitting section (116). as a result, by shaking a usb memory device (100), a user can easily determine the available storage capacity. selected drawing: figure 3.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a shape measurement device, shape measurement method and point sensor alignment means that allow a detection point of a point sensor to be easily and quickly aligned with a measurement start point.solution: alignment means comprises two or more laser light sources 231 and 232 that are disposed around a point sensor 200, and laser light la from the two or more laser light sources 231 and 232 crosses at an adjustment point pc further separated from the point sensor 200 by a prescribed distance from a detection point ps. the alignment means is configured to: align the adjustment point pc with a desired measurement start point pm on a work w; cause the point sensor 200 to get closer to the work w by the prescribed distance; and start a measurement scanning of the work w.selected drawing: figure 5
rewritten: objective: provide a device, method, and sensor alignment mechanism for shape measurement that enables a point sensor’s detection point to be quickly and easily aligned with a designated measurement start point. solution: the alignment mechanism includes two or more laser light sources (231 and 232) arranged around a point sensor (200). the laser beams from these sources intersect at an adjustment point (pc), which is located a specified distance from the sensor's detection point (ps). the mechanism is designed to: 1. align the adjustment point (pc) with the desired measurement start point (pm) on the workpiece (w), 2. move the point sensor (200) closer to the workpiece by the predetermined distance, and 3. initiate the scanning measurement on the workpiece. reference drawing: figure 5.

raw: the moquah barrens research natural area (rna) was established by the chequamegon national forest and the lakes states forest experiment station in 1935 with a research objective well-suited to the needs of the forest service and the scientific understanding of ecosystem function prevalent at the time of establishment. the original research plan was never implemented, which led to a joint forest-station decision in 1956 to disestablish the rna. however, that decision was never implemented. a series of management decisions made after 1956 led to the loss of the pine barrens ecosystem originally encompassed by the rna. this loss is not irretrievable and the work necessary to recover the original ecosystem is possible under existing rna management guidelines. the experience of the moquah barrens rna can be used by the forest service to improve overall management of the entire system of research natural areas. two main areas of opportunity are identified: 1) implement an improved approach to managing official records associated with rnas; and 2) adopt a management framework suitable for long-term ecological projects.
rewritten: the moquah barrens research natural area (rna) was established in 1935 by the chequamegon national forest and the lakes states forest experiment station with a research goal that matched both the needs of the forest service and the contemporary scientific understanding of ecosystem functions. although an initial research plan was developed, it was never put into practice, which led to a joint decision in 1956 by the forest and the experiment station to disestablish the rna—though that decision was never carried out. subsequent management choices made after 1956 ultimately resulted in the loss of the pine barrens ecosystem that the rna originally aimed to preserve. however, this ecosystem loss is not permanent; restoring it is possible under the current rna management guidelines. the experience with the moquah barrens rna offers the forest service valuable lessons that can enhance the management of all research natural areas. two key opportunities have been identified: first, to implement a better system for managing official records related to rnas, and second, to establish a management framework that supports long-term ecological projects.

raw: the invention discloses a data importing method based on a spark-sql big data processing platform. according to the method, data is imported into the spark-sql big data processing platform through a warehousing program, wherein the warehousing program comprises a partitioning part, a data compression part and a write-in hdfs part, and the portioning part, the data compression part and the write-in hdfs part respectively program with c language and compile and link to form an optimized target code warehousing program. the method uses the c language to realize partitioned logic, supports a plurality of partitions, and supports users to customize a partitioning format. with the data importing method provided by the invention, a warehousing speed is improved greatly, by practical test for general sample data, a comparison test is carried out on a normal server platform, an original warehousing program speed is 20000 pieces/second, and the warehousing program speed optimized and modified by the method provided by the invention can be 190000 pieces/second. for such improvement of the warehousing speed, the main reason is that the program is rewritten by the c language, and efficiency is greatly faster than that of a jvm mode.
rewritten: the invention introduces a method for importing data into a spark-sql big data processing platform. in this method, data is imported using a warehousing program that includes three components: a partitioning module, a data compression module, and a module for writing to hdfs. each of these modules is developed in c and then compiled and linked to produce an optimized target code warehousing program. by implementing partition logic in c, the method supports multiple partitions and allows users to define custom partition formats. testing with standard sample data on a typical server platform showed that while the original warehousing program achieved a speed of 20,000 pieces per second, the optimized program reached 190,000 pieces per second. this significant improvement in warehousing speed is mainly because the program was rewritten in c, which offers much higher efficiency compared to a jvm-based approach.

raw: a new approach for optical path switch interconnect network is proposed. in the proposed network, optical switches are controlled locally without communications with a centralized controller, which offers high affinity with existing packet-based interconnect networks.
rewritten: a novel optical path switch interconnect network is introduced. in this design, optical switches are managed locally rather than through a centralized controller, ensuring strong compatibility with existing packet-based interconnect networks.

raw: acute pharmacological activation of adenosine monophosphate (amp)-kinase using 5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide-1-b-d-ribofuranoside (aicar) has been shown to improve muscle mitochondrial function by increasing mitochondrial biogenesis. we asked whether prolonged aicar treatment is beneficial in a mouse model of slowly progressing mitochondrial myopathy (cox10-mef2c-cre), and whether the compensatory mechanism is indeed an increase in mitochondrial biogenesis. we treated the animals for 3 months and found that sustained amp-dependent kinase activation improved cytochrome c oxidase activity, rescued the motor phenotype and delayed the onset of the myopathy. this improvement was observed whether treatment started before or after the onset of the disease. we found that aicar increased skeletal muscle regeneration thereby decreasing the levels of deleted cox10-floxed alleles. we conclude that although increase in mitochondrial biogenesis and other pathways may contribute, the main mechanism by which aicar improves the myopathy phenotype is by promoting muscle regeneration.
rewritten: acute pharmacological activation of amp-kinase with 5-aminoimidazole-4-carboxamide-1-β-d-ribofuranoside (aicar) has been demonstrated to enhance muscle mitochondrial function by boosting mitochondrial biogenesis. in our study, we investigated whether long-term aicar treatment would be beneficial in a mouse model of slowly progressing mitochondrial myopathy (cox10-mef2c-cre) and if its compensatory mechanism indeed involved an increase in mitochondrial biogenesis. we treated the animals for three months and observed that sustained amp-kinase activation not only improved cytochrome c oxidase activity and rescued the motor phenotype but also delayed the onset of the myopathy. these benefits were evident regardless of whether treatment began before or after the disease manifested. additionally, we discovered that aicar enhanced skeletal muscle regeneration, leading to a reduction in the levels of deleted cox10-floxed alleles. therefore, although increased mitochondrial biogenesis and other pathways may contribute to the improvement, our findings suggest that the primary mechanism by which aicar ameliorates the myopathy phenotype is through the promotion of muscle regeneration.

raw: in this paper, a terminal sliding mode (tsm) controller is designed for boost converter with purpose of robustness and better response against circuit nonlinearities and disturbances. instead of using the commonly used average model or switching model for current-mode controlled (cmc) boost converter, a novel energy storage function (esf) model is proposed for tsm controller, which contains all of the nonlinear elements of inductor current and capacitor voltage and further converges the both to the zero, unlike the cmc control ignoring the transient control of capacitor voltage. by adopting tsm instead of the traditional linear sliding mode (lsm), the system can track the given output voltage with faster response due to its finite time convergence. meanwhile, an exponential reaching law is designed for tsm controller to eliminate the unexpected chattering phenomenon. simulation results with comparison of the traditional cmc lsm control can prove the better control performance of the proposed scheme.
rewritten: this paper proposes a terminal sliding mode (tsm) controller for a boost converter to achieve robustness and enhanced response against circuit nonlinearities and disturbances. instead of employing the conventional average or switching models typically used for current-mode controlled (cmc) boost converters, a new energy storage function (esf) model is introduced for the tsm controller. this model captures all the nonlinear aspects of both the inductor current and capacitor voltage, ensuring that both converge to zero—unlike cmc control, which overlooks the transient behavior of capacitor voltage. by using tsm rather than traditional linear sliding mode (lsm), the system can track the desired output voltage more rapidly due to its finite-time convergence property. an exponential reaching law is also incorporated into the tsm controller to prevent unexpected chattering. comparative simulation results, alongside those obtained using conventional cmc lsm control, demonstrate the superior control performance of the proposed method.

raw: problem of calculating transition band sample value (transition coefficient) of low pass frequency sampling filters is considered. the analytical expressions for calculating transition coefficient based on minimum of the sum of squared frequency response values in selected points in the stopband are obtained. in this case, the value of maximum difference between resultant frequency response and desired one in the stopband is close to minimum.
rewritten: the problem of determining the transition band sample value (also called the transition coefficient) for low-pass frequency sampling filters is examined. analytical expressions have been derived to calculate the transition coefficient by minimizing the sum of the squared frequency response values at selected stopband points. this method ensures that the maximum deviation between the resulting and desired frequency responses in the stopband is nearly minimized.

raw: the purpose of this note is to point out analogies between causal analysis in statistics and the correlation-response theory in statistical physics. it is further shown that for some systems the dynamic cavity offers a way to compute the stationary state of a non-equilibrium process effectively, which could then be taken an alternative starting point of causal analysis.
rewritten: this note aims to highlight the similarities between causal analysis in statistics and correlation-response theory in statistical physics. it is also demonstrated that, for certain systems, the dynamic cavity method can effectively compute the stationary state of a non-equilibrium process, offering an alternative foundation for causal analysis.

raw: the utility model discloses a relate to hospital information -based technical field, especially an infusion alarm system of hospital suitable for extensive deployment. it comprises backend server module, base station module and infusion control appearance module, the backend server module is passed through the cable network and is connected a plurality of base station module, and base station module passes through a plurality of infusions of wireless network connection control appearance module, infusion control appearance module mainly comprises transfer line, liquid level supervision module, transfer line gate control module, microprocessor unit module and wireless communication module, the liquid level keep watch on module and transfer line gate control module parallel connection on the transfer line, the liquid level is kept watch on module and transfer line gate control module's output and is being connected the microprocessor unit module, microprocessor unit module connection wireless communication module and process are reversible. after adopting above -mentioned structure, can simplify the network, reduce cost can also realize the safe transmission and the long distance conveying of big data.
rewritten: this utility model is related to the field of hospital information and specifically discloses an infusion alarm system designed for wide-scale implementation in hospitals. the system consists of a backend server module, a base station module, and an infusion control appearance module. the backend server module is connected to several base station modules through a cable network, while each base station module links to multiple infusion control appearance modules via a wireless network. the infusion control appearance module primarily includes a transfer line, a liquid level monitoring module, a transfer line gate control module, a microprocessor unit module, and a wireless communication module. the liquid level monitoring module and the transfer line gate control module are connected in parallel on the transfer line, with their outputs interfaced to the microprocessor unit module. the microprocessor unit module is connected, in turn, to the wireless communication module, allowing a reversible process. this design simplifies the network, reduces costs, and ensures the secure, long-distance transmission of large volumes of data.

raw: the utility model relates to a burnisher field, concretely relates to multi -purpose clean dustpan. including dustpan body and handheld pole, the bottom of dustpan body is equipped with front wheel, rear wheel, the dustpan body is fixed with the clean wheel that is located dustpan mouth department through the connecting rod, the wheel areal distribution of clean wheel has clean, clean wheel is rotated by front wheel or rear wheel drive. the utility model discloses structural design is ingenious, and low cost can accomplish the function of cleaning with splendid attire rubbish simultaneously, also can prevent to stir up a cloud of dust and a shredded paper bits condition of wafing, is convenient for transport rubbish in addition and prevents that rubbish from falling out.
rewritten: the present utility model falls within the cleaning device field and specifically relates to a multi-purpose cleaning dustpan. the device comprises a dustpan body and a handheld pole. at the bottom of the dustpan body, both a front wheel and a rear wheel are attached. a cleaning wheel is fixed at the dustpan mouth area via a connecting rod; this cleaning wheel features a specific distribution of cleaning elements and is rotated through the driving action of either the front or rear wheel. the design is ingenious and cost-effective, enabling the simultaneous cleaning and collection of various types of waste. moreover, it prevents the stirring up of dust clouds and scattered fragments, while also facilitating the convenient transportation of the waste without it falling out.

raw: the subject matter of the invention is a series spark gap comprising at least a first spark gap (fs1) comprising a first main electrode (fsa1) and a second main electrode (fsa2) and a second spark gap (fs2) comprising a first main electrode (fsa3) and a second main electrode (fsa4), wherein the second main electrode (fsa2) of the first spark gap (fs1) is connected in series with the first main electrode (fsa3) of the second spark gap (fs2), wherein each of the spark gaps (fs1, fs2) has an auxiliary striking electrode (h1, h2), wherein the auxiliary striking electrodes (h1, h2) are connected to one another via a circuit (zk), wherein the first main electrode (fsa1) of the first spark gap (fs1) and the second main electrode (fsa4) of the second spark gap (fs2) are provided as connections (a1, a2) for the series spark gap.
rewritten: the invention relates to a series spark gap that includes at least a first spark gap (fs1) with a first main electrode (fsa1) and a second main electrode (fsa2), as well as a second spark gap (fs2) having a first main electrode (fsa3) and a second main electrode (fsa4). in this configuration, the second main electrode (fsa2) of the first spark gap is connected in series with the first main electrode (fsa3) of the second spark gap. each spark gap (fs1 and fs2) is also equipped with an auxiliary striking electrode (h1 and h2, respectively), and these auxiliary striking electrodes are interconnected by a circuit (zk). moreover, the first main electrode (fsa1) of the first spark gap and the second main electrode (fsa4) of the second spark gap serve as the connection points (a1 and a2) for the series spark gap.

raw: semiconductor packages with interconnects having passivation thereon is disclosed. the passivation layer may be any suitable dielectric material that may overlie a build-up dielectric layer and metal traces of an interconnect layer in a semiconductor package. via holes may be formed in the build-up dielectric and the passivation layer may be removed from the bottom of the via hole. by removing the passivation layer at the bottom of the via hole, any residual build-up dielectric may also be removed from the bottom of the via hole. thus removal of the residual build-up dielectric may not require a desmear process that would otherwise roughen metal and/or dielectric surfaces. the resulting smoother metal and/or dielectric surfaces enabled by the use of the passivation layer may allow greater process latitude and/or flexibility to fabricate relatively smaller dimensional interconnect features and/or relatively improved signaling frequency and integrity.
rewritten: a semiconductor package is described that includes interconnects coated with a passivation layer. this layer can be any appropriate dielectric material covering both the build-up dielectric layer and the metal traces of an interconnect layer. via holes are formed in the build-up dielectric, and the passivation layer is subsequently removed from the bottom of these holes. by doing so, any residual build-up dielectric at the base of the via hole is also removed. this process eliminates the need for a desmear process, which would otherwise roughen the metal or dielectric surfaces. as a result, the smoother surfaces achieved through the use of the passivation layer offer greater process latitude, enabling the fabrication of smaller interconnect features and enhancing signaling frequency and integrity.

raw: the application aims to provide a method and a device for determining a search result. specifically, the method comprises: obtaining segmented words associated with one or more business objects; establishing a first reverse index database of one or more business objects according to the segmented words; configuring sku attribute information for one or more business objects; establishing a second reverse index database of one or more business objects according to the sku attribute information for one or more business objects; receiving a query sequence of a user; obtaining a first search result in the first reverse index database according to at least one segmented word in the query sequence; extracting the sku attribute information in the query sequence, and obtaining a second search result in the second reverse index database according to at least one piece of extracted sku attribute information; and determining a target search result according to the first search result and the second search result. compared with the prior art, the method and the device greatly improve the search efficiency and enhance the user experience.
rewritten: the application introduces a method and device for producing search results. in summary, the method involves the following steps: obtaining segmented words related to one or more business objects; building an initial reverse index database for these business objects based on the segmented words; setting up sku attribute information for the business objects; and constructing a second reverse index database using this sku attribute information. next, the method receives a user’s query sequence and retrieves a first search result from the first reverse index database based on at least one segmented word within the query. it then extracts the sku attribute information from the query sequence and obtains a second search result from the second reverse index database using this extracted information. finally, it determines the final target search result by combining the first and second search results. compared to earlier techniques, this method and device greatly enhance search efficiency and improve user experience.

raw: the invention discloses a detachable crack-resistant and antitheft all-wood door. by the detachable crack-resistant and antitheft all-wood door, the problems that veneers which are widely used in building materials in the prior art deform, warp and crack easily, and the like are solved. the detachable crack-resistant and antitheft all-wood door comprises core plates, door edges, transverse beams and vertical beams, wherein the core plates are positioned in the middle of the all-wood door; the door edges are positioned on two sides of the core plates; the transverse beams are positioned at two ends of the core plates; and the door edges, the transverse beams, the vertical beams and the core plates are fastened by grooves, projections, wooden dowel pins and hammer screws, when various materials are connected, natural seams exist, steel plates are added in the core plates and the vertical beams, a certain space exists when the various materials expand or contract, the plates are prevented from cracking and deforming, and the antitheft effect is achieved.
rewritten: this invention presents a detachable, crack-resistant, and antitheft all-wood door. it solves the common problems found in traditional building veneers—such as deformation, warping, and cracking—by employing a unique design. the door is composed of core plates, door edges, transverse beams, and vertical beams. here, the core plates are centrally located, with door edges arranged on either side, and the transverse beams positioned at the ends of the core plates. the door's components are interconnected using grooves, projections, wooden dowel pins, and hammer screws. additionally, when various materials join, natural seams may form; however, by incorporating steel plates within the core plates and vertical beams, and allowing a slight gap for expansion or contraction, the design effectively prevents cracking and deformation while also providing an antitheft function.

raw: the invention relates to a chicken feed for improving the quality of eggs. the chicken feed is prepared through mixing 55-60% of corn, 5-10% of soybean meal, 15-20% of peanut cakes, 1-3% of bone meal, 3-5% of seaweed meal, 0.5-2% of folium eucommiae powder, 8-10% of stone flour, 0.3% of edible salt and 1-3% of a premix. compared with common feed, the chicken feed used to feed laying hens improves the laying rate by 5%, reduces the death and culling rate by 7.2%, improves the egg-feed ratio by 10.9%, improves the economic income by 6.1%, improves the quality of eggs, makes the appearance of the eggs be ruddy and bright, reduces the phenomena of rough spots and thin shells of eggs laid by old hens, and reduces the raw smell of the eggs.
rewritten: this invention concerns a chicken feed specifically designed to enhance egg quality. the feed is produced by blending the following ingredients in these proportions: 55-60% corn, 5-10% soybean meal, 15-20% peanut cakes, 1-3% bone meal, 3-5% seaweed meal, 0.5-2% folium eucommiae powder, 8-10% stone flour, 0.3% edible salt, and 1-3% premix. when fed to laying hens, this specialized feed increases the laying rate by 5%, lowers the death and culling rate by 7.2%, and improves the egg-feed ratio by 10.9%, ultimately boosting economic returns by 6.1%. additionally, it enhances egg quality by giving them a bright, ruddy appearance, reducing the occurrence of rough spots and thin shells in eggs laid by older hens, and diminishing any raw odor.

raw: the application discloses a signal generating, sending and receiving method and device based on a filter set. on one hand, a signal generating device is provided and comprises a cs-dft expansion unit used for carrying out the conjugate symmetry discrete fourier transform (dft) expansion operation on an inputted first complex symbolic data stream to generate two symbolic data streams; a subcarrier distribution unit used for distributing the two symbolic data streams to the subcarriers respectively; and an offset quadrature amplitude modulation (oqam) modulator used for carrying out the oqam on the symbolic data streams distributed to the subcarriers to generate an oqam signal. according to the embodiment of the application, the own characteristics of a filter bank multiple carrier (fbmc)/oqam system are kept, and a peak-to-average power ratio (papr) performance of the fbmc/oqam system approximates the papr performance of the single carrier transmission.
rewritten: the disclosed application presents a method and device for generating, transmitting, and receiving signals using a filter set. in one aspect, the signal generating device includes: • a cs-dft expansion unit that applies a conjugate symmetry discrete fourier transform (dft) expansion on an input first complex symbolic data stream to produce two symbolic data streams; • a subcarrier distribution unit that assigns these two symbolic data streams to specific subcarriers; and • an offset quadrature amplitude modulation (oqam) modulator that performs oqam on the data streams assigned to the subcarriers, thereby generating an oqam signal. according to the embodiment described, the inherent characteristics of a filter bank multicarrier (fbmc)/oqam system are preserved, and the peak-to-average power ratio (papr) performance of the fbmc/oqam system comes close to that of a single carrier transmission.

raw:
an updated set of (anti)neutrino-nucleon charged and neutral current cross sections at $3~{m gev} \lesssim e_
u \lesssim 100~{m gev}$ is presented. these cross sections are of particular interest for the detector optimization and data processing and interpretation in the future megaton-scale experiments like pingu, orca, and hyper-kamiokande. finite masses of charged leptons and target mass corrections in exclusive and deep inelastic $( ar
u)
u n$ interactions are taken into account. a new set of qcd nnlo parton density functions, the abmp15, is used for calculation of the dis cross sections. the sensitivity of the cross sections to phenomenological parameters and to extrapolations of the nucleon structure functions to small $x$ and $q^2$ is studied. an agreement within the uncertainties of our calculations with experimental data is demonstrated.
rewritten: this work presents a revised set of (anti)neutrino-nucleon charged and neutral current cross sections for energies between approximately 3 gev and 100 gev. these cross sections are especially important for optimizing detectors, as well as for processing and interpreting data in future megaton-scale experiments like pingu, orca, and hyper-kamiokande. in the analysis, finite charged lepton masses and target mass corrections in both exclusive and deep inelastic (anti)neutrino–nucleon interactions are considered. additionally, the calculations of the deep inelastic scattering (dis) cross sections utilize a new set of qcd nnlo parton density functions, known as abmp15. the study also examines how sensitive the cross sections are to phenomenological parameters and to the extrapolations of nucleon structure functions to low x and q² values. finally, the results are shown to be in agreement with experimental data within the calculated uncertainties.

raw: the invention discloses an informatization bidding system being convenient for product management. the informatization bidding system being convenient for product management includes a basic information management module, a tendering procurement module, a competitive bidding procurement module, a negotiation procurement module and a direct procurement module, wherein each module can be connected with a first party client and a second party client through network, and check the client information of the first party client and the second party client, and judges each information; and then the each information enters the corresponding module; a product list management module is connected with the basic information management module through the network; the product list management module is used for setting a product list of a unit, and setting a product list release management flow and a product list browse operation authority management flow; and for the products which are frequently purchased, a shortcut list can also be automatically generated. the informatization bidding system being convenient for product management sets the product list management module to manage the product list, thus being able to help customers to update the product list in real time, guaranteeing the advancement of the product list, and being more aligned with the market requirement.
rewritten: the invention introduces an informatization bidding system designed to simplify product management. this system comprises several modules, including a basic information management module, a tendering procurement module, a competitive bidding procurement module, a negotiation procurement module, and a direct procurement module. each of these modules is network-connected to both first party and second party clients, enabling the system to retrieve and evaluate client information before directing each piece of data to its corresponding module. additionally, the system features a product list management module that connects to the basic information management module over the network. this module is responsible for configuring a unit’s product list, establishing a workflow for releasing and browsing the product list, and for items that are purchased frequently, an automatic shortcut list can be generated. by using this product list management module, the system helps customers update their product lists in real time, ensuring they remain current and better aligned with market demands.

raw: the integral process of depositing thin layers of material, one after another, until the designed component is created is collectively referred to as additive manufacturing (am). fused deposition process (fdp) is a type of am where feedstock is extruded into filaments which then are deposited by 3d printing, and the solidification occurs during cooling of the melt. currently, complex structures are being fabricated by commercial and open source desktop 3d printers. recently, metal powder containing composite filaments based on polylactic acid (pla) and acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (abs) have emerged, which could be utilized for multifunctional applications. for further deployment in the field, especially for aerospace and ground-based applications, it is critical to understand the tribological behavior of 3d printed materials. in this presentation, we will report the tribological behavior of different polymer matrix composites fabricated by fused deposition process. these results will be compared with the base polymer systems. during this study, the tribological behavior of all the samples will be evaluated with tab-on-disc method and compared for different metallic powder reinforcements.
rewritten: additive manufacturing (am) is the process of successively depositing thin layers of material until a fully designed component is built. one variant of am is the fused deposition process (fdp), where feedstock material is extruded into filaments that are subsequently deposited through 3d printing, with solidification occurring as the melt cools. today, both commercial and open-source desktop 3d printers are capable of fabricating complex structures. recently, composite filaments that incorporate metal powders into polymer matrices such as polylactic acid (pla) and acrylonitrile butadiene styrene (abs) have emerged, opening up possibilities for multifunctional applications. for further adoption in fields such as aerospace and ground-based engineering, it is essential to understand the tribological behavior of these 3d printed materials. in this presentation, we will examine the tribological characteristics of various polymer matrix composites produced via the fused deposition process and compare these findings with those from the base polymer systems. the study will employ the tab-on-disc method to evaluate and compare the tribological performance of all samples with different metallic powder reinforcements.

raw: experiments were conducted for two successive summer seasons 2004 and 2005 at the horticultural field of hudeiba agricultural research station out to investigate the effect of five tillage systems and two irrigation water regimes on some soil physical properties. the studied tillage systems were, disc plow with 20cm plowing depth, chisel plow with 20cm plowing depth, oxen driven plow with 15- 20cm plowing depth, disc harrow with 10 cm plowing depth and no tillage. the water regimes were 100% and 75% of crop water requirement. generally disc plowing and chisel plowing were the most effective tillage on soil parameters. the highest resistance to penetration after tillage treatments was recorded by disc harrow, 6.9 kg/cm 2 at 10cm depth. the soil resistance after harvest with plots with 75% of crop water requirement was found to be 8.8 kg/ cm 2 for the disc plow, 8.6 kg/ cm 2 for the chisel plow, 9.2 kg/cm 2 for the disc harrow, 6.5 kg/ cm 2 for the oxen driven plow and 9.3 kg/cm2 for no tillage. the soil penetration resistance after harvest for plots with 100% crop water requirement was found to be 8.6 kg/ cm 2 for the disc plow, 4.5 kg/ cm 2 for chisel plow, 15.2 kg/ cm 2 for the disc harrow, 3.9 kg/ cm 2 for the oxen driven plow and 6.5 kg/cm 2 for no tillage. the bulk densities after conventional tillage treatments were generally lower than the minimum tillage treatments. disc ploughing recorded 0.9 gmcm -3 at soil depth 0-15 cm while chisel recorded 1.01 cm -3 at the same depth. the differences between treatments at the upper two depths 0-15 cm & 15-30 cm were significant at 5% level
rewritten: experiments were carried out over two consecutive summer seasons (2004 and 2005) at the hudeiba agricultural research station's horticultural field to examine how five tillage systems and two irrigation water regimes affect various soil physical properties. the tillage systems studied included a disc plow (20 cm plowing depth), a chisel plow (20 cm plowing depth), an oxen-driven plow (15–20 cm plowing depth), a disc harrow (10 cm plowing depth), and a no-tillage system. the irrigation regimes applied were equivalent to 100% and 75% of the crop water requirement. in general, disc and chisel plowing were the most effective in influencing the soil parameters. after the tillage treatments, the highest penetration resistance was observed with the disc harrow, recording 6.9 kg/cm² at a depth of 10 cm. after harvest, under the 75% water regime, soil penetration resistance values were 8.8 kg/cm² for the disc plow, 8.6 kg/cm² for the chisel plow, 9.2 kg/cm² for the disc harrow, 6.5 kg/cm² for the oxen-driven plow, and 9.3 kg/cm² for the no-tillage plots. under the 100% water regime, the corresponding figures were 8.6 kg/cm² for the disc plow, 4.5 kg/cm² for the chisel plow, 15.2 kg/cm² for the disc harrow, 3.9 kg/cm² for the oxen-driven plow, and 6.5 kg/cm² for the no-tillage system. additionally, conventional tillage treatments generally resulted in lower bulk densities compared to minimum tillage treatments. specifically, disc plowing recorded a bulk density of 0.9 g/cm³ at the 0–15 cm soil depth, while the chisel plow recorded 1.01 g/cm³ at the same depth. the differences among treatments in the upper soil layers (0–15 cm and 15–30 cm) were statistically significant at the 5% level.

raw: porous metals can be created through a wide variety of processing techniques, and the pore morphology resulting from these processes is equally diverse. the structural and functional properties of metal foams are directly dependent on the size, shape, interconnectedness and volume fraction of pores, so accurately quantifying the pore characteristics is of great importance. methods for analyzing porous materials are presented here and applied to a copper-based metallic foam generated through solid state foaming via oxide reduction and expansion. this process results in large voids (10s of microns) between sintered particles and small pores (10 microns to less than 50 nm) within particles. optical and electron microscopy were used to image the porosity over this wide range, and the pore characteristics were quantified using image segmentation and statistical analysis. two-dimensional pore analysis was performed using the chan-vese method, and two-point correlation and lineal path functions were used to assess three-dimensional reconstructions from fib tomography. two-dimensional analysis reveals distinct size and morphological differences in porosity between particles and within them. three-dimensional analysis adds further information on the high level interconnectedness of the porosity and irregular shape it takes, forming tortuous pathways rather than spherical cells. mechanical polishing and optical microscopy allow large areas to be created and analyzed quickly, but methods such as focused ion beam (fib) sectioning can provide additional insight about microstructural features. in particular, after fib milling is used to create a flat surface, that surface can be analyzed for structural and compositional information.
rewritten: porous metals can be produced using a wide range of processing techniques, resulting in equally diverse pore structures. the structural and functional properties of metal foams depend directly on the size, shape, connectivity, and volume fraction of the pores, making accurate quantification of these characteristics critically important. in this work, various methods for analyzing porous materials are described and then applied to a copper-based metallic foam produced by solid-state foaming through oxide reduction and expansion. this process creates large voids—on the order of tens of microns—between sintered particles, as well as smaller pores ranging from around 10 microns down to less than 50 nanometers within the particles. optical and electron microscopy were used to capture images of the porosity over this broad range, and image segmentation along with statistical analysis was employed to quantify the pore features. two-dimensional pore analysis was carried out using the chan-vese method, while two-point correlation and lineal path functions were utilized to evaluate three-dimensional reconstructions obtained through focused ion beam (fib) tomography. the 2d analysis reveals distinct size and morphological differences between the porosity within individual particles and that existing between them. moreover, the 3d analysis provides further insight into the highly interconnected nature of the porosity, which manifests as irregular, tortuous pathways rather than uniform, spherical cells. although mechanical polishing combined with optical microscopy allows for rapid examination of large areas, techniques such as fib sectioning can offer additional insights into microstructural details. specifically, after fib milling produces a flat surface, that surface can be further analyzed to obtain both structural and compositional information.

raw: the utility model relates to a balance car is with luminous motor, the motor is installed in the wheel hub of wheel, a plurality of led lamps are installed to one side of the coil of motor, and the power?connection of led lamp and motor is connected. the led lamp is installed in the annular chamber of coil through the looping -in board, and the circuit board is installed to the another side of coil, and the power?connection of circuit board and motor is connected, the led lamp with circuit board electricity is connected. the closing cap is installed to one side of wheel hub, and the side of closing cap has many strengthening ribs, the outer end of strengthening rib has the transparent window that corresponds the position with the led lamp. the utility model discloses an installation led lamp on the motor, along with turning?wheels forms the ring of light, its aesthetic property that has not only improved motor and electric motor car, operational situation that can also direct perusal motor, the utility model has the characteristics of simple structure, practicality height, pleasing to the eye.
rewritten: this utility model relates to a balance car featuring a luminous motor. the motor is mounted within the wheel hub, and multiple led lamps are arranged on one side of the motor’s coil. the led lamps are electrically connected to the motor. they are installed inside the coil’s annular chamber via a looping-in board, while a circuit board is fixed on the opposite side of the coil; this circuit board is also connected to the motor and supplies power to the led lamps. additionally, a closing cap is fitted to one side of the wheel hub. this cap is reinforced by several ribs, each equipped at its outer end with a transparent window that aligns with the corresponding led lamp. overall, the utility model presents an innovative configuration in which the led lamps on the motor, together with the rotating wheels, create a ring of light. this not only enhances the motor and electric car’s aesthetic appeal but also improves its operational effectiveness by drawing attention to the motor. the design is characterized by its simple structure, high practicability, and pleasing appearance.

raw: technologies for managing the power usage of components of a computing device, while the components and the computing device are in a low-power state, such as a connected standby state. an embedded controller includes a wake-up timer designed to wake up the embedded controller during a low-power state to allow the embedded controller to perform its tasks. a power control system is configured to dynamically alter the timing cycle of the wake-up timer of the embodied controller based on operation data received. the dynamically altered timing cycle is designed to conserve power, but maintain functionality of the embedded controller.
rewritten: this technology manages the power usage of a computing device's components when they are in low-power states, such as a connected standby mode. it involves an embedded controller fitted with a wake-up timer that activates the controller during these low-power periods so that it can carry out its tasks. additionally, a power control system is designed to dynamically adjust the interval of this wake-up timer based on operational data, ensuring that power is conserved while the controller continues to function effectively.

raw: in this paper we present empirical tests of an extended version of the capital asset pricing model that replaces the single period horizon with a probability distribution over diff erent horizons. adopting a simple parameterization of the probability distribution of the length of the horizon, we estimate the parameters of the distribution as well as the parameters of the capm. we find that the extended model is not rejected, and that the estimated stock turnover rate rises from 82.8% in the period 1926-62 to 266.4% in the period 1963-2009. we also find that long horizon betas are determined by identi able rm characteristics as well as by short horizon betas.
rewritten: this paper presents empirical tests of an extended version of the capital asset pricing model which substitutes the single-period horizon with a probability distribution over multiple horizons. by employing a straightforward parameterization of the horizon-length probability distribution, we estimate both its parameters and those of the capm. our findings indicate that the extended model is not rejected and reveal that the estimated stock turnover rate increases from 82.8% during the period 1926–1962 to 266.4% during 1963–2009. additionally, we show that long-horizon betas are influenced not only by short-horizon betas but also by identifiable firm characteristics.

raw: evidence from in vitro and in vivo studies shows that ski may act as both a tumor proliferation-promoting factor and a metastatic suppressor in human pancreatic cancer and also may be a therapeutic target of integrative therapies. at present, pancreatic cancer stem cells (cscs) are responsible for tumor recurrence accompanied by resistance to conventional therapies. sonic hedgehog (shh) signaling pathway is found to be aberrantly activated in cscs. the objectives of this study were to investigate the role of ski in modulating pancreatic cscs and to examine the molecular mechanisms involved in pancreatic cancer treatment both in vivo and in vitro. in in vitro study, the results showed that enhanced ski expression could increase the expression of pluripotency maintaining markers, such as cd24, cd44, sox-2, and oct-4, and also components of shh signaling pathway, such as shh, ptch-1, smo, gli-1, and gli-2, whereas depletion of ski to the contrary. then, we investigated the underlying mechanism and found that inhibiting gli-2 expression by short interfering rna (sirna) can decrease the effects of ski on the maintenance of pancreatic cscs, indicating that ski mediates the pluripotency of pancreatic cscs mainly through shh pathway. the conclusion is that ski may be an important factor in maintaining the stemness of pancreatic cscs through modulating shh pathway.
rewritten: evidence from both in vitro and in vivo studies indicates that ski may function as a factor that promotes tumor proliferation while simultaneously suppressing metastasis in human pancreatic cancer, suggesting its potential as a target in integrative therapies. currently, pancreatic cancer stem cells (cscs) are known to drive tumor recurrence and exhibit resistance to standard treatments, and the sonic hedgehog (shh) signaling pathway is abnormally activated in these cells. this study aimed to explore the role of ski in regulating pancreatic cscs and to analyze the molecular mechanisms relevant to pancreatic cancer treatment in both in vivo and in vitro settings. in the in vitro portion of the study, results demonstrated that increased expression of ski led to elevated levels of pluripotency markers—such as cd24, cd44, sox-2, and oct-4—as well as heightened expression of components of the shh signaling pathway, including shh, ptch-1, smo, gli-1, and gli-2. conversely, reducing ski levels produced the opposite effects. further investigation revealed that knocking down gli-2 expression using short interfering rna (sirna) diminished ski’s ability to maintain pancreatic cscs. this finding suggests that ski primarily sustains the pluripotency of pancreatic cscs through the shh pathway. in conclusion, ski appears to be a critical factor for maintaining the stemness of pancreatic cancer stem cells by modulating the shh signaling pathway.

raw: this resource provides information about creating and managing an orcid profile in order to better manage your scholarship and to increase the visibility of your work.
rewritten: this resource offers guidance on setting up and maintaining an orcid profile, which can help you manage your scholarship more effectively and enhance the visibility of your work.

raw: imaging technology has made clinical trials in rheumatology shorter and more sensitive, but more research is needed on how best to use ultrasound and mri in practice.
rewritten: advancements in imaging technology have resulted in shorter, more sensitive clinical trials in rheumatology; however, further investigation is needed to determine the most effective ways to implement ultrasound and mri in clinical practice.

raw: this publication reviews key structures, policies and reforms in the area of early leaving from education and training (elet) in europe. it contains six indicators on policies for collecting national data on elet, increasing the flexibility and permeability of education pathways, providing language support for students with a mother tongue other than the language of instruction, addressing elet in initial teacher education and continuing professional development, offering education and career guidance in schools and providing support to early leavers to re-enter the education and training system. the publication is based on a chapter in the eurydice report 'structural indicators for monitoring education and training systems in europe 2016' [indexed in vocedplus at td/tnc 128.459].
rewritten: this publication examines key structures, policies, and reforms related to early leaving from education and training (elet) in europe. it highlights six policy indicators that include: gathering national data on elet; enhancing the flexibility and permeability of educational pathways; providing language support for students whose first language differs from the language of instruction; addressing elet within initial teacher education and ongoing professional development; delivering education and career guidance in schools; and offering support to help early leavers re-enter the education and training system. the content is adapted from a chapter in the eurydice report "structural indicators for monitoring education and training systems in europe 2016" (indexed in vocedplus under td/tnc 128.459).

raw: power required analysis is an important element of aircraft performance. the primary method for measuring this parameter, the classic piw-viw method, works well for manned aircraft but has some short comings when applied to unmanned aircraft, specifically those that are remotely piloted. maximum likelihood estimation is used to fit a lift and drag model to data acquired through acceleration and deceleration runs. the data is reduced using the piw-viw technique to remove weight and density effects. a low cost raspberrypi data collection system was designed to be used on two test aircraft, the commercially available nexstar and a reconfigurable research aircraft call astraeus. data points using the classic piw-viw method were also collected for comparison against the dynamic method. the results show good agreement between the deceleration runs and the piw-viw method. the acceleration runs generally had a poorer overall fit and a larger spread. this is most likely due to excessive sensor noise caused by imbalances in the motor/prop combination. overall the dynamic power analysis was found to be a valid analysis tool for power required.
rewritten: analyzing the power required is a key aspect of assessing aircraft performance. the most common technique, known as the classic piw-viw method, effectively measures this parameter in manned aircraft; however, it encounters certain limitations when used with unmanned, particularly remotely piloted, aircraft. to address this, maximum likelihood estimation was employed to fit a lift and drag model to data obtained from acceleration and deceleration runs. the piw-viw method was used to reduce the data by removing the effects of weight and air density. a low-cost data collection system based on a raspberry pi was developed for use on two test aircraft: the commercially available nexstar and a reconfigurable research aircraft called astraeus. additionally, data points gathered using the classic piw-viw method were used as a benchmark to compare against this dynamic approach. the findings demonstrated strong agreement between the deceleration runs and the traditional piw-viw results, while the acceleration runs showed a generally poorer fit and a greater variance, likely due to significant sensor noise caused by imbalances in the motor/propeller setup. in summary, the dynamic power analysis proved to be a valid tool for evaluating the power required by the aircraft.

raw: the utility model relates to a well head technical field, concretely relates to quick assembly disassembly fracturing well head device, it includes bottom tube and four -way pipe, the bottom tube upper end is provided with the four -way pipe, sleeve pipe and outer tube in the bottom tube is provided with with the four -way pipe is inside, interior sleeve pipe and outer tube adopt coaxial sleeving structure, be provided with the water injection pipe dish in the outer tube, be provided with the thread groove between water injection coil pipe and the interior sleeve pipe. the utility model discloses it adopts and set up outer tube and interior sleeve pipe in the four -way pipe to set up the water injection coil pipe in the outer tube, have good partition effect, easy dismounting, pipe fitting separation is more obvious, and it has convenient to use, easy operation, the practicality is good, characteristics such as low cost.
rewritten: this utility model is in the field of wellhead technology and specifically concerns a fracturing wellhead device designed for rapid assembly and disassembly. the device comprises a bottom tube and a four-way pipe, with the four-way pipe mounted at the upper end of the bottom tube. within the bottom tube, an inner sleeve pipe and an outer tube are arranged in a coaxial configuration, while the outer tube houses a water injection coil pipe. additionally, a threaded groove is formed between the water injection coil pipe and the inner sleeve pipe. this design, which places the outer tube and inner sleeve pipe within the four-way pipe along with the water injection coil pipe positioned in the outer tube, ensures a good partitioning effect and facilitates easy disassembly, with clearly defined connections between the pipes. overall, the utility model is user-friendly, easy to operate, practical, and cost-effective.

raw: the utility model provides a normal -pressure water heating boiler relates to the boiler field, including the casing, one side of casing is equipped with the combustion chamber, the inside liquid heat -conducting medium that is equipped with of casing, and casing inside is equipped with a plurality of heating members, and the inside of heating member is equipped with " n " shape passageway, is equipped with the connecting pipe that the intercommunication passageway formed the gas flue on the heating member, and first heating member communicates with the combustion chamber, terminal and the outlet flue intercommunication of heating member, the water pipe of having arranged of buckling in the space between the heating member, the water pipe passes one side fixed connection of casing, the passageway of first heating member is the front and back and sets up, and the passageway of all the other heating members sets up for controlling, the connecting pipe sets up in heating member below both sides in turn, and buckling on the formation horizontal direction is arranged. the utility model discloses make the flue gas effectively increase the stroke at the inside in -process that descends that rises of heating member, heat time increases, and the utilization ratio improves, external dwell time increases, the below of heating member is equipped with the deashing mouth and effectively solves impurity clearance and the inconvenient problem of maintenance.
rewritten: a utility model for a normal-pressure water heating boiler is disclosed, relating to the field of boilers. the boiler comprises a casing with a combustion chamber positioned on one side. inside the casing, there is a liquid heat-conducting medium, and multiple heating members are arranged. each heating member features an "n"-shaped passageway, and a connecting pipe links these passageways together to form a gas flue. in particular, the first heating member is connected to both the combustion chamber and the outlet flue. additionally, a water pipe arranged in a buckled configuration is installed in the space between the heating members, with the water pipe being fixedly connected to one side of the casing. the first heating member is designed with a front-to-back passageway, while the passageways in the other heating members are arranged for controlled flow. furthermore, connecting pipes are sequentially installed along both sides at the bottom of the heating members, and the buckling is arranged horizontally. this design ensures that the flue gases can effectively extend their stroke as they descend and rise within the heating members, thus increasing the heating duration and improving energy utilization. moreover, the increased dwell time outside, along with a deashing port provided at the bottom of the heating members, effectively addresses impurity clearance and maintenance challenges.

raw: due to the high stability and biocompatibility gold nanoparticles (aunp) are extensively exploited for biomedical purposes.
rewritten: gold nanoparticles (aunp) are widely used in biomedical applications because of their exceptional stability and excellent biocompatibility.

raw: background: the authors assessed the variability of bronchoscopy practice in australia and new zealand through survey in the setting of recently published guidelines. methods: in june 2014, an email survey was sent to every member of the thoracic society of australia and new zealand who was a doctor. results: responses were received from 258 of 824 doctors. australia and new zealand was the location of training for 91% and the united kingdom for 9%. united kingdom-trained doctors are more likely to conduct bronchoscopy from the patient's side rather than from behind. the services of anesthetists are utilized by 81% of private bronchoscopists compared with 38% of public ones. two sedatives are used by 94% of bronchoscopists, 96% of whom use midazolam and fentanyl and 4% use midazolam and propofol. when anesthesia is provided by anesthetists, 77% use 2 agents, of which 53% use midazolam and fentanyl and 37% use midazolam and propofol. those who work publicly conduct more procedures and are younger. for patients with heart valve defects, 39% prescribe prophylactic antibiotics and 16% do not cease clopidogrel where biopsy is anticipated. in 2013, pneumothoraces complicated 0.22% of procedures, whereas the mortality rate was 0.019%. almost all monitor oxygen saturation during the procedures. conclusions: this survey shows that there is a large variation in bronchoscopy practices both generally and between different groups of bronchoscopists, yet it is a safe procedure. an important finding was the significantly high utilization of the services of anesthetists for sedation and their different sedative choices, including the use of propofol.
rewritten: background: the authors examined the differences in bronchoscopy practices across australia and new zealand via a survey conducted in the context of recently issued guidelines. methods: in june 2014, an email survey was distributed to every doctor who was a member of the thoracic society of australia and new zealand. results: out of 824 doctors contacted, 258 responded. of those surveyed, 91% received their training in australia and new zealand, while 9% were trained in the united kingdom. doctors trained in the united kingdom are more inclined to perform bronchoscopy at the patient’s side rather than from behind. in addition, 81% of bronchoscopists in private practice use anesthetists compared to 38% in the public sector. nearly all bronchoscopists (94%) employ two sedatives: 96% of them use midazolam and fentanyl, while 4% use midazolam and propofol. when anesthetists provide anesthesia, 77% of cases involve the use of two agents; among these, 53% use midazolam and fentanyl and 37% use midazolam and propofol. those working in the public sector tend to perform more procedures and are generally younger. for patients with heart valve defects, 39% of respondents administer prophylactic antibiotics, and 16% do not discontinue clopidogrel when a biopsy is planned. in 2013, pneumothoraxes occurred in 0.22% of procedures, while the mortality rate was 0.019%. almost all practitioners monitor oxygen saturation during procedures. conclusions: the survey reveals considerable variation in bronchoscopy practices both overall and among different groups of bronchoscopists. despite these differences, bronchoscopy remains a safe procedure. notably, there is a high reliance on anesthetists for sedation, with variations in the sedative agents used, including the use of propofol.

raw: the utility model discloses an optic fibre coiling device belongs to light coiling technical field. light coiling device includes: the design of first anchor clamps upper surface circumference has annular boss, is provided with the stopper on the boss, a ring core terminal surface is connected with first anchor clamps, and the ring core includes and first secter pat, second secter pat, third secter pat and fourth secter pat are provided with the recess in the terminal surface of first secter pat, second secter pat, third secter pat and fourth secter pat, and the through -hole has been offered to the boss phase -match of recess and first anchor clamps in the recess, the second anchor clamps are connected with another terminal surface of ring core, coaxial locating hole is seted up at the center of first anchor clamps, second anchor clamps and ring core, and fixing device passes in proper order and sets up coaxial locating hole on first anchor clamps, second anchor clamps and the ring core. the utility model discloses breaking away from smoothly between the periphery of optic fibre coil and winding optic fibre can be realized to light coiling device, has guaranteed the precision in addition, when dismantling, can not result in the fiber strain.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an optical fibre coiling device, which falls within the field of light coiling technology. the device comprises a first anchor clamp whose upper surface features an annular boss, on which a stopper is provided. a ring core is connected to the terminal surface of the first anchor clamp. the ring core itself is divided into four sectors: the first, second, third, and fourth sectors. on the terminal surface of each sector, a recess is formed, and a through-hole is provided within the annular boss such that it aligns with the recess on the first anchor clamp. additionally, a second anchor clamp is connected to the opposite terminal surface of the ring core. at the center of the first anchor clamp, the second anchor clamp, and the ring core, respective coaxial locating holes are provided. a fixing device is properly arranged to pass through these coaxial locating holes in the first anchor clamp, second anchor clamp, and ring core. this design allows the optical fibre to be wound smoothly around the coil while ensuring precision during winding and preventing fibre strain during dismantling.

raw: the term big data is being used widely by companies and researchers who consider your relevant functionalities or applications to create value and business innovation. however some questions arise about what is this phenomenon and, more precisely, how it occurs and under what conditions it can create value and innovation in business. in our view, the lack of depth related to the principles involved in big data and the very absence of a conceptual definition, made it difficult to answer these questions that have been the basis for our research. to answer these questions we did a bibliometric study and extensive literature review. the bibliometric studies were realized based in articles and citation of web of knowledge database. the main result of our research is the providing a conceptual definition for the term big data. also, we propose which principles discovered can contribute with other researches that intend value creation by big data . finally we propose see the value creation through big data using the resource based view as the main theory used for discuss that theme.
rewritten: the term “big data” is increasingly used by companies and researchers who view its functionalities and applications as drivers of value creation and business innovation. yet, questions remain about what this phenomenon truly is, specifically how it works and under what conditions it can generate value and spark innovation in the business sector. we believe that the insufficient exploration of the underlying principles of big data—coupled with the lack of a clear conceptual definition—has hindered efforts to answer these questions, which form the basis of our research. to address these issues, we conducted a bibliometric study and an extensive literature review, drawing on articles and citations from the web of knowledge database. the main outcome of our research is a proposed conceptual definition of big data. additionally, we identify several principles that could assist other researchers in using big data for value creation. finally, we advocate for examining value creation through big data using the resource-based view as the primary theoretical framework for discussing this topic.

rewritten: the invention is related to the field of waste plastic oil refining, specifically to a vibration-damping and supporting device for waste plastic oil refining equipment. this device consists of a base, a vibration-damping pad, a wheel seat, and rolling wheels. the vibration-damping pad is secured to the top surface of the base using screws, while the wheel seat is fastened to the upper side of the vibration-damping pad with screws. rolling wheels are installed in the wheel seat via a wheel shaft, and each part—the base, the vibration-damping pad, and the wheel seat—features screw holes. this vibration-damping and supporting device enhances the stability of the cracking processor, lessens the vibration amplitude of the base, extends the base’s service life, and reduces the noise produced during operation.

raw: the invention provides a novel exoskeleton finger rehabilitation robot which is characterized by comprising a control unit, motor drive units, transmission units and execution units. a working method of the robot comprises the steps of control signal output by the control unit, drive motor power input, finger mcp (metacarpophalangeal) joint exercise, finger pip (proximal interphalangeal) joint exercise and exercise parameter adjustment. the robot has the advantages that the robot is intelligent, portable and wearable, is efficient in rehabilitation training and controls rehabilitation exercises of patients by utilizing the bending and stretching motions of finger joints.

raw: the traffic alert and collision avoidance system (tcas) is a world-wide accepted last-resort means of reducing the probability and frequency of mid-air collisions between aircraft. unfortunately, it is widely known that in congested airspace, the use of the tcas may actually lead to induced collisions. therefore, further research regarding tcas logic is required. in this paper, an encounter model is formalised to identify all of the potential collision scenarios that can be induced by a resolution advisory that was generated previously by the tcas without considering the downstream consequences in the surrounding traffic. the existing encounter models focus on checking and validating the potential collisions between trajectories of a specific scenario. in contrast, the innovative approach described in this paper concentrates on quantitative analysis of the different induced collision scenarios that could be reached for a given initial trajectory and a rough specification of the surrounding traffic. this approach provides valuable information at the operational level. furthermore, the proposed encounter model can be used as a test-bed to evaluate future tcas logic changes to mitigate potential induced collisions in hot spot volumes. in addition, the encounter model is described by means of the coloured petri net (cpn) formalism. the resulting state space provides a deep understanding of the cause-and-effect relationship that each tcas action proposed to avoid an actual collision with a potential new collision in the surrounding traffic. quantitative simulation results are conducted to validate the proposed encounter model, and the resulting collision scenarios are summarised as valuable information for future air traffic management (atm) systems.
rewritten: the traffic alert and collision avoidance system (tcas) is globally recognized as a last-resort method to decrease both the likelihood and frequency of mid-air collisions between aircraft. however, it is well known that when operating in congested airspace, tcas can inadvertently result in induced collisions. this highlights the need for further research into tcas logic. in this paper, we formalize an encounter model designed to identify every potential collision scenario that might be induced by a resolution advisory previously generated by tcas, which did not account for the downstream effects on surrounding traffic. unlike existing encounter models that primarily focus on verifying and validating potential collisions between specific trajectory pairs in a given scenario, our innovative approach centers on the quantitative analysis of various induced collision scenarios that could stem from a predefined initial trajectory combined with a rough outline of the surrounding traffic. this method offers valuable insights from an operational perspective. moreover, the proposed encounter model serves as a test-bed for evaluating future changes in tcas logic aimed at mitigating potential induced collisions in high-traffic areas. the model is further detailed using the colored petri net (cpn) formalism, and the resulting state space provides an in-depth understanding of the cause-and-effect relationship, wherein each tcas action intended to avoid an existing collision might lead to a new collision in the surrounding traffic. quantitative simulation results validate our proposed encounter model, and the summarized collision scenarios offer crucial information for future air traffic management (atm) systems.

raw: the invention discloses a latin hypercube sampling method probabilistic power flow calculation method based on a normal copula function. the method is characterized by comprising the following steps: 1) according to the correlation coefficient matrix of a new energy generated output variable, utilizing the normal copula function to generate a random number matrix which meets the correlation of the new energy generated output variable; 2) utilizing the latin hypercube sampling method to sample the random number matrix generated in the 1), and establishing a sample matrix of the new energy generated output variable according to the inverse function of the cumulative distribution function of the new energy generated output variable; and 3) taking the sample matrix, which is established in the 2), of the new energy generated output variable as an input quantity to carry out probabilistic power flow calculation, obtaining a discrete result of an output variable, and utilizing nuclear density estimation to fit the discrete result of the output variable to obtain the probability density function of the output variable. calculation time is shortened while calculation precision is improved.
rewritten: this invention proposes a probabilistic power flow calculation method that uses latin hypercube sampling based on a normal copula function. the method includes the following steps: 1. based on the correlation coefficient matrix of the output variable generated from new energy sources, a normal copula function is used to produce a random number matrix that reflects the same correlation as the output variable. 2. the latin hypercube sampling method is applied to the random number matrix from step 1. then, using the inverse function of the cumulative distribution function for the output variable, a sample matrix of the new energy-generated output variable is constructed. 3. the sample matrix obtained in step 2 is used as the input for the probabilistic power flow calculation, producing a discrete set of results for an output variable. finally, kernel density estimation is used to smooth the discrete results and obtain the probability density function of the output variable. this approach shortens the calculation time while improving computational accuracy.

raw: glasswort (salicornia spp) belonging to (chenopodiaceae) family is widely distributed in salt marshes on beachesand among mangroves. it shows great biotechnological potential as a salt-water irrigated crop. glasswort has been prescribed in traditional medicines to treat a variety of diseases like nephropathy, hepatitis, intestinal ailments, atherosclerosis,hyperlipidemia, diabetes and cancer. a variety of pharmacological experiments have revealed that glasswort solvent extract has anti-oxidative, anti-microbial, anti-proliferative, and anti-inflammatory activities. many bioactive compounds have been isolated from salicornia such as tungtungmadic acid, quercetin 3-o-glucoside, isorhamnetin 3-o-glucoside and betalain. due to easy collection of the plant and its remarkable biological activities, this genus has been used nearly worldwide as food and folk medicine. the present review focuse on bioactive compounds isolated from salicornia species and its pharmacological aspects.
rewritten: glasswort (salicornia spp.), a member of the chenopodiaceae family, is commonly found in salt marshes along beaches and among mangroves. it has significant biotechnological promise as a crop that can be irrigated with saltwater. historically, glasswort has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments including nephropathy, hepatitis, intestinal disorders, atherosclerosis, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, and cancer. numerous pharmacological studies have demonstrated that extracts of glasswort exhibit anti-oxidative, anti-microbial, anti-proliferative, and anti-inflammatory properties. several bioactive compounds such as tungtungmadic acid, quercetin 3-o-glucoside, isorhamnetin 3-o-glucoside, and betalain have been isolated from this plant. thanks to its easy accessibility and impressive biological activities, salicornia has been embraced worldwide as both a food source and a component of folk medicine. this review focuses on the bioactive compounds derived from salicornia species and examines their pharmacological properties.
