raw: communication and tracking capabilities during and immediately after a large-scale natural disaster are one of the most important components of speedy response and recovery. in that, it discovers affected people and connects them with their families, friends, and communities with first responders and/or their support computational systems. capabilities of current mobile technologies can be expanded to become effective large-scale disaster tool aid. to facilitate effective communication and coordination across different parties and domains, ontologies are becoming crucial in providing assistance during natural disasters, especially where affected locations are remote, affected population is large and centralized coordination is poor. although there are several existing competing methodologies with regard to as how an ontology may be built, there is not a single right way to build an ontology. furthermore, there is not a (de facto standard) disaster relief ontology, although separated related ontologies may be combined to create an initial version. this article discusses our on-going development of an ontology for a communication and tracking system (cts), based on existing related ontologies, that is aimed to be used by mobile applications to support disaster relief at the real-time. for future work, this ontology will be used to provide a multi-disciplinary knowledge foundation in a distributed multi-agent based environment, where mobile devices, rescue workers and their organizations are modelled and functioned as distributed and collaborative agents to support each other in the event of a large-scale natural disaster.
rewritten: effective communication and tracking during and immediately following a large-scale natural disaster are critical for fast response and recovery. these systems help identify those affected and reconnect them with loved ones, communities, first responders, and support computational systems. enhancing current mobile technologies can transform them into powerful tools for disaster management. to support effective communication and coordination among various parties and domains, the use of ontologies is increasingly vital, particularly in remote areas with large affected populations and limited centralized coordination. although multiple methodologies exist for constructing ontologies, there is no singular correct approach, and no universally accepted disaster relief ontology exists—though combining several related ontologies can produce an initial version. this article describes our ongoing work to develop an ontology for a communication and tracking system (cts), which builds on existing ontologies to support real-time disaster relief through mobile applications. in future work, this ontology will serve as a multi-disciplinary knowledge base within a distributed, multi-agent environment, where mobile devices, rescue personnel, and their organizations operate as collaborative agents to assist one another during large-scale disasters.

raw: the invention provides a method for calculating executive capacity of an actuator node. the method comprises the following steps of a step 100, determining the type of a wireless sensor actuator network and the type of the actuator node, and if the type of the wireless sensor actuator network is energy-oriented type and the type of the actuator node is fixed, entering a step 200; the step 200, defining actuator node parameters which are required for calculating the executive capacity of the actuator node; a step 300, performing normalization on the actuator node parameters; a step 400, defining the weight of each actuator node parameter in calculation; and a step 500, calculating the executive capacity of the actuator node according to the type of the wireless sensor actuator network, the type of the actuator node, the numbers and the weights of the parameters after normalization.
rewritten: the invention provides a method for calculating an actuator node’s executive capacity. this method includes the following steps: first, determine both the type of the wireless sensor actuator network and the type of the actuator node. if the network is of an energy-oriented type and the actuator node is fixed, proceed to the next step. in this subsequent step, define the actuator node parameters necessary for calculating its executive capacity. then, perform normalization on these parameters. next, assign a weight to each normalized actuator node parameter. finally, calculate the actuator node’s executive capacity based on the network type, actuator node type, as well as the numbers and weights of the normalized parameters.

raw: the utility model relates to an intelligent filter exhaust pipe lid, structure to present exhaust pipe designs brand -new constructional device, connects on the exhaust pipe exhaust port, and air velocity transducer (6) passing through design realize being directed against the testing result simultaneously, the adoption by intellectual detection system to wind speed orientation in the exhaust pipe and wind speed size the utility model particularly relates to a filter circuit (9) of design are carried out filtering to it and are handled to the filtering that obtains results in according to judging whether exhaust pipe works, and the rethread is to the design intelligent control who rotates motor (7), so realize to the institute baffle that design (3) intelligent control, realize intelligently closed and open to the exhaust pipe exhaust port, piling up of the interior debris of exhaust pipe has effectively blockked, effectively guaranteed exhaust pipe's ventilation efficiency.
rewritten: this utility model concerns an intelligent exhaust pipe lid equipped with a built-in filter. it represents a novel design specifically for the structure of exhaust pipes. the device attaches to the exhaust opening and incorporates an air velocity transducer (6) that measures airflow direction and speed simultaneously. an intelligent detection system evaluates both the orientation and magnitude of the wind within the exhaust pipe, while a dedicated filter circuit (9) processes these measurements to determine whether the exhaust pipe is functioning correctly. additionally, an intelligent control system manages a motor (7) that adjusts the position of a baffle (3), enabling the exhaust port to open and close automatically. this design effectively prevents debris from accumulating inside the exhaust pipe, thus maintaining optimal ventilation efficiency.

raw: lane monitoring equipment monitoring device belongs to traffic management instrument technical field. the problem of current lane controller often stolen or destroyed is solved. the utility model discloses u type fixed knot structure is used for the joint in the outside of lane monitoring equipment body, and the inboard that u type fixed knot constructs is equipped with a plurality of fixed magnetic parts, and the surface subsides that u type fixed knot constructs are equipped with solar cell panel, and the circuit mounting box builds in on the lateral wall that u type fixed knot constructs, the wireless routing device that wifi radio communication circuit is used for detecting in the pavilion with the lane controller is connected, and switch detection circuit is used for detecting the switch of lane controller, and power source detection circuit is used for detecting electrical energy storage converting circuit's output current signal, alarm device's alarm trigger signal input connection director's alarm trigger signal output, sim radio communication circuit's signal input part connection director's wireless alarm signal output. the utility model is suitable for a monitoring vehicle says the monitoring equipment.
rewritten: the lane monitoring device is part of the traffic management instrument technical field and addresses the problem of current lane controllers being frequently stolen or damaged. this utility model features a u-shaped fixed knot structure that serves as a joint on the exterior of the lane monitoring device. the inner side of this u-shaped structure is equipped with several fixed magnetic components, while its lower surface is fitted with a solar cell panel. a circuit mounting box is installed on the lateral wall of the u-shaped structure. the device also incorporates a wireless routing unit with a wi-fi radio communication circuit that connects with the pavilion housing the lane controller. additionally, it includes a switch detection circuit for monitoring the lane controller’s switch, a power source detection circuit for monitoring the output current signal from the electrical energy storage conversion circuit, an alarm trigger circuit that links the alarm device’s input with the director's alarm trigger output, and a sim radio communication circuit whose signal input is connected with the director's wireless alarm signal output. this utility model is appropriately designed for use in monitoring vehicles equipped with the lane monitoring system.

raw: power electronic converters are crucial components of the wind turbines (wts), and its power semiconductors is susceptible to power fluctuations of the wts. therefore, correct reliability evaluation of wind power converter is key for the lifetime extension and also for cost reduction of wind power turbines. on the basis of analyzing the reliability evaluation methods of devices based on physical failure mechanism of power devices, this paper proposes the lifetime estimation method of power modules by using multi-time scale of the mission profiles. a 1.2mw wind turbine of permanent magnetism synchronous generator (pmsg) is regarded as a case study, and then the fundamental frequency scale and low frequency scale lifetime consumption of devices are primarily evaluated by the calculation method of junction temperature considering mission profiles. finally, the effect of multi-time scale mission profiles of power semiconductors is deeply analyzed.
rewritten: power electronic converters are essential components of wind turbines, and their power semiconductors are vulnerable to the turbines’ power fluctuations. therefore, accurately assessing the reliability of wind power converters is crucial for extending their operational lifetime and reducing turbine costs. this paper, building on an analysis of reliability evaluation methods based on the physical failure mechanisms of power devices, introduces a lifetime estimation approach for power modules that incorporates mission profiles across multiple time scales. using a 1.2mw wind turbine equipped with a permanent magnet synchronous generator (pmsg) as a case study, the paper primarily evaluates device lifetime consumption at both the fundamental and low frequency scales through junction temperature calculations that account for mission profiles. finally, it delves into the effects of multi-time scale mission profiles on power semiconductors.

raw: we consider a system of weak* closed sets of finite-dimensional distributions. we show that a corresponding system of random variables can be defined on a probability space with a probability measure determined up to some set of measures, provided that the sets of finite-dimensional distributions are consistent.
rewritten: we examine a collection of weak*-closed sets of finite-dimensional distributions. under the condition that these finite-dimensional distributions are consistent, we demonstrate that it is possible to define a corresponding system of random variables on a probability space, with the probability measure determined up to a certain set of measures.

raw: common features visible on a large majority of the seismic cross sections are chimneys or pipe structures. they represent regions of focused fluid flow in porous media. as seismic surveys are widely performed in many regions where the subsurface is of economic interest, a better understanding of the formation and evolution process of these chimneys is vital. they should be considered when performing risk assessment linked to leakage within subsurface waste storage projects. they might also lead to a better understanding of fluid migration pathways and subsurface fluid localization. in that context, we propose a new physical model that predicts the formation and the evolution in space and time of these chimneys. we use a two-dimensional (2d) implicit solver and the three-dimensional (3d) high-resolution iterative parallel gpu code to solve a thermodynamically consistent system of nonlinear equations for two-phase flow in deforming porous media. we will show that the different 2d implicit and 3d iterative methods used to solve the fully coupled system of nonlinear equations are in good agreement. they predict the formation and the propagation of a nonlinear solitary wave, resulting in a chimney formation. these chimneys, with order of magnitude permeability increase, are a natural outcome of an interplay between buoyancy forces driving upward fluid propagation and a resistance of a deformable rock to locally increasing fluid pressure. we will also discuss and highlight the importance of a proper coupling between the geomechanics (stokes solver) and the reservoir fluid flow (nonlinear darcy solver).
rewritten: many seismic cross sections typically display features known as chimneys or pipe structures, which are regions of concentrated fluid flow in porous media. given that seismic surveys are conducted in many areas of economic interest, understanding how these chimneys form and evolve is crucial. they must be taken into account for risk assessments related to leakage in subsurface waste storage projects, and could also improve our understanding of fluid migration and accumulation in the subsurface. within this framework, we introduce a new physical model that forecasts the spatial and temporal evolution of these chimneys. our approach employs a two-dimensional (2d) implicit solver alongside a three-dimensional (3d) high-resolution, iterative, parallel gpu code to solve a thermodynamically consistent system of nonlinear equations governing two-phase flow in a deforming porous medium. results demonstrate that both the 2d implicit and 3d iterative methods closely agree in solving the fully coupled system of nonlinear equations, successfully predicting the development and spread of a nonlinear solitary wave that leads to chimney formation. these chimneys, characterized by a permeability increase by orders of magnitude, naturally emerge from the interplay between buoyancy forces driving the upward movement of the fluid and the resistance of the deformable rock to locally increased fluid pressure. additionally, we highlight the significance of properly coupling geomechanics (via a stokes solver) with reservoir fluid flow (through a nonlinear darcy solver).

raw: background: hypertension is a leading cause of morbidity and mortality globally. it is a well-known risk factor for an array of adverse cardiovascular outcomes. obesity is considered as one of the major contributing factors to essential hypertension in humans. obesity in itself is a risk factor for conditions like insulin resistance, hypertension, stroke, ischemic heart diseases, chf etc. undoubtedly in recent times we have achieved great advances in terms of management of hypertension but still we have miles to cover to have dominance over it. gymnema sylvestre is a valuable indigenous herb. a number of animal and human studies have shown the potential role of gymnema sylvestre (gs) as an anti-diabetic and anti-obesity agent. methods: adult female wistar rats, weighing between 150-200 gm, were included in the study. they were randomly divided into five groups with six rats in each group. high fat diet (hfd) was given for 4 weeks to induce hypertension in all the groups except group i which was fed with normal chow. drugs along with respective diets were given to the rats for next 4 weeks by oral feeding cannula. systolic blood pressure was measured by nibp controller machine. results: on feeding rats with hfd for 4 weeks the mean systolic blood pressure increased significantly. after giving drugs gs (100mg/kg), gs (200mg/kg) and amlodipine (10mg/kg) to groups iii, iv, v respectively for next 4 weeks, mean systolic blood pressure fell significantly (p <0.05) as compared to group ii (hfd control group). at final evaluation at week 8 (as compared to group 2) sbp got maximally reduced in group 5 (35.1%) followed by group 4 (26.4%), group 3 (20.1%). on comparing amlodipine standard (group 5) with other groups, a significant difference was found. this showed that gymnema sylvestre reduced the elevated systolic blood pressure significantly but this anti-hypertensive effect was inferior to amlodipine. conclusions: the present study concludes that gymnema sylvestre has a potent dose- dependent antihypertensive action but the effect is inferior to amlodipine. hence it can be used as an add-on to standard drugs for hypertension.
rewritten: background: hypertension is a major contributor to global illness and death and is recognized as a significant risk factor for numerous adverse cardiovascular events. obesity, which itself predisposes individuals to conditions such as insulin resistance, stroke, ischemic heart disease, and congestive heart failure, is also one of the key factors leading to essential hypertension. although there have been considerable advances in the management of hypertension, achieving complete control remains a challenge. gymnema sylvestre, an important indigenous herb, has been studied in both animals and humans and shows promise as an anti-diabetic and anti-obesity agent. methods: in this study, adult female wistar rats weighing between 150 and 200 grams were used. the rats were randomly assigned to five groups, with six rats per group. all groups except group i, which received a normal chow diet, were fed a high-fat diet for four weeks to induce hypertension. for the following four weeks, the rats were administered specific drugs via an oral feeding cannula along with their respective diets. systolic blood pressure was measured using a non-invasive blood pressure (nibp) controller. results: the high-fat diet raised the mean systolic blood pressure significantly after four weeks. over the subsequent four weeks, groups iii, iv, and v received gymnema sylvestre at doses of 100 mg/kg, 200 mg/kg, and amlodipine at 10 mg/kg, respectively. these treatments led to a significant reduction in mean systolic blood pressure (p < 0.05) compared to the high-fat diet control group (group ii). at the final evaluation at week 8, compared to group ii, the systolic blood pressure was reduced by 20.1% in group iii, 26.4% in group iv, and 35.1% in group v. when comparing the amlodipine standard (group v) with the gymnema sylvestre groups, a significant difference was observed, indicating that while gymnema sylvestre effectively lowered systolic blood pressure, its antihypertensive effect was less pronounced than that of amlodipine. conclusions: the findings of this study indicate that gymnema sylvestre has a potent, dose-dependent antihypertensive effect, although it is not as effective as amlodipine. consequently, it may serve as a useful adjunct to standard antihypertensive therapies.

raw: production, consumption and waste managment of clothing is causing severe environmental damage and needs to be transformed. in this thesis the question of how circular economy business models can c ...
rewritten: the current methods of producing, consuming, and managing clothing waste are inflicting significant environmental harm and must be overhauled. this thesis investigates how business models based on the circular economy can address this issue.

raw: purpose the purpose of this paper is to provide knowledge on barriers to preventive action on early childhood overweight in non-western migrant families. it investigates the underlying understandings of the parental role in relation to weight control present in health-care professionals and in families. design/methodology/approach the study is based on qualitative interviews with parents who are engaged in interventions aimed at helping them and their children to adopt a healthier life style, and on interviews with health-care professionals. findings this study shows that the participating parents, all low ses and living under different forms of insecurity, perceived their parental task for the present as creating well-being for their children, and they were, therefore, reluctant to enforce dietary changes. the health-care professionals, in contrast, considered the need for change through a perspective on future risks. research limitations/implications the results are based on a rather small sample and the link between insecurity, family dynamics and health practice needs further research. originality/value the participating parents represented a group that is rarely included in scientific research and the study, therefore, contributes valuable knowledge on health behavior in ethnic minority families. the empirical analysis provides new insights for health professionals regarding the suitability of the universal model of parental feeding styles. it illuminates the implications of implicitly applying this model in health interventions which involve vulnerable categories of parents such as refugees to western societies.
rewritten: the aim of this paper is to shed light on the obstacles to preventive measures against early childhood overweight within non-western migrant families. it examines how both healthcare professionals and families understand the parental role in managing weight. the study employs a qualitative approach, drawing on interviews with parents who are participating in interventions designed to promote a healthier lifestyle for themselves and their children, as well as interviews with healthcare professionals. the findings reveal that the parents—who are all from a low socioeconomic background and experience various forms of insecurity—view their primary responsibility as ensuring their children's immediate well-being, making them hesitant to implement dietary changes. in contrast, the healthcare professionals focus on the necessity of change from the perspective of long-term health risks. although the study is based on a relatively small sample and highlights the need for further exploration of the connections among insecurity, family dynamics, and health practices, it offers valuable insights. this research is particularly significant because it includes a group—ethnic minority families—that is typically underrepresented in scientific studies. the empirical analysis contributes new perspectives for healthcare professionals by questioning the universal applicability of current parental feeding style models, especially in interventions targeting vulnerable populations like refugees in western societies.

raw: the present invention discloses a brain-strenghtening corncob powder grill sausage with walnuts, made from the following materials according to parts by weight: 120-130 parts of lean, 60-80 parts of drumstick, 20-25 parts of fat pork, 12-15 parts of corncob powder, 8-10 parts of corn starch, 7-8 parts of walnut kernel, 4-5 parts of arimillaria mellea, 3-4 parts of rhizoma dioscoreae, 1-1.2 parts of rosemary, 2.3-2.5 parts of mint leaf, 1.2-1.3 parts of fructus schisandrae, 5-6 parts of edible salt, 0.02-0.03 part of sodium nitrite, 0.08-0.12 part of sodium erythorbate, 3-4 parts of white granulated sugar, 0.6-0.7 part of monosodium glutamate, 1-2 parts of spices, suitable normal-temperature water and suitable ice water. the added walnut kernel is functional in tonifying kidney and reinforcing strength, and moistening intestines to relax bowels, and the added herbs such as fructus schisandrae are effective in astringing lung to nourish kidney and calming heart and nerves.
rewritten: the invention introduces a brain-strengthening grill sausage that incorporates corncob powder and walnuts. it is formulated with the following ingredients, measured in parts by weight: 120–130 parts lean meat, 60–80 parts drumstick, 20–25 parts pork fat, 12–15 parts corncob powder, 8–10 parts corn starch, 7–8 parts walnut kernel, 4–5 parts arimillaria mellea, 3–4 parts rhizoma dioscoreae, 1–1.2 parts rosemary, 2.3–2.5 parts mint leaf, 1.2–1.3 parts fructus schisandrae, 5–6 parts edible salt, 0.02–0.03 part sodium nitrite, 0.08–0.12 part sodium erythorbate, 3–4 parts white granulated sugar, 0.6–0.7 part monosodium glutamate, and 1–2 parts spices, along with suitable amounts of room-temperature and iced water. the walnut kernels serve to tonify the kidneys, reinforce strength, and moisten the intestines to ease bowel movements, while herbs like fructus schisandrae contribute by astringing the lungs, nourishing the kidneys, and calming the heart and nerves.

raw: the utility model discloses an electronic product short -circuit protection circuit, including pwm chip u1, pwm chip u2 and pwm chip u3, pwm chip u1's 4 feet and an electric capacity c13's one end are connected, and the 5v power is connected to c13's the other end, and pwm chip u1's 4 feet still are connected with a resistance r11's one end, and the 5v power is connected to resistance r11's the other end, and resistance r11 connects in parallel has a diode d8. operational amplifier ic2d and operational amplifier ic3d are sent into respectively to the electric current that the during operation was taken a sample, operational amplifier ic3b output high level when the output heavy load, make pwm chip u1 output pulse width reduce, output voltage reduces, thereby reach the effect that starts the heavy load, when the short circuit, the operational amplifier ic2d output high level, the pwm chip u1 close rapidly, output voltage is zero, make electronic equipment do not not burnt out, short -circuit protection is effectual and start -up bearing force is strong.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a short-circuit protection circuit for electronic products. the circuit includes pwm chips u1, u2, and u3. the fourth pin of pwm chip u1 connects to one end of capacitor c13, with the other end of c13 tied to the 5v power supply. additionally, the fourth pin of pwm chip u1 connects to one end of resistor r11, while the other end of r11 is also connected to the 5v power source. resistor r11 is placed in parallel with diode d8. during normal operation, the current is sampled by operational amplifiers ic2d and ic3d. when a heavy load is detected, operational amplifier ic3b outputs a high level, causing pwm chip u1 to reduce its pulse width, which in turn lowers the output voltage and facilitates a heavy load start. in the case of a short circuit, operational amplifier ic2d outputs a high level, which rapidly shuts off pwm chip u1, dropping the output voltage to zero. this quick response prevents damage to the electronic components, ensuring effective short-circuit protection and robust start-up capability.

raw: the implicit difference scheme has been suggested for the solution of diffusion-kinetic problem describing the ion implantation by intermetallic phase formation. the model corresponds to irreversible conditions and includes finite relaxation times for mass fluxes. the linear difference equations are solved by double-sweep method. the result illustrates the convergence of difference scheme at variation of its parameters. qualitative picture of phase evolution in the surface layer is obtained different for problem with finite relaxation times and for zero relaxation times.
rewritten: an implicit finite difference scheme is proposed to solve the diffusion-kinetic problem that describes ion implantation leading to the formation of intermetallic phases. this model, which operates under irreversible conditions, incorporates finite relaxation times for mass fluxes. the resulting linear difference equations are solved using the double-sweep method. the findings demonstrate the convergence of the difference scheme as its parameters vary, and they reveal that the qualitative evolution of the phase in the surface layer differs between cases with finite relaxation times and those with zero relaxation times.

raw: sampling-based algorithms for path planning, such as the rapidly-exploring random tree (rrt), have achieved great success, thanks to their ability to efficiently solve complex high-dimensional problems. however, standard versions of these algorithms cannot guarantee optimality or even high-quality for the produced paths. in recent years, variants of these methods, such as t-rrt, have been proposed to deal with cost spaces: by taking configuration-cost functions into account during the exploration process, they can produce high-quality (i.e., low-cost) paths. other novel variants, such as rrt*, can deal with optimal path planning: they ensure convergence toward the optimal path, with respect to a given path-quality criterion. in this paper, we propose to solve a complex problem encompassing this two paradigms: optimal path planning in a cost space. for that, we develop two efficient sampling-based approaches that combine the underlying principles of rrt* and t-rrt. these algorithms, called t-rrt* and at-rrt, offer the same asymptotic optimality guarantees as rrt*. results presented on several classes of problems show that they converge faster than rrt* toward the optimal path, especially when the topology of the search space is complex and/or when its dimensionality is high.
rewritten: sampling-based algorithms for path planning—like the rapidly-exploring random tree (rrt)—have been very successful due to their efficient handling of complex, high-dimensional problems. however, the standard versions of these algorithms do not guarantee the optimality or even the high quality of the resulting paths. in recent years, variations such as t-rrt have emerged to address cost spaces; by incorporating configuration-cost functions into the exploration process, these methods can generate high-quality (i.e., low-cost) paths. other innovative variants, like rrt*, focus on optimal path planning by ensuring convergence toward the optimal path in terms of a specified path-quality criterion. in this paper, we tackle a challenging problem that combines these two paradigms: optimal path planning in a cost space. to achieve this, we introduce two efficient sampling-based approaches that merge the core principles of rrt* and t-rrt. our proposed algorithms, named t-rrt* and at-rrt, maintain the same asymptotic optimality guarantees as rrt*. experimental results across several problem classes show that these methods converge to the optimal path more rapidly than rrt*, particularly when dealing with complex search space topologies and/or high-dimensional environments.

raw: imagery is the basic element of poetry. the same image might be given different realistic meanings by different writers under different circumstances. lin geng,a poet in fujian,his poems are lingering between classical and modern and integrate the classical aesthetic sentiment with modern aesthetic connotation which are embodied in the choices and process of the imagery of shadow,twilight,light and dream. he is said to have set a good example for the modernization of traditional classical poetry.
rewritten: imagery is a fundamental aspect of poetry, with the same image capable of conveying distinct realistic interpretations depending on the writer and the context. lin geng, a poet from fujian, seamlessly bridges the worlds of classical and modern poetry. his work marries the traditional aesthetic sentiment with modern connotations, particularly through his use of imagery such as shadow, twilight, light, and dream. he is widely regarded as a prime example of how traditional classical poetry can be modernized.

raw: the invention discloses a method for preparing chrysanthemum flower spice powder. chrysanthemum flower extract is extracted by adopting drinking chrysanthemum flower as raw materials through a supercritical co2 method and then subjected to emulsify embedding through an embedding agent, then homogenizing and spray drying are carried out, and the high-expansion chrysanthemum flower spice powder with the characteristic aroma and taste of the chrysanthemum flowers is prepared. the problems that in a traditional process, organic solvent residues are generated, the production period is long, the aroma concentration of aroma extract is not high, aroma ingredients are likely to be oxidized, and thermosensitive substances are likely to be damaged can be effectively avoided, and the chrysanthemum flower spice powder high in quality is obtained at low temperature within a short production time. the prepared chrysanthemum flower spice powder is lasting and fresh in aroma, the fragrance of food is improved, use is convenient, and a very good fragrance increasing effect is achieved. the chrysanthemum flower spice powder can be widely applied to biscuits, beverages, sauce products, cooked wheaten food seasoning and the like.
rewritten: the invention presents a method for producing chrysanthemum flower spice powder. in this process, chrysanthemum flower extract is obtained from drinking chrysanthemum flowers using a supercritical co2 extraction technique. this extract is then encapsulated with an embedding agent through emulsification, followed by homogenization and spray drying. the result is a highly expandable spice powder that retains the distinctive aroma and flavor of chrysanthemum flowers. this approach effectively overcomes common issues found in traditional methods, such as the generation of organic solvent residues, long production times, low aroma concentration, potential oxidation of aromatic components, and the degradation of thermosensitive substances. instead, high-quality chrysanthemum flower spice powder is produced at low temperatures in a short period. the final product not only maintains a lasting and fresh aroma, enhancing the fragrance of foods, but it is also convenient to use, making it an excellent choice for boosting flavor. this spice powder has versatile applications and can be incorporated into biscuits, beverages, sauces, and seasonings for wheat-based dishes, among other products.

raw: the reversible helical pitch change of polyphenylacetylenes by a thermoresponsive rotaxane switch inserted into the side chain was demonstrated through an accompanying color change. similar to an ethynyl rotaxane monomer, the corresponding polyphenylacetylene having a rotaxane moiety in its side chain exhibited a reversible helical pitch change induced by the thermoresponsive rotaxane switch, i.e., the amine/ammonium salt was converted by treatment with trichloroacetic acid (tca) and subsequent heating, which led to thermal decomposition of tca to chloroform and carbon dioxide. such a rotaxane switch caused the helical pitch change and the accompanying color change of the polymer main chain in both solution and solid state.
rewritten: a reversible change in the helical pitch of polyphenylacetylenes was achieved by incorporating a thermoresponsive rotaxane switch into the side chain, and this process was accompanied by a visible color change. similar to an ethynyl rotaxane monomer, the related polyphenylacetylene containing a rotaxane unit in its side chain exhibited a reversible helical pitch alteration controlled by the thermally responsive rotaxane switch. specifically, treating the material with trichloroacetic acid (tca) converted the amine to its corresponding ammonium salt, and subsequent heating induced the thermal decomposition of tca into chloroform and carbon dioxide. this rotaxane switch triggered both the helical pitch adjustment and the associated color change in the polymer's main structure, whether in solution or in the solid state.

raw: power grid vulnerability is a key issue with large blackouts, causing power disruption for millions of people. the complexity of power grid, together with excessive number of components, makes it difficult to be modeled. currently, researchers use complex networks to model and study the performance of power grids. in fact, power grids can be modeled into a complex network by making use of ring network topology, with substations and transmission lines denoted as nodes and edges, respectively. in this paper, three protection schemes are proposed and their effectiveness in protecting the power network under high and low-load attacks is studied. the proposed schemes, namely, cascaded load cut-off (clc), cascaded load overflow (clo) and adaptive-cascaded load overflow (a-clo), improve the robustness of the power grids, i.e., decrease the value of critical tolerance. simulation results show that clc and clo protection schemes are more effective in improving the robustness of networks than the a-clo protection scheme. however, the clc protection scheme is effective only at the expense that certain percentage of the network will have no power supply. thus, results show that the clo protection scheme dominates the other protection schemes, clc and a-clo, in terms of the robustness of the network, improved with the precise amount of load cut-off determined.
rewritten: the vulnerability of power grids is a major concern when it comes to widespread blackouts, which can leave millions without electricity. due to the intricate nature of power grids and their numerous components, modeling them poses significant challenges. currently, researchers represent and analyze power grids as complex networks, often employing a ring network topology where substations are treated as nodes and transmission lines as edges. this paper introduces three protection schemes and examines their effectiveness under conditions of both high and low load attacks. the schemes—cascaded load cut-off (clc), cascaded load overflow (clo), and adaptive-cascaded load overflow (a-clo)—aim to enhance grid robustness, effectively reducing the critical tolerance values. simulation results indicate that both the clc and clo schemes better improve network robustness compared to the a-clo approach. however, while the clc scheme works, it does so by intentionally disconnecting a portion of the network from power supply. therefore, the findings suggest that the clo protection scheme outperforms the other two, as it boosts network robustness with an optimal, precisely determined load cut-off.

raw: this study was conducted to determine tick burden and immunological parameters of resistance to east coast fever (ecf) in tarime and sukuma cattle. tick load, packed cell volume (pcv), theileria parva (t. parva) specific antibody percent positivity (pp), and prevalence of t. parva parasites were studied in relation to dipping regime, strains, and season. a total of 50 experimental cattle were included in this study. tick load was determined by whole body counts, antibody percent positivity was determined by the polymorphic immunodominant molecule (pim)-based t. parva enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (elisa), and prevalence of t. parva parasites was detected by a nested polymerase chain reaction (pcr) based on the p104 gene. dipping frequency on tick burden showed no statistically significant differences when cattle of either strain were dipped either once every 2 or 3 weeks in the dry and wet seasons. however, tarime cattle had higher (p 0.05). during the study period, 20% (5/25) of sukuma cattle contracted ecf, but none of the tarime cattle showed clinical signs for the disease. the differences between the two strains shown in terms of pp and t. parva parasite prevalence may indicate the ability of individual cattle to resist tick infestation and ecf infection under natural challenge. higher antibody levels but lower parasite prevalence attained by tarime cattle, suggests inherent ability of tarime cattle to resist clinical development of ecf infection, but to remain as t. parva carriers. key words: carrier state, theileria parva, ticks, seropositivity.
rewritten: this study aimed to assess tick infestation levels and immune response parameters associated with resistance to east coast fever (ecf) in tarime and sukuma cattle. researchers examined tick burden, packed cell volume (pcv), theileria parva-specific antibody percent positivity (pp), and the prevalence of t. parva parasites in relation to dipping frequency, cattle strains, and seasonal variations. a total of 50 experimental cattle were evaluated. tick burden was measured by counting ticks on the entire body, while antibody pp was determined using a polymorphic immunodominant molecule (pim)-based t. parva enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (elisa), and t. parva parasite prevalence was detected using a nested polymerase chain reaction (pcr) targeting the p104 gene. the frequency of dipping—whether cattle were dipped every 2 or 3 weeks during the dry and wet seasons—did not result in statistically significant differences in tick burden for either strain. however, tarime cattle exhibited higher (p 0.05) values. over the study period, 20% (5 out of 25) of sukuma cattle developed ecf, whereas none of the tarime cattle showed clinical signs of the disease. the observed differences in pp and t. parva parasite prevalence between the two strains may reflect their individual capacities to resist tick infestation and ecf infection under natural conditions. specifically, the higher antibody levels yet lower parasite prevalence seen in tarime cattle suggest an inherent ability to prevent the clinical development of ecf while still potentially harboring t. parva parasites. keywords: carrier state, theileria parva, ticks, seropositivity.

raw: ber estimation from measured evm values is shown experimentally for qpsk and 16qam optical signals with 28 gbd. various impairments, such as gain imbalance, quadrature error and timing skew, are introduced into the transmitted signal in order to evaluate the robustness of the method. the evm was measured using two different realtime sampling systems and the evm measurement accuracy is discussed.
rewritten: experimental results demonstrate bit error rate estimation derived from measured evm values for qpsk and 16qam optical signals operating at 28 gbd. to assess the robustness of the approach, various impairments—such as gain imbalance, quadrature error, and timing skew—were intentionally introduced into the transmitted signal. the evm was then quantified using two different real-time sampling systems, and the accuracy of these evm measurements is analyzed.

raw: this thesis entitled " financial analysis and economic assessment panel to the credit union inti wasi inti ltda coop saraguro city , 2013-2014 . " ; allowed to know the economic and financial position of the cooperative through the analysis and evaluation of data presented in the financial statements ; its importance is that it allows to be aware of the levels of profitability , risk, competitiveness and sustainability of activities over time; to determine the effectiveness of financial management , the success of the cooperative and the maximization of value for the shareholders. the development work was based on the fulfillment of the overall objective to analyze and assess economic and financial support to the credit union inti wasi ltda. saraguro city during the period 2013-2014. similarly, the fulfillment of specific objectives was necessary: apply and interpret financial indicators of solvency, profitability, indebtedness, activity, and the governing body established by the regulator, which determined the economic and financial situation of the cooperative and were compared standards issued by regulators. it's that same assess the financial structure, working capital, cash conversion cycle and equilibrium point and financial risk, to diagnose the financial position of the cooperative and its economic and financial performance measure. finally prepare the final report and argue easily understood by managers and directors in which they presented the results of the analysis and economic evaluation and financial situation of the cooperative.
rewritten: this thesis, titled "financial analysis and economic assessment panel to the credit union inti wasi inti ltda coop saraguro city, 2013-2014," provides insights into the cooperative's economic and financial state by analyzing and evaluating information from its financial statements. its significance lies in revealing the levels of profitability, risk, competitiveness, and long-term sustainability of its activities, as well as assessing the efficiency of financial management, the cooperative’s overall success, and the enhancement of shareholder value. the study was driven by the main objective of analyzing and assessing the economic and financial support given to the credit union inti wasi ltda in saraguro city during the period 2013-2014. this involved several specific objectives: to apply and interpret key financial indicators related to solvency, profitability, indebtedness, and activity, and to use the governance standards set by the regulator to determine the cooperative's economic and financial condition by comparing them against the benchmark standards. additionally, the research evaluated the financial structure, working capital, cash conversion cycle, break-even point, and financial risk to diagnose the cooperative’s overall financial performance. ultimately, the project culminated in a final report, designed in clear and straightforward language, which presented the results of the economic evaluation and financial analysis to managers and directors.

raw: magnetic valve controllable reactor (mcr) is a kind of dynamic reactive power compensation device. the exciting current of mcr is one of the important indexes of operating condition of mcr. this paper firstly analyzed the principle of mcr, and deduced the relationship between the exciting current and the output current, verified by matlab/simulink. and then an effective method to detect the exciting current was proposed by this paper. the method has the advantages of optical fiber isolation, high detection precision, strong anti-interference, etc. finally, a 220v/1200var mcr was experimented in detail. the results demonstrate the accuracy of the theoretical analysis.
rewritten: the magnetic valve controllable reactor (mcr) is a type of dynamic reactive power compensation device, with its excitation current being a key indicator of its operational state. this paper begins by analyzing the operating principles of the mcr and deriving the relationship between the excitation current and the output current, a relationship that is then confirmed using matlab/simulink. following this, the paper proposes an effective method for detecting the excitation current, which offers benefits such as optical fiber isolation, high precision, and strong resistance to interference. lastly, a detailed experiment on a 220v/1200var mcr demonstrates the accuracy of the theoretical analysis.

raw: x-ray single-photon ionization and fragmentation of the chiral molecule halothane from a racemic mixture have been investigated using the cold target recoil ion momentum spectroscopy technique. two important facets related to the core ionization of this species are examined: firstly, the distinction of enantiomers (mirror isomers) and the determination of absolute configuration on a single-molecule level by four-body coulomb explosion; secondly, the interplay of site-selective excitation and fragmentation patterns. these results are easily transferable to other molecular species and show the wealth of features that can be investigated by coincidence spectroscopy of chiral molecules.
rewritten: x-ray single-photon ionization and fragmentation of the chiral molecule halothane from a racemic mixture have been studied using the cold target recoil ion momentum spectroscopy technique. two key aspects concerning the core ionization of this compound are explored: first, the differentiation of enantiomers (mirror isomers) and the determination of absolute configuration on a single-molecule level through four-body coulomb explosion; second, the influence of site-selective excitation on the resulting fragmentation patterns. these findings can be readily applied to other molecular species and demonstrate the wide range of features that can be examined using coincidence spectroscopy of chiral molecules.

raw: the construction of traditional housing has solved the problems of habitability in the rural community by improving the quality of life of this sector for their socioeconomic level it is essential to give them a decent and comfortable housing, taking into account the effects that are generated in manufacturing building materials essential elements for building that generate benefits for the economy and equally farms inadequate natural resources daily show negative the environment impacts which have become less important in the effort to solve the many housing needs of the inhabitants of the regions of colombia.
rewritten: traditional housing construction has addressed habitability challenges in rural communities by enhancing their overall quality of life within their socioeconomic context. it is crucial to provide decent and comfortable homes, especially when considering that the production of essential building materials benefits the economy. meanwhile, the improper use of natural resources continues to harm the environment, although these negative impacts have become a secondary concern compared to meeting the substantial housing needs of colombia’s regional populations.

raw: the utility model discloses a gas heater and tolerance of discharging fume adjusting device thereof, the device includes: acquire the module, acquire the module and be used for acquireing the parameter of electric machine in the fan, flue gas monitoring module, flue gas monitoring module are used for establishhing in real time the discharge fume tolerance of amount of wind model with the exhaust port that acquires gas heater according to the parameter of electric machine, control module, control module are used for through dc -to -ac converter accommodate motor parameter keeping invariable so that discharge fume tolerance in real time to prevent that external wind -fall from irritating. therefore, the utility model discloses gas heater's the tolerance adjusting device that discharges fume can reach discharge fume the in real time purpose of tolerance of closed -cycle control, prevents that external wind -fall from irritating to effectively avoid irritating the phenomenon that the gas heater that leads to extinguishes because of external wind -fall, the security that has improved gas heater has promoted user experience.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a gas heater equipped with a device for adjusting fume discharge tolerance. this device includes an acquisition module that retrieves parameters from the fan’s electric motor, and a flue gas monitoring module that uses these parameters to continuously determine the appropriate fume discharge tolerance at the exhaust port. additionally, a control module employs a dc-to-ac converter to maintain stable motor parameters, ensuring that the discharge tolerance remains constant and effectively counters the effects of external wind gusts. as a result, the gas heater can achieve closed-loop control of fume discharge in real time, thereby preventing external winds from extinguishing the heater and significantly enhancing its safety and user experience.

raw: this guide provides you with the resources you need to find credible websites, reference works, and general interest books for your encyclopedia entry assignment.
rewritten: this guide equips you with the resources necessary to identify credible websites, reference materials, and general-interest books for your encyclopedia entry assignment.

raw: the present subject matter relates to generation of a geometry corresponding to a cross-sectional profile of an object. in an example method, a sketch corresponding to the cross-sectional profile of the object is received by a computing device (102). the sketch is drawn by moving a pointer over a display unit (104) of the computing device (102). pixel points generated by the display unit (104) are obtained by the computing device (102). the pixel points correspond to the sketch. low-speed pixel points, from amongst the pixel points, are joined with straight lines. the low-speed pixel points are the pixel points at which a speed of drawing of the sketch is below a predefined speed threshold. the straight lines are processed by the computing device (102) into the geometry of the object.
rewritten: this subject relates to creating a geometric model that corresponds to an object’s cross-sectional profile. in one example, a computing device (102) receives a sketch representing this cross-sectional profile. the sketch is created by moving a pointer over a display unit (104) on the computing device (102), and the device captures the resulting pixel points from the display. among these pixel points, those recorded at drawing speeds below a predefined threshold—the low-speed pixel points—are connected with straight lines. the computing device (102) then processes these straight lines to generate the object's geometry.

raw: an outer rearview mirror and side turning lamp structure of automobile with flow lamp function including a light source of side turning lamp, a light source of flow lamp and a power drive assembly, the light source of side turning lamp and light source of flow lamp both connect with the power drive assembly, and the power drive assembly having a power circuit module, a control circuit module, a drive circuit module of side turning lamp and a drive circuit module of flow lamp, and the power circuit module simultaneously connects with the drive circuit module of side turning lamp and drive circuit module of flow lamp by the control circuit module. the outer rearview mirror and side turning lamp structure of automobile added the flow lamp function, and different degrees of brightness change of the light source, and the control methods of the side turning lamp with flow lamp.
rewritten: an automotive outer rearview mirror and side turning lamp assembly equipped with a flow lamp function comprises a side turning lamp light source, a flow lamp light source, and a power drive assembly. both light sources connect to the power drive assembly, which includes a power circuit module, a control circuit module, a drive circuit module for the side turning lamp, and a drive circuit module for the flow lamp. the power circuit module is simultaneously linked to both drive circuit modules via the control circuit module. this configuration integrates the flow lamp function into the assembly, allowing the light source brightness to vary and providing control methods for the side turning lamp with the flow lamp feature.

raw: this paper investigates a dynamic buffer management scheme for qos control of multimedia services in beyond 3g wireless systems. the scheme is studied in the context of the state-of-the-art 3.5g system i.e. the high speed downlink packet access (hsdpa) which enhances 3g umts to support high-speed packet switched services. unlike earlier systems, umts-evolved systems from hsdpa and beyond incorporate mechanisms such as packet scheduling and harq in the base station necessitating data buffering at the air interface. this introduces a potential bottleneck to end-to-end communication. hence, buffer management at the air interface is crucial for end-to-end qos support of multimedia services with multiplexed parallel diverse flows such as video and data in the same end-user session. the dynamic buffer management scheme for hsdpa multimedia sessions with aggregated real-time and non real-time flows is investigated via extensive hsdpa simulations. the impact of the scheme on end-to-end traffic performance is evaluated with an example multimedia session comprising a real-time streaming flow concurrent with tcp-based non real-time flow. results demonstrate that the scheme can guarantee the end-to-end qos of the real-time streaming flow, whilst simultaneously protecting the non real-time flow from starvation resulting in improved end-to-end throughput performance.
rewritten: this paper explores a dynamic buffer management approach designed to control qos for multimedia services in wireless systems beyond 3g. the study is conducted in the context of the cutting-edge 3.5g system, specifically the high speed downlink packet access (hsdpa), which upgrades 3g umts to support high-speed, packet-switched services. unlike earlier systems, umts-evolved networks from hsdpa onward include mechanisms such as packet scheduling and harq at the base station, which require data buffering at the air interface. this buffering can become a potential bottleneck for end-to-end communication. as a result, effective buffer management at the air interface is vital for ensuring end-to-end qos in multimedia services that involve multiplexed, parallel flows of diverse types (for example, video and data within a single end-user session). the paper evaluates the dynamic buffer management scheme for hsdpa multimedia sessions—where real-time and non-real-time flows are aggregated—through extensive hsdpa simulations. the impact of this scheme on the overall traffic performance is further assessed using an example multimedia session that includes a real-time streaming flow running concurrently with a tcp-based non-real-time flow. the simulation results indicate that the scheme can secure the end-to-end qos of the real-time streaming flow while also preventing the non-real-time flow from suffering starvation, thereby improving overall end-to-end throughput performance.

raw: abstract inhibitory control is an important part of successfully functioning in society. low inhibitory control is related to behavior problems in adolescents. however the etiology of this relationship is unclear. in this study we use a genetically informative twin sample to disentangle the genetic and environmental effects that influence this association. we conclude that there is a significant genetic component in the relationship between inhibitory control and externalizing problems, but not internalizing problems.
rewritten: abstract: inhibitory control plays a crucial role in effective social functioning. lower levels of inhibitory control have been linked to behavioral issues during adolescence, although the underlying causes of this connection remain unclear. this study employs a twin sample with genetic information to separate and examine the genetic and environmental factors influencing this relationship. our findings indicate that there is a significant genetic contribution linking inhibitory control to externalizing problems, while no such genetic link was found with internalizing problems.

raw: docetaxel comprises one of the most effective anti-cancer drugs despite of serious side effects. liposomes encapsulation is practically feasible to deliver the drug. however, due to the significant hydrophobicity, docetaxel will be integrated into the lipid bilayer resulting in poor encapsulation capacity. here, we evaluated a remote loading strategy using a solubility gradient made between the two solvents for 7-glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel, which has enhanced water solubility of docetaxel with a coupled glucose moiety. therefore, 7-glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel was more effectively encapsulated into liposomes with 71.0% of encapsulation efficiency than docetaxel. while 7-glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel exhibited 90.9% of tubulin stabilisation activity of docetaxel, 7-glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel encapsulated in liposomes significantly inhibited the growth of tumour in vivo with side effects less than unencapsulated drug. collectively, the encapsulation of 7-glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel into liposomes by remote loading under the solubility gradient is considered to be a promising application to prepare practical drug delivery system.
rewritten: docetaxel is among the most potent anti-cancer drugs, although it comes with significant side effects. while liposome encapsulation offers a practical means of delivering the drug, its high hydrophobicity causes it to integrate into the lipid bilayer, thereby limiting encapsulation efficiency. in our study, we explored a remote loading technique that employs a solubility gradient between two solvents for 7‑glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel—a derivative of docetaxel modified with a glucose moiety to improve its water solubility. this modification allowed 7‑glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel to achieve an encapsulation efficiency of 71.0% in liposomes, markedly better than that of docetaxel itself. furthermore, despite exhibiting 90.9% of docetaxel’s tubulin-stabilizing activity, the liposomally encapsulated 7‑glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel significantly suppressed tumor growth in vivo while inducing fewer side effects compared to the non-encapsulated drug. overall, encapsulating 7‑glucosyloxyacetyldocetaxel in liposomes using remote loading under a solubility gradient appears to be a promising strategy for developing an effective drug delivery system.

raw: the invention provides a strain of chryseobacterium for efficient degradation of picolinic acid, and applications thereof, and belongs to the technical field of biological engineering. according to the present invention, a strain of picolinic acid degradation bacterial chryseobacterium is separated and screened, wherein the strain is classified and named chryseobacterium sp, is preserved in the china general microbiological culture collection center, and has the preservation number of cgmcc no.10699; and the chryseobacterium sp can effectively degrade picolinic acid in a wide ph value range or wide temperature range or in a wide ph value range and wide temperature range, can be adopted as a biological strengthening preparation so as to be used in aerobic biological treatment of picolinic acid wastewater within a weak alkali to weak acidity range and a wide temperature range, and has broad application prospects.
rewritten: this invention presents a chryseobacterium strain that effectively degrades picolinic acid, along with its various applications in the field of biological engineering. specifically, a bacterial strain capable of degrading picolinic acid has been isolated and screened, classified under the genus chryseobacterium, and deposited in the china general microbiological culture collection center with the preservation number cgmcc no. 10699. this strain efficiently degrades picolinic acid across a broad range of ph levels and temperatures. it can be employed as a biological enhancement agent in the aerobic treatment of picolinic acid wastewater, functioning effectively within a range from weakly alkaline to weakly acidic conditions and across various temperatures, thus offering wide application prospects.

raw: the invention discloses a multifunctional table used for a hotel. the table comprises a supporting base and a table top arranged on the supporting base. a telescoping support column is connected between the supporting base and the table top. the support column comprises a plurality of sleeves which are sleeved with each other in sequence. the table top comprises a plurality of sectors which are spliced together. each sector is fixedly connected with one sleeve. the lengths of sleeves reduce in sequence from inside out. the table can be changed into a desk or a storage rack according to requirement of a hotel, and the table is convenient to use.
rewritten: the invention describes a multifunctional table designed for hotel use. the table includes a supporting base with a table top mounted on it, and a telescopic support column positioned between the base and the top. this column consists of several sleeves that are sequentially nested within one another. the table top is made up of multiple sectors joined together, with each sector being securely attached to one of the sleeves. additionally, the sleeves are arranged so that their lengths decrease progressively from the innermost to the outermost. this design allows the table to be converted into a desk or a storage rack to meet various hotel requirements, ensuring its convenience and functionality.

raw: the invention discloses a human acupuncture point balance stimulator which is composed of a circular bottom plate (1) and columnar protrusions (2). the 81 columnar protrusions (2) are densely distributed in the middle of the sunny side formed by the circular bottom plate (1) in a criss-cross mode and outwards distributed from the center of the circular bottom plate (1) in a square shape. the distance between the columnar protrusions (2) is equal to the diameter of the columnar protrusions (2). the height of the columnar protrusions (2) is set according to the designated height ratio. integral combination design and one-time punch forming are adopted with metal as the material, the sunny side is aligned with human acupuncture point parts for press stimulation, the strength of acupuncture stimulation sensed by a human body changes a lot, the structure is simple, the human body feels comfortable when touching the stimulator, and the stimulator is easy to clean and convenient to carry and use; by stimulating the acupuncture points and main and collateral channels around the acupuncture points, qi-blood circulation and blood circulation are improved, and the positive effects of accumulating energy and strengthening body are achieved.
rewritten: the invention pertains to a human acupuncture point balance stimulator that includes a circular base plate (1) and a series of columnar projections (2). a total of 81 projections (2) are arranged densely on the central, sunlit surface of the circular base plate (1) in an interlaced pattern, and they extend outward from the center in a square formation. the spacing between each projection (2) is equal to the diameter of the projections. the height of each projection (2) is determined based on a predetermined height ratio. using a one-piece design formed by a single punch process and crafted from metal, the sunlit surface is positioned to align with the human acupuncture points for stimulating pressure. this configuration results in significant variations in the acupuncture stimulation perceived by a human body, a simple structure, a comfortable tactile experience, and ease of cleaning as well as portability. by stimulating the acupuncture points along with the primary and collateral channels surrounding them, the device enhances the flow of qi and blood, thereby fostering energy accumulation and overall physical strengthening.

raw: the purpose of this study was to design curriculum content structure integrating three systems of actions for 2009 revised current home economics curriculum. for the development of content structure and content elements, a development team of two home economics scholars, a cooperating team of three home economics teachers, and an expert team of 15 home economics teachers and professors were organized. phase 1 of curriculum development was developed by integrating three systems of actions with traditional home economics content areas of clothing, food, housing, consumption, and family by the development team. it is examined by the cooperating team and in phase 2 is recommended to build matrix of units of the current curriculum and the three systems of actions. the final phase of curriculum development illustrated a content structure integrating the sub-units of 2009 revised current curriculum and the three systems of action following the expert group"s professional opinion.
rewritten: this study aimed to design a curriculum content structure by integrating three systems of action into the 2009 revised current home economics curriculum. a collaborative effort involved a development team of two home economics scholars, a cooperating team of three home economics teachers, and an expert team composed of 15 home economics teachers and professors to develop the content structure and elements. in phase 1, the development team created an initial curriculum by integrating the three systems of action with conventional home economics content areas—namely clothing, food, housing, consumption, and family. this draft was then reviewed by the cooperating team, and in phase 2, they recommended constructing a matrix that linked the current curriculum units with the three systems of action. the final phase of curriculum development produced a content structure that merged the sub-units of the 2009 revised curriculum with the three action systems, reflecting the professional input of the expert group.

raw: background: tapioca has been the staple food for the people of kerala, india. recent studies show that it has become more toxic due to changes in environment. there have been few studies in recent years to observe the effects of consuming more toxic tapioca. objective: the combination of pregnancy and tapioca diet on blood pressure (bp), serum alkaline phosphatase, serum globulin and a:g ratio are studied to assess the general health and liver function. materials and methods: a descriptive cross-sectional study design was conducted in the obstetrics out-patient department of a tertiary care hospital of central kerala. the systolic and diastolic blood pressures were determined. the serum alkaline phosphatase, total proteins, and serum albumin levels were tested in a sample of blood collected under aseptic conditions. the serum globulin values were calculated from total protein and serum albumin values by subtraction. the a:g values were calculated by dividing the values of serum albumin and serum globulin. this study was conducted after obtaining ethical clearance from the institute. result: there were significant differences (p = 0.002) in the mean values of systolic and diastolic blood pressure in the third-trimester pregnancy. there was significant difference in the mean value of serum alkaline phosphatase levels in the first trimester (p = 0.007), the second trimester (p = 0.001) and the third trimester (p = 0.000001). there was significant difference in the mean value of serum globulin in the first trimester (p = 0.03), the second trimester (p = 0.0003), and the third trimester (0.000008). the a:g ratio showed significant difference in the first trimester (p = 0.0003), the second trimester (p = 0.0001), and the third trimester (p = 0.001). conclusion: the above study shows that there is significant decrease in systolic and diastolic blood pressure towards the third trimester compared to normal controls. this is comparable to other similar studies. the alkaline phosphatase levels are also comparable with the values obtained in other studies. the serum globulin values were also comparable to other similar studies. the decreasing a:g ratio values were unique to this population.
rewritten: background: in kerala, india, tapioca has traditionally been the staple food. however, recent research indicates that environmental changes have increased its toxicity, and only a few studies have examined how consuming this more toxic tapioca affects health. objective: this study investigates the impact of a tapioca-based diet during pregnancy on blood pressure (bp), serum alkaline phosphatase, serum globulin, and the albumin/globulin (a:g) ratio as indicators of overall health and liver function. materials and methods: a descriptive cross-sectional study was carried out in the obstetrics outpatient department of a tertiary care hospital in central kerala. both systolic and diastolic blood pressures were measured. blood samples were collected under aseptic conditions and tested for serum alkaline phosphatase, total proteins, and serum albumin. serum globulin levels were determined by subtracting serum albumin from total protein, and the a:g ratio was calculated by dividing serum albumin by serum globulin. the study was approved by the institute’s ethical committee. results: there were statistically significant differences (p = 0.002) in the mean systolic and diastolic blood pressures during the third trimester of pregnancy. serum alkaline phosphatase levels showed significant differences in the first (p = 0.007), second (p = 0.001), and third trimesters (p = 0.000001). similarly, serum globulin values differed significantly in the first (p = 0.03), second (p = 0.0003), and third trimesters (p = 0.000008). the a:g ratio also displayed significant changes across the first (p = 0.0003), second (p = 0.0001), and third trimesters (p = 0.001). conclusion: the study demonstrates a notable decrease in both systolic and diastolic blood pressure during the third trimester when compared with normal controls, consistent with findings from other studies. likewise, serum alkaline phosphatase and serum globulin levels were comparable to previously reported values. however, the observed decline in the a:g ratio appears to be unique to this population.

raw: rechargeable ion batteries based on the intercalation of multivalent ions are attractive due to their high energy density and structural stability. surface of cathode materials plays an important role for the electrochemical performance of the rechargeable ion batteries. in this work we calculated surface energies of (001), (110) and (111) facets with different terminations in spinel mgmn2o4 and mgni0.5mn1.5o4 cathodes. results showed clearly that atomic reconstruction occurred due to surface relaxation. the surface energies for the (001), (110) and (111) surfaces of the mgni0.5mn1.5o4 were 0.08, 0.13 and 0.11 j/m2, respectively, whereas those of the ni-doped mgmn2o4 showed less dependence on the surface structures.
rewritten: rechargeable ion batteries that utilize the intercalation of multivalent ions are appealing due to their high energy density and strong structural stability. the cathode material's surface is crucial for the electrochemical performance of these batteries. in our study, we calculated the surface energies of the (001), (110), and (111) facets, each with different terminations, in spinel mgmn2o4 and mgni0.5mn1.5o4 cathodes. our findings clearly showed that atomic reconstruction occurred as a result of surface relaxation. specifically, the surface energies of mgni0.5mn1.5o4 for the (001), (110), and (111) facets were 0.08, 0.13, and 0.11 j/m² respectively, while the ni-doped mgmn2o4 demonstrated less dependency on surface structure variations.

raw: one of the most significant researches in location-based services is the development of effective indoor localization. in this work, we propose a novel model of fingerprint localization, which divides location area into different subareas by fuzzy c-means and calculates location via relative distance fuzzy localization. in offline training stage, fuzzy c-means algorithm is used in localization model to divide localization area into different subareas and then to select the useful access points in subareas to reduce the dimensions of fingerprint. in online location stage, we use the nearest neighbor algorithm to select the subareas and to calculate the coordinate of the target point according to relative distance fuzzy localization algorithm, which converts traditional fingerprint of reference points into distance fingerprint and calculates the coordinate of the target point by fuzzy c-means algorithm. the noise and non-linear attenuation between the wireless signal and distance are taken into full conside...
rewritten: indoor localization is a pivotal area in location-based services research. in this study, we introduce an innovative fingerprint localization model that first partitions a given area into several subareas using the fuzzy c-means algorithm, then determines positions through relative distance fuzzy localization. in the offline training phase, the fuzzy c-means algorithm segments the overall area, and useful access points within each subarea are selected to reduce the dimensionality of the fingerprint data. during the online positioning phase, the nearest neighbor algorithm is employed to identify the relevant subareas, after which the target point’s coordinates are computed using the relative distance fuzzy localization method. this approach converts traditional reference point fingerprints into distance-based fingerprints and determines the target's location through the fuzzy c-means algorithm, while also fully accounting for noise and the non-linear attenuation effects between wireless signals and distance.

raw: porphyria cutanea tarda (pct) requires long-term treatment and follow-up, although many patients experience life-long remission. the aim of this cross-sectional postal survey was to describe and investigate the association between illness perception, health complaints, self-reported symptoms and distress in persons with pct. the participants perceived pct as a chronic condition with high levels of personal and treatment control. persons who reported active symptoms scored higher on perceived illness threat, total health complaints and psychological distress compared with those in remission or latent phases. however, a higher perception of illness threat and the total burden of health complaints were more closely associated with psychological distress than were perceived pct symptoms activity. this has implications for clinical consultation; dermatologists should be attentive to symptoms activity, but also recognize that patients in remission with a high perceived illness threat and multiple health complaints might be especially vulnerable to psychological distress with regards to pct.
rewritten: porphyria cutanea tarda (pct) often requires long-term management and regular monitoring, though many patients eventually enter lifelong remission. this cross-sectional postal survey aimed to describe and examine the relationship between illness perception, health complaints, self-reported symptoms, and distress among individuals with pct. participants viewed pct as a chronic condition in which they had a strong sense of personal and treatment control. those reporting active symptoms experienced higher levels of perceived illness threat, overall health complaints, and psychological distress compared to individuals in remission or latent phases. importantly, a greater perception of illness threat and a higher total burden of health complaints were more strongly linked to psychological distress than the perceived activity of pct symptoms. these findings suggest that during clinical consultations, dermatologists should consider not only symptom activity but also recognize that patients in remission who report a high perceived threat of illness and multiple health complaints may be particularly vulnerable to psychological distress concerning pct.
