raw: the pressures to transform the modell deutschland have been culminated, particularly by the triumph of the ordo-liberal coallitions. reunification process, european union regionalism, and economic liberalization waves were the historical momentum in which the ordo-liberal groups gain its legitimacy. such transformation also brought about in the context of globalizing production system which has massively insisted the german industries to transnationalize and to improve its competitiveness. the expansion of german economy arround the world went hand in hand with the massive regionalization in asia. the industrial shape since 1980s has been highly attractive for the developed countries to take part in the asian regionalization. besides presenting the political economic transformation in germany, this paper also seeks to examine the significance and the dynamics of its economic networks in asian regionalization. kata kunci: modell deutschland, transformasi ekonomi-politik, restrukturasi industrial, jejaring ekonomi, regionalisasi asia
rewritten: the drive to transform the german model reached its peak, especially with the successful rise of ordo-liberal coalitions. the processes of reunification, european union regionalism, and waves of economic liberalization provided the historical momentum that legitimized the ordo-liberal groups. this transformation also took place in the context of a globalizing production system, which compelled german industries to internationalize and enhance their competitiveness. the expansion of the german economy worldwide coincided with significant regionalization efforts in asia. since the 1980s, the industrial landscape has been highly attractive for developed countries seeking involvement in asian regionalization. in addition to outlining germany’s politico-economic transformation, this paper aims to explore the importance and dynamics of its economic networks within asian regionalization. keywords: modell deutschland, economic-political transformation, industrial restructuring, economic networks, asian regionalization.

raw: the utility model discloses a power lithium cell of high security, including casing and block, the block is including the rubber ring, from last top cap, ptc board, bursting diaphragm and the aluminium base board that down is equipped with coaxial setting in proper order in the rubber ring, be equipped with a plurality of exhaust holes on the top cap, the undercut forms the recess in the middle of the bursting diaphragm, be equipped with the through -hole in the middle of the ptc board to make the recess be linked together with the external world through through -hole, exhaust hole, the recess bottom is connected with aluminium base board spot welding, be equipped with a plurality of vents on the aluminium base board. structure through to the block is improved, the security that can improve the lithium cell, pressure in the casing exceedes the certain limit, the recess up remove with aluminium base board phase separation, and split mutually with the bursting diaphragm to messenger's casing passes through the through -hole, the exhaust hole links to each other with the external world, has improved the security greatly.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a high-security power lithium cell that comprises a casing and a block. the block contains a rubber ring into which several components are coaxially arranged in order from top to bottom: a top cap, a ptc board, a bursting diaphragm, and an aluminum base board. the top cap is provided with multiple exhaust holes. an undercut in the middle of the bursting diaphragm forms a recess, and the ptc board features a central through-hole that connects this recess to the external environment via the through-hole and exhaust holes. additionally, the bottom of the recess is spot-welded to the aluminum base board, which is also equipped with several vents. this improved structure greatly enhances the safety of the lithium cell. when the internal pressure in the casing exceeds a certain limit, the assembly allows the recess to separate from the aluminum base board. at the same time, the bursting diaphragm splits, allowing the pressurized contents to escape through the through-hole and exhaust holes, thereby significantly improving the overall safety of the cell.

raw: objective: to investigate serum neurofilament light chain (nfl) concentrations in frontotemporal dementia (ftd) and to see whether they are associated with the severity of disease. methods: serum samples were collected from 74 participants (34 with behavioral variant ftd [bvftd], 3 with ftd and motor neuron disease and 37 with primary progressive aphasia [ppa]) and 28 healthy controls. twenty-four of the ftd participants carried a pathogenic mutation in c9orf72 (9), microtubule-associated protein tau ( mapt ; 11), or progranulin ( grn ; 4). serum nfl concentrations were determined with the nf-light kit transferred onto the single-molecule array platform and compared between ftd and healthy controls and between the ftd clinical and genetic subtypes. we also assessed the relationship between nfl concentrations and measures of cognition and brain volume. results: serum nfl concentrations were higher in patients with ftd overall (mean 77.9 pg/ml [sd 51.3 pg/ml]) than controls (19.6 pg/ml [sd 8.2 pg/ml]; p c9orf72 and mapt subgroups (79.2 and 40.5 pg/ml [sd 48.2 and 20.9 pg/ml], respectively) with a trend to a higher level in the grn subgroup (138.5 pg/ml [sd 103.3 pg/ml). however, there was variability within all groups. serum concentrations correlated particularly with frontal lobe atrophy rate ( r = 0.53, p = 0.003). conclusions: increased serum nfl concentrations are seen in ftd but show wide variability within each clinical and genetic group. higher concentrations may reflect the intensity of the disease in ftd and are associated with more rapid atrophy of the frontal lobes.
rewritten: objective: this study aimed to examine serum neurofilament light chain (nfl) levels in individuals with frontotemporal dementia (ftd) and determine if these levels correlate with disease severity. methods: blood serum samples were obtained from 74 participants (34 diagnosed with behavioral variant ftd [bvftd], 3 with ftd combined with motor neuron disease, and 37 with primary progressive aphasia [ppa]) as well as 28 healthy control subjects. among the ftd participants, 24 carried a pathogenic mutation in one of three genes: c9orf72 (9 individuals), microtubule-associated protein tau (mapt; 11 individuals), or progranulin (grn; 4 individuals). serum nfl levels were quantified using the nf-light kit adapted for the single-molecule array platform. these levels were compared between the ftd group and healthy controls, as well as across different clinical presentations and genetic subtypes within ftd. additionally, the study investigated the relationship between nfl concentrations, cognitive function, and brain volume. results: overall, ftd patients exhibited higher serum nfl levels (mean 77.9 pg/ml, sd 51.3 pg/ml) compared to controls (mean 19.6 pg/ml, sd 8.2 pg/ml). within the ftd subtypes, the c9orf72 and mapt groups had mean serum nfl levels of 79.2 pg/ml (sd 48.2 pg/ml) and 40.5 pg/ml (sd 20.9 pg/ml), respectively, while the grn group showed a trend toward even higher nfl levels (mean 138.5 pg/ml, sd 103.3 pg/ml), though there was considerable variability in all groups. notably, serum nfl concentrations were significantly correlated with the rate of atrophy in the frontal lobe (r = 0.53, p = 0.003). conclusions: increased serum nfl concentrations are evident in ftd patients; however, there is significant variation within both clinical and genetic subgroups. elevated nfl levels may reflect a higher disease activity in ftd, as they are associated with a more rapid rate of frontal lobe atrophy.

raw: a direction of the first power transmission antenna is detected in a power transmission stop state from the slave power transmission apparatus after detection of a power reception apparatus, by using a master power transmission apparatus including a first power transmission device and a first power transmission antenna and a slave power transmission apparatus including a second power transmission device and a plurality of the second power transmission antennas, a second power transmission antenna in which an amount of electric power received by the power reception apparatus becomes maximum is detected in the power transmission stop state from the master power transmission apparatus, and a phase of at least one power transmission radio wave of the master power transmission apparatus and the slave power transmission apparatus is adjusted to a phase in which the amount of the electric power received by the power reception apparatus becomes maximum.
rewritten: after a power reception device is detected, the direction of the primary power transmission antenna is determined while the slave power transmission apparatus is in a power transmission stop state. this process involves a master power transmission apparatus—comprising a primary power transmission device and a corresponding antenna—and a slave power transmission apparatus that includes a secondary power transmission device and several secondary power transmission antennas. while the master apparatus is also in a power transmission stop state, the secondary antenna that enables the power reception device to receive the highest amount of electric power is identified. finally, the phases of at least one of the power transmission radio waves from both the master and slave apparatuses are adjusted to the phase that maximizes the electric power received by the power reception device.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a display control unit capable of controlling a display device to display a piece of information appropriate for an environmental condition based on the environmental condition of the display device, a display device including the display control unit, and a control method of the display unit.solution: the display control unit includes: a main power supply; an auxiliary power supply 12; a first detection section 49 that detects an output from the main power supply; and a second detection section 48 that detects ambient brightness. on the basis of a detection result by the first detection section 49 and a detection result by the second detection section 48, the drive by the main power supply is switched to a drive by the auxiliary power supply 12, and the display on the display unit 31 is also changed.selected drawing: figure 4
rewritten: problem to be solved: to offer a display control unit that manages a display device, ensuring that it shows the appropriate information based on its current environmental condition. this includes a display device that incorporates the display control unit and a method for controlling the display unit. solution: the display control unit is comprised of a main power supply, an auxiliary power supply 12, a first detection section 49 that monitors the output of the main power supply, and a second detection section 48 that measures ambient brightness. based on the readings from the first and second detection sections, the system switches from the main power supply to the auxiliary power supply 12, and the information on the display unit 31 is adjusted accordingly. selected drawing: figure 4.

raw: the invention relates to an assembly line type conveying mechanism for a filling machine. the assembly line type conveying mechanism for the filling machine comprises a conveying section and a discharge section. the assembly line type conveying mechanism for the filling machine is characterized in that the conveying section comprises a plurality of sections of first steel channels with horizontal surfaces, the discharge section comprises second steel channels with slope-shaped surfaces, the head end of each second steel channel is connected with the tail end of the corresponding first steel channel, and the tail end of each second steel channel is provided with a stop block. the head end, facing the corresponding second steel channel, of each stop block is arc, and each arc surface is provided with an eraser. a plurality of carrier rollers are evenly distributed on the first steel channels and the second steel channels, and the carrier rollers are connected through chains. the head end of each first steel channel is provided with a motor, and the power output end of each motor is connected with the corresponding chain. a plurality of leg cups are installed at the bottoms of the first steel channels and the second steel channels. according to the assembly line type conveying mechanism for the filling machine, the stop blocks are arranged in the discharge area to prevent filled products from turning over, and product packages are protected against damage.
rewritten: the invention pertains to an assembly line-style conveying mechanism for a filling machine, comprising a conveying section and a discharge section. in this design, the conveying section includes multiple segments of primary steel channels with horizontal surfaces, while the discharge section features secondary steel channels with inclined surfaces. each secondary channel is connected at its head to the tail of its corresponding primary channel, and a stop block is positioned at the tail end of every secondary channel. the stop block’s head, which faces the secondary channel, is curved, and an eraser is mounted on each curved surface. uniformly distributed along both the primary and secondary channels, a series of carrier rollers are connected by chains. motors are installed at the head end of each primary channel, with the output of each motor linked to the corresponding chain. moreover, leg cups are attached to the bottoms of both channel types. this arrangement of stop blocks in the discharge area prevents the filled products from overturning, thereby safeguarding the product packages from damage.

raw: the article presented here, demonstrates analytical design of a triple vertically coupled optical microring resonator (tvcomrr) with asymmetrical rings is theoretically designed by applying delay line signal processing method. the frequency response characteristic along with its corresponding group delay characteristic and dispersion characteristic are determined. the performance analysis of tvcomrr with regard to free spectral range (fsr), crosstalk and fwhm are obtained from the performance characteristics.
rewritten: the article describes the theoretical design of a triple vertically coupled optical microring resonator (tvcomrr) with asymmetrical rings, employing the delay line signal processing method. it outlines the derivation of the device's frequency response, group delay, and dispersion characteristics, and provides an analysis of its performance in terms of free spectral range (fsr), crosstalk, and full width at half maximum (fwhm).

raw: research on the phenomenon of business model (&) innovation is taking place in related perspectives. next to innovation and entrepreneurship literature, theories of firm strategy closely relate to the ongoing conceptualization of the business model concept. we first review in this article the linkage between the business model, innovating the business model and firm strategy in extant literature. we focus specifically on the explanatory power of the business model concept to develop insights for strategic firm behaviour, and we explore the role of business model innovation as analytic perspective for identifying sources of firm performance. building on this conceptual overview, we subsequently present how the articles in this special issue contribute in the light of strategy theory to the understanding of the phenomenon of business model innovation. finally, we develop some themes for future research on the topic.
rewritten: research on business model innovation is advancing from several related perspectives. along with literature on innovation and entrepreneurship, theories in firm strategy are closely tied to the evolving concept of the business model. in this article, we begin by examining the connection between the business model, its innovation, and firm strategy as discussed in existing literature. we specifically highlight how the business model concept can explain strategic firm behavior and explore business model innovation as a framework for identifying sources of firm performance. building on this conceptual overview, we then demonstrate how the articles in this special issue, viewed through a strategy theory lens, enhance our understanding of business model innovation. finally, we outline several themes for future research on this topic.

raw: objective: the present study analyzed the geographical location and prognostic, clinical, physiological, and biochemical factors associated with breast cancer (bc) in women based on their treatment. methods: we conducted a retrospective cohort study encompassing a 5-year follow-up period of 114 women from rural and urban areas who were diagnosed with bc in 2009 at the state cancer center (cecan) in xalapa, veracruz, mexico. the probability of survival was calculated using the kaplan-meier estimator and log rank test with a confidence interval of 95%. we determined the prognostic factors in a multivariate analysis using the cox proportional hazards model. the point estimate was the hazard ratio (hr) and 95% confidence interval (ci). results: the overall survival ratio for the study participants was 68% and 63% after 52 and 60 months, respectively. the lowest survival ratio corresponded to clinical stages iiib (38%) and iv (10%) and patients showing tumor cell metastasis (24%). there were significant differences between groups (p<0.001), including women under 40 years of age (36%, p<0.003) and those with positive hr (83%, p=0.006). women who received adjuvant treatment and had a tumor size less than 2 cm lived longer (75%, p<0.001). the multivariate analysis identified a number of prognostic factors that are unfavorable for women with bc, including a diagnosis of clinical stage iv (hazard ratio=11.88; 95% ci=2.88-44.88) and the presence of metastasis (hazard ratio=4.95; 95% ci=1.78-13.76). conclusion: general tumor characteristics, such as metastasis, disease stage and family history, are important for survival and can serve as prognostic factors for bc patients. moreover, the lower survival of women less than 40 years of age should be considered as a decision-making factor when selecting from treatment options.
rewritten: objective: this study examined how geographical location and various prognostic, clinical, physiological, and biochemical factors relate to treatment outcomes in women diagnosed with breast cancer. methods: we performed a retrospective cohort study with a 5-year follow-up that included 114 women from both rural and urban settings who were diagnosed with breast cancer in 2009 at the state cancer center (cecan) in xalapa, veracruz, mexico. survival probabilities were estimated using the kaplan-meier method and compared with the log-rank test, utilizing a 95% confidence interval. prognostic factors were assessed through multivariate analysis with the cox proportional hazards model, with hazard ratios (hr) and corresponding 95% confidence intervals (ci) serving as our estimates. results: overall, the survival rates for study participants were 68% at 52 months and 63% at 60 months. the lowest survival rates were observed in patients at clinical stages iiib (38%) and iv (10%), as well as in those with metastatic tumor cells (24%). statistically significant differences were noted between groups (p<0.001); this included women under 40 years of age (36%, p<0.003) and those with positive hormone receptor status (83%, p=0.006). additionally, women who underwent adjuvant treatment and had tumors smaller than 2 cm experienced longer survival (75%, p<0.001). the multivariate analysis identified several negative prognostic factors for women with breast cancer, notably a clinical stage iv diagnosis (hr=11.88; 95% ci=2.88–44.88) and the presence of metastasis (hr=4.95; 95% ci=1.78–13.76). conclusion: key tumor characteristics, such as the presence of metastasis, disease stage, and family history, are crucial for determining survival and may serve as prognostic indicators for breast cancer patients. additionally, the reduced survival observed in women under 40 should be considered when deciding on treatment options.

raw: the present invention discloses a non-network garage map path planning method. the method comprises the following steps: s1: employing an intelligent terminal to scan two-dimensional codes, and obtaining the garage electronic map information and terminal codes, wherein the intelligent terminal is provided with a garage navigation electronic map application program; s2: employing the intelligent terminal to scan the two-dimensional codes once again, and obtaining the starting point codes; and s3: performing the path planning of the garage navigation electronic map application program according to the starting point codes, the terminal point codes and the garage electronic map obtained by the step s1 and the step s2. the non-network garage map path planning method provides convenience for people to realize path planning in the garage and find vehicles in the garage; and moreover, the method is simple in enforcement mode and low in cost, does not need the network when the path planning is realized in the garage, is convenient to use, and solves the defect that there is no network and no signal in the garage.
rewritten: the present invention relates to a non-network method for planning paths on a garage map. the method includes the following steps: 1. an intelligent terminal scans qr codes to retrieve the electronic map information of the garage and the associated terminal codes. (here, the intelligent terminal is equipped with a garage navigation electronic map application.) 2. the intelligent terminal scans qr codes a second time to obtain starting point codes. 3. using the starting point codes, terminal point codes, and the electronic garage map from the previous steps, the application performs path planning. this non-network approach simplifies navigation within a garage, making it easier for users to plan routes and locate their vehicles. additionally, the method is cost-effective and straightforward to implement without requiring a network connection, effectively addressing issues related to the absence of network or signal in garages.

raw: multispectral imaging (msi) can provide information about tissue oxygenation, perfusion and potentially function during surgery. in this paper we present a novel, near real-time technique for intrinsic measurements of total haemoglobin (thb) and blood oxygenation (so2) in tissue using only rgb images from a stereo laparoscope. the high degree of spectral overlap between channels makes inference of haemoglobin concentration challenging, non-linear and under constrained. we decompose the problem into two constrained linear sub-problems and show that with tikhonov regularisation the estimation significantly improves, giving robust estimation of the thb. we demonstrate by using the co-registered stereo image data from two cameras it is possible to get robust so2 estimation as well. our method is closed from, providing computational efficiency even with multiple cameras. the method we present requires only spectral response calibration of each camera, without modification of existing laparoscopic imaging hardware. we validate our technique on synthetic data from monte carlo simulation % of light transport through soft tissue containing submerged blood vessels and further, in vivo, on a multispectral porcine data set.
rewritten: multispectral imaging (msi) can offer insights into tissue oxygenation, perfusion, and even function during surgery. in this study, we introduce a new, near-real-time method for intrinsically measuring total hemoglobin (thb) and blood oxygenation (so2) in tissue using only rgb images captured by a stereo laparoscope. the significant spectral overlap between rgb channels makes inferring hemoglobin concentration a challenging, nonlinear, and under-constrained problem. to address this, we break the issue into two constrained linear sub-problems and demonstrate that applying tikhonov regularization greatly enhances the estimation, resulting in robust thb measurements. moreover, by utilizing co-registered stereo images from two cameras, our approach also yields reliable so2 estimates. the method is self-contained and computationally efficient, even when multiple cameras are used. it requires only the spectral response calibration of each camera, without needing any changes to existing laparoscopic imaging hardware. we validate our technique using synthetic data from monte carlo simulations that model light transport through soft tissue with embedded blood vessels, and further confirm its effectiveness in vivo on a multispectral porcine dataset.

raw: both brachial blood pressure (bp) level and its variability (bpv) have significant association with cardiac structure and function. recent studies indicated that central bp was superior to brachial bp in the association with cardiac abnormities. however, it remains unknown that whether central bpv is better associated with cardiac structure and function. we were, therefore, aimed to investigate and compare central and brachial bpv, in terms of the identification of cardiac abnormities. 445 consecutive outpatientsfor consultation on hypertension were recruited and 203 participants undergoing echocardiography were included in the present analysis. twenty-four hour ambulatory bp was measured by a validated bp monitor (mobil-o-graph, stolberg, germany). left ventricular (lv) mass and lv diastolic dysfunction (lvdd) were evaluated by echocardiography. after adjustment for age, gender, body mass index, smoke, hypertension and mean 24hr bp, central average real variability (arv), but not 24hr weighted standard deviation (wsd, brachial or central) or brachial arv, was significantly associated with lv mass index (lvmi) (p = 0.02). similar result was found in multivariate logistic regression. only central arv significantly associated with lv hypertrophy (p = 0.04). as for lvdd, not the brachial or central 24hr wsd, but the central and brachial arv, associated with lvdd significantly, with or = 2.28 and or = 2.36, respectively. in summary, central bpv, especially the central arv, shows its superiority over brachial bpvs, in terms of cardiac structure and function.
rewritten: both brachial blood pressure (bp) levels and their variability (bpv) are significantly linked to cardiac structure and function. recent studies have suggested that central bp has a stronger association with cardiac abnormalities than brachial bp. however, it remains unclear whether central bp variability correlates more closely with cardiac structure and function. therefore, we aimed to investigate and compare central and brachial bpv in identifying cardiac abnormalities. in our study, 445 consecutive outpatients consulting for hypertension were recruited, and 203 participants who underwent echocardiography were included in the analysis. twenty-four-hour ambulatory bp was measured using a validated bp monitor (mobil-o-graph, stolberg, germany). left ventricular (lv) mass and lv diastolic dysfunction (lvdd) were evaluated via echocardiography. after adjusting for age, gender, body mass index, smoking, hypertension, and mean 24-hour bp, central average real variability (arv)—but not the 24-hour weighted standard deviation (wsd) for either brachial or central bp, nor brachial arv—was significantly associated with the lv mass index (lvmi) (p = 0.02). similar findings were observed in multivariate logistic regression; only central arv was significantly associated with lv hypertrophy (p = 0.04). regarding lvdd, both central and brachial arv, rather than the brachial or central 24-hour wsd, were significantly associated with lvdd, with odds ratios of 2.28 and 2.36 respectively. in summary, central bpv—particularly the central arv—demonstrates superiority over brachial bpv in its association with cardiac structure and function.

raw: biotic resistance and disturbance are fundamental processes influencing plant invasion outcomes; however, the role of consumers in regulating the establishment and spread of plant invaders and how disturbance modifies biotic resistance by consumers is unclear. we document that fire in combination with experimental exclusion of rodent consumers shifted a native desert shrubland to a low-diversity, invasive annual grassland dominated by bromus tectorum (cheatgrass). in contrast, burned plots with rodents present suppressed invasion by cheatgrass and developed into a more diverse forb community. rodents created strong biotic resistance to the establishment of aggressive plant invaders likely through seed and seedling predation, which had cascading effects on plant competition and plant community diversity. fire mediated its positive effects on plant invaders through native plant removal and by decreasing the abundance and diversity of the rodent community. the experimental disruption of plant and consumer-mediated biotic resistance of plant invaders using fire and rodent exclusion treatments provides strong evidence that native plants and rodents are important regulators of plant invasion dynamics and plant biodiversity in our study system. while rodents conferred strong resistance to invasion in our study system, fluctuations in rodent populations due to disturbance and climatic events may provide windows of opportunity for exotic plant species to escape biotic resistance by rodent consumers and initiate invasions.
rewritten: both biotic resistance and disturbance are key drivers shaping the outcomes of plant invasions. yet, it remains uncertain how consumer activities affect the establishment and spread of invasive plants and how disturbances modify consumer-driven biotic resistance. in our study, we found that a combination of fire and the experimental removal of rodent consumers transformed a native desert shrubland into a low-diversity invasive annual grassland dominated by bromus tectorum (cheatgrass). in contrast, burned plots where rodents were present showed suppressed cheatgrass invasion and instead developed a more diverse community of forbs. it appears that rodents provided strong biotic resistance against aggressive plant invaders—likely through their predation on seeds and seedlings—which in turn influenced plant competition and overall community diversity. additionally, fire enhanced the success of plant invaders by eliminating native vegetation and reducing both the abundance and diversity of the rodent community. our experimental manipulation, which disrupted both plant- and consumer-mediated biotic resistance through fire and rodent exclusion, strongly supports the idea that native plants and rodents play crucial roles in regulating plant invasion dynamics and maintaining plant biodiversity in our study area. however, even though rodents offered robust resistance against invasion in our system, variations in their populations due to disturbance and climatic events might create opportunities for exotic plant species to overcome rodent-mediated biotic resistance and establish invasions.

raw: problem to be solved: to improve efficiency in reading a stimulable plate.solution: a stimulable plate reading device includes: at least one stimulable plate 12 that holds image data and has two surfaces on the opposite sides to each other; irradiation means 20 for uniformly irradiate a first surface of the two surfaces on the opposite sides to each other of the at least one stimulable plate with light emitted within a first wavelength range, and causing the at least one stimulable plate 12 to perform, through the irradiation, both emission of light within a second wavelength range and scattering of the light within a first wavelength range by stimulable light emission; a filter 22 that does not transmit the light scattered in the first wavelength and can transmit the light emitted within a second wavelength range, and faces a second surface of the two surfaces on the opposite sides to each other of the at least one stimulable plate 12; and a detector 24 that is constituted of a two-dimensional array of pixels that detects the transmitted light and acquires image data from the light.selected drawing: figure 1
rewritten: objective: enhance the efficiency of reading a stimulable plate. solution: a stimulable plate reading device comprises the following components: • at least one stimulable plate (12) that holds image data and features two opposite surfaces. • irradiation means (20) which uniformly irradiate one of the two opposite surfaces of the stimulable plate with light from a first wavelength range. this irradiation prompts the plate to both emit light in a second wavelength range and scatter light within the first wavelength range via stimulable light emission. • a filter (22) that faces the second surface of the stimulable plate. this filter blocks the light scattered in the first wavelength range while permitting the light emitted in the second wavelength range to pass through. • a detector (24) composed of a two-dimensional pixel array that captures the transmitted light and acquires the corresponding image data. selected diagram: figure 1.

raw: field: physics.substance: test-chart of every channel in the combined optoelectronic system (coes) is composed by a series of lines creating the high spatial frequency (hsf) toward the mphr line and stretched toward frame scan. test-chart lines hsf images in the mphr plane approximate to nyquist frequency but are not equal thereto. phase shift of said test-chart lines images over its entire length relative to mphr elements are selected to exceed the mhpr element sizes some 2-10 times. pair of lines with low spatial frequency (lsf) are applied on the edges of both lines. left boundaries of hsf central lines of the test-chart are located on one vertical line. lower boundary of test-chart in channel 1 and upper boundary of channel 2 contact each other. at control over channels, measured is the shift of loops and instabilities in the series of hsf signals to calculate the deflection of optical axis of every coes channel in the direction of line scan. calculated is the swing of signal from lsf lines in both channels to calculate from their amplitude the angle turn of channels boresights in direction perpendicular to line scan.effect: higher accuracy of measurement of residual spatial misalignment of channels, location of boundaries and mutual turn of the fields.9 dwg
rewritten: field: physics substance: in the combined optoelectronic system (coes), each channel’s test chart is made up of a series of lines that generate high spatial frequencies (hsf) oriented toward the mphr line and extended in the direction of the frame scan. the hsf test chart line images in the mphr plane approach the nyquist frequency but do not exactly match it. their phase shifts along the entire length, relative to the mphr elements, are intentionally set to be approximately 2–10 times larger than the sizes of the mphr elements. additionally, a pair of low spatial frequency (lsf) lines are positioned at the edges of both lines. the left edges of the central hsf test chart lines are aligned along one vertical line, while the lower boundary of channel 1 and the upper boundary of channel 2 make contact. during channel control, measurements are taken of the loop shifts and instabilities in the hsf signal series to determine the deflection of the optical axis for each coes channel in the direction of the line scan. the swing in the lsf signal amplitudes from both channels is also computed to assess the angular deviation of the channels’ boresights in a direction perpendicular to the line scan. effect: this method enhances the precision in measuring the residual spatial misalignment between channels, allows for exact boundary detection, and accurately determines the mutual angular displacement of the fields.

raw: the utility model relates to an air compressor machine with alarm device, including case body, manometer and alarm device, the manometer sets up the surface at case body, an output pressure for showing the air compressor machine, the output port signal connection of alarm device and air compressor machine, as long as output pressure is in security?range, the alarm does not have any unusually, in case atmospheric pressure output surpasss security?range, the scintillation of air compressor machine alarm device red light is sent flute sound and is reported to the police, be convenient for in time discover and handle, prevent the unusual production of production, the stability of equipment is improved, and assurance reliability of an item.
rewritten: this utility model discloses an air compressor machine equipped with an alarm device. the machine comprises a case body, a manometer, and an alarm device. the manometer is mounted on the case body’s surface and displays the air compressor’s output pressure. the alarm device is connected to the air compressor via the output port signal. when the output pressure remains within the safe range, the alarm remains inactive; however, if the atmospheric pressure exceeds the safe range, the alarm device activates by flashing a red light, emitting a flute-like sound, and sending a notification to the police. this design facilitates timely detection and resolution of issues, prevents production anomalies, improves equipment stability, and ensures overall product reliability.

raw: background the relationship between mental health and obesity has been studied in different types of research studies as it is considered very important. a lot of research studies have proposed several theories and mechanisms on how the two are linked, which should be reviewed and considered in the management plan of psychiatric disorders. objectives the aim of this study was to find out the prevalence and correlates of obesity and overweight in a sample of psychiatric patients (inpatients and outpatients) with no history of pharmacological treatment in al-hussein university hospital. patients and methods this cross-sectional study was conducted during a 6-month-period on 130 psychiatric patients who were subjected to a semistructured clinical interview according to dsm-iv-tr criteria to diagnose psychiatric disorders. obesity assessment was carried out by measuring the weight (kg) using a scale and measuring the height (m) to calculate the bmi, which is based on the bmi equation wt (kg)/ht2 (m2). results the prevalence of obesity and overweight in psychiatric patients was 66.93% (22.31% were obese, and 44.62% were overweight). the prevalence of obesity was highest in bipolar disorder (41.38%), followed by depression (37.93%), schizophrenia (10.34%), anxiety disorder (6.9%), and finally substance abuse disorder (3.45%), but the difference was not statistically significant. there was a significant correlation between sociodemographic characteristics of patients and obesity and the distribution of psychiatric disorders. conclusion the prevalence of obesity and overweight in psychiatric patients was relatively high, and this can occur with most psychiatric disorders, especially mood disorders, and were supposed to be due to other several mechanisms and risk factors other than the effect of psychotropic medications on the weight of psychiatric patients. moreover, there are some demographic and social factors that may moderate or mediate the association between obesity and psychiatric disorders; thus, identification of overweight and obesity, associated risk factors, and efforts to prevent weight gain should begin at the initiation of mental health treatment.
rewritten: background: the relationship between mental health and obesity has been examined through various research studies due to its importance. many studies have suggested multiple theories and mechanisms explaining how these conditions are connected. these ideas need to be reviewed and integrated into the management plans for psychiatric disorders. objectives: this study aimed to determine the prevalence and associated factors of obesity and overweight in a group of psychiatric patients (both inpatients and outpatients) at al-hussein university hospital who had no history of pharmacological treatment. patients and methods: in this cross-sectional study, 130 psychiatric patients underwent semi-structured clinical interviews based on dsm-iv-tr criteria during a six‑month period to diagnose psychiatric disorders. obesity was assessed by measuring weight in kilograms and height in meters to calculate the bmi using the formula: weight (kg) divided by height (m²). results: among the psychiatric patients, 66.93% were either obese or overweight, with 22.31% classified as obese and 44.62% as overweight. obesity rates were highest in patients with bipolar disorder (41.38%), followed by those with depression (37.93%), schizophrenia (10.34%), anxiety disorders (6.9%), and substance abuse disorders (3.45%). although these differences were observed, they were not statistically significant. a significant correlation was found between patients’ sociodemographic characteristics and both obesity and the distribution of psychiatric disorders. conclusion: the study revealed a relatively high prevalence of obesity and overweight among psychiatric patients, occurring across various psychiatric disorders—particularly mood disorders. this may be attributed to several mechanisms and risk factors beyond the impact of psychotropic medications on weight. additionally, certain demographic and social factors might moderate or mediate the relationship between obesity and psychiatric conditions. therefore, it is essential to identify overweight and obesity and their associated risks and to implement weight gain prevention strategies from the beginning of mental health treatment.

raw: this paper analyzes the influence of family, sociocultural and university environments on entrepreneurial intention in a european peripheral region, with a less developed innovation system: the canary islands. to do this, the perspective of theory of planned behavior is adopted and an empirical study is developed using a sample of spanish university students in the context of guesss project. the main results obtained indicate the importance of a university context in entrepreneurial intention through its antecedents, especially attitudes.
rewritten: this paper examines how family, sociocultural, and university environments affect entrepreneurial intentions in a european peripheral region with a less developed innovation system—the canary islands. it adopts the theory of planned behavior and presents an empirical study based on a sample of spanish university students from the gues project. the main findings highlight the significant role of the university setting in shaping entrepreneurial intentions through its antecedents, especially attitudes.

raw: this article explores the uk, usa and canadian interpretation of membership of a particular social group (ejusdem generis). i examine the interpretation in the context of refugee claims based on sexual orientation. in addition, i examine 83 unreported same-sex oriented cases from the uk, usa and canada to determine the effect of the ejusdem generis approach in relation to refugee claims based on sexual orientation. based on the result of the findings, this article proposes that the interpretation of a particular social group should be anchored to the principles of non-discrimination in order to deal with refugee claims based on sexual orientation more effectively.
rewritten: this article examines how the uk, usa, and canada interpret membership of a particular social group under the ejusdem generis principle, particularly in the context of refugee claims based on sexual orientation. it also analyzes 83 unreported same-sex cases from these countries to assess the impact of the ejusdem generis approach on such claims. based on the findings, the article argues that interpreting a particular social group should be grounded in non-discrimination principles to better address refugee claims related to sexual orientation.
