rewritten: please note that the totals for some variables might not match the overall total because of missing geographic or demographic data. additionally, any rate or percentage calculated with a numerator less than 10 or a denominator less than 100 is not considered reliable. a reported rate or percentage of zero indicates that the frequency recorded is zero. when computing percentages, unknown values are excluded from the denominator.

raw: this bonding method of a first member and a second member comprises: a step for forming a first wire bonding bump (12) on a first electrode (14) that is arranged on the first member; a step for forming a second wire bonding bump (22) on a second electrode 24 that is arranged on the second member; a step for forming a flat bump surface (221) by flattening a front end portion of the second wire bonding bump; and a compression bonding step for compression-bonding a front end portion (120) of the first wire bonding bump and the flat bump surface (221) with each other.
rewritten: the bonding method for a first member and a second member involves the following steps: first, forming a first wire bonding bump (12) on a first electrode (14) located on the first member; next, forming a second wire bonding bump (22) on a second electrode (24) positioned on the second member; then, creating a flat bump surface (221) by flattening the front end of the second wire bonding bump; and finally, performing a compression bonding step to bond the front end (120) of the first wire bonding bump to the flat bump surface (221).

raw: abstract here, we report the complete genome sequence of streptomyces venezuelae atcc 15439, a producer of the methymycin/pikromycin family of macrolide antibiotics and a model host for natural product studies, obtained exclusively using pacbio sequencing technology. the 9.03-mbp genome harbors 8,775 genes and 11 polyketide and nonribosomal peptide natural product gene clusters.
rewritten: in this study, we present the entire genome sequence of streptomyces venezuelae atcc 15439, a known producer of methymycin/pikromycin macrolide antibiotics and a valuable model for natural product research. the sequence was acquired solely through pacbio sequencing technology. the genome, spanning 9.03 mbp, contains 8,775 genes and features 11 clusters dedicated to polyketide and nonribosomal peptide natural product synthesis.

raw: the invention relates to the field of spr sensor chips, in particular to a histamine molecularly imprinted spr sensor chip and a preparation method thereof. according to the histamine molecularly imprinted spr sensor chip, a method for spin coating of a grafting initiator is adopted, and a histamine mip membrane is prepared on the surface of an spr chip in an in-situ polymerization mode. the histamine molecularly imprinted spr chip has high-specificity adsorptivity on histamine and has obvious response signal changes for low-concentration histamine in a sample. the prepared histamine molecularly imprinted spr chip is adopted for analyzing histamine residues, the steps are simple, the analysis time is short, and high specificity and sensitivity are achieved to analysis at low concentration.
rewritten: this invention pertains to the field of spr sensor chips, and more specifically to a histamine molecularly imprinted spr sensor chip along with its preparation method. the histamine mip spr sensor chip utilizes a spin coating technique to apply a grafting initiator, after which a histamine molecularly imprinted membrane is formed on the chip’s surface through in-situ polymerization. this sensor chip exhibits high-specificity adsorption for histamine and produces significant response signal changes even at low histamine concentrations. consequently, when used for analyzing histamine residues, the sensor chip offers a simple procedure, rapid analysis time, and high specificity and sensitivity for detecting low concentrations.

raw: this chapter discusses the requirements for developing a standardized pipeline for conducting large-scale genomics research projects. proper project planning is essential for optimizing operations and achieving project goals. the chapter highlights best practices for starting a project: creating a clear purpose statement, identifying stakeholders, developing a project plan, defining milestones, and establishing a workflow diagram for the project pipeline. for genomics research projects, the pipeline should generally have the following components: sample acquisition, data generation, data storage and dissemination, data analysis, and quality control and reporting systems. readers are given points to consider for establishing each component of the pipeline.
rewritten: this chapter outlines the prerequisites for creating a standardized workflow for large-scale genomics research projects. it emphasizes that thorough project planning is crucial for streamlining operations and reaching project objectives. the chapter also details best practices for initiating a project, which include establishing a clear purpose statement, identifying key stakeholders, developing a comprehensive project plan, setting milestones, and designing a workflow diagram for the pipeline. specifically, for genomics research, the pipeline should typically comprise sample acquisition, data generation, data storage and dissemination, data analysis, as well as quality control and reporting systems. additionally, readers are provided with key points to consider when setting up each component of the pipeline.

raw: the need for a consistent and accurate production forecast is of upmost importance in field development planning and even more so in times of a challenging oil price. results are also required in an ever shortening timeframe; however accurate simulation and forecasting can only be achieved using accurate 3d static models. the fundamental task is not only to create and simulate just one model version but use a variety of 3d models to get a range of results, e.g. low, middle and high cases (p90-p50-p10). uncertainty analysis can be used to get a range of many equiprobable stochastic model realisations, from which we can choose the low to high cases. however, this approach if not tested and calibrated correctly, could lead to model versions that do not represent a realistic geological description of a reservoir. for this purpose, a more beneficial approach is to create several alternative model versions, based on geological concepts, upscaling methods, input data and decisions or variations applied in the static modelling workflow. the results are a number of models which give a consistent range of hydrocarbon production profiles allowing the assessment of the potential of the reservoir and quantification of the reservoir uncertainties and challenges
rewritten: a reliable and precise production forecast is crucial in field development planning, particularly during periods of volatile oil prices. although results are needed within increasingly shorter timeframes, achieving accuracy in simulation and forecasting relies on the use of precise 3d static models. the essential task extends beyond creating and simulating a single model version; it involves producing a variety of 3d models to capture a range of outcomes, such as low, medium, and high cases (p90, p50, p10). uncertainty analysis can generate many equiprobable stochastic model realizations, from which low to high scenarios can be selected. however, if this method is not properly tested and calibrated, the resulting model versions might not accurately reflect the reservoir's geological characteristics. therefore, a more effective strategy is to develop several alternative model versions based on geological concepts, upscaling methods, input data, and specific decisions or variations in the static modeling process. this approach produces a set of models that consistently represent a spectrum of hydrocarbon production profiles, allowing for a thorough evaluation of reservoir potential as well as the quantification of uncertainties and challenges.

raw: systems, methods, and devices are disclosed herein for identifying, analyzing, and extending audiences associated with online advertising. systems include a first processing node configured to generate a first plurality of data categories that includes a plurality of seed data categories. systems include a query node configured to retrieve a second plurality of data categories that includes a plurality of candidate data categories. systems include a second processing node configured to generate a plurality of relevance metrics including a relevance metric for each candidate data category based on a comparison between each of the plurality of seed data categories and each of the plurality of candidate data categories. systems include a third processing node configured to generate a third plurality of data categories that includes at least some of the seed data categories and at least some of the candidate data categories based on the generated plurality of relevance metrics.
rewritten: the disclosed systems, methods, and devices provide techniques for identifying, analyzing, and expanding audiences in online advertising. the system comprises a first processing node that creates an initial group of data categories, which includes multiple seed data categories. it also includes a query node that retrieves a second group of data categories containing various candidate data categories. additionally, a second processing node is designed to compute multiple relevance metrics—that is, it assigns a relevance score to each candidate data category by comparing them with the seed data categories. finally, a third processing node generates a third group of data categories that features some of the seed data categories and some of the candidate data categories, based on the calculated relevance metrics.

raw: a common physiological response to various environmental stresses in plants is accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ros). ros can cause oxidative damage to macro biomolecules. on the other hand, ros have increasingly been recognized as important regulators in physiological and developmental pathways. ros signaling is mediated largely through actions on redox-sensitive proteins that undergo oxidative modifications in response to perturbation of cellular redox states. we have developed gel-based and gel-free (oxitraq) quantitative redox proteomics methods to identify arabidopsis proteins whose thiols underwent oxidative modifications in response to treatments of ros and the defense elicitors (salicylate and flagellin). the redox-sensitive proteins are involved in a variety of biological processes including chromatin remodeling and transcription, mrna processing, post-translational modifications and primary and secondary metabolism. a redox-sensitive bzip transcription factor in arabidopsis has been found to act as a redox sensor and mediate expression of oxidative stress responsive genes through its oxidation/reduction. our progresses in developing redox proteomics methods and in characterizing the role of the transcription factor in redox sensing and stress responses will be presented.
rewritten: plants often react to various environmental stresses by accumulating reactive oxygen species (ros), which can damage major biomolecules through oxidative stress. however, ros are also increasingly recognized as key regulators of various physiological and developmental processes. their signaling effects largely occur through redox-sensitive proteins that undergo oxidative modifications when the cellular redox state is disturbed. to study these changes, we have developed both gel-based and gel-free (oxitraq) quantitative redox proteomics techniques to identify arabidopsis proteins whose thiol groups are oxidatively modified following exposure to ros, as well as defense elicitors such as salicylate and flagellin. the proteins sensitive to redox changes play roles in numerous biological processes, including chromatin remodeling and transcription, mrna processing, post-translational modifications, and both primary and secondary metabolism. additionally, a redox-sensitive bzip transcription factor in arabidopsis has been identified as a redox sensor that regulates the expression of genes involved in the oxidative stress response through cycles of oxidation and reduction. in this presentation, we will share our advancements in redox proteomics methodologies and detail our findings on the transcription factor’s role in redox sensing and managing stress responses.

raw: field: food industry.substance: invention relates to food industry, in particular, to biologically active dietary supplement in form of alcohol-free tonic beverage. biologically active food supplement contains herb extracts: garden angelica with root, ginger, large burdock roots, nutmeg, rosemary leaves, sarsaparilla roots, paprika fruits and cinnamon bark tree, fructose, honey, chicory inulin, concentrates of lemon and apple juice, preserving agent and water in certain ratio of components.effect: invention enables to obtain a finished product which speeds up alcohol metabolism in a human body.1 cl, 2 ex
rewritten: field: food industry. substance: this invention pertains to the food industry, particularly to a biologically active dietary supplement available as an alcohol-free tonic beverage. the supplement is made from a blend of herbal extracts—garden angelica with root, ginger, large burdock roots, nutmeg, rosemary leaves, sarsaparilla roots, paprika fruits, and cinnamon bark—combined with fructose, honey, chicory inulin, lemon and apple juice concentrates, a preserving agent, and water in specific proportions. effect: the finished product is designed to help speed up alcohol metabolism in the human body. 1 cl, 2 ex

raw: the invention discloses a coplanar waveguide feed triple-band antenna applied to wlan/wimax, and relates to the triple-band antenna. the coplanar waveguide feed triple-band antenna is provided with a dielectric substrate, wherein the dielectric substrate is coated with a metal layer; four pairs of left-right symmetrical rectangular slots are etched in the metal layer; the four pairs of left-right symmetrical rectangular slots are symmetrically arranged along one side center line of a feed structure of the dielectric substrate; and a metal layer does not exist on the lower surface of the dielectric substrate. the triple-band antenna adopts the coplanar waveguide feed, and performs radiations through the slots; and the s11 parameters of a 2.4ghz frequency band, a 5.2ghz frequency band and a 5.8ghz frequency band are all less than -10db, so that the coplanar waveguide feed triple-band antenna can be applied to the 2.4ghz frequency band, the 5.2ghz frequency band and the 5.8ghz frequency band of the wlan/wimax.
rewritten: the invention describes a triple-band antenna with a coplanar waveguide feed design for wlan/wimax applications. this antenna features a dielectric substrate that has a metal layer on its top surface, with four pairs of left-right symmetrical rectangular slots etched into the metal layer. these slot pairs are arranged symmetrically along the center line of one side of the substrate’s feed structure, while the bottom surface of the dielectric substrate remains uncoated by metal. the antenna uses the coplanar waveguide feed approach to achieve radiation via the slots, and its s11 parameters are measured at less than -10 db for the 2.4 ghz, 5.2 ghz, and 5.8 ghz frequency bands, making it suitable for use in these wlan/wimax frequency ranges.

raw: the invention provides a fuel gas forced circulation waste heat boiler. the fuel gas forced circulation waste heat boiler comprises a boiler body, a steam drum, a circulating pump, a fuel gas channel, a heating surface assembly and a downcorner, wherein the heating surface assembly comprises a pipe group, a left header and a right header, the pipe group comprises a plurality of water pipes, the two ends of the water pipes are respectively communicated with the left header and the right header, the water pipes adopt horizontally coiled pipes, honeycomb structures formed by a plurality of grooves are arranged on the outer surfaces of the water pipes, a thermal insulating layer is arranged outside the boiler body, and the thermal insulating layer is sequentially provided with a rock wool layer, a glass fiber cloth layer, an aluminum silicate needled felt layer from the inside to the outside. through arranging the honeycomb structures formed by the plurality of grooves on the outer surfaces of the water pipes, the contact area of the water pipes with fuel gas in the fuel gas channel can be increased, the exchange and utilization of waste heat are better facilitated, and the heat recovery rate can be effectively improved.
rewritten: this invention relates to a fuel gas forced circulation waste heat boiler. the boiler includes a boiler body, a steam drum, a circulating pump, a fuel gas channel, a heating surface assembly, and a downcorner. the heating surface assembly features a collection of pipes along with a left header and a right header. the collection consists of several water pipes, each of whose ends connect to one of the headers. these water pipes are arranged in a horizontal coil configuration and have honeycomb structures—formed by multiple grooves—on their outer surfaces. positioned outside the boiler body is a thermal insulation layer that is built up in sequence from the inside out with a rock wool layer, a glass fiber cloth layer, and an aluminum silicate needled felt layer. the incorporation of the honeycomb design on the water pipes increases their contact area with the fuel gas in the channel, thereby enhancing the exchange and utilization of waste heat and effectively improving the heat recovery rate.

raw: a point cloud splicing method comprises the following steps: after a user pastes a mark point on a rotary table of a measuring device, making the rotary table rotate according to a preset angle interval to form different measurement angles, and making a scanning device scan the mark point at each measurement angle; acquiring the coordinate of the mark point at each measurement angle; calculating a common point according to the coordinates of the mark point at all the measurement angles; calculating the transformation matrix of each measurement angle according to the common point; after the user puts a work piece on the rotary table, making the scanning device scan the work piece at each measurement angle to get the point cloud of the work piece at each measurement angle; acquiring the coordinates of all the points in the point cloud of the work piece at each measurement angle; and transforming the coordinates of all the points in the point cloud of the work piece at each measurement angle to the same point of view based on the transformation matrix of each measurement angle to get the transformed point cloud at each measurement angle, and splicing the transformed point clouds at all the measurement angles.
rewritten: a method for splicing point clouds involves the following steps: first, a user attaches a reference mark to the rotary table of a measuring device. the table then rotates in preset angular intervals, allowing a scanning device to capture the mark at each measurement angle. the coordinates of the mark from each angle are recorded and used to compute a common reference point. next, for each measurement angle, a transformation matrix is calculated based on this common point. after this setup, the user places a workpiece on the rotary table. the scanning device then scans the workpiece at each measurement angle, generating a point cloud for each position. all point coordinates from each cloud are collected, and using the corresponding transformation matrix for each angle, these coordinates are adjusted to a consistent viewpoint. finally, the adjusted point clouds from all measurement angles are combined to form the final spliced point cloud.

raw: abstract a new system for building and measuring real analog electronic circuits has been developed and validated in the area of remote laboratories. the innovation of this work lies in the use of standardized solutions and non-proprietary solutions for designing and developing remote labs. this decision facilitates and improves the set-up, maintenance and update of the hardware used during the configuration, control and measurement of the instruments and experiments included in the lab. the experimental setup does not reduce the performance of the lab, allowing the users to carry out the same actions and activities as if they were in a hands-on laboratory. this paper describes how this application has been designed and developed, starting with an introduction to the requirements and motivations of this work, following with the description of the proposed architecture. the validation of the prototype that has been built to test the application concludes the presented work.
rewritten: a new system for constructing and evaluating real analog electronic circuits has been developed and validated within the realm of remote laboratories. the key innovation of this work is its reliance on standardized, non-proprietary solutions for designing and creating remote labs. this approach simplifies and enhances the setup, maintenance, and updating of the hardware used to configure, control, and measure the instruments and experiments in the lab. furthermore, the experimental setup maintains the lab's performance, allowing users to perform the same actions and activities as they would in a traditional hands-on laboratory. this paper outlines the design and development of the application, beginning with an introduction that details the requirements and motivations behind the work, followed by a description of the proposed architecture, and concluding with the validation of the prototype built to test the application.

raw: this research was aimed to determine the effectiveness of second degree combustion wound healing after the administration of jatropha (jatropha curcas linn.) sap oinment 10% and gentamicin ointment 0.1% on mice skin (mus musculus). this research used 12 mice divided into 3 treatment groups: yellow vaseline lubrication (k0), jatropha sap oinment 10% (ki), and ointment gentamicin 0.1% (kii). the parameter measured in this study was healing time (day) based on wound not visible/healed (diameter length=0 cm). data of wound healing were analyzed using analysis of variance (anova) and continued with least significant difference test. the time of wound healing on p0, p1, and p2 was 22.25, 17.75, and 20.25 days (p<0.01). in conclusion, sap of jatropha oinment 10% is potensial to accelerate the combustion injury ii healing process in mice.
rewritten: this study aimed to determine the effectiveness of healing second-degree burn wounds in mice (mus musculus) after applying jatropha (jatropha curcas linn.) sap ointment 10% and gentamicin ointment 0.1%. twelve mice were used and divided into three treatment groups: one received yellow vaseline lubrication (group k0), another received jatropha sap ointment 10% (group ki), and the last received gentamicin ointment 0.1% (group kii). the parameter evaluated was the healing time in days, determined by the point when the wound was no longer visible (wound diameter = 0 cm). the wound healing data were analyzed using analysis of variance (anova) and a least significant difference test. healing times were recorded as 22.25, 17.75, and 20.25 days for groups p0, p1, and p2 respectively (p < 0.01). in conclusion, jatropha sap ointment 10% has potential to accelerate the healing process of second-degree burn injuries in mice.

raw: the invention relates to a method for producing a multi-segment filter (46) of the tobacco-processing industry, wherein firstly a group (47, 47 ') of filter segments (40, 40', 41, 41 ', 42, 43) is compiled, and the group ( 47, 47 ') of filter segments (40, 40', 41, 41 ', 42, 43) is conveyed in a direction transverse to its longitudinal axis (50). the invention further relates to a corresponding multi-segment filter (10). the inventive method is characterized in that the group (47, 47 ') of filter segments (40, 40', 41, 41 ', 42, 43) has a first end face (44) and a second end face (45), a filter segment (40) of the group (47, 47 ') of filter segments (40, 40', 41, 41 ', 42, 43) having the first face (44) into a first part (42) and a second part (43) is cut, and the first part (42) or the second part (43) on the outside of the side of the group (47, 47 ') of filter segments (40, 40', 41, 41 ', 42, 43) is reshuffled with the second end face (45).
rewritten: the invention discloses a method for producing a multi-segment filter (46) used in the tobacco-processing industry. in this process, a group (47, 47′) of filter segments (40, 40′, 41, 41′, 42, 43) is first assembled and then moved in a direction perpendicular to its longitudinal axis (50). the invention also pertains to the corresponding multi-segment filter (10). in the inventive method, the group (47, 47′) of filter segments (40, 40′, 41, 41′, 42, 43) has a first end face (44) and a second end face (45). a filter segment (40) from the group is cut along its first face (44) to create a first portion (42) and a second portion (43). subsequently, either the first portion (42) or the second portion (43), located at the outer side of the group, is repositioned to align with the second end face (45).

raw: women who carry a pathogenic mutation in the breast cancer susceptibility genes brca1 or brca2 ( brca ) have markedly increased risks of developing breast and ovarian cancers during their lifetime. it has been estimated that their breast and ovarian cancer risks are in the range of 46-87% and 15-68%, respectively. therefore it is of utmost clinical importance to identify brca mutation carriers in order to target unaffected women for prevention and/or close surveillance and to help affected women choose the best chemotherapy regimen. genetic testing for brca germline mutations is expanding in clinical oncology centers worldwide. given the high costs of complete brca gene screens, a lot of effort has been expended on deciding upon whom to test. relevant issues involved in decision making include the prior probability of a woman having a brca mutation, which is a function of her age and her disease status, her ethnic group, and her family history of breast or ovarian cancer. the frequency and spectrum of mutations in these genes show considerable variation by ethnic groups and by geographic regions. most studies have been conducted in european and north american populations, while studies in asian, hispanic, and african populations are fewer. in most populations, many brca mutations were identified, which were distributed all over the genes. however, in some populations, a relatively small number of specific brca mutations are recurrent and account for the majority of all mutations in that population. many of the recurrent mutations are founder mutations, which were derived from a common ancestor. founder mutations are present in ashkenazi jewish, european, and islander (faroe, easter, and pitcairn) populations. such mutations have also been identified in patients from several asian, south american, and african countries. population-specific genetic risk assessment and genetic mutation screening have been facilitated at low costs. given that mutations in the brca genes are distributed in populations throughout the world, it is important that the benefits of genetic testing and of targeted therapies be made available not only to women from developed countries in europe and north america, but also to those from less developed countries in asia, africa and south america.
rewritten: women carrying a harmful mutation in the breast cancer susceptibility genes brca1 or brca2 (brca) face substantially higher lifetime risks of developing breast and ovarian cancers. estimated risks for carriers range from 46% to 87% for breast cancer and 15% to 68% for ovarian cancer. therefore, identifying individuals with brca mutations is critically important—not only to offer preventative measures and intensive monitoring for women who have not yet developed cancer, but also to help those already affected select the most effective chemotherapy options. genetic testing for brca germline mutations is increasingly available in oncology centers around the world. however, the high cost of comprehensive brca gene screening has led to significant efforts to determine which individuals should be tested. decision-making factors include a woman’s prior probability of carrying a brca mutation, which depends on her age, disease status, ethnic background, and family history of breast or ovarian cancer. the frequency and range of mutations in these genes vary considerably across different ethnic and geographic groups. most research has been conducted in european and north american populations, with fewer studies available in asian, hispanic, and african populations. in many groups, numerous brca mutations have been identified, scattered throughout the genes. in contrast, some populations exhibit a pattern where a small number of specific brca mutations recur and represent the majority of all mutations. many of these recurrent mutations are founder mutations that originate from a common ancestor. such founder mutations have been found in ashkenazi jewish, european, and islander (faroe, easter, and pitcairn) populations, as well as in patients from several asian, south american, and african countries. advances in population-specific genetic risk assessment and mutation screening have helped reduce costs, making these services more accessible. since brca mutations occur in populations worldwide, it is essential that the advantages of genetic testing and targeted therapies extend not only to women in developed regions such as europe and north america, but also to those in less developed areas in asia, africa, and south america.

raw: the invention provides a distribution network reactive power optimization method considering multiple wiring modes. the method comprises steps as follows: i, initializing a distribution network reactive power optimization particle swarm; ii, performing distribution network reactive power optimization loop iteration solution; iii, performing distribution network reactive power optimization forward back substitution load flow calculation; iv, calculating a distribution network reactive power optimization objective function and updating an individual optimum position and a global optimum position of the distribution network reactive power optimization particle swarm; v, judging whether distribution network reactive power optimization is converged, if yes, ending the calculation, and otherwise, returning to step vi; vi, updating the speed and position of the distribution network reactive power optimization particle swarm; vii, performing discrete treatment on positions of distribution network reactive power optimization particles; viii, adding 1 to the distribution network reactive power optimization iteration number and returning to step ii. with the adoption of the technical scheme, multi-objective reactive power optimization can be performed on a three-phase imbalance radiation type medium voltage distribution network; optimization calculation can be performed on the capacity of a discrete reactive power compensation device; the method is simple in calculation, easy to implement and high in robustness.
rewritten: the invention describes a method for optimizing reactive power in a distribution network that accounts for various wiring configurations. the method involves the following steps: 1. initialize a particle swarm algorithm designed for optimizing reactive power in the distribution network. 2. execute a loop for solving the reactive power optimization problem. 3. carry out a forward and backward substitution load flow calculation related to the reactive power optimization. 4. compute an objective function for the reactive power optimization and update both the individual best and global best positions within the particle swarm. 5. determine whether the optimization has converged. if it has, end the calculation; if not, proceed to the next step. 6. update the speed and position of each particle in the swarm. 7. apply a discrete process to adjust the positions of the particles. 8. increment the iteration counter by one and return to step 2. this technical approach enables multi-objective optimization of reactive power in a three-phase unbalanced, radiation-type medium voltage distribution network. it also allows for optimization of the capacity of a discrete reactive power compensation device. the method is characterized by its straightforward calculations, ease of implementation, and high robustness.

raw: pristine single crystal graphene is the strongest known two-dimensional material, and its nonlinear anisotropic mechanical properties are well understood from the atomic length scale up to a continuum description. however, experiments indicate that grain boundaries in the polycrystalline form reduce the mechanical behavior of polycrystalline graphene. herein, we perform atomistic-scale molecular dynamics simulations of the deformation and fracture of graphene grain boundaries and express the results as continuum cohesive zone models (czms) that embed notions of the grain boundary ultimate strength and fracture toughness. to facilitate energy balance, we employ a new methodology that simulates a quasi-static controlled crack propagation which renders the kinetic energy contribution to the total energy negligible. we verify good agreement between griffith's critical energy release rate and the work of separation of the czm, and we note that the energy of crack edges and fracture toughness differs by about 35%, which is attributed to the phenomenon of bond trapping. this justifies the implementation of the czm within the context of the finite element method (fem). to enhance computational efficiency in the fem implementation, we discuss the use of scaled traction-separation laws (tsls) for larger element sizes. as a final result, we have established that the failure characteristics of pristine graphene and high tilt angle bicrystals differ by less than 10%. this result suggests that one could use a unique or a few typical tsls as a good approximation for the czms associated with the mechanical simulations of the polycrystalline graphene.
rewritten: pristine single-crystal graphene is the strongest two-dimensional material known, with its nonlinear anisotropic mechanical properties well characterized from the atomic scale to continuum models. however, experiments show that the presence of grain boundaries in polycrystalline graphene diminishes its mechanical performance. in this study, we conduct atomistic molecular dynamics simulations to examine the deformation and fracture behavior of graphene grain boundaries, and we translate these findings into continuum cohesive zone models (czms) that capture the ultimate strength and fracture toughness of the grain boundaries. to ensure an accurate energy balance, we introduce a novel approach that simulates quasi-static, controlled crack propagation, effectively rendering the kinetic energy contribution negligible. our analysis reveals a strong agreement between griffith’s critical energy release rate and the czm work of separation. additionally, we observe that the energy associated with crack edges and fracture toughness differ by roughly 35%, a discrepancy attributed to bond trapping. this observation supports the integration of the czm within a finite element method (fem) framework. furthermore, to boost computational efficiency in the fem implementation, we discuss the adoption of scaled traction-separation laws (tsls) for larger element sizes. ultimately, we established that the failure characteristics of pristine graphene and high tilt-angle bicrystals vary by less than 10%, suggesting that a single or a few representative tsls could serve as reliable approximations for the czms used in mechanical simulations of polycrystalline graphene.

raw: this paper presents efficient simd optimizations for the open-source kvazaar hevc intra encoder. the c implementation of kvazaar is accelerated by intel avx2 instructions whose effect on kvazaar ultrafast preset is profiled. according to our profiling results, c functions of satd, dct, quantization, and intra prediction account for over 60% of the total intra coding time of kvazaar ultrafast preset. this work shows that optimizing primarily these functions doubles the coding speed of a single-threaded kvazaar intra encoder for the same rate-distortion performance. the highest performance boost is obtained by deploying the proposed optimizations jointly with multithreading. on the intel 8-core i7 processor, the avx2-optimized 16-threaded kvazaar ultrafast preset achieves real-time (30 fps) intra coding speed up to 1080p resolution. compared to avx2-optimized ultrafast preset of x265, kvazaar is 20% times faster and still obtains 9.1% bit rate gain for the same quality. these results justify that kvazaar is currently the leading open-source hevc intra encoder in terms of real-time coding speed and efficiency.
rewritten: this paper introduces efficient simd optimizations for the open-source kvazaar hevc intra encoder. its c implementation is accelerated using intel avx2 instructions, and their impact on kvazaar's ultrafast preset is analyzed. profiling indicates that the c functions for satd, dct, quantization, and intra prediction together consume over 60% of the total intra coding time in the ultrafast preset. the study demonstrates that focusing optimization on these functions can double the coding speed of a single-threaded encoder while maintaining the same rate-distortion performance. furthermore, the greatest performance improvements are observed when these optimizations are combined with multithreading. on an intel 8-core i7 processor, the avx2-optimized, 16-threaded version of the kvazaar ultrafast preset reaches real-time intra coding speeds (30 fps) at resolutions up to 1080p. in comparison to the avx2-optimized ultrafast preset of x265, kvazaar is 20% faster and still achieves a 9.1% bit rate reduction for equivalent quality. these findings confirm that kvazaar currently leads open-source hevc intra encoders in terms of real-time coding speed and overall efficiency.

raw: pemphigoid gestationis (pg) is a rare autoimmune dermatosis whose incidence during pregnancy is extremely low (1:50.000 pregnancies) (gan et al. 2012). pg is characterised by an intensely pruritic ...
rewritten: pemphigoid gestationis (pg) is an uncommon autoimmune skin condition that affects pregnant individuals, with an incidence of approximately 1 in 50,000 pregnancies (gan et al. 2012). it is primarily marked by severe itching...

raw: this study reports the first mems multiplexed coaxial electrospray sources in the literature. coaxial electrospraying is a microencapsulation technology based on electrohydrodynamic jetting of two immiscible liquids, which allows precise control with low size variation of the geometry of the core-shell particles it generates, which is of great importance in numerous biomedical and engineering applications, e.g., drug delivery and self-healing composites. by implementing monolithic planar arrays of miniaturized coaxial electrospray emitters that work uniformly in parallel, the throughput of the compound microdroplet source is greatly increased, making the microencapsulation technology compatible with low-cost commercial applications. miniaturized core-shell particle generators with up to 25 coaxial electrospray emitters (25 emitters cm-2) were fabricated via stereolithography, which is an additive manufacturing process that can create complex microfluidic devices at a small fraction of the cost per device and fabrication time associated with silicon-based counterparts. the characterization of devices with the same emitter structure but different array sizes demonstrates uniform array operation. moreover, the data demonstrate that the per-emitter current is approximately proportional to the square root of the flow rate of the driving liquid, and it is independent of the flow rate of the driven liquid, as predicted by the theory. the core/shell diameters and the size distribution of the generated compound microparticles can be modulated by controlling the flow rates fed to the emitters.
rewritten: this study introduces the first mems multiplexed coaxial electrospray sources documented in the literature. coaxial electrospraying—a microencapsulation method based on the electrohydrodynamic jetting of two immiscible liquids—allows for precise control over the geometry of the core-shell particles produced, with minimal size variation. this precision is crucial for many biomedical and engineering applications, such as drug delivery and self-healing composites. by employing monolithic planar arrays of miniaturized coaxial electrospray emitters that operate uniformly in parallel, the throughput of the compound microdroplet source is significantly increased, making the technology suitable for cost-effective commercial use. the researchers fabricated miniaturized core-shell particle generators featuring up to 25 coaxial electrospray emitters per square centimeter using stereolithography, an additive manufacturing process that produces complex microfluidic devices at a small fraction of the cost and time required for silicon-based counterparts. characterization of devices with the same emitter structure but varying array sizes confirmed uniform operation across the array. moreover, the data show that the current per emitter is approximately proportional to the square root of the flow rate of the driving liquid and is independent of the driven liquid's flow rate, as theory predicts. by adjusting the flow rates supplied to the emitters, it is possible to control the core/shell diameters and the size distribution of the resulting compound microparticles.

raw: efficient management of plant diseases is mainly dependent on accurate and timely detection of pathogen, severity of infection, availability of disease resistant genotypes and cost effective disease control measures. sugarcane suffers from various fungal, viral, bacterial and phytoplasmal diseases in its crop cycle. disease indexing in sugarcane was so far based on visual detection of symptoms and it was extremely difficult to detect the pathogen in asymptomatic plants. recent advances in molecular biology techniques have facilitated the development of specific and sensitive, diagnostic tools for detection of pathogens of various diseases of sugarcane. pcr based detection methods are now available for red rot and smut diseases, sugarcane mosaic, yellow leaf syndrome and white leaf/grassy shoot diseases, ratoon stunting disease, leaf scald, and gumming disease to name a few. there is further need to develop highly sensitive, specific and cost effective detection tools for key pathogens, which can be used for large scale sugarcane seed certification, germplasm quarantine programmes and diagnostic laboratories in order to detect these pathogens in seed cane.
rewritten: efficient plant disease management largely depends on correctly and quickly identifying the pathogen, assessing the infection's severity, having access to disease-resistant genotypes, and employing cost-effective control measures. sugarcane faces a range of fungal, viral, bacterial, and phytoplasmal diseases throughout its growth cycle. traditionally, disease assessment in sugarcane relied on visually identifying symptoms, which made detecting pathogens in plants without visible symptoms very challenging. however, recent advances in molecular biology have led to the development of specific, sensitive diagnostic tools for identifying pathogens responsible for various sugarcane diseases. currently, pcr-based detection methods are available for diseases such as red rot, smut, sugarcane mosaic, yellow leaf syndrome, white leaf/grassy shoot diseases, ratoon stunting disease, leaf scald, and gumming disease, among others. there remains a need to develop even more sensitive, specific, and cost-effective detection tools for key pathogens. such tools would be invaluable for large-scale sugarcane seed certification, germplasm quarantine programs, and diagnostic laboratories to effectively identify these pathogens in seed cane.

raw: the invention discloses a super-high power ultrasonic sump oil emulsion breaking dehydration method.the method comprises the steps that water-containing sump oil in a sump oil stock tank (1) is preheated and pressurized through a water-containing sump oil pump (2) and fed into a sump oil heater (3) to be heated to 50-85 deg c; the heated water-containing sump oil is fed into an ultrasonic actuator (5) for continuous ultrasonic initial emulsion breaking; the sump oil obtained after initial emulsion breaking is fed into a pulse type ultrasonic emulsion breaking tank (6) through a pipeline for secondary emulsion breaking; the water-containing sump oil obtained after secondary emulsion breaking flows into a settlement separation dehydration tank (7) to be heated to 40-80 deg c for settlement and layering to obtain sump oil, sewage and solid flocculent impurities; the sump oil located at the upper portion of the settlement separation dehydration tank (7) automatically flows into a finished sump oil tank (8); sewage and impurities enter a settling pond from the bottom of the settlement separation dehydration tank (7) through a sewage disposal well.according to the two-time ultrasonic emulsion breaking, sump oil dehydration is achieved, the sump oil dehydration rate is larger than 90%, and the sewage oil content is smaller than 500 mg/l.
rewritten: the disclosed invention presents a method for super-high power ultrasonic sump oil emulsion breaking dehydration. the process involves several steps. first, water-containing sump oil stored in a sump oil stock tank (1) is preheated and pressurized by a water-containing sump oil pump (2) before entering a sump oil heater (3) where it is heated to a temperature between 50°c and 85°c. next, the heated water-containing sump oil passes through an ultrasonic actuator (5) for a continuous initial emulsion breaking process. the resulting sump oil, now partially treated, is then conveyed via pipeline into a pulse-type ultrasonic emulsion breaking tank (6) for secondary emulsion breaking. following this, the water-containing sump oil is directed to a settlement separation dehydration tank (7) where it is heated to 40°c-80°c, allowing it to settle and separate into distinct layers that yield sump oil, sewage, and solid flocculent impurities. the sump oil, which rises to the upper portion of the settlement separation dehydration tank (7), automatically flows into a finished sump oil tank (8), while the sewage and impurities are discharged to a settling pond via a sewage disposal well at the bottom of the tank (7). through the two-stage ultrasonic emulsion breaking process, the method achieves sump oil dehydration, with the dehydration rate exceeding 90% and the sewage oil content reduced to below 500 mg/l.

raw: pen-based interaction is universally available on smart devices and especially on tablet pcs. previous studies compared various input methods like fingers, a mouse or a stylus pen on pcs or on a touchscreen based devices such as smart phones. at the same time, various soft keyboard applications are being developed on application stores of smart devices. however, these previous studies did not suggest which one is a suitable keyboard application for tablet pcs when users perform a certain interaction as input type. in this study, we compared two types of input methods (finger and pen) and three types of soft keyboard applications (qwerty, gesture and swype) in a tablet pc using performance measurements (accuracy and input speed) and discussed what types of applications showed better performance with each interaction on tablet pc. from these results, recommendations for the keyboard types depending on the input methods on tablet pcs were developed.
rewritten: pen-based interaction is now universally accessible on smart devices, especially tablet pcs. while earlier research compared various input methods—such as using fingers, a mouse, or a stylus—on pcs and touchscreen devices like smartphones, these studies did not determine which soft keyboard application would be most suitable for tablet pcs under specific input interactions. in our study, we evaluated two types of input methods (finger and pen) alongside three types of soft keyboard applications (qwerty, gesture, and swype) on a tablet pc. we measured performance in terms of accuracy and input speed, discussed which configurations delivered better performance with each type of interaction, and ultimately developed recommendations for selecting keyboard types based on the input method used on tablet pcs.

raw: in this paper we give a linear time algorithm for computing the number of spanninig trees in double nested graphs.
rewritten: this paper presents a linear-time algorithm that computes the number of spanning trees in double nested graphs.

raw: polarization converters based on metamaterial have broad application in imaging, sensing and communication from microwave to optical frequency. however, its performance is limited by single function and narrowband. in this paper, a new type of polarization converter based on square loop shaped metamaterial has been presented. it works in the reflection mode to achieve broadband polarization conversion for both circular and x/y linear polarization waves. the incident linearly polarized wave will be converted to its cross-polarized state with a polarization conversion ratio (pcr) lager than 0.9 in two distinct broad frequency ranges; on the other hand, circularly polarized wave will be reflected to its co-polarized state efficiently in the same spectrum regimes. good agreements have been observed for both simulation and measurement results. this work offers a further step in developing high performance multi-function microwave or optical devices.
rewritten: polarization converters based on metamaterials have wide-ranging applications in imaging, sensing, and communication across frequencies from microwave to optical. however, their performance is often constrained by single-function operation and narrow bandwidth. in this paper, we introduce a novel polarization converter utilizing a square loop metamaterial design. operating in reflection mode, this device achieves broadband polarization conversion for both circular and x/y linear polarized waves. for incident linearly polarized waves, it converts them to their cross-polarized state with a polarization conversion ratio (pcr) exceeding 0.9 across two distinct, broad frequency bands. conversely, circularly polarized waves are efficiently reflected into their co-polarized state within the same spectral regions. the simulation and experimental results show excellent agreement. this work represents a significant advancement toward the development of high-performance, multifunctional devices for microwave and optical applications.

raw: the utility model relates to a 10kv cubical switchboard device temperature monitors alarm system belongs to the technical field that substation equipment monitored, the problem of the technique is solved but the cubical switchboard device temperature monitoring alarm system who provides the interior device temperature of real -time supervision cubical switchboard and gone on reporting to the police, the technical scheme who adopts does: a 10kv cubical switchboard device temperature monitors alarm system, includes: infrared temperature probe, infrared temperature measurement terminal, transformer substation's management host computer, remote management host computer, video monitoring device and alarm device, infrared temperature probe setting needs in the cubical switchboard on the equipment of temperature measurement, infrared temperature probe through infrared temperature measurement terminal with transformer substation's management host computer electricity is connected, the remote management host computer respectively with transformer substation's management host computer, video monitoring device and alarm device electricity are connected, the utility model discloses but wide application in electric power transformer substation temperature monitoring field.
rewritten: the utility model pertains to a 10kv cubical switchboard device temperature monitoring alarm system within the technical field of substation equipment monitoring. it addresses the problem of enabling real-time supervision of the internal temperature of cubical switchboards while also providing the capability to report issues to the police. the technical solution involves a 10kv cubical switchboard device temperature monitoring alarm system that comprises an infrared temperature probe, an infrared temperature measurement terminal, a transformer substation’s management host computer, a remote management host computer, a video monitoring device, and an alarm device. the infrared temperature probe is installed on the cubical switchboard equipment that requires temperature measurement, and it is electrically connected to the transformer substation’s management host computer via the infrared temperature measurement terminal. additionally, the remote management host computer is electrically connected to the transformer substation’s management host computer, the video monitoring device, and the alarm device. this utility model finds broad application in the field of temperature monitoring for electric power transformer substations.

raw: for decades, mutant ras (mut-ras) proteins have been identified as drivers of multiple cancers including pancreatic, lung, and colon cancers. however, targeting this oncogene has been challenging and no ras inhibitors are on the market to date. lately several candidates targeting the downstream pathways of ras signaling, including pi3k and raf, were approved for cancer treatment. however, they do not present promising therapeutic effects on patients harboring ras mutations. recently, a variety of compounds have been reported to impair the activity of ras, and these exciting discoveries reignite the hope for development of novel drugs targeting mut-ras. in this article, we will review the progress made in this field and the current state-of-the-art technologies to develop ras inhibitors. also we will discuss the future direction of targeting ras.
rewritten: for decades, mutated ras proteins have been recognized as key drivers in cancers such as those affecting the pancreas, lungs, and colon. despite their critical role, targeting this oncogene has proved difficult, and to date, no ras inhibitors have been approved. recently, several drugs that target the downstream signaling pathways of ras—namely pi3k and raf—have been approved for cancer treatment; however, they have not demonstrated effective therapeutic outcomes in patients with ras mutations. lately, a range of compounds that inhibit ras activity has been identified, renewing hope for the development of new drugs aimed at mutant ras. in this article, we review the advancements made in this area, the state-of-the-art technologies used in developing ras inhibitors, and discuss potential future strategies for targeting ras.

raw: black rice ( oryza sativa l.) is one of germ plasm where there are many found in indonesia. the popularity of black rice growing in line with public awareness for consuming a functional food. but the scientific study of black rice germ plasm in indonesia still classified as low, especially the scientific study of the molecular genetik diversity. information of molecular genetik diversity adequately on black rice germ plasm is one of the cornerstone for the development of action strategies for conservation the varieties of black rice in indonesia in a sustainable way. the scientific study which is expected to be implemented in the form of research on black rice molecular genetik diversity is an approach of dna barcode. most of the genes that utilized as dna barcode on plants is on chloroplasts dna (cpdna). cpdna having the benefit characteristics for genetik diversity study compared with nuclei dna (ndna ) and mitochondria mtdna. characteristic of these is (1) the cpdna genome have a small size and stable structure, (2) more conservative with lower average nucleotide substitution and (3) no recombination and uniparental heritable. this article discussed a potential dna sequences of nucleotides from chloroplast dna to give information on the genetik diversity of black rice, namely rbc l gene. until now, ncbi database collected 143 partial sequence of rbc l gene. the number of partial sequence that has been collected is using as comparation facilitate to investigation the genetik diversity of indonesia black rice. in the future, dna barcode os expected to be implemented in the research. keywords: genetik diversity, dna barcode,rbcl gene,indonesian local black rice
rewritten: black rice (oryza sativa l.) is one of the germplasms found abundantly in indonesia. its popularity is growing alongside increasing public interest in functional foods. however, scientific research on the black rice germplasm in indonesia remains limited, particularly the studies focusing on its molecular genetic diversity. comprehensive data on the molecular genetic diversity of black rice germplasm is crucial as it forms the foundation for developing sustainable conservation strategies for the various black rice varieties in indonesia. the anticipated research on the molecular genetics of black rice will utilize dna barcoding, an approach that primarily focuses on chloroplast dna (cpdna) for plants. compared to nuclear dna (ndna) and mitochondrial dna (mtdna), cpdna offers several advantages for genetic diversity research. these include: (1) a small genome size and a stable structure, (2) a lower average nucleotide substitution rate due to its more conserved nature, and (3) the fact that it does not undergo recombination and is uniparentally inherited. this article explores potential nucleotide sequences from chloroplast dna to provide insights into the genetic diversity of black rice, specifically using the rbcl gene. to date, the ncbi database has collected 143 partial sequences of the rbcl gene, which serve as a reference framework for comparing and investigating the genetic diversity of indonesian black rice. looking ahead, it is expected that dna barcoding will play a significant role in future research. keywords: genetic diversity, dna barcode, rbcl gene, indonesian local black rice.

raw: we previously demonstrated that rapamycin, an inhibitor of the mammalian target of rapamycin (mtor), protects against n-methyl-d-aspartic acid (nmda)-induced retinal damage in rats. rapamycin inhibits mtor activity, thereby preventing the phosphorylation of ribosomal protein s6, which is a downstream target of s6 kinase. therefore, we aimed to determine whether pf-4708671, an inhibitor of s6 kinase, protects against nmda-induced retinal injury. intravitreal injection of nmda (200 nmol/eye) caused cell loss in the ganglion cell layer and neuroinflammatory responses, such as an increase in the number of cd45-positive leukocytes and iba1-positive microglia. surprisingly, simultaneous injection of pf-4708671 (50 nmol/eye) with nmda significantly attenuated these responses without affecting phosphorylated s6 levels. these results suggest that pf-4708671 and rapamycin likely protect against nmda-induced retinal damage via distinct pathways. the neuroprotective effect of pf-4708671 is unlikely to be associated with inhibition of the s6 kinase, even though pf-4708671 is reported to be a s6 kinase inhibitor.
rewritten: in earlier research, we showed that rapamycin—a compound that inhibits the mammalian target of rapamycin (mtor)—can protect rats from retinal damage caused by n-methyl-d-aspartic acid (nmda). by suppressing mtor activity, rapamycin prevents the phosphorylation of ribosomal protein s6, which is a downstream target of s6 kinase. this led us to explore whether pf-4708671, known as an s6 kinase inhibitor, could offer similar protection against nmda-induced retinal injury. when nmda was injected into the eye (200 nmol/eye), it resulted in cell loss in the ganglion cell layer and triggered neuroinflammatory responses, including an increased number of cd45-positive leukocytes and iba1-positive microglia. interestingly, co-injecting pf-4708671 (50 nmol/eye) with nmda significantly reduced these effects, even though it did not change the levels of phosphorylated s6. these observations suggest that pf-4708671 and rapamycin may protect the retina from nmda-induced damage through different mechanisms. in fact, the protective effect of pf-4708671 appears to be unrelated to its role as an s6 kinase inhibitor.

raw: in present-day the most common issues that concerns everyone is traffic congestion on road at crossings where traffic light poles are placed. the objective is to flow and maintain traffic system automatically but unfortunately jamming queue exists while everything is fine from technology point of view because of predefined static time duration. this predefined time slots fail to capture real time scenarios in terms of traffic load and that leads to congestion. to avoid such type of problem, this work contributes the concept of dynamic traffic light slots as a best possible solution. dynamic traffic light is a concept of controlling the time duration of red and green light according to traffic flow or by sensing flow of traffic on road in movement. this can be achieved by using global positioning system which will convey the details of traffic flow and by considering that, time slots are provided to traffic lights i.e. whole controlling and managing will work like real time system.
rewritten: today, one of the most prevalent concerns is road traffic congestion at intersections controlled by traffic lights. the goal is to create and sustain an automated traffic flow system. however, despite technological advancements, congestion still occurs due to the reliance on fixed, predetermined time intervals for traffic signals. these static time slots fail to adapt to real-time traffic conditions, resulting in jams. to address this issue, the present work proposes the concept of dynamic traffic light timing as an optimal solution. this approach involves adjusting the duration of red and green lights based on the current traffic flow, achieved by monitoring the movement of vehicles. by utilizing a global positioning system to relay real-time traffic flow information, traffic lights can be controlled and managed as part of a dynamic, real-time system.

raw: even more modern to us. in his method and terminology for defining and redefining the notions of narrator and of "poet" in the middle ages, brownlee's constructs reflect the influence of uitti, hollander, kelly, benveniste, and genette in varying degrees, as the author himself acknowledges, yet his own formulation persuades by its very seamlessness. because of his unfailing cogency in sustaining his central point throughout the book, the author rightly finds no reason to devote his concluding chapter to a closing defense. rather he entitles it "ramifications of machaut's achievement" and proceeds to enlarge upon poirion's and kelly's insights while still respecting the earlier, monumental langlois (1902). he thereby underscores the transformations of machaut's "new kind of first-person, lyric-based poetic voice" in the works of deschamps, froissart, and villon. it is curious that in such a discussion of narrative voice, lyric je, poetprotagonists, and their evolution throughout each poet's oeuvre with a constant eye toward machaut, brownlee manages to jump to villon without any mention of alain chartier and, just as noticeably, christine de pizan, whose debt to machaut in this regard has only been superficially treated in schilperoort's dissertation (1936). is there any significant difference between a woman's poetic identity and a man's from that time? by the same token, surely the belle dame sans mercy, so influential in modern centuries, deserves some attention. yet such are not really failings, for brownlee has already accomplished a great deal in this book. rather the above suggestions only attest to the stimulating nature of his ideas, which will reward students of medieval literature and machaut specialists alike.
rewritten: brownlee's reinterpretation of the medieval concepts of narrator and “poet” is strikingly modern. in developing his method and terminology, he reveals influences from scholars like uitti, hollander, kelly, benveniste, and genette to varying extents—a fact he openly acknowledges—yet his own framework impresses with its fluid coherence. maintaining a consistently persuasive central argument throughout the book, he sees no need for a final, defensive chapter. instead, he names his concluding chapter "ramifications of machaut's achievement," where he expands on the insights of poirion and kelly while still paying homage to the foundational work of langlois (1902). in doing so, he underscores how machaut's “new kind of first-person, lyric-based poetic voice” transformed in the hands of later figures like deschamps, froissart, and villon. it is intriguing, however, that in his exploration of narrative voice and the evolution of the poet-protagonist—with an unwavering focus on machaut—brownlee leaps to discussing villon without mentioning alain chartier or christine de pizan, the latter of whom, in terms of her debt to machaut, was only marginally addressed in schilperoort’s 1936 dissertation. one might wonder if there really is any significant difference between the poetic identities of women and men from that period. similarly, the famous figure of belle dame sans merci, so influential in modern times, arguably merits further exploration. still, these observations do not detract from brownlee’s overall accomplishment; rather, they highlight the richness of his ideas, which promise to be both stimulating and rewarding for students of medieval literature and specialists on machaut alike.

rewritten: in the last ten chapters, we've explored straightforward, effective ways to put student-centered learning into practice. this chapter, however, takes a broader, more strategic look at implementing the full-scale paradigm shift.

raw: the invention discloses a calibrating method, a testing method for mobile phone detection and a system thereof. the calibrating method includes the following steps: passing a mobile phone calibration object through a sensing area of a receiving coil of a mobile phone detector; acquiring a phase feature value t of the mobile calibration object in correspondence to the receiving coil; writing the phase feature value t and type information of the mobile phone calibration object to a calibration object phase feature table in an alarm database. the testing method comprises the following steps: passing a to-be-detected object through the sensing area of the receiving coil of the mobile phone detector; acquiring a phase feature value r of the to-be-detected object; finding the calibration object phase feature table in the alarm database, determining whether the phase feature value r of the to-be-detected object meets determining conditions in the calibration phase feature table; if the phase feature value r of the to-be-detected object meets determining conditions in the calibration phase feature table, transmitting a suspected signal to an alarm module. the calibrating method, the test method for mobile phone detection and the system thereof can detect mobile phones, shorten detection time, and increase detection efficiency.
rewritten: the invention relates to a calibrating method, a mobile phone detection testing method, and a corresponding system. in the calibrating method, a mobile phone calibration object is first passed through the sensing area of the receiving coil of the mobile phone detector. next, the system acquires a phase feature value t for the calibration object corresponding to the receiving coil and then stores both the phase feature value t and the mobile phone calibration object's type information in a calibration object phase feature table within an alarm database. the testing method involves passing a to-be-detected object through the sensing area of the mobile phone detector's receiving coil and obtaining a phase feature value r from it. the system then retrieves the calibration object phase feature table from the alarm database and checks if the phase feature value r from the object meets the conditions specified in the table. if the conditions are met, a suspected signal is transmitted to an alarm module. this approach can detect mobile phones, reduce detection time, and enhance overall detection efficiency.

raw: adult craniofacial morphology results from complex processes that involve growth by bone modelling and interactions of skeletal components to keep a functional and structural balance. previous analyses of growth dynamics in humans revealed critical changes during late ontogeny explaining particular morphological features in our species. data on bone modelling patterns from other primate species could help us to determine whether postnatal changes in the growth dynamics of the craniofacial complex are human specific or are shared with other primates. however, characterizations of bone modelling patterns through ontogeny in non-human hominids are scarce and restricted to isolated data on facial and mandibular regions. in the present study, we analyse the bone modelling patterns in an ontogenetic series of pan and gorilla to infer the growth dynamics of their craniofacial complex during postnatal development. our results show that both pan troglodytes and gorilla gorilla are characterized by species-specific bone modelling patterns indicative of a mainly forward growth direction during postnatal development. both species show minor but consistent ontogenetic changes in the distribution of bone modelling fields in specific regions of the face and mandible, in contrast to other regions which show more constant bone modelling patterns. in addition, we carry out a preliminary integrative study merging histological and geometric morphometric data. both approaches yield highly complementary data, each analysis providing details on specific growth dynamics unavailable to the other. moreover, geometric morphometric data show that ontogenetic variation in the modelling pattern of the mandibular ramus may be linked to sexual dimorphism.
rewritten: adult craniofacial morphology arises from intricate processes involving bone modeling growth and the interaction of skeletal elements to maintain both functionality and structural balance. previous studies on human growth dynamics have identified crucial changes during late development that help explain specific morphological traits in our species. gathering data on bone modeling patterns from other primates could clarify whether postnatal alterations in the growth dynamics of the craniofacial complex are unique to humans or shared with other species. however, research detailing bone modeling throughout ontogeny in non-human primates is limited and has mainly focused on isolated data from the facial and mandibular regions. in our current study, we investigate the bone modeling patterns in an ontogenetic series of pan and gorilla to infer the postnatal growth dynamics of their craniofacial structures. our results indicate that both pan troglodytes and gorilla gorilla exhibit species-specific bone modeling patterns characterized by a predominantly forward growth direction after birth. additionally, both species display small but consistent ontogenetic shifts in the distribution of bone modeling regions in certain areas of the face and mandible, while other regions maintain more stable patterns. furthermore, we conducted a preliminary combined analysis integrating histological and geometric morphometric data. the complementary nature of these two methods provides detailed insights into specific growth dynamics that were not captured by a single approach. notably, the geometric morphometric analysis suggests that the developmental variation in the modeling pattern of the mandibular ramus may be associated with sexual dimorphism.

raw: a novel semiconductor device, a semiconductor device capable of operating at high speed, or a semiconductor device with low power consumption is provided. the semiconductor device includes a memory cell, a first circuit, a second circuit, and a wiring. the memory cell has a function of storing first data and has a function of supplying a first current corresponding to the first data to the wiring. the first circuit has a function of supplying a second current corresponding to second data to the wiring input from the outside. the second circuit has a function of performing correction of a current flowing in the wiring in the case where a value of the first current and a value of the second current are different from each other. the second circuit has a function of generating a signal including information on whether the correction is performed or not.
rewritten: a novel semiconductor device is provided that can operate at high speeds and/or consume low power. this device comprises a memory cell, a first circuit, a second circuit, and wiring. the memory cell is responsible for storing "first data" and for supplying a corresponding "first current" to the wiring. the first circuit supplies a "second current" to the wiring based on "second data" received from an external source. in situations where there is a discrepancy between the first and second currents, the second circuit corrects the wiring current and generates a signal indicating whether the correction has been executed.

raw: spontaneous effort-induced thrombosis is a rare but reported phenomena that was originally described over 100 years ago. the pathogenesis of this thrombosis arises from an abnormality of the thoracic outlet usually combined with a history of physical activity that includes repetitive arm motions, usually of the dominant hand. we present the case of an adolescent patient who presented to a pediatric emergency department with progressive pain, discoloration, and swelling of the shoulder of his nondominant hand. the pain became acutely worse with graying appearance of his arm. the patient was diagnosed with spontaneous thrombosis of the upper extremity extending from the left subclavian vein extending to the axillary vein. treatment of this patient included aggressive anticoagulation, thrombolysis, and costectomy.
rewritten: spontaneous effort-induced thrombosis is a rare condition that has been documented for over a century. its development is linked to an abnormality in the thoracic outlet, often in combination with a history of repetitive arm movements, typically involving the dominant hand. in this report, we describe an adolescent patient who came to a pediatric emergency department with progressively worsening pain, discoloration, and swelling in the shoulder area of his nondominant hand. the pain suddenly intensified, and his arm took on a grayish hue. he was diagnosed with spontaneous thrombosis of the upper extremity, extending from the left subclavian vein to the axillary vein. his treatment consisted of aggressive anticoagulation, thrombolysis, and costectomy.

raw: the parameters of an equivalent circuit of power transmission lines in the unpg single-line model are estimated from synchrophasor measurements of nodal voltages and currents. systematic and random errors in pmu measurements that lead to errors in the estimates of the transmission line parameters are considered.
rewritten: in the unpg single-line model, the equivalent circuit parameters of power transmission lines are determined using synchrophasor measurements of nodal voltages and currents. additionally, both systematic and random errors in pmu measurements that affect the accuracy of these transmission line parameter estimates are taken into account.

raw: the present invention relates to a method for producing an aromatic carbonate compound comprising introducing a cyclic acid anhydride during trans esterification of an aliphatic carbonate compound and an aromatic alcohol compound. through this, the present invention can provide a method for producing an aromatic carbonate compound that can effectively remove the aliphatic alcohol compound produced as a by-product, effectively reduce the reverse reaction, control the equilibrium constant to increase the amount of aromatic carbonate compound.
rewritten: the present invention concerns a technique for synthesizing an aromatic carbonate compound. this method incorporates the introduction of a cyclic acid anhydride during the transesterification reaction between an aliphatic carbonate and an aromatic alcohol. as a result, the process efficiently eliminates the aliphatic alcohol by-product, reduces the reverse reaction, and controls the equilibrium constant to increase the yield of the aromatic carbonate compound.

raw: we propose a topological model of induced gravity (pregeometry) where both newton's coupling constant and the cosmological constant appear as integration constants in solving field equations. the matter sector of a scalar field is also considered, and by solving field equations it is shown that various types of cosmological solutions in the frw universe can be obtained. a detailed analysis is given of the meaning of the brst transformations, which make the induced gravity be a topological field theory, by means of the canonical quantization analysis, and the physical reason why such brst transformations are needed in the present formalism is clarified. finally, we propose a dynamical mechanism for fixing the lagrange multiplier fields by following the higgs mechanism. the present study clearly indicates that the induced gravity can be constructed at the classical level without recourse to quantum fluctuations of matter and suggests an interesting relationship between the induced gravity and the topological quantum field theory.
rewritten: we introduce a topological model of induced gravity (or pregeometry) in which both newton’s coupling constant and the cosmological constant naturally emerge as integration constants when solving the field equations. in addition, we incorporate a scalar field for the matter sector, showing through the field equations that a variety of cosmological solutions within the frw universe can be realized. we offer an in-depth examination of the brst transformations—which ensure that the induced gravity model functions as a topological field theory—using canonical quantization to clarify their significance and to explain the necessity of these transformations in our approach. lastly, we propose a dynamic mechanism, inspired by the higgs mechanism, for fixing the lagrange multiplier fields. overall, our study demonstrates that induced gravity can be established at the classical level without the need for matter’s quantum fluctuations, and it suggests an intriguing link between induced gravity and topological quantum field theory.
