raw: the invention discloses a technique for separating high-molecular-weight heparin sodium by an enzymolysis process. by using the method, the molecular weight of the heparin sodium refined product is 15000-19000 dalton, and the ratio of the heparin sodium with the molecular weight of 8000-16000 dalton to the heparin sodium with the molecular weight of 16000-24000 dalton is not less than 1.0. the technical scheme is as follows: the method comprises the following steps: decomposing high-molecular-weight heparin sodium in the heparin sodium by regulating the consumptions of trypsinase and pepsin in the enzymolysis process, and carrying out repeated precipitation-oxidation refinement by using ethanol to thoroughly separate the heparin sodium from other ribonucleic acids and other impurities. the heparin sodium refined product yield reaches 90% or above, the xa valence/ iia valence ratio is 0.9-1.1, and all the other quality standards conform to the specifications in chinese pharmacopoeia, us pharmacopoeia, british pharmacopoeia and western europe pharmacopoeia.
rewritten: the invention presents a method for isolating high-molecular-weight heparin sodium using an enzymatic digestion process. by employing this technique, the refined heparin sodium product has a molecular weight between 15,000 and 19,000 daltons, and the proportion of heparin sodium with a molecular weight of 8,000–16,000 daltons relative to that with 16,000–24,000 daltons is at least 1.0. the technical approach is as follows: the process involves these steps: first, high-molecular-weight heparin sodium is decomposed by carefully controlling the amounts of trypsin and pepsin during enzymatic digestion. then, repeated ethanol-induced precipitation and oxidation refinement are performed to thoroughly separate heparin sodium from ribonucleic acids and other impurities. as a result, the yield of the refined heparin sodium product reaches 90% or more, with an xa valence/ija valence ratio between 0.9 and 1.1. additionally, all quality attributes comply with the standards set forth in the chinese, u.s., british, and western european pharmacopoeias.

raw: in individually perfused intact microvessels, we showed that circulating tumor cells prefer to adhere to the microvessel sites with higher no concentrations, which are generated by the localized sh...
rewritten: in individually perfused, intact microvessels, we found that circulating tumor cells are more likely to attach at sites where nitric oxide levels are elevated due to localized shear forces.

raw: at the end of the pleistocene, during folsom occupation, the estancia basin contained the eastern-most pluvial lake in the american southwest. the basin has a long history of archaeological research, and its story of changing lake levels has played an important part in understanding the paleoindian occupation of the new world. within the basin, geoarchaeological assessment at the martin site can be used as a baseline for understanding environmental change during the late pleistocene. the large well documented martin and lucy folsom artifact assemblages provide a window into lithic technological organization. combining these data within a broader basin-wide analysis provides a glimpse at folsom occupation and land use between the well-studied southern high plains and the middle rio grande. by combining the stories of environmental change, fluctuating lake levels, lithic variability, and human mobility, a better picture of life at the pleistocene/holocene transition emerges.
rewritten: at the close of the pleistocene, during folsom occupation, the estancia basin was home to the easternmost pluvial lake in the american southwest. this basin, with its extensive archaeological record, has been key to reconstructing paleoindian settlement through its documented fluctuations in lake levels. in particular, geoarchaeological studies at the martin site within the basin serve as a baseline for understanding environmental changes that occurred during the late pleistocene. additionally, the comprehensive martin and lucy folsom artifact collections offer valuable insights into the organization of lithic technology. when these findings are integrated into a broader, basin-wide framework, they shed light on folsom occupation and land use patterns between the well-studied southern high plains and the middle rio grande. by combining perspectives on environmental change, variations in lake levels, lithic diversity, and human mobility, a clearer picture of life at the pleistocene–holocene transition emerges.

raw: the invention provides a half-bridge wheatstone bridge strain measuring system capable of eliminating conductor resistance influence. the system comprises a half-bridge wheatstone bridge, a contact switch k1, a contact switch k2 and two calibration resistors r0. the first input end a of the half-bridge wheatstone bridge is connected with a stationary contact s0 of the contact switch k1 through a conductor; the first input end a is connected with a stationary contact s1 of the contact switch k1 through one calibration resistor r0; a movable contact s2 of the contact switch k1 is connected with a power source e; a second input end b of the half-bridge wheatstone bridge is connected with a stationary contact s4 of the contact switch k2 through a conductor; the second input end b is also connected with a stationary contact s3 of the contact switch k2 through the other calibration resistor s0; a movable contact s5 of the contact switch k2 is grounded. the influence of conductor resistance on strain measuring results can be effectively eliminated and the strain measuring precision is greatly improved.
rewritten: the invention discloses a half-bridge wheatstone bridge strain measuring system that eliminates the influence of conductor resistance. the system includes a half-bridge wheatstone bridge, two contact switches (k1 and k2), and a pair of calibration resistors (r0). specifically, the first input terminal (a) of the wheatstone bridge is connected to the stationary contact s0 of contact switch k1 via a conductor, and it is also linked to the stationary contact s1 of k1 through one calibration resistor (r0). meanwhile, the movable contact s2 of k1 is connected to a power source (e). on the other side, the second input terminal (b) of the wheatstone bridge connects to the stationary contact s4 of contact switch k2 by a conductor, and it is further connected to the stationary contact s3 of k2 using the other calibration resistor (r0). the movable contact s5 of k2 is grounded. as a result, any effects of conductor resistance on strain measurements are effectively eliminated, greatly improving the measurement precision.

raw: disclosed is a soil conditioner using byproducts from mining industry. the soil conditioner is manufactured by mixing raw ore, molybdenum tailing, and decomposed granite soil. the soil conditioner can be manufactured to be rich in minerals while having uniform particle sizes, and can be optimally manufactured to fit vegetation of plants, thereby enabling a use of waste minerals causing environmental contamination.
rewritten: the invention is a soil conditioner created from byproducts of the mining industry. it is produced by mixing raw ore, molybdenum tailings, and decomposed granite soil. the resulting product can be engineered to have a uniform particle size and be rich in minerals, and it can be optimized to support plant growth, thereby repurposing mineral waste that might otherwise harm the environment.

raw: we obtain classification, solvability, and nonexistence theorems for positive stationary states of reaction-diffusion and schrodinger systems involving a balance between repulsive and attractive terms. this class of systems contains pde arising in biological models of lotka--volterra type, in physical models of bose--einstein condensates, and in models of chemical reactions. we show, with different proofs, that the results obtained in [a. montaru, b. sirakov, and p. souplet, arch. ration. mech. anal., 213 (2014), pp. 129--169] for models with homogeneous diffusion are valid for general heterogeneous media, and even for controlled inhomogeneous diffusions.
rewritten: we derive classification, solvability, and nonexistence results for positive steady states of reaction-diffusion and schrödinger systems that balance repulsive and attractive forces. this family of systems includes the partial differential equations found in lotka–volterra biological models, bose–einstein condensate physics, and chemical reaction models. moreover, we demonstrate through various proofs that the outcomes obtained in [a. montaru, b. sirakov, and p. souplet, arch. rational mech. anal., 213 (2014), pp. 129–169] for models with homogeneous diffusion also extend to more general heterogeneous media, including cases with controlled inhomogeneous diffusions.

raw: abstract background arterial repair after intracoronary stenting depends on stent types and plaque vulnerability. the aim of this study was angioscopic comparison of arterial repair after implantation of the second-generation drug-eluting stents (g2-des) between the stable and vulnerable plaques. methods four types of g2-des were successfully implanted into stable plaques (n=41) and vulnerable plaques (n=34): 13 and 9 xience-everolimus-, 7 and 6 endeavor-zotarolimus-, 15 and 9 resolute-zotarolimus-, and 6 and 10 nobori-biolimus-eluting stents (ees, e-zes, r-zes, and bes), respectively. coronary angioscopy at 1-year follow-up revealed in-stent appearance, such as neointimal stent coverage (nsc), presence of yellow plaques (yp), and in-stent mural thrombus (mt). nsc was graded into poor and good coverage. results yellow plaques and mural thrombi were found more frequently in the vulnerable plaques than in the stable plaques (29% vs. 12%, p=0.06; 12 vs. 0%, p=0.02; respectively). in ees, poor nsc was observed more frequently in the vulnerable plaques than in the stable plaques (54% vs. 11%, p=0.04). in bes, yp was observed more frequently in the vulnerable plaques than in the stable plaques (80% vs. 17%, p=0.01). in e-zes and r-zes, there were no significant differences with regards to angioscopic parameters between the stable and vulnerable plaques. conclusions arterial repair after ees and bes implantation into the vulnerable plaques remained vulnerable even at 1-year follow-up.
rewritten: abstract background: the process of arterial repair following intracoronary stenting is influenced by the type of stent used as well as the vulnerability of the plaque. this study aimed to compare, via angioscopy, the arterial healing after implantation of second-generation drug-eluting stents (g2-des) in both stable and vulnerable plaques. methods: four types of g2-des were implanted successfully into stable plaques (n = 41) and vulnerable plaques (n = 34). the stents used were: xience-everolimus-eluting stents (ees; 13 in stable plaques vs. 9 in vulnerable plaques), endeavor-zotarolimus-eluting stents (e-zes; 7 vs. 6), resolute-zotarolimus-eluting stents (r-zes; 15 vs. 9), and nobori-biolimus-eluting stents (bes; 6 vs. 10). at a 1-year follow-up, coronary angioscopy assessed stent appearance by looking at neointimal stent coverage (nsc), the presence of yellow plaques (yp), and in-stent mural thrombus (mt). neointimal stent coverage was categorized as either poor or good. results: vulnerable plaques showed a higher incidence of yellow plaques and mural thrombi compared to stable plaques (yp: 29% vs. 12%, p = 0.06; mt: 12% vs. 0%, p = 0.02). in the ees group, poor nsc was significantly more common in vulnerable plaques than in stable ones (54% vs. 11%, p = 0.04). similarly, in the bes group, yellow plaques were found more often in vulnerable plaques than in stable plaques (80% vs. 17%, p = 0.01). however, for e-zes and r-zes, no significant differences in angioscopic findings were observed between the stable and vulnerable plaques. conclusions: the findings suggest that arterial repair following the use of ees and bes in vulnerable plaques remains suboptimal, with the vulnerable characteristics persisting even at one year post-implantation.

raw: teaching english for children requires thoughtful considerations of what and how to teach. it needs full considerations such as what components of the language should be taught, what method should be applied, and what teaching technique should be employed. teaching english components i.e. grammar, vocabulary, and pronunciation, a teacher must take the nature of children learning into account. teaching vocabulary, teacher should plan and organize the material to learn carefully. concrete objects should come before the abstract ones. teaching vocabulary is not only about teaching words as separate units but formulaic chunks. in addition, teaching strategies play an important role in children's learning success. learning grammar is closely related to learning vocabulary. when children learn vocabulary through learning formulaic chunks, the teacher can discuss the grammar too. one important thing that teacher should bear in her mind is that the opportunity to use the language in a meaning-focused situation is as important as learning the grammatical patterns. the other language component to learn is pronunciation. as the other components, teaching pronunciation to young learners also need thoughtful consideration. normally native speakers say an expression in blended sounds. they will not say each word clearly as separate unit. hence, teachers should drill students to say expressions and guide students to feel and experience saying them in normal and natural way.
rewritten: when teaching english to children, educators need to carefully plan what to teach and how to teach it. this involves deciding which language components to cover, selecting appropriate methods, and choosing effective teaching techniques. when addressing elements like grammar, vocabulary, and pronunciation, a teacher must consider the unique way children learn. for example, when introducing vocabulary, it is important to organize the material thoughtfully—starting with concrete objects before moving on to abstract concepts—and to teach words not just as individual terms but also as part of common phrases. furthermore, instructional strategies are crucial to a child's success. learning grammar is intertwined with learning vocabulary; when children acquire vocabulary through set phrases, teachers have the opportunity to explain the related grammar. additionally, it is essential that teachers provide meaningful contexts in which children can actively use the language, as this practical application is just as important as understanding grammatical structures. similarly, when teaching pronunciation, careful consideration is needed. native speakers typically blend sounds in natural speech rather than pronouncing each word distinctly. therefore, teachers should practice whole expressions with their students and guide them to experience and produce these blended, natural sounds.

raw: chelating ligands in the field of coordination chemistry and their metal complexes are of great interest since many years. it is well known that n, s and o atoms play a key role in the coordination of metals at the active sites of numerous metallo biomolecules. chelating ligands containing o, n and s donor atoms show broad biological activity and are of special interest because of the variety of ways in which they are bonded to metal ions.
rewritten: for many years, chelating ligands and their metal complexes have attracted significant interest in coordination chemistry. it is well known that nitrogen, sulfur, and oxygen atoms are crucial in binding metals at the active sites of many metallo biomolecules. chelating ligands featuring o, n, and s donor atoms exhibit a wide range of biological activities and are particularly noteworthy due to the various ways they can coordinate with metal ions.

raw: the invention discloses a total protein extraction method for pitaya fleshy stem segments. the pitaya fleshy stem segments are crushed and then added with a protein cracking agent, the crushed pitaya fleshy stem segments are subjected to ice cracking for 40-60 minutes and then subjected to intermittent ultrasonic crushing, the protein cracking agent is added into the upper-layer sticky solution and then the mixture is subjected to ice cracking for 10-30 minutes to obtain an upper-layer clear liquid, the upper-layer clear liquid is added with pre-cooled acetone and allowed to precipitate overnight at 20 deg c to obtain a protein precipitate containing the colloidal sticky substance, the protein precipitate is added with pre-cooled acetone, lightly shaken and oscillated to obtain the protein precipitate, the protein precipitate is aired at the natural state and added with the protein cracking agent, and then filtered by a plasmid extraction adsorption column, and the lower layer is the total protein, wherein the protein cracking agent is a protein cracking agent added with a protein inhibitor. the clear total protein solution is obtained by the method, the total protein of the pitaya fleshy stem segments can be highly efficiently extracted, be suitable for proteomics analysis and also be suitable for total protein extraction of other meat plants.
rewritten: the invention presents a method for extracting total protein from pitaya fleshy stem segments. in this process, the pitaya stems are first crushed, and a protein disruption agent (which includes a protein inhibitor) is added. the crushed material then undergoes an ice treatment for 40–60 minutes, followed by intermittent ultrasonic crushing. next, the protein disruption agent is mixed into the upper-layer sticky solution, and the mixture is subjected to ice treatment for an additional 10–30 minutes to obtain a clear liquid layer. pre-cooled acetone is then added to this clear liquid, and the mixture is allowed to precipitate overnight at 20°c, yielding a protein precipitate that contains colloidal sticky substances. additional pre-cooled acetone is combined with the precipitate, and the mixture is gently shaken and oscillated to form the protein precipitate. after air drying naturally, the precipitate is treated again with the protein disruption agent and then filtered using a plasmid extraction adsorption column, where the lower layer constitutes the total protein. the process ultimately yields a clear total protein solution, enabling highly efficient extraction from pitaya fleshy stem segments. moreover, this method is not only suitable for proteomics analysis but can also be applied to the total protein extraction of other fleshy plants.

raw: a planetary gear speed reduction device may include a magnetic sun gear; a plurality of magnetic planetary gears which each revolve around the magnetic sun gear while rotating; and a magnetic internal gear arranged surrounding the multiple magnetic planetary gears from an outer circumferential side. an outside diameter of the magnetic sun gear and an outside diameter of the magnetic planetary gears may be equal.
rewritten: a planetary gear speed reduction device can comprise a magnetic sun gear, several magnetic planetary gears that both rotate around and spin on their own axes in relation to the sun gear, and a magnetic internal gear positioned concentrically around these planetary gears. additionally, the magnetic sun gear and the magnetic planetary gears may share the same outer diameter.

raw: in this study, robust brown-forsythe and robust modified brown-forsythe anova tests are proposed to take into consideration heteroscedastic and non-normality data sets with outliers. the non-normal data is assumed to be a two parameters weibull distribution. robust proposed tests are obtained by using robust mean and variance estimators based on median=mad and median=qn methods instead of maximum likelihood. the behaviors of the robust proposed and classical anova tests are examined by simulation study. the results shows that the proposed robust tests have good performance especially in the presence of heteroscedasticity and contamination.
rewritten: this study introduces robust brown–forsythe and modified brown–forsythe anova tests designed to handle data sets that exhibit heteroscedasticity and non-normality in the presence of outliers. the non-normal data are assumed to follow a two-parameter weibull distribution. instead of the maximum likelihood approach, the proposed robust tests utilize robust estimators of mean and variance based on the median absolute deviation (mad) and the median qn methods. a simulation study is conducted to evaluate the behavior of both the proposed robust tests and traditional anova tests, showing that the robust tests perform well, particularly when heteroscedasticity and data contamination are present.

raw: the invention provides a waterproof ultraviolet-resistant cable sheath material. the cable sheath material is prepared from, by weight, 80-100 parts of ethylene-propylene-diene monomer, 60-80 parts of acrylic rubber, 50-75 parts of ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 0.2-0.8 part of ultraviolet light absorber uv-9, 2-6 parts of a vulcanizing agent, 1.5-5 parts of a vulcanization accelerator, 1-4.5 parts of vulcanization accelerating auxiliaries, 0.5-3.5 parts of a scorch retarder, 1-5 parts of an antiager, 10-25 parts of a flame retardant, 8-20 parts of a plasticizer, 0.5-3 parts of a coupling agent, 3-10 parts of a stabilizer, 15-25 parts of a softener, 35-45 parts of a reinforcing agent and 10-40 parts of a filling agent. due to addition of the acrylic rubber and the ultraviolet light absorber uv-9, the cable sheath material has an excellent ultraviolet-proof performance; due to addition of the ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, the cable sheath material is further excellent in low temperature resistance and water-resistant and waterproof performances.
rewritten: the invention relates to a waterproof, ultraviolet-resistant cable sheath material. it is composed of, by weight, 80–100 parts ethylene-propylene-diene monomer, 60–80 parts acrylic rubber, 50–75 parts ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 0.2–0.8 part of an ultraviolet light absorber (uv-9), 2–6 parts of a vulcanizing agent, 1.5–5 parts of a vulcanization accelerator, 1–4.5 parts of vulcanization accelerating auxiliaries, 0.5–3.5 parts of a scorch retarder, 1–5 parts of an antiager, 10–25 parts of a flame retardant, 8–20 parts of a plasticizer, 0.5–3 parts of a coupling agent, 3–10 parts of a stabilizer, 15–25 parts of a softener, 35–45 parts of a reinforcing agent, and 10–40 parts of a filling agent. the inclusion of acrylic rubber along with uv-9 ensures excellent ultraviolet protection, while the integration of ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer provides outstanding low-temperature resistance as well as water-resistant and waterproof capabilities.

raw: phase balancing is essential to safe power system operation. we consider a substation connected to multiple phases, each with single-phase loads, generation, and energy storage. a representative of the substation operates the system and aims to minimize the cost of all phases and to balance loads among phases. we first consider ideal energy storage with lossless charging and discharging, and propose both centralized and distributed real-time algorithms taking into account system uncertainty. the proposed algorithm does not require any system statistics and asymptotically achieves the minimum system cost with large energy storage. we then extend the algorithm to accommodate more realistic non-ideal energy storage that has imperfect charging and discharging. the performance of the proposed algorithm is evaluated through extensive simulation and compared with that of a benchmark greedy algorithm. simulation shows that our algorithm leads to strong performance over a wide range of storage characteristics.
rewritten: phase balancing plays a crucial role in ensuring safe power system operations. in our scenario, a substation is connected to multiple phases, each supplying single-phase loads, generation, and energy storage. a substation representative is tasked with managing the system by minimizing the overall cost across all phases and ensuring that loads are evenly distributed. initially, we focus on ideal energy storage systems that allow lossless charging and discharging, and we introduce both centralized and distributed real-time algorithms designed to handle system uncertainties. notably, these algorithms require no prior system statistics and, given ample energy storage, asymptotically reach the minimum possible system cost. we further adapt our approach to address more practical situations involving non-ideal energy storage systems that experience inefficiencies in charging and discharging. finally, extensive simulations are used to evaluate the performance of our proposed algorithm, and its effectiveness is compared against a benchmark greedy algorithm. the results indicate that our algorithm consistently performs well across a diverse range of storage characteristics.

raw: the invention provides a credit-based parking management system, at least comprising parking lot equipment including a amera, a standard barrier gate, and a digit video stall detection terminal; a cloud calculating platform supporting operation of the database, the middleware and other correlated software of an intelligent parking comprehensive management platform; a parking management service center including stall management, parking lot equipment management and a command center; and a parking business center including parking management, charging management and credit management. the system and method can manage all parking sources in urban areas, uniformly schedule stall resources and increase a stall utilization rate; through credit-based dynamic stall prices, the system and method can guide legal parking, and raise illegal parking costs; in addition, an advanced oto-based operation management mode is introduced, thereby more effectively raising parking income, vitalizing urban complex economy, and guiding urban traffic and parking standardization.
rewritten: the invention introduces a credit-based parking management system that includes, at minimum, the following components: • parking lot equipment, which comprises a camera, a conventional barrier gate, and a digital video stall detection terminal. • a cloud computing platform that supports the operation of a comprehensive intelligent parking management platform—this platform manages databases, middleware, and other related software. • a parking management service center, responsible for stall management, parking lot equipment management, and acting as a command center. • a parking business center, which handles parking management, charging, and credit management. this system and method are designed to manage all urban parking spaces, allowing for the uniform scheduling of stall resources and enhancing stall utilization rates. by employing credit-based dynamic pricing for stalls, the system can encourage legal parking behavior while increasing the costs associated with illegal parking. moreover, by introducing an advanced oto-based operational management model, it more effectively boosts parking revenue, revitalizes the urban complex economy, and promotes the standardization of urban traffic and parking.

raw: the utility model discloses a blue tooth surface board for become the power amplifier signal with the bluetooth signal conversion of output, accept including one the nfc antenna receiving module of output bluetooth signal, nfc antenna receiving module can be with received bluetooth signal transmission to a stereo bluetooth input module, stereo bluetooth input module can be stereo signal and transmission to class d digit power amplifier module with received bluetooth signal conversion, classd digit power amplifier module is used for sending stereo signal conversion to receiving terminal for the power amplifier signal and plays, still include a synchronous?rectifier switching?power supply?module, this module can for stereo bluetooth input module and class d digit power amplifier module provide the power. the utility model discloses a blue tooth surface board, can be very convenient with the mode of signal corresponding in the cell -phone through wireless transmission, realize broadcasting the sound source in the cell -phone under family or other places, realize the function of individualized music broadcast.
rewritten: the utility model describes a bluetooth board that converts bluetooth signals into power amplifier signals. it includes an nfc antenna receiving module that captures the bluetooth signal and sends it to a stereo bluetooth input module. this stereo input module then converts the received bluetooth signal into stereo signals, which are forwarded to a class d digital power amplifier module. the power amplifier module converts these stereo signals into power amplifier signals for the receiving terminal to play. additionally, the design comprises a synchronous rectifier switching power supply module that provides power to both the stereo bluetooth input module and the class d digital power amplifier module. overall, this bluetooth board offers a convenient way to connect wirelessly with a cell phone, allowing sound sources from the phone to be broadcast in environments such as homes or other areas and enabling personalized music playback.

raw: animals have an innate curiosity that drives them to explore their world because this aids their survival. if you place a rat in a new environment, it systematically moves about this environment until it has covered the whole of it. human beings in addition have an innate tendency to seek explanations about the nature of their world. this tendency is most obviously seen in the curiosity of young children, who drive their parents frantic by endlessly asking questions about the universe, many of which the parents cannot answer. i recall asking my parents why the sky is blue, and they were unable to tell me. historically, there have been two quite distinct ways by which adult humans try to explain the universe. philosophers call these two ways, supernaturalism and naturalism.
rewritten: animals naturally exhibit curiosity that leads them to explore their environments—a behavior essential for their survival. for instance, when a rat enters a new space, it methodically traverses the area until it has covered every part of it. similarly, humans also possess an intrinsic drive to understand the world around them. this urge to seek explanations is most clearly observable in young children, who often keep their parents busy with constant questions about the universe, many of which go unanswered. i once asked my parents why the sky is blue, but they couldn’t provide an explanation. over the course of history, adult humans have tended to explain the universe using two fundamentally different approaches, which philosophers refer to as supernaturalism and naturalism.

raw: managing diabetes poses substantial challenges to the over 29.1 million americans afflicted, and is financially overwhelming to the us health care system. one potential strategy is utilizing a group approach to care delivery or shared medical appointment (sma). the purpose of this 3-year retrospective va study was to investigate differences in clinical and quality outcome measures in veterans with type 2 diabetes who used smas and those who received only usual care (uc) one-on-one with their doctor. this observational, 2-group cohort study used abstracted medical records from a large midwestern veterans administration hospital. clinical outcome metrics included hemoglobin a1c (hba1c), systolic blood pressure, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and emergency department (ed) visits. quality outcomes included veterans' administration (va) department of defense clinical practice guidelines for the management of diabetes. a total of 988 total va cases were examined retrospectively over 3 years: 371 cases had used smas and 617 were in the uc cohort, and had never attended a diabetes sma. the study period used abstracted va medical records from 2008 to 2010. there were no statistically significant differences in hba1c, systolic blood pressure, and ed visits between groups; however, hba1c for individuals who attended smas was 8.55 (standard deviation [sd] = 1.72) and uc was 7.49 (sd = 1.28) (p < .001). all clinical outcomes were worse at baseline for the sma cohort. uc had mean ed visits/3 years (mean = 18.62, sd = 13.53, p < .001) versus sma participants (mean = 27.97, sd = 14.00, p <. 001), revealing a propensity for high health care utilization. sma providers had statistically significant differences over uc cases on quality measures, including ordering annual ophthalmology and podiatry examinations (p < .001) and prescribing aspirin and angiotension-converting enzyme inhibitors (ace-i). smas may provide a venue for assessing and delivering quality care for patients with type 2 diabetes. more research is needed to ascertain effective strategies for diabetes disease management in high-risk patients.
rewritten: managing diabetes presents significant challenges for the more than 29.1 million americans affected by the condition and places a heavy financial burden on the us healthcare system. one possible strategy to counter these issues is to adopt a group-based care model, commonly known as shared medical appointments (smas). this 3-year retrospective study conducted within the va system aimed to compare clinical and quality outcomes in veterans with type 2 diabetes who participated in smas versus those who received traditional one-on-one care (uc) with their physicians. the observational, two-group cohort study analyzed medical records from a large veterans administration hospital in the midwest. clinical outcomes included measurements such as hemoglobin a1c (hba1c), systolic blood pressure, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, and the number of emergency department (ed) visits. quality outcomes were assessed based on the clinical practice guidelines issued by the veterans administration and the department of defense for managing diabetes. a total of 988 va cases were examined over a three-year period, with 371 cases in the sma group and 617 in the uc group (which never attended a diabetes sma). the study used records from 2008 to 2010. although there were no statistically significant differences between groups in systolic blood pressure and ed visits, the hba1c levels for sma participants were higher (8.55 with a standard deviation [sd] of 1.72) compared to those in the uc group (7.49, sd = 1.28; p < .001). notably, all clinical outcomes were worse at baseline for the sma group. furthermore, the uc group averaged 18.62 ed visits over three years (sd = 13.53, p < .001) compared to 27.97 visits (sd = 14.00, p < .001) among sma participants, indicating a propensity for higher healthcare utilization. on quality measures, providers managing smas showed statistically significant improvements over those handling uc cases by more consistently ordering annual ophthalmology and podiatry examinations (p < .001) and by prescribing aspirin and angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors (ace-i). in conclusion, smas may offer an effective framework for evaluating and delivering quality care to patients with type 2 diabetes. however, further research is necessary to determine the most effective management strategies for high-risk patients with diabetes.

raw: this paper presents an ultrasensitive and inexpensive method for ricin detection by surface enhanced raman scattering (sers) on shape-controlled gold nanocrystals substrates fabricated by integrating electrodeposition with dielectrophoresis (dep). the ricin is captured by the rabbit anti-ricin polyclonal antibody which immobilized on the substrates. it was show that the limit of detection(lod) could reach 0.1ng/ml in phosphate buffered saline (pbs), which is 2000 times less than the minimum lethal dose(with 500ml liquor).
rewritten: this paper introduces a highly sensitive and cost-effective approach for detecting ricin using surface-enhanced raman scattering (sers) on gold nanocrystal substrates with controlled shapes. these substrates are fabricated by combining electrodeposition with dielectrophoresis (dep). the detection method involves capturing ricin with rabbit anti-ricin polyclonal antibodies that are immobilized on the substrates. it was demonstrated that the limit of detection (lod) could reach 0.1 ng/ml in phosphate buffered saline (pbs), which is 2000 times lower than the minimum lethal dose (considering 500 ml of liquor).

raw: probabilistic safety/risk assessment provides a quantitative framework to estimate risk and identify risk contributors. this chapter introduces the concept of risk and gives an overview of probabilistic safety assessment steps. special emphasis is given to event tree analysis, importance measures, common cause failure analysis, and human reliability analysis, which are essential and important elements of any safety assessment. several examples are given to supplement the understanding of these approaches. some of the remaining important analysis approaches such as fault tree analysis and uncertainty analysis are already covered in rest of the book.
rewritten: probabilistic safety/risk assessment offers a quantitative method for estimating risk and pinpointing its contributing factors. this chapter introduces the idea of risk and provides an overview of the steps involved in probabilistic safety assessment. particular focus is placed on event tree analysis, importance measures, common cause failure analysis, and human reliability analysis—key components in any safety evaluation. multiple examples are included to enhance understanding of these methods. other significant analytical techniques, like fault tree analysis and uncertainty analysis, are discussed in other sections of the book.

raw: understanding how nanoparticles (nps) diffuse, stick, and assemble into larger structures within polymers is key to the design and fabrication of np-polymer composites. here we describe an approach for inferring the dynamic parameters of np assembly from spatially and temporally disjointed images of composites. the approach involves iterative adjustment of the parameters of a kinetic model of assembly until the computed size statistics of np clusters match those obtained from high-throughput analysis of the experimental images. application of this approach to the assembly of shaped, metal nps in polymer films suggests that np structures grow via a cluster-cluster aggregation mechanism, where nps and their clusters diffuse with approximately stokes-einstein diffusivity and stick to other nps or clusters with a probability that depends strongly on the size and shape of the nps and the molecular weight of the polymer.
rewritten: grasping how nanoparticles spread out, adhere, and form larger assemblies within polymers is essential for creating np-polymer composites. in this work, we introduce a method to deduce the dynamic parameters of nanoparticle assembly by using images of composites taken at different times and locations. this method entails repeatedly fine-tuning the parameters of a kinetic assembly model until the derived size distribution of nanoparticle clusters aligns with those obtained from extensive experimental image analysis. when applied to the assembly of shaped metal nanoparticles in polymer films, the technique indicates that nanoparticle structures develop through a cluster-cluster aggregation process, where both individual nanoparticles and their clusters move with roughly stokes-einstein diffusion rates and adhere to other particles or clusters with a likelihood that strongly varies based on nanoparticle size, shape, and the polymer's molecular weight.

raw: the invention relates to a rechargeable folding table lamp with an lcd screen. the rechargeable folding table lamp with the lcd screen comprises a base plate (1), a connecting supporting plate (2) and a lamp base plate (3). a first hinge joint seat (5) is arranged at the front end of the base plate (1) and a second hinge joint seat (6) is arranged at the rear end of the lamp base plate (3). the upper and lower ends of the connecting supporting plate (2) are hinged to the first hinge joint seat (5) and the second hinge joint seat (6) separately by rotary shafts. a touching switch (4) is arranged at the front face of the base plate (1) and an led area light source (7) is arranged at the back face of the lamp base plate (3). an lcd screen (8) is arranged at the front face of a middle supporting plate and a battery compartment (9) is arranged below the lcd screen (8). a usb charging port is arranged at the rear side of the base plate (1). the rechargeable folding table lamp with the lcd screen is capable of being folded conveniently, small in occupied space when not in use, and convenient to store and carry.
rewritten: the present invention concerns a rechargeable, folding table lamp that integrates an lcd screen. this lamp includes a base plate (1), a connecting support plate (2), and a lamp base plate (3). a first hinge joint seat (5) is located at the front edge of the base plate (1), while a second hinge joint seat (6) is positioned at the rear end of the lamp base plate (3). the connecting support plate (2) is connected at its upper and lower ends via rotary shafts to the first and second hinge joint seats (5 and 6) respectively. additionally, a touch switch (4) is installed on the front face of the base plate (1), and an led area light source (7) is attached to the back face of the lamp base plate (3). an lcd screen (8) is mounted on the front face of a middle supporting plate, with a battery compartment (9) arranged below the lcd screen (8). a usb charging port is also provided at the rear side of the base plate (1). this design allows the lamp to be folded easily, occupies minimal space when not in use, and is simple to store and carry.

raw: with as/rs corbel shelf as research object, the orthogonal experiment was designed, which is composed of 21 groups of schemes and 3 kinds of load conditions. by using the rack computer-aided fea system based on sap84, this paper presents the effects of grounding back-stayed and the ground floor height of back-stayed on the strength, rigidity and stability of corbel shelf under normal load, horizontal load in x direction and horizontal load in y direction. the results show that in all conditions, grounding back-stayed improves the strength and stability of column, and the strength and stability of corbel shelf will be enhanced with the decrease of the ground floor height of back-stayed. this research provides a theoretical basis for the analysis and design of corbel shelf.
rewritten: using the as/rs corbel shelf as the research subject, an orthogonal experiment was developed comprising 21 scheme groups and 3 types of load conditions. utilizing a rack computer-aided finite element analysis system based on sap84, the study examines how grounding the back-stayed structure and varying the ground floor height of the back-stayed affect the strength, stiffness, and stability of the corbel shelf under normal loads, as well as horizontal loads in both the x and y directions. the findings reveal that in every scenario, grounding the back-stayed structure enhances the column's strength and stability, and that reducing the ground floor height of the back-stayed further improves the strength and stability of the corbel shelf. this research provides a theoretical foundation for the analysis and design of corbel shelves.

raw: background early diagnosis of postoperative pancreatic fistula (popf) is important for proper interventions. the preoperative, intraoperative and early postoperative biochemical markers have predictive value of popf. the present study was to evaluate several simple biochemical parameters in the prediction of popf. methods patients who underwent pancreaticoduodenectomy in our center between 2006 and 2015 were reviewed retrospectively. preoperative and early postoperative biochemical parameters were evaluated. additionally, the relationship between popf and ph and lactate level at the end of surgery were analyzed, and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr), platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (plr), and red cell distribution width-to-platelet ratio (rpr) were calculated for postoperative days (pods) 1 and 3. diagnosis and grading of popf were performed according to the standards of the international study group on pancreatic fistula. the patients were divided into two groups: group 1 with no fistula or grade-a fistula; group 2 with grade-b or -c fistula. these simple biochemical markers were then compared between the two groups. results serum amylase level was significantly higher at pod3, and ph level was significantly lower at the end of operation in group 2 compared with those in group 1. however, the serum amylase was below the upper limit of normal serum level and therefore, the difference was not significant in clinical practice. receiver operating charecteristic curve analysis showed that ph level was a reliable predictor of popf (area under the curve: 0.713; 95% ci: 0.573-0.853). conclusions a low ph level at the end of pancreaticoduodenectomy was a risk factor of popf. nlr, plr, and rpr had no predictive value of popf after pancreaticoduodenectomy.
rewritten: background: early detection of postoperative pancreatic fistula (popf) is crucial for implementing proper interventions. both preoperative and intraoperative, as well as early postoperative biochemical markers, have been shown to predict the occurrence of popf. this study aimed to evaluate several simple biochemical parameters for their ability to predict popf. methods: we retrospectively reviewed patients who underwent pancreaticoduodenectomy at our center between 2006 and 2015. preoperative and early postoperative biochemical markers were assessed. additionally, we analyzed the association between popf and the ph and lactate levels measured at the end of surgery. the neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (nlr), platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (plr), and red cell distribution width-to-platelet ratio (rpr) were calculated for postoperative days (pods) 1 and 3. the diagnosis and grading of popf were based on the guidelines established by the international study group on pancreatic fistula. patients were divided into two groups: group 1, comprising those with no fistula or a grade-a fistula, and group 2, consisting of patients with grade-b or grade-c fistulas. the simple biochemical markers were then compared between these two groups. results: group 2 showed a significantly higher serum amylase level on pod 3 and a significantly lower ph level at the end of surgery compared to group 1. however, because the serum amylase levels remained below the upper normal limit, this difference did not have clinical significance. receiver operating characteristic curve analysis indicated that the ph level was a reliable predictor of popf (area under the curve: 0.713; 95% confidence interval: 0.573–0.853). conclusions: a low ph level at the conclusion of pancreaticoduodenectomy was associated with an increased risk of popf, whereas nlr, plr, and rpr did not predict popf in patients undergoing this procedure.

raw: the utility model provides a rig of restoreing fixed operation of skull is exclusively used in, including fixation clamp and fixed pincers, the fixation clamp is installed on fixed pincers, fixed pincers include two handles, a forceps holder, no. two forceps holders, two handles pass through bolt swing joint, a forceps holder and no. two forceps holders are fixed with the front end of two handles respectively, all are provided with the draw -in groove in a forceps holder and no. two forceps holders, two draw -in grooves correspond the intercommunication mutually, the fixation clamp include a card strip, no. two card strips, base and chucks, the lower extreme of no. two card strips is fixed in a card strip, the bottom at a card strip is fixed to the base, open at the chuck center has the card cylindrical void, and a card strip and no. two card strips embolia in the card cylindrical void, no. two card strips penetrate in the draw -in groove of fixed pincers. the utility model has the advantages of reasonable design, the clamp is tightly effectual, and convenient operation can not influence the x -ray transmission, guarantees the operation and goes on smoothly.
rewritten: the utility model introduces a device specifically designed for performing skull fixation operations, which comprises a fixation clamp and fixed pincers. the fixation clamp is mounted on the fixed pincers. these fixed pincers consist of two handles and three forceps holders—one primary and two additional ones. the two handles are connected by a bolt swing joint, with one forceps holder attached to the front end of one handle and one of the additional forceps holders fixed to the front end of the other handle. each forceps holder is equipped with a draw-in groove, and these grooves are aligned so that they interconnect. the fixation clamp itself includes one main card strip, two additional card strips, a base, and chucks. the lower end of one of the additional card strips is secured within the main card strip, and the bottom of this card strip is attached to the base. at the center of the chuck, there is an open cylindrical void, into which the main card strip and the two additional card strips are inserted. moreover, the two additional card strips extend into the draw-in grooves of the fixed pincers. this design offers several advantages: it features a well-conceived structure that ensures a tight and effective clamp, enables convenient operation without interfering with x-ray transmission, and thereby guarantees a smooth and reliable operation.

raw: we describe and implement a symbolic algebra for scalar and vector-valued finite elements, enabling the computer generation of elements with tensor product structure on quadrilateral, hexahedral, and triangular prismatic cells. the algebra is implemented as an extension to the domain-specific language ufl, the unified form language. this allows users to construct many finite element spaces beyond those supported by existing software packages. we have made corresponding extensions to fiat, the finite element automatic tabulator, to enable numerical tabulation of such spaces. this tabulation is consequently used during the automatic generation of low-level code that carries out local assembly operations, within the wider context of solving finite element problems posed over such function spaces. we have done this work within the code-generation pipeline of the software package firedrake; we make use of the full firedrake package to present numerical examples.
rewritten: we present and implement a symbolic algebra for both scalar and vector-valued finite elements, which facilitates the computer-driven generation of elements with tensor product structures on quadrilateral, hexahedral, and triangular prismatic cells. this algebra is incorporated as an extension to the domain-specific language ufl (unified form language), enabling users to construct a wide range of finite element spaces beyond the capabilities of existing software. in parallel, we have extended fiat, the finite element automatic tabulator, to support the numerical tabulation of these spaces. this enhanced tabulation process is then used to automatically generate low-level code to perform local assembly operations, all within the broader framework of solving finite element problems defined over such function spaces. our work is fully integrated into the code-generation pipeline of the firedrake software package, and we present numerical examples using the complete firedrake system.

raw: background significant side effects, including weight gain, hyperglycemia, hyperlipidemia and tardive dyskinesia may occur with the use of antipsychotic medications, particularly with long term use in pediatric patients. an antipsychotic medication safety project was developed to monitor these side effects to improve the safety of antipsychotic treatment. objectives the aim was to increase compliance with the antipsychotic medication monitoring protocol in patients seen during outpatient psychiatry visits for ongoing care from 48% in 1st quarter 2012 to 85% by 4th quarter 2013 and sustain for 2 years. methods a multidisciplinary team established a side effect monitoring protocol for all pediatric patients receiving antipsychotic medications for more than 30 days. the monitoring protocol consisted of 8 elements such as lipid profiles, bmi and documentation of vitals in emr. the qi team developed key drivers and implemented interventions, such as best practice alerts in emr. adherence to the protocol was measured by sampling medical records and auditing compliance to all 8 elements. detailed chart audit results were provided to the providers quarterly to improve compliance. results by 4th quarter 2013, protocol compliance increased from 48% to 85% and with quarterly feedback and support, the outpatient psychiatry team was able to sustain 85% compliance for the past two years. conclusions we have achieved a reliable system for monitoring the use of antipsychotic medication, which has since been spread to other disciplines such as autism and developmental disabilities resulting in the majority of patients being monitored for antipsychotic medication safety and has also resulted in patient care standardization.
rewritten: background: antipsychotic medications, especially when used long-term in pediatric patients, can lead to notable side effects such as weight gain, hyperglycemia, hyperlipidemia, and tardive dyskinesia. to enhance patient safety, a safety project was initiated to monitor these adverse effects. objectives: the project aimed to boost adherence to the antipsychotic medication monitoring protocol for outpatient psychiatry patients receiving ongoing care—from 48% in the first quarter of 2012 to 85% by the fourth quarter of 2013—and to maintain that level of compliance for two years. methods: a multidisciplinary team developed a side effect monitoring protocol for all pediatric patients on antipsychotic medications for more than 30 days. this protocol included eight components such as lipid profiles, bmi assessments, and vital sign documentation in the electronic medical record (emr). the quality improvement team identified key drivers and implemented measures like best practice alerts within the emr. compliance was evaluated by sampling medical records and auditing adherence to all eight elements. detailed quarterly chart audit results were provided to healthcare providers to facilitate improved compliance. results: by the fourth quarter of 2013, adherence to the protocol increased from 48% to 85%. with consistent quarterly feedback and support, the outpatient psychiatry team successfully maintained an 85% compliance rate for the following two years. conclusions: a reliable system for monitoring antipsychotic medication use has been established and successfully expanded to other specialties such as autism and developmental disabilities. this initiative has led to a majority of patients being routinely monitored for antipsychotic medication safety, thereby standardizing patient care.

raw: objective the objective of this analysis was to compare patient-reported outcomes and health-related quality of life (hrqol) in a pivotal phase iii trial of sunitinib versus placebo in patients with progressive, well-differentiated pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (nct00428597).
rewritten: the aim of this analysis was to compare patient-reported outcomes and health-related quality of life (hrqol) in a pivotal phase iii trial that evaluated sunitinib versus placebo in patients with progressive, well-differentiated pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (nct00428597).

raw: the invention discloses a laser path tracker. the laser path tracker comprises a hollow guide handle, the tail end of the guide handle is in a disc structure, and the center of the tail end is provided with a glass micropore convenient for laser to pass through. a mirror forming a 45-degree bevel with the center line of the guide handle is arranged in the head end of the guide handle, a reflection surface of the mirror faces the tail end, and only the center of the mirror is provided with reflecting surface. the side wall, opposite to the mirror, of the guide handle is provided with a transparent ground glass window, when laser is emitted through the glass micropore in the tail end of the guide handle, the laser is reflected by the mirror and then emitted out through the transparent ground glass window, constant emission of the laser through the transparent ground glass window is guaranteed by position adjustment of the guide handle, accordingly uniformity of a laser path and a center line of the guide handle is guaranteed, and laser path tracking is realized. the invention further provides application of the laser path tracker to puncture or screw setting navigation. the laser path tracker has the advantages of simple structure, low cost, convenience in manufacturing and quickness and accuracy in positioning.
rewritten: the invention discloses a laser path tracker that comprises a hollow guide handle. at the tail end of the guide handle, there is a disc structure with a centrally located glass micropore to allow the laser to pass through. at the head end, a mirror is placed so that it forms a 45-degree bevel relative to the guide handle's center line; the mirror’s reflective surface, which is only present at its center, faces the tail end. on the opposite side of the guide handle from the mirror, there is a transparent frosted glass window. when a laser is emitted through the glass micropore at the tail end, it is reflected by the mirror and then exits via the transparent frosted glass window. adjusting the guide handle’s position ensures that the laser is continuously emitted through the window, thus aligning the laser path uniformly with the guide handle's center line and enabling accurate laser path tracking. furthermore, the invention includes the application of this laser path tracker for navigation in puncture procedures or screw placements. the device offers benefits such as a simple design, low cost, ease of manufacturing, and both rapid and precise positioning.

raw: cell walls are likely to be essential determinants of the amazing strength and flexibility of the guard cells that surround each stomatal pore in plants, but surprisingly little is known about cell wall composition, organization, and dynamics in guard cells. recent analyses of cell wall organization and stomatal function in the guard cells of arabidopsis thaliana mutants with defects in cellulose and xyloglucan have allowed for the development of new hypotheses about the relative contributions of these components to guard cell function. advanced image analysis methods can allow for the automated detection of key structures, such as microtubules (mts) and cellulose synthesis complexes (cscs), in guard cells, to help determine their contributions to stomatal function. a major challenge in the mechanical modeling of dynamic biological structures, such as guard cell walls, is to connect nanoscale features (e.g., wall polymers and their molecular interactions) with cell-scale mechanics; this challenge can be addressed by applying multiscale computational modeling that spans multiple spatial scales and physical attributes for cell walls.
rewritten: cell walls likely play a crucial role in the remarkable strength and flexibility of the guard cells that envelop each stomatal pore in plants. however, little is known about the composition, organization, and dynamics of these walls in guard cells. recent studies examining cell wall structure and stomatal function in arabidopsis thaliana mutants—with specific defects in cellulose and xyloglucan—have led to new hypotheses regarding the relative roles these components play in guard cell function. moreover, advanced image analysis techniques now enable the automated identification of important structures, such as microtubules (mts) and cellulose synthesis complexes (cscs), within guard cells to further delineate their contributions to stomatal performance. a significant challenge in the mechanical modeling of dynamic biological structures, like guard cell walls, is linking nanoscale features (for instance, wall polymers and their molecular interactions) to cell-scale mechanics. this challenge can be met by employing multiscale computational modeling that spans various spatial scales and incorporates the diverse physical properties of cell walls.

raw: we study the evolution of opinions (or beliefs) over a social network modeled as a signed graph. the sign attached to an edge in this graph characterizes whether the corresponding individuals or end nodes are friends (positive links) or enemies (negative links). pairs of nodes are randomly selected to interact over time, and when two nodes interact, each of them updates its opinion based on the opinion of the other node and the sign of the corresponding link. this model generalizes the degroot model to account for negative links: when two adversaries interact, their opinions go in opposite directions. we provide conditions for convergence and divergence in expectation, in mean-square, and in almost sure sense and exhibit phase transition phenomena for these notions of convergence depending on the parameters of the opinion update model and on the structure of the underlying graph. we establish a no-survivor theorem, stating that the difference in opinions of any two nodes diverges whenever opinions in the network diverge as a whole. we also prove a live-or-die lemma, indicating that almost surely, the opinions either converge to an agreement or diverge. finally, we extend our analysis to cases where opinions have hard lower and upper limits. in these cases, we study when and how opinions may become asymptotically clustered to the belief boundaries and highlight the crucial influence of (strong or weak) structural balance of the underlying network on this clustering phenomenon.
rewritten: we investigate how opinions (or beliefs) develop within a social network represented by a signed graph. in this representation, the sign on an edge indicates whether the connected individuals are friends (positive links) or foes (negative links). over time, pairs of nodes are randomly chosen to interact, and during each interaction, each node updates its opinion by considering both the other’s opinion and the sign of the linking edge. this framework extends the degroot model to include negative interactions: when two adversaries engage, their opinions shift in opposite directions. we outline the conditions under which convergence and divergence occur—in expectation, in the mean-square sense, and almost surely—and we reveal phase transition phenomena in these convergence behaviors that depend on both the opinion update parameters and the network’s structure. a notable result is our “no-survivor theorem,” which asserts that if the overall opinions in the network diverge, then the opinion difference between any two nodes also diverges. additionally, our “live-or-die lemma” shows that, with probability one, the opinions will either converge to a consensus or diverge. lastly, we broaden our analysis to scenarios where opinions are confined by hard lower and upper bounds. in these bounded situations, we explore the conditions and mechanisms by which opinions may eventually cluster at these boundary values, drawing attention to the significant impact of the network's structural balance—be it strong or weak—on this clustering behavior.

raw: abstract light harvesting strategy using plasmonic metal nanostructures as subwavelength light concentrators provides a highly attractive solution to enhancing the performance of dye-sensitized solar cells (dsscs). through comprehensive optical spectroscopy and electrical characterizations together with a theoretical analysis, we demonstrate a strong competition between the surface energy transfer induced non-radiative quenching and the plasmonic electromagnetic enhancement effect in metal-dielectric-semiconductor core-shell-shell nanoparticle doped dsscs, a generic yet unavoidable phenomenon in all types of plasmonic solar cells. the competition of the two effects results in a non-monotonic relationship between the device efficiency and the thickness of the dielectric shell covering the metal nanoparticles, and leads to an optimal thickness for the highest power conversion efficiency. this observation is further corroborated by photoluminescence spectroscopic measurements. our experimental results are in good agreement with the persson model that predicts a strong energy quenching effect when the distance between the photogenerated charge carrier and the metal core is short enough. both experiment and theory show that the localized surface plasmon resonance enhanced light harvesting efficiency is suppressed by the surface energy transfer to the metal cores for the dielectric shell thickness shorter than a characteristic value (~7 nm in our study). our work sheds new insights into the fundamental understanding of the photophysics mechanisms of plasmonic dsscs and could push forward the study of plasmonic solar cells in terms of device design and fabrication.
rewritten: an innovative light harvesting approach employing plasmonic metal nanostructures as subwavelength light concentrators offers a promising pathway to improving dye-sensitized solar cell (dssc) performance. through detailed optical spectroscopy, electrical characterization, and theoretical analysis, we reveal a significant competition between the non-radiative quenching caused by surface energy transfer and the plasmonic electromagnetic enhancement effect in metal-dielectric-semiconductor core-shell-shell nanoparticle-doped dsscs—a common and unavoidable occurrence in all plasmonic solar cells. this interplay produces a non-linear relationship between device efficiency and the thickness of the dielectric shell surrounding the metal nanoparticles, ultimately leading to an optimal shell thickness for maximum power conversion efficiency. photoluminescence spectroscopy further supports this finding. our experimental observations align well with the persson model, which predicts a pronounced energy quenching effect when the distance between photogenerated charge carriers and the metal core becomes sufficiently small. both experimental data and theoretical analysis confirm that, for dielectric shell thicknesses below a critical value (approximately 7 nm in our study), the enhanced light harvesting efficiency provided by localized surface plasmon resonance is diminished by surface energy transfer to the metal cores. this work provides new insights into the fundamental photophysical mechanisms in plasmonic dsscs and could advance the design and fabrication of plasmonic solar cells.

raw: the utility model discloses a limit device is visited to levelling machine aims at providing the limit device is visited to levelling machine that a convenience changed the pulley, and its technical scheme main points are: a limit device is visited to levelling machine, includes guide margin guide rail and guide margin platform, be equipped with the bearing block that is used for bearing the guide margin guide rail between guide margin guide rail and the guide margin platform, be equipped with on the bearing block and supply the bearing block at the gliding pulley of guide margin bench, the guide margin bench is provided with and is used for driving the gliding driving motor of guide margin guide rail, the coaxial coupling has the lead screw in the driving motor pivot, threaded connection has the movable block on the lead screw, be provided with the articulated elements that is used for roling out the bearing block level guide margin platform between movable block and the bearing block, roll out the bearing block through the articulated elements, lost convenient change and the maintenance to the pulley of blockking of guide margin guide rail.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a limit device for a leveling machine that facilitates easy replacement of the pulley. the technical solution comprises: • a limit device for a leveling machine that includes a guide rail and a corresponding platform along the edge. • a bearing block mounted between the guide rail and the platform to support the guide rail. this bearing block is also provided with a gliding pulley. • a guide bench that not only supplies the gliding pulley but also houses a driving motor responsible for moving the guide rail. the driving motor’s pivot is coaxially connected to a lead screw via a coupling. • a movable block that is threadedly engaged on the lead screw, along with articulated elements that connect the movable block and the bearing block on the platform. these articulated components allow the bearing block to roll out, thereby simplifying the replacement and maintenance of the pulley on the guide rail.

raw: the invention relates to a detection kit of ischemia modified albumins. the kit concretely comprises cobalt ions and dithiothreitol. a stabilizer is added to the kit, so the storage stability of the kit is increased; and a solubilizer is also added, so pollution of reagents to a colorimetric device is removed.
rewritten: this invention pertains to a detection kit for ischemia-modified albumins. the kit includes cobalt ions and dithiothreitol as its key components. additionally, a stabilizer is incorporated to enhance its storage stability, and a solubilizer is added to prevent reagent contamination of a colorimetric device.

raw: the purpose of this study is for analyzing the financial performance use the analysis du pont system in telecommunication company listed on the indonesia stock exchange. du pont systemanalysis is an analysis provides information on the factors that led to the rise and fall the company financial performance shows the relationship between net profit margin, total assets turnover and return on investment to determine the return on equity of the company. this research uses descriptive research. the data used is secondary data. data collection methods used in this research is the method of documentation or literature study. the analysis technique used is descriptive qualitative analysis. based on the results of this study concluded that financial performance of the telecommunication company listed on the indonesia stock exchange 2012-2014 period is that the financial performance is generally not good because as the value of return on investment and return on equity is negative and even below the industry standard, except pt. telekomunikasi indonesia, tbk financial performance is quite good for the value of return on investment and return on equity positive, although below the industry standard. therefore, the company must improve the performance of the company, in order to achieve the company's financial performance above industry standards.
rewritten: this study aims to analyze the financial performance of telecommunication companies listed on the indonesia stock exchange using the dupont analysis. dupont analysis examines the factors contributing to changes in a company's financial performance by exploring the relationship between net profit margin, total asset turnover, and return on investment to determine the company’s return on equity. this research is descriptive in nature and relies on secondary data gathered through documentation or literature reviews. the analysis technique employed is descriptive qualitative analysis. the results for the 2012-2014 period indicate that the overall financial performance of these telecommunication companies was generally poor, as reflected by negative return on investment and return on equity figures that fell below industry standards—with the exception of pt telekomunikasi indonesia tbk, whose performance was comparatively better, showing positive return on investment and return on equity figures, though still under the industry benchmark. consequently, it is essential for companies to improve their performance to exceed industry standards.

raw: no abstract supplied.presented at: standards australia; 2007 mar 6; place unknown. 17 leaves.handle 1959.1/51210pid monash:7615
rewritten: no abstract is available. this work was presented at standards australia on march 6, 2007, at an unspecified location. it consists of 17 leaves, and its handle is 1959.1/51210 with pid monash:7615.

raw: a safety switching circuit adapted for a light tube having two side portions is installed to one side portion of the light tube. the safety switching circuit has an input/output terminal located at a power supply path. the safety switching circuit comprises a switch unit, a full-bridge converter unit, a trigger circuit and a start capacitor. the switch unit has a first switch terminal, a second switch terminal and a trigger terminal. the full-bridge converter unit has a first power terminal, a second power terminal, a first output terminal and a second output terminal. the first output terminal and the second output terminal are respectively coupled to the second switch terminal and the first switch terminal. the first power terminal is coupled to the input/output terminal. the trigger circuit is coupled to the side portion installed with the safety switching circuit, and coupled to the switch unit. the switch unit determines whether to be conducted according to the trigger voltage outputted from the trigger circuit to the trigger terminal. the start capacitor has a first capacitor terminal and a second capacitor terminal respectively coupled to the second power terminal and the trigger circuit. the trigger circuit determines the trigger voltage according to the voltage level of the start capacitor.
rewritten: a safety switching circuit designed for a light tube with two sides is mounted on one side of the light tube. this safety switching circuit features an input/output terminal connected along a power supply path. it consists of a switch unit, a full-bridge converter unit, a trigger circuit, and a start capacitor. the switch unit includes a first switch terminal, a second switch terminal, and a trigger terminal. the full-bridge converter unit comes with a first power terminal, a second power terminal, a first output terminal, and a second output terminal, where the first output terminal is connected to the second switch terminal and the second output terminal to the first switch terminal. the first power terminal is linked to the input/output terminal. additionally, the trigger circuit—mounted on the same side as the safety switching circuit—is connected to the switch unit. based on the trigger voltage provided by the trigger circuit at the trigger terminal, the switch unit decides when to conduct. furthermore, the start capacitor has two terminals: its first capacitor terminal is connected to the second power terminal, and its second capacitor terminal is connected to the trigger circuit, enabling the trigger circuit to set the trigger voltage according to the capacitor's voltage level.

raw: the utility model discloses a novel shoe -cleaning machine, its structure includes casing, touch function display screen, brush, footboard and oil box, the footboard is being connected to the casing, switch is being connected to the casing, the footboard is being connected the brush with the oil box, the ash box is equipped with in the footboard below, inside anterior brush group, lateral part brush group and the rear portion brush of being equipped with of casing, brush switching mechanism to anterior brush group link, belt brush band pulley to lateral part brush group link. the utility model discloses the product is fast, and it is powerful to clean clean effect to more the environmental protection power consumption is lower.
rewritten: this utility model introduces an innovative shoe-cleaning machine featuring a casing, a touch-controlled display screen, brushes, a footboard, and an oil box. the design connects the footboard and switch to the casing, while the oil box is integrated with the brush mechanism attached to the footboard. additionally, a dust or ash box is installed beneath the footboard. inside the casing, there are three sets of brushes—located at the front, the sides, and the rear. the front brush group is linked via a brush switching mechanism, and the lateral brush group is connected using a belt, brush band, and pulley system. overall, the product is designed to operate quickly and efficiently, providing a powerful cleaning effect with lower power consumption and an environmentally friendly approach.

raw: this paper studies the effect of climate change on wheat production in kansas using annual time series data from 1949 to 2014. for the study, an error correction model is developed in which the price of wheat, the price of oats (substitute good), average annual temperature and average annual precipitation are used as explanatory variables with total output of wheat being the dependent variable. time series properties of the data series are diagnosed using unit root and cointegration tests. the estimated results suggest that kansas farmers are supply responsive to both wheat as well as its substitute (oat) prices in the short run as well as in the long run. climate variables; temperature has a positive effect on wheat output in the short run but an insignificant effect in the long run. precipitation has a positive effect in the short run but a negative effect in the long run.
rewritten: this paper examines how climate change impacts wheat production in kansas by analyzing annual time series data spanning from 1949 to 2014. an error correction model is constructed for the analysis, where the dependent variable is the total wheat output and the independent variables include wheat price, oat price (representing a substitute good), average annual temperature, and average annual precipitation. the study employs unit root and cointegration tests to explore the time series properties of the data. findings indicate that kansas farmers adjust their wheat supply in response to both wheat and oat prices in the short term as well as over the long haul. additionally, climate factors reveal that temperature positively influences wheat production in the short run, although its long-run effect is insignificant, while precipitation boosts production in the short term but exerts a negative impact in the long run.

raw: the invention provides a process method of recycling propylene glycol from epoxy propane wastewater, wherein the method includes the steps of: regulating the ph of the wastewater to 5-8 and performing normal pressure distillation to obtain a fraction and bottom residual liquid; performing suction filtration salt removal to the bottom residual liquid to obtain a filtrate and salt; and performing reduced pressure distillation to the filtrate and collecting the fraction at the gas phase temperature of 100-120 deg c to prepare the propylene glycol. the method can remove toxic pollutants in the wastewater and recycle the propylene glycol, wherein recycled fraction can be subjected to biochemical treatment with cod removal rate being higher than 70%, thereby ensuring stable and qualified emission of the wastewater.
