rewritten: the invention discloses a method for recycling propylene glycol from epoxy propane wastewater. the process comprises the following steps: first, adjust the ph of the wastewater to 5-8 and perform atmospheric-pressure distillation to separate a fraction and a bottom residual liquid; next, subject the bottom residual liquid to suction filtration for salt removal to produce a filtrate and separated salt; finally, perform reduced-pressure distillation on the filtrate and collect the fraction at a gas-phase temperature of 100-120°c to obtain propylene glycol. this method not only removes toxic pollutants from the wastewater but also recovers the propylene glycol. additionally, the recovered fraction can undergo biochemical treatment with a cod removal rate exceeding 70%, ensuring that the discharged wastewater remains stable and meets emission standards.

raw: although promising results have been achieved in the areas of traffic-sign detection and classification, few works have provided simultaneous solutions to these two tasks for realistic real world images. we make two contributions to this problem. firstly, we have created a large traffic-sign benchmark from 100000 tencent street view panoramas, going beyond previous benchmarks. it provides 100000 images containing 30000 traffic-sign instances. these images cover large variations in illuminance and weather conditions. each traffic-sign in the benchmark is annotated with a class label, its bounding box and pixel mask. we call this benchmark tsinghua-tencent 100k. secondly, we demonstrate how a robust end-to-end convolutional neural network (cnn) can simultaneously detect and classify trafficsigns. most previous cnn image processing solutions target objects that occupy a large proportion of an image, and such networks do not work well for target objects occupying only a small fraction of an image like the traffic-signs here. experimental results show the robustness of our network and its superiority to alternatives. the benchmark, source code and the cnn model introduced in this paper is publicly available1.
rewritten: although encouraging results have been achieved in traffic-sign detection and classification individually, very few studies have tackled both tasks concurrently using realistic, real-world images. we address this gap with two main contributions. first, we have developed an extensive traffic-sign benchmark derived from 100,000 tencent street view panoramas, surpassing previous datasets. this benchmark comprises 100,000 images that include 30,000 traffic-sign instances, capturing a broad range of lighting and weather conditions. each traffic sign is annotated with a class label, its bounding box, and a pixel mask, and the dataset is named tsinghua-tencent 100k. second, we showcase how a robust, end-to-end convolutional neural network (cnn) can be employed to both detect and classify traffic signs simultaneously. unlike most previous cnn solutions—which are designed for objects occupying a large portion of an image and struggle with smaller targets like traffic signs—our approach effectively handles these challenges. experimental results demonstrate the robustness of our network and its superiority over alternative methods. furthermore, the benchmark, source code, and cnn model presented in this paper are publicly available.

raw: a thorough understanding of properties of neutron stars requires both a reliable knowledge of the equation of state (eos) of super-dense nuclear matter and the strong-field gravity theories simultaneously. to provide information that may help break this eos-gravity degeneracy, we investigate effects of nuclear symmetry energy on the gravitational binding energy of neutron stars within gr and the scalar-tensor subset of alternative gravity models. we focus on effects of the slope l of nuclear symmetry energy at saturation density and the high-density behavior of nuclear symmetry energy. we find that the variation of either the density slope l or the high-density behavior of nuclear symmetry energy leads to large changes in the binding energy of neutron stars. the difference in predictions using the gr and the scalar-tensor theory appears only for massive neutron stars, and even then is significantly smaller than the difference resulting from variations in the symmetry energy.
rewritten: a complete understanding of neutron star properties demands both an accurate grasp of the equation of state (eos) for super-dense nuclear matter and strong-field gravity theories. to help disentangle the eos–gravity interplay, we explore how nuclear symmetry energy influences the gravitational binding energy of neutron stars, examining the case under both general relativity and a scalar-tensor model from alternative gravity theories. our focus is on the impact of the slope (l) of nuclear symmetry energy at saturation density, as well as its behavior at high densities. we observe that any changes in either the density slope l or in the high-density characteristics of nuclear symmetry energy lead to considerable variations in the binding energy of neutron stars. notably, discrepancies between predictions based on general relativity and those from the scalar-tensor approach emerge only for highly massive neutron stars—and even in such cases, they are considerably smaller than those induced by shifts in the symmetry energy.

raw: the invention belongs to the technical field of life services and specifically provides a collar capable of recording pet motion tracks. the collar can detect the pet motion tracks by a displacement sensor, an acceleration sensor and a gyro and draw information by upper computer software into plane figures, so that places at which a pet moves frequently and vividly in one day can be observed clearly, and direct observation of a user can be replaced. the collar is composed of the displacement sensor, a processor, a bluetooth transmitting module, a bluetooth receiving module, the gyro, a battery, the acceleration sensor, a collar body, a mobile phone and the upper computer software. the collar solves the problem that people cannot accurately determine the pet motion tracks and motion places loved by the pets in one day in day life. the collar is characterized by high accuracy and high timeliness.
rewritten: this invention falls within the field of life services and specifically introduces a pet collar that records movement tracks. it uses a displacement sensor, an acceleration sensor, and a gyro to capture the pet’s motions, then utilizes computer software to represent this information as graphical figures. this allows users to clearly identify areas where a pet is especially active throughout the day, effectively replacing the need for direct observation. the collar's components include a displacement sensor, processor, bluetooth transmitting module, bluetooth receiving module, gyro, battery, acceleration sensor, the collar housing, a mobile phone, and upper computer software. it addresses the challenge of accurately determining a pet’s daily movement patterns and frequently visited spots, and is noted for its high accuracy and real-time performance.

raw: the invention discloses a concentrated discharging type precision rice direct-seeding device. the device is characterized by comprising a distributor, a seed box, a slanting fluted-wheel type seed discharging device, a fan, an air capacity control valve, a seed guiding device, a conveying pipe, a seed discharging pipe and a pressure measuring device, wherein the bottom of the distributor is connected with the conveying pipe and the seed discharging pipe, and the pressure measuring device is arranged on the distributor; the seed guiding device is connected with the conveying pipe, the slanting fluted-wheel type seed discharging device and the seed box are connected above the seed guiding device, an air inlet of the air capacity control valve is connected with the fan, an air outlet is connected with the conveying pipe, and the fan is driven under external power; the slanting fluted-wheel type seed discharging device right under the seed box supplies stable seed flow, the fan provides constant power air flow, the distributor uniformly distributes mixed flow of seeds and air to the seed discharging pipe, and the seeds are sown in seed ditches. compared with the prior art, the device has the advantages that uniform and continuous precision sowing of rice can be realized, and back flowing of the seeds due to partial pressure of air can be prevented; the concentrated discharging type precision rice direct-seeding device can have the walking speeds higher than that of a conventional mechanical sowing machine and has higher sowing efficiency.
rewritten: the invention relates to a concentrated discharging type precision rice direct-seeding device. this device includes a distributor, a seed box, a slanting fluted-wheel seed discharging device, a fan, an air capacity control valve, a seed guiding device, a conveying pipe, a seed discharging pipe, and a pressure measuring device. specifically, the bottom of the distributor connects to both the conveying pipe and the seed discharging pipe, with the pressure measuring device mounted on the distributor. the seed guiding device links to the conveying pipe, while the seed box is positioned above it and connected to the slanting fluted-wheel seed discharging device. the air inlet of the air capacity control valve is connected to the fan, its air outlet connects to the conveying pipe, and the fan operates under external power. in operation, the slanting fluted-wheel seed discharging device, located directly beneath the seed box, ensures a stable seed flow. the fan supplies a constant air stream, and the distributor evenly disperses the mixture of seeds and air through the seed discharging pipe, allowing the seeds to be deposited into seed ditches. compared to prior art, this device offers the benefits of achieving uniform and continuous precision rice sowing while preventing seed backflow due to partial air pressure. additionally, the concentrated discharging type precision rice direct-seeding device can operate at higher walking speeds than conventional mechanical sowing machines, resulting in improved sowing efficiency.

raw: we test the distance--duality relation $\eta \equiv d_l / [ (1 + z)^2 d_a ] = 1$ between cosmological luminosity distance ($d_l$) from the jla sne ia compilation (arxiv:1401.4064) and angular-diameter distance ($d_a$) based on baryon oscillation spectroscopic survey (boss; arxiv:1607.03155) and wigglez baryon acoustic oscillation measurements (arxiv:1105.2862, arxiv:1204.3674). the $d_l$ measurements are matched to $d_a$ redshift by a statistically consistent compression procedure. with monte carlo methods, nontrivial and correlated distributions of $\eta$ can be explored in a straightforward manner without resorting to a particular evolution template $\eta(z)$. assuming independent constraints on cosmological parameters that are necessary to obtain $d_l$ and $d_a$ values, we find 9% constraints consistent with $\eta = 1$ from the analysis of snia + boss and an 18% bound results from snia + wigglez. these results are contrary to previous claims that $\eta < 1$ has been found close to or above the $1 \sigma$ level. we discuss the effect of different cosmological parameter inputs and the use of the apparent deviation from distance--duality as a proxy of systematic effects on cosmic distance measurements. the results suggest possible systematic overestimation of snia luminosity distances compared with $d_a$ data when a planck {\lambda}cdm cosmological parameter inference (arxiv:1502.01589) is used to enhance the precision. if interpreted as an extinction correction due to a gray dust component, the effect is broadly consistent with independent observational constraints.
rewritten: we examine the distance–duality relation, defined as η ≡ dₗ⁄[(1 + z)² dₐ] = 1, by comparing the cosmological luminosity distance (dₗ) from the jla sne ia compilation (arxiv:1401.4064) with the angular-diameter distance (dₐ) obtained from the baryon oscillation spectroscopic survey (boss; arxiv:1607.03155) and wigglez baryon acoustic oscillation measurements (arxiv:1105.2862, arxiv:1204.3674). the dₗ measurements were statistically compressed to match the redshifts of the dₐ data. utilizing monte carlo techniques, we explore complex, correlated distributions of η without assuming any specific evolution model for η(z). under independent constraints on the cosmological parameters required to derive dₗ and dₐ, our analysis of sne ia plus boss data yields a 9% constraint consistent with η = 1, while using sne ia plus wigglez data produces an 18% bound. these findings stand in contrast to earlier reports suggesting that η < 1 is detected at roughly the 1σ level or higher. we also discuss how various inputs for the cosmological parameters, as well as the apparent departures from distance–duality, can serve as proxies for systematic issues in cosmic distance measurements. notably, our results hint at a possible systematic overestimation of sne ia luminosity distances relative to the dₐ measures when applying a planck λcdm cosmological parameter inference (arxiv:1502.01589) to improve precision. if this discrepancy is interpreted as an extinction correction due to a gray dust component, it is broadly in line with independent observational constraints.

raw: to investigate the reflective crack-propagation behavior and provide control techniques for asphalt pavements widening, the finite-element models were built to simulate the widened asphalt pavement with a latent joint. the developed finite-element model considered the influence factors, including the loading modes (i.e., the symmetrical and unsymmetrical loading modes), the thickness and modulus of the asphalt concrete surface, the modulus of the new and existing stabilized bases, and the sheet stiffness of the reinforced geosynthetic. the finite-element simulation results show that increasing the thickness or reducing the modulus of the asphalt concrete surface effectively delays the propagation speed of the reflective cracks. the cracking potential reaches the minimum when the new and existing bases have a uniform modulus. additionally, geosynthetic reinforcement across the joints significantly reduces the stress concentration around the crack tip and slows down the propagation of the reflective cracks. these benefits become greater when increasing the sheet stiffness of the geosynthetic. finally, an experimental study was conducted to investigate the influence of the types of the asphalt overlay and the geosynthetic reinforcement on the reflective crack-propagation behavior. the experimental results indicate that the geosynthetic-reinforced structure with a lower modulus of the asphalt concrete surface significantly increases the fatigue life of widened pavements, and the polypropylene geotextile performs better than the glass-fiber grid in terms of the extension of the fatigue life.
rewritten: to examine the behavior of reflective crack propagation and develop control strategies for widening asphalt pavements, finite-element models were created to simulate a widened pavement with a hidden joint. this model incorporated various factors such as the type of loading (symmetrical versus unsymmetrical), the thickness and modulus of the asphalt concrete surface, the moduli of both the new and existing stabilized bases, and the sheet stiffness of the reinforcing geosynthetic. the simulation results revealed that increasing the asphalt concrete’s thickness or lowering its modulus effectively slowed the rate of reflective crack propagation. furthermore, the risk of cracking was minimized when the new and existing bases shared the same modulus. additionally, using geosynthetic reinforcement across the joints significantly reduced stress concentration near the crack tip and decelerated crack propagation, with improvements becoming more pronounced as the sheet stiffness of the geosynthetic increased. finally, an experimental study was performed to assess the impact of different asphalt overlays and geosynthetic reinforcements on reflective crack behavior. the results indicated that a geosynthetic-reinforced structure paired with an asphalt concrete surface of lower modulus substantially enhanced the fatigue life of widened pavements, with polypropylene geotextile outperforming the glass-fiber grid in extending fatigue life.

raw: abstract introduction female genital mutilation (fgm), the partial or total removal of the external genitalia for non-medical reasons, can affect female sexuality. however, only few studies are available, and these have significant methodologic limitations. aim to understand the impact of fgm on the anatomy of the clitoris and bulbs using magnetic resonance imaging and on sexuality using psychometric instruments and to study whether differences in anatomy after fgm correlate with differences in sexual function, desire, and body image. methods a cross-sectional study on sexual function and sexual anatomy was performed in women with and without fgm. fifteen women with fgm involving cutting of the clitoris and 15 uncut women as a control group matched by age and parity were prospectively recruited. participants underwent pelvic magnetic resonance imaging with vaginal opacification by ultrasound gel and completed validated questionnaires on desire (sexual desire inventory), body image (questionnaire d'image corporelle [body image satisfaction scale]), and sexual function (female sexual function index). main outcome measures primary outcomes were clitoral and bulbar measurements on magnetic resonance images. secondary outcomes were sexual function, desire, and body image scores. results women with fgm did not have significantly decreased clitoral glans width and body length but did have significantly smaller volume of the clitoris plus bulbs. they scored significantly lower on sexual function and desire than women without fgm. they did not score lower on female sexual function index sub-scores for orgasm, desire, and satisfaction and on the questionnaire d'image corporelle but did report significantly more dyspareunia. a larger total volume of clitoris and bulbs did not correlate with higher female sexual function index and sexual desire inventory scores in women with fgm compared with uncut women who had larger total volume that correlated with higher scores. conclusion women with fgm have sexual erectile tissues for sexual arousal, orgasm, and pleasure. women with sexual dysfunction should be appropriately counseled and treated.
rewritten: abstract introduction: female genital mutilation (fgm), defined as the partial or complete removal of the external genitalia for non-medical reasons, can impact female sexuality. however, the number of studies available is limited, and those that exist have notable methodological flaws. the objective was to assess the impact of fgm on the anatomy of the clitoris and bulbs using magnetic resonance imaging (mri) and on sexuality through psychometric tools, as well as to evaluate whether anatomical differences post-fgm are associated with variations in sexual function, desire, and body image. methods: a cross-sectional study was conducted comparing sexual function and anatomy in women with and without fgm. fifteen women who had undergone fgm with clitoral cutting were prospectively recruited along with 15 uncut women serving as an age- and parity-matched control group. all participants underwent pelvic mri with vaginal opacification using ultrasound gel and completed validated questionnaires measuring desire (sexual desire inventory), body image (questionnaire d’image corporelle [body image satisfaction scale]), and sexual function (female sexual function index). main outcome measures: the primary outcomes were the measurements of the clitoris and bulbs obtained from the mri. secondary outcomes included scores for sexual function, desire, and body image. results: women with fgm did not show significantly reduced clitoral glans width and body length; however, they did have a significantly smaller combined volume of the clitoris plus bulbs. additionally, these women scored significantly lower on assessments of sexual function and desire compared to uncut women. their scores were not lower on the female sexual function index subscales for orgasm, desire, and satisfaction, nor on the questionnaire d’image corporelle, although they did report significantly more dyspareunia. moreover, while a larger total volume of the clitoris and bulbs correlated with higher female sexual function index and sexual desire inventory scores in uncut women, this correlation was not observed in women with fgm. conclusion: women with fgm retain sexual erectile tissues that contribute to sexual arousal, orgasm, and pleasure. it is important that women experiencing sexual dysfunction receive proper counseling and treatment.

raw: we present a monte carlo generator that implements significant theoretical improvements in the simulation of top-quark pair production and decay at the lhc. spin correlations and off-shell effects in top-decay chains are described in terms of exact matrix elements for $$pp\rightarrow \ell ^+\nu _{\mathchoice{\displaystyle }{\scriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }\ell }\, l^-\bar{\nu }_{\mathchoice{\displaystyle }{\scriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }l}\,b \,\bar{b} $$ at order $$\alpha ^4 \alpha _{\mathchoice{\displaystyle }{\scriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }{\scriptscriptstyle }\mathrm s}^2$$ plus full nlo qcd corrections, where the leptons $$\ell $$ and l belong to different families, and b quarks are massive. thus, the contributions from $$t\bar{t}$$ and wt single-top production, plus contributions without top resonances and all relevant quantum interferences between different channels are fully included. matrix elements are matched to the $$\mathtt pythia8$$ parton shower using a recently proposed method that allows for a consistent treatment of resonances in the $$\mathtt powheg$$ framework. these theoretical improvements are especially important for the interpretation of precision measurements of the top-quark mass, for single-top analyses in the wt channel, and for $$t\bar{t}$$ and wt backgrounds in the presence of jet vetoes or cuts that enhance off-shell effects. the new generator is based on a process-independent interface of the $$\mathtt openloops$$ amplitude generator with the powheg-box framework.
rewritten: we introduce a monte carlo generator that incorporates substantial theoretical advancements for simulating top-quark pair production and decay at the lhc. in this approach, spin correlations and off-shell effects in top decay chains are modeled using exact matrix elements for the process pp → ℓ⁺ν_ℓ ℓ⁻ν̄_ℓ b b̄ calculated at order α⁴αₛ² and including full nlo qcd corrections. here, the leptons ℓ and l come from different families, and the b quarks are treated as massive. consequently, our simulation fully encompasses the contributions from t-t̄ and wt single-top production, as well as those from diagrams without top resonances and all significant quantum interference effects among different channels. the matrix elements are matched to the pythia8 parton shower utilizing a novel method that ensures a consistent treatment of resonances within the powheg framework. these theoretical enhancements are particularly crucial for precisely interpreting top-quark mass measurements, conducting single-top analyses in the wt channel, and handling t-t̄ and wt backgrounds in scenarios with jet vetoes or cuts that amplify off-shell effects. this new generator is built on a process-independent interface that links the openloops amplitude generator with the powheg-box framework.

raw: movement of the knee joint of a human includes rolling and sliding. there also exist rotations in the frontal and horizontal planes. to assist the standing movement of a human, we developed a bioinspired knee joint and torque adjustment mechanism. we evaluated the motion, torque characteristics, and stress of the developed mechanism. this joint allows deep flexion of the knee with small resistance for both the user and the device. in addition, in spite of 33% error in deep flexion, the measured torque over less than 120 degrees fits the designed torque curve. we conducted evaluation tests for a human subject. the electromyogram emg of musculus rectus femoris was measured during standing with or without the assistance. the result shows 30% and 63% reduction with the assistance from 100-degree and 80-degree knee angles, respectively. in addition, the proposed device reduced up to 80% of stress in the frontal plane during standing.
rewritten: the human knee joint moves by rolling and sliding, with additional rotations occurring in the frontal and horizontal planes. to aid in standing, we created a bioinspired knee joint paired with a torque adjustment mechanism. we evaluated its motion, torque characteristics, and stress levels. this design enables deep knee flexion with minimal resistance for both the user and the mechanism. although there was a 33% error during deep flexion, the torque measured for angles under 120 degrees closely followed the intended torque curve. in tests with a human subject, we measured the electromyogram (emg) of the rectus femoris during standing with and without the device’s assistance. the results revealed reductions of 30% and 63% in muscle activity at knee angles of 100° and 80°, respectively. furthermore, the device decreased stress in the frontal plane by up to 80% during standing.

raw: to assess the effect of adding solid manure fractions on the biomethane potential (bmp) of liquid dairy cow manure and on the biokinetic parameters of the process. the methanogenic potential of liquid dairy cow manure was strongly effected by adding a solid manure fraction. the 90/10 % (w/w) liquid/solid manure fraction mixture was the best substrate for ch4 production. this substrate mixture improved by 50 % the final ch4 production per g substrate and decreased the lag time by 220 % relative to the reference bmp test without the addition. moreover, the addition of 20 % solid manure fraction adversely affected both the final ch4 production and the maximum methane production rate, while increased the lag time by 400 % compared to the reference bmp test without addition. liquid dairy cow manure should be supplemented with no more than 10 % of solid manure fraction in order to improve the biomethane potential of this important agro-industrial residue.
rewritten: this study evaluated how incorporating solid manure fractions impacts both the biomethane potential (bmp) of liquid dairy cow manure and the biokinetic parameters of the digestion process. the addition of solid manure notably affected the methanogenic performance of the liquid manure. in particular, a mixture consisting of 90% liquid and 10% solid manure (w/w) proved to be the most effective substrate for ch4 production—improving the final methane output per gram of substrate by 50% and reducing the lag phase by 220% compared to the bmp test using only liquid manure. conversely, using a mixture with 20% solid manure had a negative impact: it not only lowered the final ch4 production and the maximum methane production rate but also increased the lag time by 400% relative to the reference test. thus, to enhance the biomethane potential of this key agro-industrial residue, liquid dairy cow manure should be supplemented with no more than 10% solid manure fraction.

raw: disclosed in the invention is an information processing method, a client, and a server. the method comprises: a first user operation is obtained; the first user operation is responded and whether the first user operation indicates collection of a plurality of pictures by means of continuous shooting is determined, and a determination result is obtained; if so, a plurality o pictures collected by means of continuous shooting are spliced into one picture according to preset configuration; and if not, when a second user operation meeting a preset condition is detected, the second user operation is responded, a sorting strategy is generated according to the demand or obtained locally and directly or by making a request to a server, and a plurality of target pictures are obtained based on the second user operation and are spliced into one picture according to the sorting strategy and the preset configuration.
rewritten: the invention discloses an information processing method along with a client and a server. the method involves the following steps: first, it receives an initial user operation. the system then responds to this operation by determining whether it indicates the need to capture multiple images through continuous shooting, thereby obtaining a corresponding determination result. if the result is affirmative, the system stitches the multiple continuously captured images into a single image according to a preset configuration. if not, upon detecting a second user operation that meets a preset condition, the system responds to this second operation by generating a sorting strategy based either on the user's demand, local data, or a server request. it then retrieves multiple target images based on the second user operation and proceeds to stitch these images into one, using the sorting strategy in conjunction with the preset configuration.

raw: the invention belongs to the field of hydraulic experimental model facilities and particularly relates to an automatic control type circulating water tank for simulating the instantaneous dam break. the circulating water tank comprises a water tank, a gate, a water level control system and a gate lifting control system. the water level control system comprises a water injection pipe, a water inlet pipe and a water guide pipe which are connected through a three-way pipe fitting, wherein the water guide pipe is connected with a circulating water pump. the water level control system further comprises a gate upstream water level gauge and a gate downstream water level gauge, wherein the gate upstream water level gauge and the gate downstream water level gauge are respectively arranged in a gate upstream water tank and a gate downstream water tank. the gate lifting control system comprises a vertical rack arranged on the front surface of a gate door plank and matched with the driving gear of a motor, and a lifting guide rail fixedly erected on the water tank along a guide rail. during the lifting process of the gate door plank, a pair of vertical guide bars on the back surface of the gate door plank are always fittingly kept in the vertical guide grooves of the lifting guide rail. an automatic air drying device is arranged in the gate downstream water tank, so that the conduction of dry bed experiments is facilitated. the circulating water tank is stable, automatic and efficient.
rewritten: this invention pertains to the field of hydraulic experimental model facilities and specifically relates to an automatic control circulating water tank designed to simulate an instantaneous dam break. the circulating water tank consists of a water reservoir, a gate, a water level control system, and a gate lifting control system. the water level control system includes a water injection pipe, a water inlet pipe, and a water guide pipe, all interconnected via a three-way pipe fitting, with the water guide pipe linked to a circulating water pump. additionally, this system features a gate upstream water level gauge and a gate downstream water level gauge, which are installed in the upstream and downstream water tanks, respectively. the gate lifting control system is comprised of a vertical rack mounted on the front side of the gate door plank that meshes with the motor's driving gear, along with a lifting guide rail securely attached to the water tank. during the lifting process, a pair of vertical guide bars on the rear side of the gate door plank consistently align with the vertical guide grooves of the lifting guide rail. moreover, an automatic air drying device is installed in the downstream water tank to facilitate dry bed experiments. overall, this circulating water tank offers stability, automation, and high efficiency.

raw: the utility model belongs to the technical field of the plant is cultivateed, concretely relates to indoor culture apparatus of tomato, including base 1, support 2, its characterized in that: 1 side of base is provided with data plate 11, still is provided with master control set 3 on the base 1, is provided with delivery pipe 21 and medicine liquid pipe 22 on the support 2, and 21 front ends of delivery pipe are connected with watering device 4, and the medicine liquid pipe 22 front end is connected with and spouts medicine device 5, the utility model discloses a set up the mode of support on the base, through when regarding the support as the pipeline that supplies water and dose, can regard the support as the support of supporting the tomato cane and growing again, realized the tomato and planted in indoor cultivation, for the user provides convenience, plant the indoor scale of tomato and provide favourable help.
rewritten: the utility model falls within the technical field of plant cultivation and specifically pertains to an indoor tomato culture apparatus. this apparatus comprises a base (1) and a support (2), and is characterized by the following features: • one side of the base (1) is equipped with a data plate (11). • a master control unit (3) is also mounted on the base (1). • the support (2) is fitted with both a delivery pipe (21) and a medicinal liquid pipe (22). • the front end of the delivery pipe (21) is connected to a watering device (4). • the front end of the medicinal liquid pipe (22) is connected to a medicine spraying device (5). in this configuration, the support not only functions as the pipeline for supplying water and medicine but also serves as a structural element that supports the tomato plant's cane. this design facilitates indoor tomato cultivation, offering users a convenient method for growing tomatoes on an indoor scale.

raw: an electrical receptacle connector includes a metallic shell, an insulated housing, a plurality of upper-row receptacle terminals, and a plurality of lower-row receptacle terminals. the insulated housing is received in the metallic shell. the upper-row receptacle terminals are held in the insulated housing and include a plurality of tail portions protruded from a bottom of the insulation body to be arranged into a row. the lower-row receptacle terminals are held in the insulated housing and include a plurality of tail portions protruded from the bottom of the insulated housing to be arranged into a first row and a second row. the tail portions of the lower-row receptacle terminals are parallel to the tail portions of the upper-row receptacle terminals.
rewritten: an electrical receptacle connector consists of a metallic shell, an insulated housing, several upper-row receptacle terminals, and several lower-row receptacle terminals. the insulated housing is placed within the metallic shell. the upper-row receptacle terminals are secured in the insulated housing and feature multiple tail portions that extend from the bottom of the insulation body, arranged in a single row. similarly, the lower-row receptacle terminals are held within the insulated housing and possess tail portions that project from the bottom, arranged into two rows—a first row and a second row. these tail portions are oriented parallel to those of the upper-row receptacle terminals.

raw: an antenna apparatus is configured in a manner that stable communication can be performed even when an antenna at the opposite comminucation side is relatively small and two communicating antennas are near in coaxial arrangement so that the maximum communication distance is increased. a coil opening part (cw) of an antenna coil module (3) and a conductor opening (ca) of a conductor layer (2) at least partially overlap. a magnetic flux (mf) to pass through the coil opening (cw) passes through the conductor opening (ca). since the magnetic flux (mf) does not pass through the conductor layer (2), the magnetic flux mf attempts to bypass the conductor layer (2) along a path in which the conductor opening (ca) of the conductor layer (2) is on the inside and the outer edge of the conductor layer (2) is on the outside. as a result, the magnetic flux mf draws relatively large loops that link the inside and the outside of a coil conductor (41) of an antenna (4) on a reader/writer side.
rewritten: an antenna apparatus is designed so that stable communication is achieved even when the antenna on the opposite communication side is relatively small and both antennas are arranged coaxially in close proximity, thereby increasing the maximum communication distance. in this design, a coil opening part (cw) of an antenna coil module (3) partially overlaps with a conductor opening (ca) in a conductor layer (2). the magnetic flux (mf) passing through the coil opening (cw) also travels through the conductor opening (ca). since the magnetic flux (mf) avoids passing through the conductor layer (2), it essentially bypasses the layer along a path where the conductor opening (ca) is positioned on the inside and the outer edge of the conductor layer (2) is on the outside. as a result, the magnetic flux (mf) forms relatively large loops that connect the inside and outside of a coil conductor (41) of an antenna (4) on the reader/writer side.

raw: the invention discloses a high fluidity, anti-seepage, and crack-resistant concrete material, which is prepared from the following raw materials: silicate cement, fly ash, medium sand, small stone, a polycarboxylate water reducer, an air entraining agent, a calcium sulfoaluminate expanding agent, and water. the concrete material has good fluidity, and can be used in a structure without vibration or under a condition of slight vibration, wherein in the structure, the density of steel bars is large, the space is narrow, and the shape of the structure is complicated, and the construction is convenient. the concrete material also has a strong anti-seepage performance, the cement base material and additives work together to effectively cut off the crack connection channels, and the compactness and anti-seepage performance of the overall structure are both enhanced. the cracking resistant performance is strong, during the hydration process, the micro expansion can effectively control the shrinkage of concrete volume, the cracking rate and degree in the early stage are largely reduced; moreover, the concrete material can be well bound with roller compacted concrete, the cement base material can penetrate into the gaps between layers of roller compacted concrete, and the local strength and binding performance of roller compacted concrete are improved therefore.
rewritten: the invention describes a concrete material that is highly fluid, resistant to water penetration, and crack-resistant. it is made from silicate cement, fly ash, medium sand, small stone, a polycarboxylate water reducer, an air entraining agent, a calcium sulfoaluminate expansion agent, and water. this concrete flows well and can be applied in structures without any vibration or only minimal vibration—especially useful when there is a high density of steel reinforcement, narrow spaces, or complex structural shapes—making construction more convenient. additionally, its excellent anti-seepage performance is achieved as the cement and additives work together to block channels where cracks could connect, thereby enhancing both the compactness and overall water resistance of the structure. its crack-resistant ability is also noteworthy; during hydration, the material undergoes micro-expansion that effectively controls concrete shrinkage and significantly reduces early cracking. furthermore, the concrete binds well with roller compacted concrete, as the cement component fills the gaps between layers, which in turn improves the local strength and bonding performance of the roller compacted concrete.

raw: the utility model provides a device of serialization production methyl tetrahydrochysene phthalic anhydride, includes four diene synthesis reation kettle, five pipe bundle condenser, two falling film evaporation wares, a film evaporator, five measuring pumps, three material pump and nine various storage tanks. in inputing first diene cauldron (101) with second measuring pump (502) with the cis -butenedioic anhydride, with five active component in the excessive carbon reaction in the recovery carbon that comes from fourth diene cauldron (104) no. 5, and then get into second diene cauldron (102), third diene cauldron (103) and fourth diene cauldron in proper order, with the carbon struggle against "five evils" of new joining should, obtain methyl tetrahydrochysene phthalic anhydride product through separating again. can realize the make full use of of five resources in the carbon, reduce the formation of oligomer in the building -up process, the operation process control automation degree is high, and the reaction is steady, can improve produce property can, reduction in production cost improves production efficiency.
rewritten: the utility model discloses an apparatus for the continuous production of methyl tetrahydrochrysene phthalic anhydride. the system includes four diene synthesis reaction kettles, five pipe bundle condensers, two falling film evaporators, one film evaporator, five metering pumps, three material pumps, and nine different storage tanks. in operation, cis-butenedioic anhydride is fed into the first diene kettle (101) using the second metering pump (502) and reacts with a five-component active mixture in excess carbon recovered from the fourth diene kettle (104, unit no. 5). the mixture is then sequentially transferred to the second (102), third (103), and fourth diene kettles. during the reaction, the carbon simultaneously reacts against the “five evils” of new joining, and the methyl tetrahydrochrysene phthalic anhydride product is isolated through subsequent separation. this process maximizes the use of carbon resources, reduces the formation of oligomers during the chain-growth process, features a high degree of automation and stable reaction control, and ultimately improves product quality while reducing production costs and enhancing efficiency.

raw: in order to enhance the development efficiency and the grade of standardization, serialization and modularization of the inertial navigation system test software,a general software development platform technique of the test software is researched. the software development platform architecture is designed based on the analysis of the inertial navigation system test requirement. the function of the software development platform framework,the design method of the component and plug-in,the state machine model on the software development platform,and the function extendibility are introduced in detail. based on a case the development model of the test software on the software development platform is described. the configuration development process shows that the general-utility of the software development platform is excellent. the development efficiency and the grade of standardization,serialization and modularization of the inertial navigation system test software based on the software development platform are advanced greatly.
rewritten: to improve development efficiency as well as enhance standardization, serialization, and modularization, a comprehensive software development platform for the inertial navigation system test software was investigated. the platform's architecture was designed after analyzing the requirements of the inertial navigation system tests. detailed explanations are provided covering the platform framework’s functionalities, component and plug-in design methodologies, state machine model, and its extendibility. furthermore, a case study illustrates the development model of the test software using this platform. the configuration development process demonstrates that this software development platform possesses excellent general utility. as a result, the efficiency of development and the level of standardization, serialization, and modularization of the inertial navigation system test software are significantly advanced.

raw: the utility model belongs to the crushing equipment field, concretely relates to cone crusher with dustproof eccentric structure, the deciding the welt and move the welt of installation in casing and casing moves the welt and is circular cone type structure, moves the welt and installs in the casing with the help of the transmission shaft, decides the welt suit and moving the welt outside, decides the dead in line setting of axis yu the transmission shaft of welt, and move the welt and decide the axis eccentric settings of welt, the transmission shaft on be provided with a plurality of flabellums, flabellum around transmission shaft evenly distributed, the air supply direction of flabellum is vertical downwards. can reduce because of the dust pollution that the breaker operation brought, help the environmental protection, guarantee operating personnel's health and safety.
rewritten: this utility model pertains to the field of crushing equipment and specifically relates to a cone crusher with a dustproof eccentric design. the design features a movable welt installed within a circular cone-type casing. a transmission shaft assists in both positioning and moving the welt inside the casing. this shaft also helps determine the proper external fit and eccentric settings of the welt’s axis. in addition, the transmission shaft is equipped with several evenly distributed flabellums that direct air vertically downward. this setup reduces dust generated during crushing operations, thereby enhancing environmental protection and ensuring the health and safety of operating personnel.
