rewritten: for sale: a residential property featuring 1.75 bathrooms, located on main street in bottineau, bottineau, north dakota, united states, 58318, listing id 1177.

raw: as a kind of probabilistic rough set model, decision-theoretic rough set is usually used to deal with binary classification problems. this paper provides a new formulation of multi-class decision-theoretic rough set by combining decision-theoretic rough set model with classical cost-sensitive learning. upper approximation, lower approximation, positive region, negative region and boundary region can be derived from the \(n\,\times \,n\) cost matrix of classical multi-class situation. the probability thresholds for three-way decisions making are defined. a cost-sensitive classification algorithm based on multi-class decision-theoretic rough set model is presented. the experimental results on several uci data sets indicate that the proposed algorithm can get a better performance on classification accuracy and total cost.
rewritten: decision-theoretic rough sets, a type of probabilistic rough set model, are traditionally used in binary classification problems. in this paper, we introduce a novel formulation of multi-class decision-theoretic rough sets by integrating the decision-theoretic rough set model with classical cost-sensitive learning. from an n × n cost matrix representing a multi-class scenario, one can derive the upper and lower approximations, as well as the positive, negative, and boundary regions. we define the probability thresholds needed for three-way decision-making and present a cost-sensitive classification algorithm based on the multi-class decision-theoretic rough set model. experiments on several uci datasets demonstrate that the proposed algorithm improves both classification accuracy and total cost.

raw: by means of enzymoiimmunoassay and flow cytofluorometry the effect of carbon monoxide and ultraviolet (uv)-light on the expression level of the receptor complex molecules (cd95 and cd8 markers) on the membranes of human blood lymphocytes has been studied. it was stated that the carbon monoxide causes decrease of cd95 receptors on the surface immune cells during (60 ^ 90 min). however, strengthening of this effect was registered in 24 hours. it is shown that uv-light in 453 and 755 j/m2 doses provides the pro-apoptotic effect as related to the expression level of cd95 receptors of analyzed cells. after daily thermostating of modified lymphocytes, different receptiveness of cd8 molecules to carbon monoxide was revealed. thus, in patients with initially higher levels of cd8 markers - decrease is registered, and in individuals with initially low - increase in this marker levels. the obtained data of blood lymphocytes receptor profile changes in the presence of low molecular ligand (co) must be taken into account in the ultraviolet blood irradiation-therapy in patients with different etiologies.
rewritten: using enzymoimmunoassay and flow cytofluorometry, researchers investigated the effects of carbon monoxide and ultraviolet (uv) light on the expression levels of receptor complex molecules (cd95 and cd8 markers) on human blood lymphocyte membranes. they found that carbon monoxide reduces the number of cd95 receptors on the surface of immune cells during a 60 to 90-minute period, and this reduction becomes even more pronounced after 24 hours. additionally, uv light at doses of 453 and 755 j/m² produces a pro-apoptotic effect by altering the expression levels of cd95 receptors on the cells. daily thermostating of the modified lymphocytes revealed a varied response of cd8 molecules to carbon monoxide: individuals with initially high levels of cd8 markers experienced a decrease, while those with initially low levels showed an increase. these findings regarding changes in the receptor profile of blood lymphocytes in the presence of a low molecular ligand (co) should be considered when applying ultraviolet blood irradiation therapy to patients with different etiologies.

raw: the utility model discloses a scattering coefficient calibration device of aerosol muller matrix measuring apparatu, it is including calibrating experiment equipment module and control processing terminal module, it mainly includes into kind unit wherein to calibrate experiment equipment module, the total mark turbidimeter, the air pump, the current meter, air outlet channel etc, its function realizes turbidimeter and the simultaneous observation of muller matrix measuring instrument to same big this light scattering of gas sample process, acquire corresponding observed data, control processing terminal module mainly includes data collection and control unit and data processing and display element, its feature pack draws together data acquisition and preliminary treatment, the integrality of data is supplyed and is encrypted, calibration coefficient's calculation and measuring result's demonstration. the utility model discloses make traditional exportable scattering coefficient value of muller matrix measuring instrument, calibration device simple structure, the scheme is practical easy goes.
rewritten: the utility model discloses a scattering coefficient calibration device for an aerosol muller matrix measuring instrument. this device comprises two main modules: a calibration experimental equipment module and a control processing terminal module. the calibration experimental equipment module includes elements such as a low-order unit, a turbidity meter, an air pump, a current meter, an air outlet channel, and so on. its function is to perform simultaneous observations with both the turbidity meter and the muller matrix measuring instrument during the light scattering process of a gas sample, thereby collecting the necessary observational data. the control processing terminal module consists mainly of a data collection and control unit, as well as a data processing and display unit. this module integrates the tasks of data acquisition, initial processing, ensuring data integrity and encryption, calculating the calibration coefficient, and displaying the measurement results. in summary, the utility model discloses a calibration device with a simpler structure compared to traditional scattering coefficient calibration methods for muller matrix measuring instruments, offering a practical and user-friendly solution.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide an assist device for a movable body, which can provide a configuration capable of restraining the height of a cabinet from increasing.solution: in an assist device 10, a shank 68a of an arm 66 and a shank 82a of a cam 80 are separately provided. thus, an angle of rotation of the arm 66 is set smaller than an angle of rotation of the can 80. consequently, a configuration capable of suppressing an increase in the height (vertical dimension of the device) of a cabinet 34 determined in consideration of a rotation area of the arm can be provided.selected drawing: figure 1
rewritten: problem to be solved: to provide an assist device for a movable body that maintains a cabinet’s height without increasing it. solution: in the assist device 10, the shank 68a of an arm 66 and the shank 82a of a cam 80 are provided as separate components. as a result, the arm 66 rotates through a smaller angle than the cam 80. this design ensures that the cabinet 34’s height (its vertical dimension), which is influenced by the arm's rotational area, does not increase. selected drawing: figure 1

raw: sickle cell disease (scd) is caused by an inherited mutation in hemoglobin that leads to sickle hemoglobin (hbs) polymerization and premature hbs denaturation. previous publications have shown that hbs denaturation is followed by binding of denatured hbs (a.k.a. hemichromes) to band 3, the consequent clustering of band 3 in the plane of the erythrocyte membrane that in turn promotes binding of autologous antibodies to the clustered band 3, and removal of the antibody-coated erythrocytes from circulation. although each step of the above process has been individually demonstrated, the fraction of band 3 that is altered by association with denatured hbs has never been determined. for this purpose, we evaluated the lateral diffusion of band 3 in normal cells, reversibly sickled cells (rsc), irreversibly sickled cells (isc), and hemoglobin sc erythrocytes (hbsc) in order to estimate the fraction of band 3 that was diffusing more slowly due to hemichrome-induced clustering. we labeled fewer than ten band 3 molecules per intact erythrocyte with a quantum dot to avoid perturbing membrane structure and we then monitored band 3 lateral diffusion by single particle tracking. we report here that the size of the slowly diffusing population of band 3 increases in the sequence: normal cells<hbsc<rsc<isc. we also demonstrate that the size of the compartment in which band 3 is free to diffuse decreases roughly in the same order, with band 3 diffusing in two compartments of sizes 35 and 71 nm in normal cells, but only a single compartment in hbsc cells (58 nm), rsc (45 nm) and isc (36 nm). these data suggest that the mobility of band 3 is increasingly constrained during scd progression, suggesting a global impact of the mutated hemoglobin on erythrocyte membrane properties.
rewritten: sickle cell disease (scd) results from an inherited mutation in hemoglobin that leads to sickle hemoglobin (hbs) polymerization and its subsequent premature denaturation. previous studies have demonstrated that once hbs denatures (forming hemichromes), these denatured hemoglobins bind to band 3 proteins. this binding causes band 3 to cluster within the erythrocyte membrane, which then facilitates the attachment of autologous antibodies to these clusters and ultimately leads to the removal of the antibody-coated erythrocytes from circulation. although each step of this process has been described independently, the specific proportion of band 3 that is modified due to its association with denatured hbs has not been quantified. to address this, we assessed the lateral diffusion of band 3 in various types of cells: normal cells, reversibly sickled cells (rsc), irreversibly sickled cells (isc), and hemoglobin sc erythrocytes (hbsc). our goal was to estimate the fraction of band 3 that exhibits reduced mobility as a result of hemichrome-induced clustering. in our experiments, we labeled fewer than ten band 3 molecules per intact erythrocyte using a quantum dot—a method chosen to avoid disturbing the membrane's structure—and then monitored the lateral diffusion of band 3 through single particle tracking. our findings indicate that the proportion of slowly diffusing band 3 increases in the following order: normal cells < hbsc < rsc < isc. additionally, we observed that the size of the diffusion compartment available to band 3 decreases in a similar manner. specifically, in normal cells, band 3 diffuses in two compartments with sizes of 35 and 71 nm, whereas in hbsc cells, rsc, and isc, band 3 is confined to a single compartment with sizes of 58 nm, 45 nm, and 36 nm, respectively. these results suggest that as scd progresses, the mobility of band 3 becomes increasingly restricted, reflecting a widespread effect of the mutated hemoglobin on the properties of the erythrocyte membrane.

raw: the invention relates to a composition for preventing and treating powdery mildew of cucumber. the composition comprises lentinan, plant activator proteins, azoxystrobin, garlic oil, ester polyoxyethylene ether, sapindus mukurossi powder, c8-10 fatty alcohol, hydroxyethyl cellulose and deionized water. the composition is a compounded pesticide for preventing and treating the powdery mildew of cucumber; obvious synergistic effects are realized after compounding lentinan, the plant activator proteins and azoxystrobin, so the dosage of effective components is substantially reduced; and the composition has the advantages of effective enhancement of the cucumber powdery mildew prevention and treatment effects, fast effectiveness, long persistent period, no influences on non-target crops, and obvious cucumber yield increase effect.
rewritten: this invention relates to a formulation aimed at preventing and treating powdery mildew in cucumbers. the formulation includes lentinan, plant activator proteins, azoxystrobin, garlic oil, ester polyoxyethylene ether, sapindus mukurossi powder, c8-10 fatty alcohol, hydroxyethyl cellulose, and deionized water. essentially, it is a compounded pesticide designed to combat cucumber powdery mildew. notably, when lentinan, plant activator proteins, and azoxystrobin are combined, they exhibit clear synergistic effects, which significantly reduces the amount of active ingredients needed. additionally, the formulation boasts advantages such as enhanced efficacy in preventing and treating cucumber powdery mildew, rapid action, a prolonged residual effect, minimal impact on non-target crops, and a noticeable increase in cucumber yield.

raw: an overview of recent research in the area of whole-body vibration measurement and management is provided. prolonged exposure to high amplitude whole-body vibration is associated with a range of adverse health effects, particularly back pain. operators of a range of workplace plant and equipment are exposed to the hazard. iso2631.1 describes frequency weightings to be applied to accelerometer data and describes measures for describing vibration amplitude. an ios application is available which may facilitate routine measurement of whole-body vibration at workplaces as a means of identifying and evaluating opportunities to reduce vibration amplitudes. modifiable factors associated with occupational whole-body vibration exposure include the environment in which equipment is operated including roadway design and maintenance; the tasks performed using the equipment; the design of equipment including suspension and seat; equipment condition including maintenance of suspension and seating; and operator behaviour, especially speed of travel for many vehicles.
rewritten: this text provides an overview of recent studies on measuring and managing whole-body vibration. it highlights that long-term exposure to high-amplitude vibrations is linked to various negative health effects, notably back pain. operators of many types of industrial machinery and vehicles are exposed to this risk. iso 2631.1 defines frequency weightings for processing accelerometer data and sets out measures for quantifying vibration amplitude. additionally, there is an ios application available that could support regular monitoring of whole-body vibration at workplaces, helping to identify and assess opportunities to lessen these vibration levels. factors that can be adjusted to reduce occupational exposure include the operating environment, such as roadway design and maintenance; the tasks performed with the equipment; the equipment’s design features, including suspension systems and seats; its condition, with an emphasis on maintaining suspension and seating; and operator behavior, particularly the speed at which vehicles are driven.

raw: the invention discloses a power control apparatus. the power control apparatus comprises a power socket, a power plug and a control circuit connected between the power socket and the power plug, wherein the control circuit comprises a power output unit, first and second relays, a first capacitor, a mechanical switch, an electronic switch and a first voltage source connected to a computer host; the mechanical switch is closed, the power socket gets electricity, the first relay is closed, the power output unit outputs a working voltage to charge the first capacitor, the electronic switch is switched on, and the second relay is closed; the mechanical switch is opened, the first voltage source charges the first capacitor, the electronic switch is switched on, the second relay is closed, and the power socket gets the electricity; and the first voltage source loses the electricity, the electronic switch is cut off, the second relay is opened, and the power socket loses the electricity. the power control apparatus is capable of switching off the computer host and an external electronic device at the same time.
rewritten: the disclosed invention relates to a power control apparatus that includes a power socket, a power plug, and a control circuit positioned between them. this control circuit incorporates a power output unit, a first relay, a second relay, a capacitor, a mechanical switch, an electronic switch, and a voltage source connected to a computer host. when the mechanical switch is closed, the power socket receives electricity, which in turn closes the first relay. the power output unit then produces a working voltage to charge the capacitor, the electronic switch is activated, and the second relay closes. conversely, when the mechanical switch is opened, the voltage source charges the capacitor while the electronic switch remains on and the second relay stays closed, allowing the socket to continue receiving power. finally, when the voltage source stops supplying power, the electronic switch is deactivated and the second relay opens, resulting in the power socket losing electricity. this design enables simultaneous shutdown of the computer host and an external electronic device.

raw: a driving device includes a mounting frame, a driving rod, a driving gear, a worm, primary and auxiliary input members, and a reduction gear train. the mounting frame is connected to a frame unit. the driving rod is connected to a blind unit and is rotatable about a first axis. the driving gear is connected to the driving rod. the primary input member is rotatable about a second axis. the worm is connected to the primary input member and meshes with the driving gear. the auxiliary input member is rotatable about the second axis. the reduction gear train is connected between the primary and auxiliary input members, such that rotation of the auxiliary input member drives the primary input member to rotate at a speed different from that of the auxiliary input member.
rewritten: a driving device is composed of a mounting frame, a driving rod, a driving gear, a worm, primary and auxiliary input components, and a reduction gear train. the mounting frame attaches to a frame unit, while the driving rod is linked to a blind unit and can rotate around a first axis. the driving gear is fixed to the driving rod. meanwhile, the primary input component rotates about a second axis and is connected to the worm, which engages the driving gear. the auxiliary input component also rotates around the same second axis. the reduction gear train is situated between the primary and auxiliary input components, ensuring that when the auxiliary component rotates, it causes the primary component to turn at a speed different from that of the auxiliary component.

raw: the inquiry into teacher identity construction has received attention among teacher educators. guided by narrative epistemology and utilizing sociocultural theoretical lenses, the author employed a portraiture methodology to investigate teacher identity construction of two female teachers. the data were collected through postmodern, reflexive, and dyadic interviews, artifacts, and observations. narrative analysis method was employed to create themes and the themes were used to craft portraits of teacher identity construction, illuminating the journey to teaching english language learners. implications for second language teacher educators were provided.
rewritten: the investigation into how teacher identities are formed has captured the interest of teacher educators. drawing on narrative epistemology and framed by sociocultural theory, the researcher used a portraiture approach to explore the identity construction of two female teachers. data were gathered through postmodern, reflexive, and dyadic interviews, alongside artifacts and observations. a narrative analysis was applied to develop themes, which in turn were used to create portraits that reveal the teachers’ journeys in teaching english language learners. the study concludes with implications for educators training second language teachers.

raw: the present invention relates to a cosmetic composition which comprises: (a) an oil-based component containing volatile oil and a dimethicone/vinyl dimethicone crosspolymer, a dimethicone crosspolymer, a natural pine resin, and caprylic/capric tryglycerides; (b) a surfactant; (c) a powdered component containing organic hectorite and and powder; and (d) water-based component containing purified water and an ammonium polyacryloyldimethyl taurate/vp copolymer. according to the present invention, the cosmetic composition is less sticky can create light feeling when applying the composition onto skin. in addition, mixing a large quantity of powder is possible as the composition can have a novel formulation by reversed micelles with reduced moisture. thus, uniform application is possible while exhibiting excellent covering ability. the composition also has uniform viscosity and can offer a user with moisturization effects owing to moisture protection film.
rewritten: the present invention pertains to a cosmetic composition comprising: (a) an oil-based component that includes a volatile oil along with a dimethicone/vinyl dimethicone crosspolymer, a dimethicone crosspolymer, natural pine resin, and caprylic/capric triglycerides; (b) a surfactant; (c) a powdered component containing organic hectorite and powder; and (d) a water-based component featuring purified water and an ammonium polyacryloyldimethyl taurate/vp copolymer. this composition, according to the invention, is less sticky, resulting in a light feeling upon application to the skin. moreover, the formulation allows for the incorporation of a significant amount of powder thanks to a unique reversed micelle structure with reduced moisture, ensuring uniform application and excellent coverage. the composition also maintains consistent viscosity and provides a moisturizing benefit through a moisture-protection film.

raw: the resection of anterior skull base meningiomas has traditionally been performed via pterional or unilateral/bilateral subfrontal craniotomies. the supraorbital keyhole approach and the endoscopic endonasal approach, techniques in which the endoscope is used to aid visualization, were developed to provide alternative, less-invasive approaches to aid the resection of these tumors. the individual characteristics of each tumor, such as location and size, are the main determinants guiding the choice of approach. in this article, the advantages and disadvantages of each approach are discussed, along with complications specific to each technique. furthermore, a detailed procedural description of each surgical approach is described.
rewritten: traditionally, anterior skull base meningiomas have been removed through pterional or unilateral/bilateral subfrontal craniotomies. more recently, techniques such as the supraorbital keyhole and endoscopic endonasal approaches have been developed; both of these methods utilize an endoscope to enhance visualization and serve as less-invasive alternatives for tumor resection. the location and size of each tumor are the primary factors that dictate the choice of surgical approach. this article examines the pros and cons of each method, highlights the complications unique to each technique, and provides a detailed step-by-step description of the procedures involved.

raw: the tires, the first parts of the vehicles exposed to the road surface, affect various performances such as the ride comfort, the safety, and the directional stability. recently, there has been a recent increase of interests in eco-friendly vehicles in accordance with recent environmental regulations, so the factors of fuel efficiency and pass-by noise of the tires are becoming more important. in this paper, we will focus on the method of prediction on radiated noise, affecting pass-by noise, using the suspension and wheels of the vehicles.
rewritten: tires, being the vehicle components that first contact the road, influence several performance aspects such as ride comfort, safety, and directional stability. with growing interest in eco-friendly vehicles driven by evolving environmental regulations, factors like fuel efficiency and pass-by noise have become increasingly important. in this paper, we concentrate on a method for predicting radiated noise—which contributes to pass-by noise—by analyzing the vehicle's suspension and wheels.

raw: this guide is for ubc library geospatial services and resources. these pages provide information about in-person resources at ubc as well as online tutorials and guides.
rewritten: this guide is intended for ubc library's geospatial services and resources. it offers information about both in-person resources available at ubc and online tutorials and guides.

raw: two of the natural topologies for infinite graphs with edge-ends are etop and itop. in this paper, we study and characterize them. we show that itop can be constructed by inverse limits of inverse systems of graphs with finitely many vertices. furthermore, as an application of the inverse limit approach, we construct a topological spanning tree in itop
rewritten: two of the most natural topologies for infinite graphs that include edge-ends are etop and itop. in this paper, we examine and characterize these topologies. specifically, we demonstrate that itop can be constructed as the inverse limit of a sequence of graphs, each with finitely many vertices. moreover, by applying the inverse limit method, we construct a topological spanning tree within itop.

raw: many teachers are turning to online professional development to meet their learning needs, but the vast array of available opportunities may be overwhelming. this article provides a framework for making sense of common online teacher learning opportunities. it also suggests situations where online professional development may be most useful and presents a challenge for educators to consider when engaging in online learning.
rewritten: many educators are increasingly relying on online professional development to address their learning needs, yet the sheer number of options can be daunting. this article offers a structured guide to understanding the most common online learning opportunities for teachers. it also highlights contexts where online professional development is especially beneficial and poses a thought-provoking challenge for educators to consider as they engage with digital learning environments.

raw: purpose: this study is a qualitative research on the adaptation of the spinal cord injury after fortuitous the accident. methods: the subjects are fifteen spinal cord injuries. as a study method, this author employed grounded theory, one of the qualitative research methods. for data analysis, the procedures of grounded theory methodology was adopted, and then the data collection were collected for six months from march to september 2009. concerning the frequence of interviewing, each participants went through individual interviewing twice at least and up to four times if necessary. results: as a result of open coding, total thirty six concepts and twenty categories were drawn. to synthesize these categories, they came to choose `going into the new self-made spinal cord injury` as a nuclear category. typological analy sis based on the result of the paradigm model divided them into a reality-avoiding, passive coping, reality-maintaining, reality-overcoming, and self-fulfillment type. conclusion: as a result of this study, family support to those with spinal cord disability is an important factor for their motive for rehabilitation and adjustment, spinal disabilities were accepted to mostly the disabilities in relations with colleagues. thus it is necessary to give them a nursing intervention through disability group for successful adjustment to life with disability.
rewritten: purpose: this study uses a qualitative approach to explore how individuals adapt to spinal cord injury after an unexpected accident. methods: the research involved fifteen subjects with spinal cord injuries. grounded theory, a common qualitative research method, was employed for this study. data were collected following the procedures of grounded theory over a six-month period from march to september 2009. each participant underwent at least two individual interviews, with up to four sessions if necessary. results: analysis through open coding resulted in 36 concepts grouped into 20 categories. these categories were integrated by identifying the core category, "going into the new self-made spinal cord injury." based on the paradigm model, participants were classified into five types: reality-avoiding, passive coping, reality-maintaining, reality-overcoming, and self-fulfillment. conclusion: the study concludes that family support is a crucial factor in motivating rehabilitation and adjustment among those with spinal cord injuries. it also found that challenges with colleagues are a prominent concern. therefore, nursing interventions, including support groups, are necessary to help individuals successfully adjust to life with a disability.

raw: the invention discloses a high flame-retardant level halogen-free polycarbonate film material and a preparation method thereof. the material is prepared from the following raw materials in parts by weight, polycarbonate resin, compound halogen-free flame retardants, flame-retardant synergists, modifiers, antioxidants, lubrication dispersing agents, light stabilizers, antistatic agents and the like which are mixed by a high-speed mixer, granulated by a double-screw extruder and prepared into the high flame-retardant level halogen-free polycarbonate film material by a three-roller calendaring sheet extruder. a polycarbonate film made of the material is high in strength, strong in tear resistance, excellent in insulation performance, non-toxic and environmentally friendly, and has 0.3mm v-0 excellent flame resistance.
rewritten: the invention presents a halogen-free polycarbonate film material with a high flame-retardant rating, along with a method for its preparation. the material is made from several raw components weighed in specific parts: polycarbonate resin, compound halogen-free flame retardants, flame-retardant synergists, modifiers, antioxidants, lubrication and dispersing agents, light stabilizers, antistatic agents, and similar additives. these ingredients are first combined in a high-speed mixer, then granulated using a twin-screw extruder, and finally formed into the high flame-retardant polycarbonate film material through a three-roller calendaring sheet extruder. the resulting polycarbonate film is noted for its high strength, excellent tear resistance, superior insulation properties, and non-toxic, environmentally friendly characteristics, while also achieving a v-0 flame resistance rating at a thickness of 0.3 mm.

raw: the invention provides a preparation method of polyaspartic acid ester resin. firstly a chemical reaction is performed on polyether amine and maleic acid ester; then cyclic carbonate is added to perform the chemical reaction. the preparation process can quickly remove primaquine, and polyurea prepared from the polyaspartic acid ester resin has good adhesive force.
rewritten: the invention describes a method for preparing polyaspartic acid ester resin. initially, a chemical reaction is carried out between polyether amine and a maleic acid ester. next, cyclic carbonate is introduced to advance the reaction. this process can rapidly remove primaquine, and the resulting polyurea derived from the polyaspartic acid ester resin exhibits strong adhesive properties.

raw: objective: patient education is an important intervention to prevent an adrenal crisis in patients with adrenal insufficiency. the objective of this study was to assess the knowledge of adjusting the dose of glucocorticoids in special circumstances in patients with adrenal insufficiency who had previously been educated on this topic. in patients with insufficient knowledge, we tried to identify the underlying causes and care needs. design: quantitative and qualitative study. methods: adult patients with chronic primary and secondary adrenal insufficiency who received glucocorticoid stress management education were invited to participate in a telephone interview in which we tested their knowledge using hypothetical situations of physical and mental stress. in respondents with insufficient knowledge, we conducted a qualitative semistructured interview to elicit the underlying reasons from patients' perspective for their lack of knowledge and determine their care needs. results: forty-three of the 83 patients who previously received education had insufficient knowledge about how to act during stressful situations. we found a significant association between education level and level of knowledge after the educational consult. the following underlying factors were identified: unawareness of the seriousness of their condition, ineffective coping strategies, the lack of experience with self-management skills and misconceptions. the most important care needs were repetition of education, the use of guidelines, learning from experience and optimizing social support. conclusion: one or two educational consults are not effective to achieve adequate self-management skills. there is a need for structural follow-up where education is repeated and practical implementation of this knowledge is tested in order to identify the potential inadequate action.
rewritten: objective: educating patients is a key strategy to prevent adrenal crisis in individuals with adrenal insufficiency. this study aimed to evaluate how well patients who had already received education on adjusting glucocorticoid doses in specific circumstances could apply that knowledge. for those with inadequate understanding, we sought to identify the reasons behind their knowledge gaps and assess their additional care needs. design: mixed-methods study incorporating both quantitative and qualitative approaches. methods: we invited adult patients with chronic primary or secondary adrenal insufficiency, all of whom had previously been taught glucocorticoid stress management, to participate in a telephone interview. during these interviews, we assessed their knowledge through hypothetical scenarios involving physical and emotional stress. for patients whose knowledge was found lacking, we followed up with a semi-structured qualitative interview to explore their personal reasons for the deficit and to identify their care requirements. results: out of 83 patients who had received prior education, 43 demonstrated insufficient knowledge regarding the appropriate actions to take during stressful situations. a significant correlation emerged between patients' education levels and their post-consultation knowledge. the underlying factors contributing to their knowledge deficit included a lack of awareness about the seriousness of their condition, ineffective coping mechanisms, limited experience with self-management skills, and prevalent misconceptions. the critical care needs identified were repeated educational sessions, clear guidelines, experiential learning opportunities, and enhanced social support. conclusion: one or two educational sessions do not suffice for developing adequate self-management skills. continuous follow-up, involving repeated education and practical assessments, is necessary to identify and address possible shortcomings in patient responses during stressful situations.

raw: mobility and convergence are the basic requirements of the present day 4g-5g communications. we have good architectures for fiber optic man/wan networks, 4g wimax / lte cellular networks and present day 4g/5g 802.11 wifi networks; however we need to have unified architectures providing very high bandwidths and seamless services for 4g/5g communication needs. an effort has been made here to present such a unified architecture by integrating the best features of fiber, wimax/lte and wifi architectures, which can support even 5g requirements.
rewritten: mobility and convergence are essential for modern 4g and 5g communications. while we have robust designs for fiber optic man/wan networks, as well as for 4g wimax/lte and current 4g/5g 802.11 wifi networks, there is a growing need for integrated architectures that deliver exceptionally high bandwidths and seamless services to meet 4g/5g demands. this work endeavors to introduce such a unified architecture by combining the best elements of fiber, wimax/lte, and wifi systems, capable of supporting the requirements even of 5g.
