rewritten: field: tobacco industry. substance: this invention relates to a fire retardant agent designed for cigarette paper. the composition comprises 10-30 wt% of a film-forming material, 10-30 wt% of a filling material, and 40-80 wt% of a solvent. the film-forming material can be selected from cationic starch, anionic starch, oxidized starch, gum arabic, methylcellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose, vinyl alcohol, alginate, or any combination thereof. meanwhile, the filling material can be chosen from calcium carbonate, magnesium oxide, magnesium hydroxide, aluminium hydroxide, talc, or any mixture of these substances. effect: provides self-extinguishing properties. 10 cl, 2 dwg.

raw: this article presents outcome data of the implementation of three group cognitive-behavioral therapy (gcbt) interventions for children with externalizing behavior problems, anxiety, and depression. school counselors and graduate students co-led the groups in two low-income urban schools. data were analyzed to assess pre-treatment to post-treatment changes in diagnostic severity level. results of the exploratory study indicated that all three gcbt protocols were effective at reducing diagnostic severity level for children who had a primary diagnosis of an externalizing disorder, anxiety disorder, or depressive disorder at the clinical or intermediate (at-risk) level. all three gcbt protocols were implemented with relatively high levels of fidelity. data on the effectiveness of the interventions for reducing diagnostic severity level for externalizing and internalizing spectrum disorders and for specific disorders are presented. a discussion of implementation of mental health evidence-based interventions in urban schools is provided.
rewritten: this article examines the outcomes of implementing three group cognitive-behavioral therapy (gcbt) interventions designed for children facing externalizing behavior issues, anxiety, and depression. in two low-income urban schools, school counselors and graduate students co-led these groups. the study analyzed data to evaluate changes in diagnostic severity from before to after treatment. findings from this exploratory study demonstrated that each of the three gcbt protocols successfully reduced diagnostic severity in children with a primary diagnosis of externalizing disorders, anxiety disorders, or depressive disorders, whether at the clinical level or at an intermediate (at-risk) level. additionally, all three protocols were delivered with a high degree of fidelity. the article provides data on the effectiveness of these interventions in reducing diagnostic severity for both externalizing and internalizing spectrum disorders, as well as for specific disorders. it concludes with a discussion on the implementation of evidence-based mental health interventions in urban school settings.

raw: floria, p, gomez-landero, la, suarez-arrones, l, and harrison, aj. kinetic and kinematic analysis for assessing the differences in countermovement jump performance in rugby players. j strength cond res 30(9): 2533-2539, 2016-the aim of this study was to ascertain the differences in kinetic and kinematic profiles between better and poorer performers of the vertical jump within a homogeneous group of trained adults. fifty rugby players were divided into low scoring (low) and high scoring (high) groups based on their performance in the vertical jump. the force, velocity, displacement, and rate of force development (rfd)-time curves were analyzed to determine the differences between groups. the analysis of the data showed differences in all the patterns of the ensemble mean curves of the high and low groups. during the eccentric phase, the differences in the high group with respect to the low group were lower crouch position, higher downward velocity, and higher force and rfd during the braking of the downward movement. during the concentric phase, the high group achieved higher upward velocity, higher force at the end of phase, and a higher position at takeoff. the higher jump performances seem to be related to a more effective stretch-shortening cycle function that is characterized by a deeper and faster countermovement with higher eccentric forces being applied to decelerate the downward movement leading to enhanced force generation during the concentric phase.
rewritten: floria, p., gomez-landero, l.a., suarez-arrones, l., and harrison, a.j. conducted a study, published in the journal of strength and conditioning research (2016; 30(9): 2533-2539), that examined the kinetic and kinematic factors underlying countermovement jump performance in rugby players. their objective was to determine the differences in kinetic and kinematic profiles between individuals with better and poorer vertical jump performance among a homogeneous group of trained adults. in this study, fifty rugby players were divided into two groups based on their vertical jump performance—one group with lower scores and one with higher scores. researchers analyzed the force, velocity, displacement, and rate of force development (rfd) over time to identify differences between these groups. the results revealed distinct variations in the ensemble mean curves of the two groups. specifically, during the eccentric phase, the high-scoring group demonstrated a lower crouch position, a higher downward velocity, and greater force and rfd during the deceleration of the downward movement. in contrast, during the concentric phase, the high-scoring group achieved a higher upward velocity, produced greater force at the end of the phase, and reached a higher takeoff position. overall, the study suggests that enhanced jump performance is associated with a more effective stretch-shortening cycle. this cycle is characterized by a deeper and faster countermovement that enables the application of higher eccentric forces to decelerate the downward movement, thereby facilitating increased force generation during the concentric phase.

raw: the present invention provides a coating modification method of a material for preventing of high temperature bloating of a spinel lithium titanate-based lithium-ion battery and the lithium-ion battery. a percentage of the coating material (such as lif, al2o3, zro2, li2co3, and the like) and pure phase or doped li4ti5o12 are fully mixed, dispersed in water, ethanol or a mixture of the water and ethanol, heated to above the boiling point, and accompanied by condensation and reflux for thorough mixing, the suspension is filtered, and the filter cake is thermally treated at a certain temperature, so that surface of the li4ti5o12 material is uniformly coated with a 3-30nm lithium-ion-conductive coating layer for preventing of direct contact of a lithium titanate electrode and an organic electrolyte, the catalytic decomposition of the electrolyte, caused by the lithium titanate can be inhibited, and gas production can be reduced. the coating method can efficiently improve high temperature cycle stability of the lithium titanate battery, and can reduce the battery bloating.
rewritten: the present invention describes a coating modification technique for a material that helps prevent high-temperature bloating in spinel lithium titanate-based lithium-ion batteries, as well as a lithium-ion battery incorporating this technology. in this process, a measured amount of a coating material (such as lif, al2o3, zro2, li2co3, etc.) is thoroughly mixed with pure-phase or doped li4ti5o12. this mixture is dispersed in water, ethanol, or a combination of both, and then heated above the boiling point while undergoing condensation and reflux to ensure complete blending. afterward, the mixture is filtered and the resultant filter cake is subjected to a thermal treatment at a specific temperature. this treatment results in the uniform formation of a 3–30 nm lithium-ion-conductive coating layer on the surface of the li4ti5o12 material. the coating layer serves to prevent direct contact between the lithium titanate electrode and the organic electrolyte, thereby inhibiting the catalytic decomposition of the electrolyte and reducing gas formation. as a result, this coating process significantly improves the high-temperature cycle stability of the lithium titanate battery and helps reduce battery bloating.

raw: previous studies suggest that antiepileptic drugs with histone deacetylase (hdac) inhibitor properties may have prostate cancer preventive effects. we evaluated the association between antiepileptic drug use and prostate cancer risk in a population-based case-control study. the study included all new prostate cancer cases diagnosed in finland in 1995-2002 and matched controls (24,657 case-control pairs) identified from the finnish cancer registry and the population register center, respectively. information on antiepileptic drug purchases was obtained from the national prescription reimbursement database. odds ratios and their 95 % confidence intervals were estimated using age-adjusted and multivariable-adjusted conditional logistic regression analysis. compared to never-users of antiepileptic drugs, the overall prostate cancer risk was decreased among users of phenobarbital, carbamazepine, and valproic acid (multivariable-adjusted odds ratio (or) 0.47, 95 % ci 0.24-0.92; or 0.82, 95 % ci 0.71-0.94, and or 0.62, 95 % ci 0.42-0.92, respectively), but not among users of other antiepileptic drugs. overall prostate cancer risk decreased in a dose-dependent manner by cumulative amount, duration and yearly dosage (intensity) of hdac inhibitors valproic acid and carbamazepine. the risk of advanced prostate cancer was decreased only among carbamazepine users (or 0.65, 95 % ci 0.44-0.96). our results support possible prostate cancer preventive effects of hdac inhibitors. however, also phenobarbital use was associated with decreased prostate cancer risk, despite not having hdac inhibiting activity. the mechanism of action for antiepileptic drugs in prostate cancer deserves further study.
rewritten: earlier research indicates that antiepileptic drugs that act as histone deacetylase (hdac) inhibitors might help prevent prostate cancer. in our study, we examined the relationship between antiepileptic drug usage and the risk of prostate cancer through a population-based case-control approach. the study encompassed all newly diagnosed prostate cancer cases in finland between 1995 and 2002, along with matched controls (24,657 case-control pairs) obtained from the finnish cancer registry and the population register center. data regarding antiepileptic drug purchases were collected from the national prescription reimbursement database. we calculated odds ratios and their 95% confidence intervals using age-adjusted and multivariable-adjusted conditional logistic regression models. when compared to individuals who never used antiepileptic drugs, those who had used phenobarbital, carbamazepine, or valproic acid exhibited a reduced overall risk of prostate cancer (with multivariable-adjusted odds ratios of 0.47 [95% ci: 0.24–0.92], 0.82 [95% ci: 0.71–0.94], and 0.62 [95% ci: 0.42–0.92], respectively), whereas users of other antiepileptic medications did not show a similar risk reduction. furthermore, the overall risk of prostate cancer declined in a dose-responsive fashion with higher cumulative amounts, longer duration, and increased yearly dosage (intensity) of the hdac inhibitors valproic acid and carbamazepine. notably, the risk of advanced prostate cancer was reduced only in patients using carbamazepine (odds ratio 0.65, 95% ci: 0.44–0.96). these findings support the potential for hdac inhibitors to prevent prostate cancer; however, since phenobarbital—which does not possess hdac inhibiting properties—was also linked to a lowered risk, further research is warranted to clarify the mechanisms by which antiepileptic drugs might influence prostate cancer development.

raw: in the tapping machine, automatic tool changer (atc) reduced working process time by changing tools rapidly, four-bar linkage-based angular transmission mechanism is the important parts in atc. required drive torque to transit an angle exists in angular transmission mechanism. because the torque, the consumed energy and fatigue load are higher, reducing torque is required for improving ability of working process. thus, a new mechanism with torsional spring is proposed to reduce peak torque and difference between the torque values. the dynamics analysis of the mechanism is performed to confirm that peak torque is lower than before. for optimal design, design parameters set stiffness, initial angle. torque compensation mechanism (tcm) is optimized to minimize the peak required torque and difference between torque values. lastly, it will be compared torque simulations with and without optimized tcm.
rewritten: in tapping machines, the automatic tool changer (atc) shortens processing time by rapidly switching tools. one critical part of the atc is the four-bar linkage-based angular transmission mechanism, which must provide the drive torque needed for angular transitions. since this torque demand leads to increased energy consumption and fatigue loads, reducing it is vital for enhancing process efficiency. to achieve this, a new mechanism that incorporates a torsional spring is proposed to lower both the peak torque and the disparity between torque values. dynamic analysis of the mechanism confirms that the peak torque is reduced compared to the previous design. for optimal performance, the design parameters—namely stiffness and initial angle—are specified, and the torque compensation mechanism (tcm) is optimized to minimize both the peak torque and the variation in torque. finally, torque simulations will be compared for designs with and without the optimized tcm.

raw: in this study, a novel task-specific ionic liquid functionalized gold nanoparticle (tsil-gnp) was successfully prepared and applied in the recognition of amino acids. particularly, the surface of gnp was modified with the ionic liquid containing carbamido and ester group via thiol, which was characterized by fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (ftir) and transmission electron microscopy (tem). the stability of this material in aqueous solution improves apparently and can remain unchanged for more than three months. the effect of ph was also discussed in this study. attractive ionic interaction would effectively weaken intensity of the covalent coupling between the metal ion and the functional groups of amino acids. thus, tsil-gnp was successfully applied to recognizing serine, aspartic acid, lysine, arginine, and histidine in the presence of cu(2+) through distinctive color changes. suspension would be generated once a spot of cysteine was added into the gnps solution. results indicated that it had a good linear relationship between extinction coefficients and concentration of amino acids in a wide range of 10(-3)-10(-6) m. moreover, the proposed strategy was successfully used to analyze the histidine in urinary samples. in brief, tsil-gnp is a suitable substrate for discrimination of five amino acids in a rapid and simple way without sophisticated instruments.
rewritten: in this study, a novel task-specific ionic liquid-functionalized gold nanoparticle (tsil-gnp) was successfully synthesized and used for amino acid recognition. specifically, gold nanoparticles were modified with an ionic liquid that contains carbamido and ester groups via a thiol linkage, and the modification was confirmed by fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (ftir) and transmission electron microscopy (tem). this modification significantly enhanced the nanoparticles' stability in aqueous solution, maintaining their integrity for over three months. the study also examined the effect of ph, noting that attractive ionic interactions can effectively reduce the strength of the covalent bond between the metal ion and the amino acid functional groups. as a result, tsil-gnps were effectively applied to detect serine, aspartic acid, lysine, arginine, and histidine in the presence of cu²⁺ through distinctive color changes. additionally, the introduction of a drop of cysteine to the nanoparticle solution led to the formation of a suspension. the findings revealed a good linear correlation between the extinction coefficients and the concentrations of amino acids over a wide range (10⁻³–10⁻⁶ m). moreover, this method was successfully employed to analyze histidine in urine samples. in summary, tsil-gnp serves as an excellent substrate for the rapid and simple discrimination of five amino acids without the need for sophisticated instrumentation.

raw: abstract we use a mathematical programming model to examine the impacts of simultaneous implementation of two us biofuel and bioenergy policies on commodity markets and spatial distribution of future cellulosic biorefineries. the key findings based on our numerical simulation are: (1) the number and average annual production capacity of cellulosic biofuel refineries depend on the total renewable fuels mandate; (2) the mix of cellulosic biomass feedstock depends on the assumptions about the production costs of energy crops and the amount of cropland that can be used for energy crops, but regardless of the assumptions crop residues are the primary biomass source to meet the demand for biomass for biofuel production and electricity generation; and (3) the biomass production areas would surround either future cellulosic biorefineries or the existing coal-based power plants to reduce the costs of biomass transportation. these findings have important implications for biorefinery investors and provide valuable policy insights for the selection of biomass crop assistance program project areas.
rewritten: in this study, we employ a mathematical programming model to analyze how two us biofuel and bioenergy policies, when implemented together, affect commodity markets and the geographic placement of potential future cellulosic biorefineries. based on our simulations, our main findings are as follows: (1) the total renewable fuels mandate influences both the number and the average annual production capacity of cellulosic biofuel refineries; (2) the composition of cellulosic biomass feedstock is determined by factors such as the production costs of energy crops and the available cropland for these crops. however, regardless of these assumptions, crop residues remain the primary source of biomass for biofuel and electricity production; and (3) biomass production areas are likely to develop around either future cellulosic biorefineries or existing coal-fired power plants in order to minimize transportation costs. these insights are significant for investors in biorefineries and offer valuable guidance for establishing biomass crop assistance program areas.

raw: an electronic cigarette vaporiser includes a heating element and a microcontroller; the microcontroller monitors or measures the airflow speed or pressure drop over an air-pressure sensor or other sensor and uses that as an input to control the power delivered to the heating element. the microcontroller can compensate for a very strong inhalation by applying more power during that inhalation as compared to a very light inhalation to ensure that the heating element is kept at its optimal heating temperature.
rewritten: an electronic cigarette vaporizer features a heating element alongside a microcontroller. this microcontroller detects or measures the airflow speed—or the pressure drop across an air-pressure sensor or another type of sensor—and uses that information to regulate the power supplied to the heating element. by doing so, it can account for a strong puff by increasing the power during that inhalation, while applying less power during a light puff, thereby maintaining the heating element at its optimal temperature.

raw: the present invention relates to a color-conversion film, a method for producing same, and a back-light unit comprising the color-conversion film, the color-conversion film comprising: a resin matrix; and an organic fluorescent substance dispersed within the resin matrix and absorbing blue or green light to emit light of a different wavelength from the absorbed light, wherein the resin matrix comprises a thermoplastic resin, and the color-conversion film has: a light-emission peak at 450 nm; a half-width of 40 nm; a half-width of 60 nm or less at the light emission peak when irradiated with light having a mono-modal distribution of luminous intensity; a quantum efficiency of 80% or greater; and an absorption coefficient of 30,000 m -1 cm -1 or greater at the maximum absorption wavelength.
rewritten: this invention concerns a color-conversion film, the method for its production, and a back-light unit that incorporates the film. the film consists of a resin matrix, which includes a thermoplastic resin, in which an organic fluorescent compound is dispersed. this fluorescent compound absorbs blue or green light and, in response, emits light at a different wavelength. the film possesses the following characteristics: it has a light-emission peak at 450 nm; a half-width of 40 nm; when exposed to light with a mono-modal luminous intensity distribution, it exhibits a half-width of 60 nm or less at the emission peak; it demonstrates a quantum efficiency of at least 80%; and it features an absorption coefficient of 30,000 m⁻¹cm⁻¹ or greater at its maximum absorption wavelength.

raw: the invention relates to economic microalgae research and development application, and aims at providing a separation method of a chlorella species for high production of polysaccharide. the method comprises the following steps: adding glycerol, rhamnose and vitamin c to a basal culture solution, so that a culture solution hpsc-m of the chlorella species for high production of the polysaccharide is obtained; and separating epiphytic algae from stems of wild dendrobium officinale, and conducting pressure screening in a high-temperature dry environment, so that the chlorella for high production of the polysaccharide is obtained. the method disclosed by the invention, compared with a conventional breeding method, has the characteristics of being simple and convenient to operate, low in cost, free from special requirements on the professional skills of operators, strong in applicability and the like; and a necessary technical method is provided for chlorella breeding.
rewritten: this invention pertains to the economical research and development of microalgae and provides a method for isolating a chlorella strain that produces high levels of polysaccharide. the procedure includes the following steps: • adding glycerol, rhamnose, and vitamin c to a basic culture solution to create an hpsc-m culture solution of the target chlorella strain. • removing epiphytic algae from the stems of wild dendrobium officinale, then applying pressure screening in a high-temperature, dry environment to extract the high-polysaccharide-producing chlorella. compared to conventional breeding methods, this method is simple, cost-effective, user-friendly (without requiring specialized skills), and highly applicable. thus, it offers a vital technical approach for breeding chlorella.

raw: in thermal spraying, uncoated substrates usually require roughening. as the most common roughening method, grit blasting increases the surface area and produces undercuts in almost all cases, which facilitate mechanical interlocking and thus promote the bonding between the substrate and coating. the effects of grit blasting parameters, i.e., the particle size, the blasting angle, the stand-off distance, and the pressure, on the resulting surface topography are investigated. furthermore, the efficiency and wear behavior of the blasting media are analyzed. influences of three different blasting media, corundum, alumina zirconia, and steel shot, on the surface roughening, are compared. by varying adjusted blasting parameters, different initial conditions (surface topography) are created. subsequently, the substrate is coated, and the coating bond strength is measured. one of the main results of this publication is that alumina zirconia and steel grit show a longer lifetime than pure alumina as a blasting media. moreover, it has been shown that the blasting parameters such as grain size, working pressure, and history (wear status) of the abrasive particles have a significant effect on the resulting surface topography. additionally, systematical analysis in this study shows that the blasting parameters such as stand-off distance and blasting angle have a small influence on the results of the blasting process. another important conclusion of this study is that the conventional surface parameters that have been analyzed in this study did not turn out to be suitable for describing the relationship between the surface topography of the substrate and resulting bond strength.
rewritten: in thermal spraying, uncoated substrates typically need to be roughened. grit blasting, the most common method, increases the surface area and creates undercuts that support mechanical interlocking, thereby enhancing the bond between the substrate and the coating. this study examines how different grit blasting parameters—namely particle size, blasting angle, stand-off distance, and pressure—affect the resulting surface topography, as well as analyzing the efficiency and wear behavior of the blasting media. it compares the effects of three blasting media, specifically corundum, alumina zirconia, and steel shot, on surface roughening. by adjusting the blasting parameters, various initial surface conditions are produced. after these surfaces are prepared, they are coated and the bond strength is measured. one significant finding is that alumina zirconia and steel grit have a longer lifespan than pure alumina when used as blasting media. additionally, the study shows that parameters such as grain size, working pressure, and the wear status of the blasting particles have a pronounced impact on surface topography, while stand-off distance and blasting angle play a much smaller role. another key conclusion is that the conventional surface parameters analyzed do not adequately describe the relationship between the substrate’s surface topography and the resulting bond strength.

raw: the mtt assay showed that the cell proliferation on hydroxyapatite (hap) and hap/bone morphogenic protein (bmp) coated group was better than the control and bmp coated groups at 5 days. and after 7 days of culture, the mrna expression levels of type i collagen, osteonectin, osteopontin, bonesialoprotein, bmp-2, alkaline phosphatase (alp) and runx-2 in the hap/bmp coated group were significantly higher than the other groups. also, in this group showed the most significant induction of osteogenic gene expression compared to mesenchymal stem cells (mscs) grown on the other groups. in addition, the cells in the hap/bmp coated group delivered higher levels of alp than the other three groups. also, silk scaffolds were implanted as artificial ligaments in knees of rabbits, and they were harvested 1 and 3 months after implantation. on gross examination, he staining showed that new bone tissue formation was more observed in the hap/bmp coated group 3 weeks postoperatively. and masson staining showed that in the hap/bmp coated group, the silk fibers were encircled by osteoblast, chondrocyte, and collagen. furthermore, the analysis showed that the width of the graft-bone interface in the hap and hap/bmp coated group was narrower than that in the other two groups 3 weeks postoperatively. so, it is concluded that bmp incorporated hap coated silk scaffold can be enhanced osseointegration and osteogenesis in bone tunnel. as a result, these experimental designs have been demonstrated to be effective in the acceleration of graft-to-bone healing by increasing new bone or fibrocartilage formation at the interface between graft and bone.
rewritten: the mtt assay revealed that cell proliferation on the hydroxyapatite (hap) and hap/bone morphogenic protein (bmp) coated samples was superior to that of the control and bmp-only groups after 5 days. after 7 days of culture, mrna expression levels of type i collagen, osteonectin, osteopontin, bone sialoprotein, bmp-2, alkaline phosphatase (alp), and runx-2 were significantly elevated in the hap/bmp coated group compared to the others. additionally, this group exhibited the most pronounced induction of osteogenic gene expression when compared to mesenchymal stem cells (mscs) grown on the other coatings. the cells on the hap/bmp coated scaffolds also produced higher levels of alp than those on the remaining three groups. silk scaffolds, used as artificial ligaments, were implanted into the knees of rabbits and harvested at 1 and 3 months post-implantation. gross examination and hematoxylin staining at 3 weeks postoperatively revealed a greater formation of new bone tissue in the hap/bmp coated group. masson staining further indicated that, in this group, the silk fibers were surrounded by osteoblasts, chondrocytes, and collagen. moreover, analysis demonstrated that the graft-bone interface was narrower in the hap and hap/bmp coated groups compared to the other two groups at the 3-week mark. in conclusion, the bmp-incorporated hap coated silk scaffold enhances osseointegration and osteogenesis within the bone tunnel. this experimental design effectively accelerates graft-to-bone healing by promoting the formation of new bone or fibrocartilage at the graft-bone interface.

raw: the invention discloses a foam gel preparation device for preventing and controlling self-combustion of coal. the device comprises a slurry preparing pool, a slurry pump, a foaming agent storage tank, an adding pump, a foaming gel-forming device and an air supply device, wherein a slurry preparing pool pipe is connected with the foaming agent storage tank and the foaming gel forming device through a tee; the slurry pump and the adding pump are mounted on the slurry preparing pool pipe and a foaming agent storage tank pipe respectively; the air supply device is connected with the foaming gel-forming device. the foam gel preparation device further comprises a liquid gelatinizing agent storage tank, a liquid gelatinizing agent adding pump and a foam gel outlet. a liquid gelatinizing agent storage tank pipe is connected with the foaming gel-forming device, the liquid gelatinizing agent adding pump is mounted on the liquid gelatinizing agent storage tank pipe, and the outlet end of the foaming gel-forming device is connected with the foam gel outlet. the problem that a lot of water precipitates out when foam breaks is solved fundamentally; foaming agents can be added immediately without pausing to wait foam forming, and continuous operation can be achieved.
rewritten: the invention describes a foam gel preparation device that prevents and controls the self-combustion of coal. the device consists of a slurry-preparing pool, a slurry pump, a foaming agent storage tank, an adding pump, a foaming gel-forming unit, and an air supply system. a tee connects a pipe from the slurry-preparing pool to both the foaming agent storage tank and the foaming gel-forming unit. the slurry pump is installed on the slurry-preparing pool pipe, while the adding pump is mounted on the foaming agent storage tank pipe. additionally, the air supply system is linked to the foaming gel-forming unit. furthermore, the device includes a liquid gelatinizing agent storage tank, a corresponding adding pump, and a foam gel outlet. a pipe from the liquid gelatinizing agent storage tank connects to the foaming gel-forming unit, with the liquid gelatinizing agent adding pump outfitted on that pipe, and the outlet of the foaming gel-forming unit connects to the foam gel outlet. this setup fundamentally resolves the issue of substantial water precipitation when foam breaks, permits the immediate addition of foaming agents without waiting for foam formation, and allows for continuous operation.

raw: childhood abuse is a risk factor for the development of externalizing characteristics and disorders, including antisocial personality disorder and psychopathy. however, the precise relationships between particular types of childhood maltreatment and subsequent antisocial and psychopathic traits remain unclear. using a large sample of incarcerated adult male criminal offenders (n = 183), the current study confirmed that severity of overall childhood maltreatment was linked to severity of both psychopathy and antisocial personality disorder in adulthood. moreover, this relationship was particularly strong for physical abuse and the antisocial facet of psychopathy. sexual abuse history was uniquely related to juvenile conduct disorder severity, rather than adult psychopathy or antisocial behaviors. additionally, there was a significantly stronger relationship between childhood maltreatment and juvenile conduct disorder than between childhood maltreatment and aspd or psychopathy. these findings bolster and clarify the link between childhood maltreatment and antisocial behavior later in life. (psycinfo database record
rewritten: research indicates that experiencing abuse during childhood is a risk factor for developing externalizing traits and disorders, such as antisocial personality disorder and psychopathy. nonetheless, the specific connections between different forms of childhood maltreatment and later antisocial or psychopathic characteristics remain uncertain. in a study involving 183 incarcerated adult male offenders, the findings demonstrated that the overall severity of childhood maltreatment was correlated with more severe manifestations of both psychopathy and antisocial personality disorder in adulthood. this connection was especially pronounced in cases of physical abuse, which was strongly linked to the antisocial component of psychopathy. in contrast, a history of sexual abuse was specifically associated with greater severity of juvenile conduct disorder, rather than adult psychopathy or antisocial behavior. additionally, the relationship between childhood maltreatment and juvenile conduct disorder was found to be significantly stronger than its relationship with either antisocial personality disorder or psychopathy. these results serve to reinforce and clarify the association between early maltreatment and later antisocial behavior.

rewritten: a crankshaft balancer suspension system designed for measuring crankshaft imbalance includes a base structure, a measurement bridge structure that supports the rotating crankshaft, and several flexural support legs positioned between the base structure and the measurement bridge. these flexural support legs are configured in size and shape to allow the measurement bridge to flex relative to the base. additionally, the system comprises a drive system with a drive shaft that connects to the crankshaft to rotate it, and a sensor attached to the measurement bridge that detects any imbalance during rotation and outputs the corresponding imbalance data.

rewritten: the invention describes a meeting table system that integrates both projection capabilities and a sound source. this system includes a compact integrated unit along with an infrared remote control. on the outer side of the integrated unit, there is a built-in control panel. inside, the unit is divided into three compartments: the left houses a projector, the middle contains an integrated computer, and the right holds a sound power amplifier. each compartment features a lifting motor at its base, with the motor’s shaft linked to a lifting rack that ends with a fixed base at the top. a microcontroller situated on the control panel connects to both a motor driver and an infrared signal receiver. the control panel also includes a power switch, a function selection button, and an interface panel, and it connects to a communication module. this configuration allows for an intelligent projection device and sound source to be seamlessly integrated into any meeting table, enabling automatic storage and easy control via an infrared remote or a mobile phone app for a user-friendly experience.

raw: breast-specific gamma imaging (bsgi) is a new technique in nuclear medicine for the diagnosis of breast cancer. after intravenous injection of the radioactive substance 99mtc-sestamibi the breasts are imaged with a gamma camera. the radionuclide assimilates into intracellular mitochondria, which are present in greater numbers in breast cancer cells than in normal cells. this causes a relatively high uptake of the radionuclide in tumours. along with mammography and ultrasound, mri is the current gold standard in breast imaging diagnostics. however, mri is a complex and expensive procedure and has low specificity leading to high false-positive rates. bsgi has equally high sensitivity but is more specific, cheaper and much simpler to interpret. bsgi could replace mri as a complementary technique to show, exclude or indicate the extent of breast cancer.
rewritten: breast-specific gamma imaging (bsgi) is an emerging nuclear medicine method used to diagnose breast cancer. after the intravenous injection of the radioactive substance 99mtc-sestamibi, the breasts are scanned with a gamma camera. the radionuclide accumulates in intracellular mitochondria, which are found in higher numbers in cancerous breast cells compared to normal ones, leading to increased uptake in tumors. currently, along with mammography and ultrasound, mri is considered one of the gold standard techniques for breast imaging diagnostics. however, mri is a costly and complex procedure that often suffers from low specificity and a high rate of false positives. bsgi matches this high sensitivity but offers greater specificity, reduced costs, and simpler interpretation. consequently, bsgi has the potential to serve as a complementary tool to mri, helping to detect, rule out, or delineate the extent of breast cancer.

raw: in sub-saharan africa, nutrition research has primarily focused on under-nutrition, particularly among vulnerable children. however, there is increasing evidence of an emerging nutrition transition with extremely high rates of obesity, and malnutrition in women may be a problem that is insufficiently recognized and inadequately documented. this analysis was based on the 2008 nigerian demographic and health survey (ndhs), which included 27,967 women aged 15-49 years. individual-level data were collected for socio-demographic characteristics and aggregated to the country's 37 states. a bayesian geo-additive mixed model was used to map the geographic distribution of under-nutrition at the state level, accounting for individual-level risk factors. the results reveal that 12.0% of the population were underweight, while 20.9% were either overweight or obese, based on bmi. the northern states of sokoto and yobe/borno and the southern state of delta had the highest prevalence of underweight, while states in the centre had the lowest underweight prevalence. underweight women were more likely to be from poorer households compared with their counterparts from the richest wealth index, which were consistently associated with lower odds of being underweight (posterior odds ratio (por) and 95% credible region (cr): 0.56 [0.46, 0.70]). on the other hand muslim women (1.61 [1.10, 2.23]), those of traditional religion (2.12 [1.44, 3.00]), those from the fulani ethnic group (2.90 [1.64, 5.55]) and those living in yobe state were all consistently associated with higher odds of being underweight. this study demonstrates that underweight is a major public health problem in nigeria affecting adult females in the northern states of nigeria. identifying risk factors and the need to account for sex, spatial and socio-cultural issues are crucial to develop and implement evidence-informed strategies and interventions for lifestyle health promotion.
rewritten: in sub-saharan africa, nutrition research has traditionally concentrated on under-nutrition in vulnerable children. however, recent evidence indicates a developing nutrition transition, marked by extremely high obesity rates, while malnutrition in women appears to be both under-recognized and poorly documented. this study utilized data from the 2008 nigerian demographic and health survey (ndhs), which included 27,967 women aged 15–49. individual socio-demographic information was gathered and then aggregated at the state level across nigeria's 37 states. a bayesian geo-additive mixed model was applied to map the geographic distribution of under-nutrition by state, taking into account individual-level risk factors. the analysis revealed that 12.0% of the women were underweight, whereas 20.9% were either overweight or obese, based on their bmi. the northern states of sokoto and yobe/borno, along with the southern state of delta, exhibited the highest rates of underweight individuals, while the central states showed the lowest prevalence. additionally, underweight women were more likely to come from poorer households compared to those from the richest wealth index, which was consistently associated with reduced odds of being underweight (posterior odds ratio (por) and 95% credible region (cr): 0.56 [0.46, 0.70]). in contrast, being muslim (1.61 [1.10, 2.23]), practicing traditional religions (2.12 [1.44, 3.00]), belonging to the fulani ethnic group (2.90 [1.64, 5.55]), or residing in yobe state were all consistently linked with higher odds of undernutrition. overall, the study underscores that undernutrition is a serious public health concern for adult women in nigeria, especially in the northern regions. identifying these risk factors, and considering gender, spatial, and socio-cultural dimensions, is essential for developing and implementing evidence-based strategies and interventions aimed at promoting healthier lifestyles.

raw: the invention provides a method for measuring the content of thorium in thorium tetrafluoride. the method includes the steps that 0.1-0.3 g of a thorium tetrafluoride sample is weighed and resolved with nitric acid; under the condition of ph ranging from 1.6 to 2.0, hydrochloric acid-potassium chloride serves as a buffer solution, xylenol orange and methylene blue serve as a mixed indicator, a solution is titrated through a sodium ethylene diamine tetraacetic acid (edta) standard solution till the color is changed from amaranth to bright green, and the content of thorium is calculated according to the concentration and the consumed volume of the standard solution. according to the method for measuring the content of thorium in thorium tetrafluoride, the detecting method of the content of thorium in thorium tetrafluoride is successfully set through an edta volumetric method. the method is suitable for measuring the content of thorium in thorium tetrafluoride and has the advantages of being high in accuracy, accurate and reliable. the rsd% of the measured content of thorium is smaller than 0.2%.
rewritten: the invention describes a procedure for quantifying thorium in thorium tetrafluoride. in this method, 0.1–0.3 g of a thorium tetrafluoride sample is weighed and dissolved using nitric acid. with the ph adjusted to between 1.6 and 2.0 by a hydrochloric acid–potassium chloride buffer, a mixed indicator composed of xylenol orange and methylene blue is employed. the resulting solution is titrated with a sodium ethylene diamine tetraacetic acid (edta) standard solution until the color transitions from amaranth to bright green, and the thorium content is calculated based on the concentration and volume of standard solution used. this edta volumetric method has been successfully developed for measuring thorium in thorium tetrafluoride, offering high accuracy, reliability, and precision, with the relative standard deviation (rsd) being less than 0.2%.

raw: an active microorganism fertilizer production method adopts bean dregs, vinasse and peanut shells as raw materials, and comprises the following steps: 1, preparing the raw materials; 2, fermenting the bean dregs; 3, fermenting peanut shell powder; 4, preparing an activated fermentation solution; 5, carrying out secondary fermentation; and 6, detecting and allowing an active microorganism fertilizer to leave a factory. the organic microorganism fertilizer is produced through fermenting by adopting a microorganism two-stage fermentation technology, and the microorganism fertilizer can be used as a normal microorganism fertilizer, can improve the output of crops by 3% or above, especially can reconstruct the soil structure and improve soil, improves the content of organic matters in soil, adjusts the ph value of soil, fundamentally improves the soil environment, reduces the fertilizer cost, improves the output, improves the quality, and reduces pesticide residues. the production method has the advantages of maturation, easy implementation, sufficient and wide sources of the production raw materials, low cost, simple preparation, innovation, and application popularization values.
rewritten: an active microorganism fertilizer production process uses bean dregs, vinasse, and peanut shells as its raw materials. the process involves the following steps: 1. preparing the raw materials; 2. fermenting the bean dregs; 3. fermenting ground peanut shells; 4. producing an activated fermentation solution; 5. conducting secondary fermentation; and 6. testing and dispatching the active microorganism fertilizer from the factory. the organic microorganism fertilizer is manufactured through a two-stage microbial fermentation technique. this fertilizer can be applied like any conventional microbial fertilizer while increasing crop yields by at least 3%. in particular, it helps rebuild and improve soil structure by increasing the soil’s organic matter content, regulating its ph, fundamentally enhancing the soil environment, reducing fertilizer costs, boosting output, improving quality, and decreasing pesticide residues. this production method is mature and easy to implement, benefits from abundant and widely available raw materials, is cost-effective, straightforward to prepare, innovative, and holds strong potential for broad application.

raw: the global 2007 financial crisis and the massive default of banking institutions revealed the need to robustify the global financial system. in this thesis we describe the meaning of an effective supervisory system of the banking system, the structures that should define an effective supervisory system and the historical evolution of the global supervisory system under the perspective of the acts of the basel committee. we also examine the greek banking system bearing in mind the existing supervisory framework. our empirical findings provide evidence in favor of the robustness of the greek banking system, regardless of the difficulties of the greek macroeconomic environment
rewritten: the global financial crisis of 2007 and the widespread defaults across banking institutions exposed the need to strengthen the global financial system. in this thesis, we explore what constitutes an effective supervisory framework for banks, identify the components that should structure such a system, and trace the historical evolution of global supervision through the lens of the basel committee’s initiatives. we also analyze the greek banking system in the context of its current supervisory environment. our empirical evidence supports the resilience of the greek banking system, even amidst the challenges posed by the nation's broader macroeconomic conditions.

raw: a magnetoelectric device comprises resistor elements (r, s, t), damping modules (2) and a drive module (5). each resistor element (r, s, t) comprises a magnetic core unit (6) having a first segment (62) of a loop shape and a second segment (61) connected to the first segment (62); a first to a third coil (l1 to l3) wound on the first segment (62) and loosely mounted; a first capacitor (c1) connected between the second coil (l2) and the third coil (l3); and a second capacitor (c2) connected in parallel with the third coil (l3). each damping module (2) receives electric energy from each resistor element (r, s, t) to discharge the electric energy to a dc power source (vdc). the drive module (5) connects the dc power source (vdc) to the first coil (l1) to produce an ac voltage on both ends of the first coil (l1).
rewritten: a magnetoelectric device includes resistor elements (r, s, t), damping modules (2), and a drive module (5). each resistor element (r, s, t) features a magnetic core unit (6) that has a loop-shaped first segment (62) connected to a second segment (61); it also has a set of coils (l1 to l3) wound on the first segment (62) in a loose configuration, a first capacitor (c1) positioned between the second coil (l2) and the third coil (l3), and a second capacitor (c2) that is connected in parallel with the third coil (l3). the damping modules (2) take the electrical energy from each resistor element (r, s, t) to discharge it into a direct current power source (vdc). the drive module (5) then couples this dc power source (vdc) to the first coil (l1) to generate an ac voltage across both ends of that coil.

raw: the present invention relates to a method and a system for providing touch data and gesture data for developing a multi-touch application program. according to the present invention, the method for providing gesture data comprises the following steps of: receiving an original touch message generated with respect to input by a user from an operation system of a first device; dividing the original touch message into a uniform size, sequentially analyzing angles for each divided section, classifying the angles into predetermined direction vectors, and converting to a chain code consisting at least one direction vector; generating a touch data representing a series of states and coordinates from the original touch message, and generating a gesture data by learning gesture data in a database pre-constructed by using the chain code; converting the touch data and the gesture data into a library file format of a second device to distribute the touch data and the gesture data to the second device which does not support touch input into the first device. therefore, a compatibility problem of touch input is solved and an application program can be developed in a gesture unit.
rewritten: the present invention concerns a method and system for supplying touch and gesture data that aids in the development of multi-touch application programs. in this method, gesture data is produced by carrying out the following steps: first, an original touch message—created by a user’s input and generated by the operating system of a first device—is received. this original message is then segmented into uniform parts, with each section being analyzed sequentially to determine angles. these angles are then grouped into predefined direction vectors and converted into a chain code that contains at least one such vector. next, touch data that reflects a sequence of states and coordinates is generated from the original message, and gesture data is derived by referencing a pre-constructed database that uses the chain code. finally, the produced touch and gesture data are converted into a library file format compatible with a second device, which does not natively support the touch input functions of the first device. this approach effectively resolves compatibility issues related to touch inputs, thereby facilitating the development of application programs within a gesture framework.

raw: provided by the present invention is a hybrid sunlight led light transmission system, which is able to turn on an led light transmission device by enabling a controller to selectively control power stored in a battery or power supplied from the outside by using a sunlight module. the hybrid sunlight led light transmission system according to an embodiment of the present invention comprises: a battery; an led light transmission device; a sunlight module which generates electricity by receiving sunlight; and a controller which charges the battery based on electricity generated from the sunlight module and turns on the light of the led light transmission device by selectively supplying one from battery power supplied from the battery and external power supplied from external power sources based on time or an illuminance sensor value.
rewritten: the present invention discloses a hybrid sunlight led light transmission system capable of activating an led light transmission device using a controller that selectively draws power either from a battery or from an external source via a sunlight module. in one embodiment, the system comprises a battery, an led light transmission device, a sunlight module that generates electricity from sunlight, and a controller that charges the battery with the electricity produced. the controller then powers the led light transmission device by choosing either the stored battery power or external power depending on factors such as time or illuminance sensor measurements.

rewritten: the invention concerns a system that utilizes municipal sewage sludge, park waste, and rural straw waste as valuable resources. the system includes several components: a sludge pyrohydrolysis unit, a waste mixture pulping unit, a mixing and stirring unit, an anaerobic fermentation unit, a biogas purification unit, a biogas residue power generation unit, a biogas residue retreatment unit, and a biogas slurry treatment unit. through multi-stage decomposition, selective differentiation treatments, and cyclical reuse of community wastes, the system efficiently converts municipal sludge, garbage, straw, and similar materials into usable resources while ensuring their safe and environmentally friendly reuse.

raw: provided is a cell culture system whereby it becomes possible to carry out the observation of cells and the replacement of culture media in many culture vessels during co-culture in a simple manner and at low cost. the cell culture system is equipped with: a first culture vessel holding section and a second culture vessel holding section, in each of which multiple culture vessels for culturing cells can be held; a space for homogenizing the culture temperature, humidity and gas concentration in each of the first culture vessel holding section and the second culture vessel holding section; and a moving mechanism for moving the culture vessels to a one-direction passage.

raw: objectives the aim of this study is to assess the functional outcomes registered in patients from the western part of romania, who have been treated for retinopathy of prematurity, using a laser diode (810mn). materials and methods in the current study 52 premature infants with gestational age 3.0 d) was found in 26 eyes (27.08 %) and anisometropia in 29 patients (55.77%). there is a strong positive correlation, statistically significant (p<0.001) between best corrected visual acuity and spherical equivalent, and a negative correlation, statistically significant (p=0.001), between best corrected visual acuity and the number of impacts during laser treatment. conclusion a long term follow-up is necessary in order to obtain an improvement in the quality of life and visual function of the patients who received laser treatment for retinopathy of prematurity.
rewritten: objective: the aim of this study is to evaluate the functional outcomes in patients from western romania who were treated for retinopathy of prematurity using an 810 nm diode laser. materials and methods: the study involved 52 premature infants. in 26 eyes (27.08%), a condition (gestational age 3.0 d)) was documented, and anisometropia was observed in 29 patients (55.77%). there is a strong, statistically significant positive correlation (p<0.001) between the best corrected visual acuity and the spherical equivalent, as well as a statistically significant negative correlation (p=0.001) between best corrected visual acuity and the number of impacts during laser treatment. conclusion: long-term follow-up is essential to improve both the quality of life and the visual function of patients who have received laser treatment for retinopathy of prematurity.

raw: the invention relates to a method for operating a gas engine, in particular to a method for operating a stationary gas engine, wherein an exhaust line adjoining the gas engine, through which the exhaust gas of the gas engine flows, is provided, wherein the gas engine is operated with a lean fuel gas/air gas mixture, wherein the gas engine is operated in accordance with a miller cycle, preferably in such a way that the closing point of the at least one intake valve of the gas engine is in a crank angle range of from about 50 degrees of crank angle before bottom dead center (bdc) to about 10 degrees of crank angle before bdc. according to the invention, the exhaust line has at least one selective catalytic reduction (scr) catalyst element, by which the level of nitrogen oxides (nox) in the exhaust gas flowing through or across the scr catalyst element is lowered using hydrocarbons (cyhz) as a reducing agent, wherein at least some of the hydrocarbons (cyhz) flowing through the scr catalyst element are constituents of the exhaust gas of the gas engine.
rewritten: the invention concerns a method for operating a gas engine, specifically a method for running a stationary gas engine. the engine is connected to an exhaust line through which its exhaust gases pass, and it operates using a lean fuel gas/air mixture. it runs according to a miller cycle, preferably with the intake valve closing occurring in a crank angle range of approximately 50 degrees before bottom dead center (bdc) to about 10 degrees before bdc. according to the invention, the exhaust line is equipped with at least one selective catalytic reduction (scr) catalyst element that reduces the level of nitrogen oxides (nox) in the exhaust gas by using hydrocarbons (cyhz) as a reducing agent. notably, some of these hydrocarbons are components of the engine's own exhaust gas.

raw: a physically-based integrated hydrological-geotechnical model (ihg) able to assess the rainfallinduced landslide susceptibility was developed, refined and applied in gis environment along the past years (passalacqua, 2002; federici et al., 2014; bovolenta et al., 2016), showing its reliability. it is a useful instrument to landslide susceptibility evaluations and land-use planning over wide areas. the present paper focuses on the modeling of water table oscillation due to rainfall, comparing different hydrological models.
rewritten: over the years, a physically based, integrated hydrological-geotechnical model (ihg) has been developed, refined, and implemented in a gis environment to assess rainfall-induced landslide susceptibility (passalacqua, 2002; federici et al., 2014; bovolenta et al., 2016), demonstrating its reliability. this model serves as a valuable tool for evaluating landslide susceptibility and guiding land-use planning over extensive areas. the current paper focuses on modeling fluctuations in the water table caused by rainfall, comparing various hydrological models.

raw: hierarchy is a fundamental phenomenon in management and organization science, a phenomenon which has marked the evolution of human societies over centuries. among the many studies on this issue, the ones that adopt a formal approach of investigation are mainly based on social network analysis. following this line, in this work we focus on organization distribution of formal direct authority in stylized, pure hierarchical archetypes. past research, analyzing the share of asymmetric links in out-tree topologies, was not able to distinguish among different types of out-trees. indeed since the out-trees can differ under substantial structural features, in order to measure the degree of hierarchy it is necessary to employ indicators of power concentration and distribution. results show that the purest archetype of hierarchy is the star form, and not the typical org chart. further, ceteris paribus, an organization with more hierarchical levels is less and not more hierarchical than an organization with fewer levels. moreover, power tend to concentrate in lower levels, and especially into the penultimate one.
rewritten: hierarchy is a fundamental aspect of management and organization science that has shaped human societies over the centuries. many studies have tackled this topic, and those using a formal approach typically rely on social network analysis. in our work, we examine how formal direct authority is distributed within idealized, purely hierarchical structures. earlier research looked at the proportion of asymmetric links in out-tree topologies but could not differentiate between various types of out-trees. since out-trees can vary significantly in their structure, measuring the degree of hierarchy requires using indicators that reflect both power concentration and distribution. our results reveal that the star configuration is the purest form of hierarchy—not the conventional organizational chart. moreover, all else being equal, organizations with more hierarchical levels tend to be less hierarchical than those with fewer levels. finally, power generally concentrates at the lower levels of the organization, especially in the penultimate level.

raw: monolayer black phosphorus (mbp) is an interesting emerging electronic material with a direct band gap and relatively high carrier mobility. in this work we report a theoretical investigation of nonequilibrium spin injection and spin-polarized quantum transport in mbp from ferromagnetic ni contacts, in two-dimensional magnetic tunneling structures. we investigate physical properties such as the spin injection efficiency, the tunnel magnetoresistance ratio, spin-polarized currents, charge currents and transmission coefficients as a function of external bias voltage, for two different device contact structures where mbp is contacted by ni(111) and by ni(100). while both structures are predicted to give respectable spin-polarized quantum transport, the ni(100)/mbp/ni(100) trilayer has the superior properties where the spin injection and magnetoresistance ratio maintains almost a constant value against the bias voltage. the nonequilibrium quantum transport phenomenon is understood by analyzing the transmission spectrum at nonequilibrium.
rewritten: monolayer black phosphorus (mbp) is a promising new electronic material characterized by a direct band gap and relatively high carrier mobility. in this study, we present a theoretical analysis of nonequilibrium spin injection and spin-polarized quantum transport in mbp, achieved via ferromagnetic nickel contacts within two-dimensional magnetic tunneling structures. we examine physical properties—including spin injection efficiency, the tunnel magnetoresistance ratio, spin-polarized currents, charge currents, and transmission coefficients—as functions of the external bias voltage for two different contact configurations: one where mbp is interfaced with ni(111) and another with ni(100). although both configurations demonstrate commendable spin-polarized quantum transport, the ni(100)/mbp/ni(100) trilayer exhibits superior performance, as its spin injection efficiency and magnetoresistance ratio remain nearly constant regardless of the bias voltage. this nonequilibrium quantum transport behavior is further clarified through an analysis of the transmission spectrum under nonequilibrium conditions.

raw: abstract knowing the effect a feed ingredient has on post-feeding glycaemic and insulin responses is important when managing conditions like equine metabolic syndrome, laminitis and the polysaccharide storage myopathy form of tying up in horses. feeds that cause minimal post-feeding disturbances to plasma glucose and insulin are desirable. this study was conducted to determine the post feeding glycaemic and insulin response in horses to copra meal (copra meal; 11% non-structural carbohydrate; nsc) and to compare this to the responses observed to low nsc pasture (pasture; 7% nsc) and higher nsc extruded pellet (pellet; 25.3% nsc) and sweetfeed (sweetfeed; 33.7% nsc) rations. copra meal did not increase plasma glucose levels above those observed in horses grazing the low nsc pasture while the pellet and sweetfeed rations caused significant post-feeding rises in plasma glucose. the pellet and sweetfeed rations also raised post feeding plasma insulin levels significantly compared to pasture and copra meal. copra meal raised plasma insulin levels significantly higher than that observed in horses grazing pasture from 15 to 60 min post feeding, after which time there was no significant difference. the results of this study demonstrated that the nsc content of a feed ingredient will influence the post-feeding glycaemic and insulin response to that feed, with low nsc feeds giving significantly lower responses than higher nsc feeds. the insulin response to copra meal observed in this study was small both in magnitude and time in comparison to levels reported in the literature needed to induce laminitis and as such are unlikely to be physiologically significant. copra meal is a feed ingredient that could be considered for use in diets for horses needing to remain on a low nsc diet that will cause minimal disturbance to their post feeding glucose and insulin levels.
rewritten: understanding how a feed ingredient influences post-feeding glycaemic and insulin responses is crucial when managing conditions such as equine metabolic syndrome, laminitis, and the polysaccharide storage myopathy variant of tying up in horses. ideally, feeds that cause minimal disruptions to plasma glucose and insulin levels after feeding are preferred. this study aimed to assess the post-feeding glycaemic and insulin responses in horses given copra meal (which contains 11% non-structural carbohydrates, or nsc) and to compare these responses with those observed in horses consuming low nsc pasture (7% nsc) as well as higher nsc extruded pellet (25.3% nsc) and sweetfeed (33.7% nsc) rations. the findings revealed that copra meal did not elevate plasma glucose levels beyond those seen in horses grazing on low nsc pasture, whereas both the pellet and sweetfeed rations produced significant increases in plasma glucose after feeding. similarly, the pellet and sweetfeed diets resulted in markedly higher post-feeding plasma insulin levels compared to both pasture and copra meal. although copra meal elevated plasma insulin levels significantly more than pasture-fed horses between 15 and 60 minutes post feeding, no significant differences were observed after that period. overall, the results demonstrate that the nsc content of a feed ingredient directly affects the post-feeding glycaemic and insulin responses, with low nsc feeds leading to considerably lower responses compared to high nsc feeds. moreover, the insulin response to copra meal in this study was both modest and short-lived relative to levels associated with laminitis in the literature, indicating that it is likely not physiologically significant. therefore, copra meal may be a suitable feed ingredient for horses requiring a low nsc diet aimed at minimizing disturbances to post-feeding glucose and insulin levels.

raw: in this article, we show that the normal contact problem between two elastic bodies in the half-space approximation can always be transformed to an equivalent problem of the indentation of a profile into an elastic winkler foundation. once determined, the equivalent profile can also be used for tangential contact problems and arbitrary superimposed normal and tangential loading histories as well as for treating of contact problems with linearly viscoelastic bodies. in the case of axis-symmetric shapes, the equivalent profile is given by the method of dimensionality reduction integral transformation. for all other shapes, the profile is deduced from the solution of the elastic contact normal problem, which can be obtained numerically or experimentally.
rewritten: this article demonstrates that the normal contact problem between two elastic bodies under the half-space approximation can always be reformulated as an equivalent problem where a profile is indented into an elastic winkler foundation. once this equivalent profile is determined, it can be applied not only to address tangential contact issues and various superimposed normal and tangential loading histories, but also to tackle contact problems involving linearly viscoelastic materials. for axisymmetric shapes, the equivalent profile is obtained using the integral transformation of the dimensionality reduction method. for any other geometries, the profile is derived from solving the elastic normal contact problem, which can be done either numerically or experimentally.

raw: to evaluate the accuracy of contrast-enhanced ultrasound (ceus) in comparison to ct angiography (cta) to identify and classify endoleaks following abdominal aortic aneurism repair with endoprosthesis. a retrospective analysis of 181 patients treated with evar, from september 2009 to september 2014, was performed. patients were evaluated with ceus, cta and angiography in the cases requiring treatment. sac diameter, sac integrity, identification and classification of endoleaks were taken into consideration. sensitivity, specificity, accuracy and negative predictive values were considered for each modality of endoleak identification. forty-two endoleaks (23.2%; type ii: 39 cases, type iii: 3 cases) were documented. sensitivity and specificity of ceus and ct were, respectively, 97.6 and 90.5, 100 and 100%. in two cases, ceus was able to better classify endoleaks compared to ct. ceus accuracy to identify endoleaks following evar is similar to ct. ceus should be considered as an effective modality for the long-term surveillance of evar because of its capability to correctly classify endoleaks with no ionizing radiation exposure.
rewritten: the study aimed to assess how accurately contrast-enhanced ultrasound (ceus) compared to ct angiography (cta) can detect and categorize endoleaks after endovascular abdominal aortic aneurysm repair (evar) with an endoprosthesis. a retrospective review was conducted on 181 patients who underwent evar between september 2009 and september 2014. these patients were monitored using ceus and cta, with angiography reserved for those requiring treatment. parameters such as sac diameter, sac integrity, and both the detection and classification of endoleaks were evaluated. sensitivity, specificity, overall accuracy, and negative predictive value were calculated for each imaging method in identifying endoleaks. out of the group, 42 endoleaks (23.2%) were identified—comprising 39 type ii and 3 type iii endoleaks. the sensitivity and specificity for ceus were 97.6% and 90.5%, respectively, while cta demonstrated 100% for both. in two instances, ceus was better than cta at correctly classifying the endoleaks. overall, the accuracy of ceus in detecting endoleaks following evar was comparable to that of cta. given its ability to accurately classify endoleaks without exposing patients to ionizing radiation, ceus should be considered an effective tool for long-term evar surveillance.

raw: steganography is the art of hiding data, in such a way that it is undetectable under traffic-pattern analysis and the data hidden is only known to the receiver and the sender. in this paper new method of text steganography over the silence interval of audio in a video file, is presented. in the proposed method first the audio signal is extracted from the video. after doing audio enhancement, the data on the audio signal is steganographed using new technique and then audio signal is rewritten in video file again. this http url to enhance the security level we apply chaotic maps on arbitrary text. furthermore, the algorithm in this paper, gives a technique which states that undetectable stegotext and cover-text has same probability distribution and no statistical test can detect the presence of the hidden message. this http url moreover, hidden message does not affect the transmission rate of video file at all.
rewritten: steganography involves concealing data in a way that makes it undetectable through traffic-pattern analysis, ensuring that only the sender and receiver are aware of the hidden content. in this paper, we present a novel method of text steganography that operates over the silent intervals of audio within a video file. the process begins by extracting the audio signal from the video, followed by audio enhancement. a new technique is then used to embed data into the audio signal, after which the modified audio is reinserted into the video file. to further boost security, chaotic maps are applied to arbitrary text. additionally, the algorithm described ensures that the statistical distribution of the stego text is identical to that of the cover text, rendering any statistical test incapable of detecting the hidden message. importantly, the embedding process does not affect the video file’s transmission rate.

raw: the invention provides a safety valve capable of automatically shutting off gas. the safety valve is a valve in which a plug is arranged, wherein the plug can seal a gas inlet; a tail rod of the plug is sleeved with a spring, the tail end of the tail rod is connected to a metal rope, the metal rope can pass through a gas hole in a gas outlet joint to pull soldering tin which is fixed at one side of a gas outlet of an end of the gas outlet joint. during use of the installed safety valve, when an accidental fire alarm occurs, the soldering tin is molten by fire, and then the metal rope is released, the plug automatically blocks the gas inlet of the valve under the action of the spring, and gas safety can be ensured.
rewritten: the invention describes a gas safety valve that can automatically shut off the gas supply. this valve features a plug arranged to seal off a gas inlet. a tail rod attached to the plug is surrounded by a spring, and at its end, it connects to a metal cable. this metal cable passes through a gas hole in the outlet joint to hold soldering tin fixed on one side of the gas outlet at the end of the joint. when a fire accident occurs, the heat melts the soldering tin, releasing the metal cable. as a result, the spring drives the plug to block the gas inlet, thereby ensuring gas safety.

raw: abstract as the ethylene glycol aqueous solution (eg/dw) with a volume fraction of 60:40% (the ratio of ethylene glycol (eg) and deionized water (dw)) was widely used as an antifreeze solution, pool boiling heat transfer (bht) characteristics including boiling heat transfer coefficient (htc) and critical heat flux (chf) of eg/dw based graphene nanosheets (gns) nanofluids are experimentally investigated under atmospheric pressure. the influence of heat flux and nanoparticles mass fraction on the boiling heat transfer has been measured and discussed. boiling curves were obtained for the nanofluids compared with the baseline case of eg/dw. it was shown that for low concentration (i.e., mass fraction up to 0.02%) gns-eg/dw nanofluids, the boiling performance of the eg/dw had been obviously enhanced by adding gns nanoparticles, which was resulted from the enhancement of surface wettability caused by nanoparticles deposition. however, further loading gns in eg/dw reduced the bht capability of eg/dw, results from the gns sedimentation and subsequent blockage of the nucleation sites. at the small concentration less than 0.02%, the chf rapidly increases with increasing nanoparticle concentration. however, the chf magnitude is almost the same for gns-eg/dw nanofluids with concentration beyond the critical concentration.
rewritten: an ethylene glycol aqueous solution (eg/dw) with a 60:40 volume ratio (ethylene glycol to deionized water), commonly used as an antifreeze, was employed in experiments to investigate the pool boiling heat transfer (bht) characteristics—specifically, the boiling heat transfer coefficient (htc) and critical heat flux (chf)—of eg/dw-based graphene nanosheet (gns) nanofluids under atmospheric pressure. the study examined how both the applied heat flux and the mass fraction of nanoparticles affect boiling heat transfer. boiling curves for the nanofluids were compared with those for the baseline eg/dw solution. the results indicated that at low concentrations (with a mass fraction up to 0.02%), the addition of gns nanoparticles significantly enhanced the boiling performance of the eg/dw solution due to improved surface wettability from nanoparticle deposition. however, further increasing the gns content in the eg/dw solution diminished its bht capability, attributed to gns sedimentation and the subsequent blockage of nucleation sites. for concentrations below 0.02%, the chf increased rapidly with rising nanoparticle levels, whereas for concentrations beyond this critical level, the chf remained relatively unchanged.

raw: this chapter is divided into three parts. the first part describes the role of natural polymers in the nonrenewable energy industry (drilling fluids) and this includes the definition, function, properties, additives, and types of drilling muds. in this part the problems of drilling fluid loss during its circulation at the oil field well and its solution using the natural polymers are also discussed. the second part deals with the role of natural polymers in the renewable energy industry (biomass), this part reviews the types of renewable energy produced from biomass. finally, the third part focuses on the role of natural polymers in the wastewater treatment technology.
rewritten: this chapter is organized into three sections. the first section explores the role of natural polymers in the nonrenewable energy industry, focusing on drilling fluids. it covers aspects such as definitions, functions, properties, additives, and the various types of drilling muds, while also addressing issues related to drilling fluid loss during circulation in oil field wells and how natural polymers can provide solutions. the second section examines the contribution of natural polymers to the renewable energy industry, specifically in biomass, and reviews the spectrum of renewable energy types derived from biomass. the final section concentrates on the application of natural polymers in wastewater treatment technology.
