raw: the intent of this paper is to summarize and analyze the data and experience earned from a practical implementation of online language learning approach specifically targeted to qur'anic arabic learning. the foundation of this work is based on the fact that in the current world, almost 80% of the muslims are non-native speakers/users of arabic language. as a religious obligation, a part of the arabic qur'an must be memorized by each of them to recite during the formal prayers. while for native arabic speaking people, it is relatively easy to understand, this approach of ours aims at achieving a level of understanding of the recited arabic words even for the non-native users. the power of information and communications technology (ict) is effectively used in this domain. the experience could be expanded to cover other language learning approaches, however, our web-based software does have some solid rationale-base to make it specific for qur'anic arabic only. alongside presenting the gained results, we provide necessary backgrounds, theoretical analysis and modeling, and details about the practical implementation of the prototype. we discuss the design of different natural language processing algorithms for analyzing the qur'anic corpus in order to identify and extract frequently occurring common language patterns to automate the production of educational material for the system. we also present some feedback of the user community built around the initial prototype which serves as a framework for capturing requirements from the world-wide user base (for each iteration) which is an essential component for the design based research methodology of the work.
rewritten: this paper aims to summarize and evaluate the data and experiences gathered from the practical application of an online language learning method tailored for teaching qur'anic arabic. the work is grounded in the observation that nearly 80% of muslims worldwide are non-native speakers of arabic. since memorizing parts of the qur'an is a religious duty required for formal prayers, it becomes challenging for non-native speakers, even though native speakers often find it easier to comprehend. our approach strives to help non-native users understand the recited arabic words. by effectively utilizing information and communications technology (ict), our experience may be extended to other language learning methods; however, our web-based software has a well-defined rationale that limits it specifically to qur'anic arabic. in addition to presenting our findings, we provide the necessary background, theoretical analysis, modeling, and detailed information about the prototype’s practical implementation. we also discuss the design of various natural language processing algorithms developed to analyze the qur'anic corpus, identify and extract commonly occurring language patterns, and automate the generation of educational material for the system. furthermore, we include user feedback from the community formed around our initial prototype, which helps capture requirements from a global audience during each iteration—an essential component of our design-based research methodology.

raw: the invention discloses an existing railway line retesting method based on the on-board laser radar technology. firstly, control marks are arranged along a railway to be tested at preset intervals; secondly, position coordinates of the center point of each control mark on a railway engineering coordinate system are measured; thirdly, laser point cloud data and image data of rails, railway equipment and facilitates (such as annunciators, turnouts and contact net rods), terrain around the railway and the control marks along the railway are obtained, and colors of the image data are assigned to corresponding laser point cloud data; fourthly, the obtained colorized laser point cloud data are subjected to precision refining treatment with the position coordinates of the control marks as reference points, so that errors in the colorized laser point cloud data are eliminated; fifthly, rail surface linear laser point data are extracted from the colorized laser point cloud data subjected to the precision refining treatment; and sixthly, the line type of the existing railway is restored through the rail surface linear laser point data. information of the existing line is accurately obtained under the condition that operation of the railway is not influenced.
rewritten: the invention outlines a method for retesting an existing railway line using onboard laser radar technology. initially, control markers are installed along the railway at predetermined intervals. next, the center point coordinates of each control marker are measured using a railway engineering coordinate system. then, laser point cloud data and image data capturing rails, railway equipment, and associated facilities (such as annunciators, turnouts, and contact net rods), along with the surrounding terrain and control markers, are collected, with the image colors assigned to the corresponding laser point cloud data. afterward, the colorized laser point cloud data is refined using the control markers’ coordinates as reference points, eliminating any errors in the data. subsequently, linear laser point data from the rail surface is extracted from the refined dataset. finally, using this extracted rail surface data, the type of the existing railway line is accurately restored, all while ensuring that railway operations remain unaffected.

raw: the invention relates to a rotary immersed water treatment ultra-micro-filtering method and a rotary immersed water treatment ultra-micro-filtering device. the two ends of each hollow fibrous membrane wire are cast to an upper membrane wire disk and a lower membrane wire disk respectively; the upper membrane wire disk is connected with a water collecting tank; the lower membrane wire disk and a component base plate are fixed; a cavity of each membrane wire is communicated with a cavity of the water collecting tank; the membrane wires and the water collecting tank are integrated to form a membrane component; the water collecting tank is connected with a hollow shaft; the hollow shaft lifts the membrane component to a bearing seat and is communicated with a negative pressure water outlet pipe; a pin gear fixedly arranged on the water collecting tank, a speed reducer, a transmission shaft and a pinion form a driving device and the driving deice can drive the membrane component to rotate forwardly and reversely, so as to enable the membrane wires to be in a movement state; the negative pressure of a system is achieved in a pumping or siphon manner, raw water is filtered by a membrane, and pure water is drained from a negative-pressure water drainage pipe; due to the movement of the membrane component, the membrane wires and water flow move relatively, dirt attached to the surface of the membrane is taken away in time by the scouring force of the water flow, blocking of the surface of the membrane is effectively prevented, and the filter flow does not attenuate.
rewritten: this invention discloses both a method and a device for rotary immersed water treatment using ultra-micro-filtering. in the device, each hollow fiber membrane wire has two ends that are respectively cast into an upper membrane wire disk and a lower membrane wire disk. the upper disk connects to a water collecting tank, while the lower disk is fixed to a component base plate. the cavity inside each membrane wire is in communication with the cavity of the water collecting tank, and together they form an integrated membrane component. the water collecting tank is connected to a hollow shaft, which lifts the membrane component onto a bearing seat. this shaft also links to a negative-pressure water outlet pipe. additionally, a pin gear fixed on the water collecting tank, together with a speed reducer, a transmission shaft, and a pinion, forms a drive mechanism that rotates the membrane component alternately in both forward and reverse directions. this rotation ensures that the membrane wires are in constant motion relative to the water flow. raw water is filtered through the membrane via a pumping or siphon mechanism that generates negative pressure, allowing pure water to be drained through the negative-pressure outlet. the continuous movement of the membrane component generates a scouring effect by the water flow, which promptly removes dirt from the surface of the membranes. this action effectively prevents clogging and ensures that the filtration rate does not diminish over time.

raw: over his career, don delillo grows to inhabit more complex and critical ideas about politics, capitalism, and strategies for opposition than the remote affect of his earlier work might suggest. as part of this project, he developed an advanced analysis of conditions under the global finance regimes gaining strength in the west. the author's papers at the harry ransom center show that he arrived at this informed position through a program of independent research, his newspaper cuttings from this period vividly demonstrating reflections on the meaning of us global power. once back in new york, his investment in public intellectual debates on the meaning of "terrorism" led to a characteristic but problematic emphasis on free speech as a political issue. more recently, his work has turned to sophisticated theoretical texts for increasingly subtle ideas about globalization and its opponents.
rewritten: throughout his career, don delillo evolves to embrace far more intricate and critical perspectives on politics, capitalism, and resistance strategies than his early, more distant work might have implied. as part of this evolution, he developed a sophisticated analysis of the conditions under which the global finance regimes—gaining prominence in the west—operate. documents housed at the harry ransom center reveal that he reached this nuanced stance through independent research, with his newspaper clippings from that period vividly capturing reflections on the nature of u.s. global power. once he returned to new york, his engagement in public intellectual debates on the definition of "terrorism" steered him toward a distinct, though sometimes contentious, emphasis on free speech as a political issue. more recently, his writings have shifted towards elaborate theoretical examinations, offering increasingly refined critiques of globalization and its opponents.

raw: we demonstrate a wireless sensor node (wsn) that operates purely on harvested ambient indoor light energy and regulates its duty cycle via voltage-triggered sensing and transmission. the extremely low-power light found in indoor environments can be considered at first sight as unsuitable for high-power load demands characteristic of radios [1], but by leveraging spectrum-tailored solar cells, trickle charging a high-power energy reservoir, and implementing triggered duty cycling, we show that these power demands can be consistently met. all energy harvesting and storage components are fabricated in our labs.
rewritten: we present a wireless sensor node that solely relies on ambient indoor light for power, adjusting its duty cycle through voltage-triggered sensing and transmission. while the very low power level of indoor lighting might initially seem insufficient for the high-power demands typically associated with radios [1], our approach uses spectrum-optimized solar cells to slowly charge a high-power energy reservoir combined with triggered duty cycling, ensuring that these requirements are reliably fulfilled. all of the energy harvesting and storage components have been fabricated in our laboratory.

raw: the invention discloses a heat radiation apparatus. the heat radiation apparatus is arranged in a mobile terminal device. a vibrator and a pump are connected to a motor through adoption of a clutch part so that the motor can provide driving for the vibrator and a liquid cooling system in a controllable mode, potential functions of the motor are fully tapped, the motor is enabled to provide power for the liquid cooling system at a normal work state, and high-efficiency heat dissipation of the mobile terminal device is realized. the heat radiation apparatus is scientific and reasonable in design and high in practicality.
rewritten: the invention introduces a heat radiation device integrated into a mobile terminal. in this design, a vibrator and a pump are linked to a motor via a clutch mechanism, enabling the motor to drive both the vibrator and the liquid cooling system in a controllable manner. this setup fully harnesses the motor's capabilities, allowing it to power the liquid cooling system during normal operation and thereby achieve efficient heat dissipation for the mobile terminal. overall, the design of this apparatus is both scientifically sound and highly practical.

raw: the invention discloses a visible component classification method based on svm. the method comprises the following steps of in a svm training phase, a, acquiring a sample picture containing several kinds of visible components and taking the sample picture as a training sample; b, segmenting the sample picture into several visible component images; c, extracting an image characteristic of the visible component images, classifying the visible component images based on the image characteristic and constructing several cascaded visible component picture databases; d, constructing several cascaded svm classifiers and using the corresponding visible component picture database to train; in a visible component identification classification phase, e, extracting the image characteristic of the visible component images to be identified and classified; f, based on the image characteristic, assigning the visible component images to the corresponding svm classifiers to carry out identification and classification. the plurality of cascaded svm classifiers are used to carry out multilevel classification on a visible component graph so that classification accuracy of the svm classifiers to the visible components is effectively increased.
rewritten: the disclosed invention presents a method for classifying visible components using svm. the method includes the following steps: during the svm training phase: a. obtain a sample image that includes various types of visible components, using this image as the training sample. b. segment the sample image into multiple visible component images. c. extract image features from these visible component images, use these features to classify them, and then construct several cascaded databases of visible component images. d. build multiple cascaded svm classifiers and train each using the corresponding visible component image database. during the visible component identification and classification phase: e. extract the image features of the visible component images that need to be identified and classified. f. according to the extracted features, assign these images to the appropriate svm classifiers for identification and classification. the series of cascaded svm classifiers performs multilevel classification on a visible component image, thereby effectively enhancing the classification accuracy for the visible components.

raw: backgroundpatients seeking vascularized composite allotransplantation (vca) often had devastating injuries requiring multiple blood transfusions to ensure acute patient survival transfusions carry ...
rewritten: background: patients seeking vascularized composite allotransplantation (vca) have often experienced catastrophic injuries, which required several blood transfusions to secure their immediate survival. these transfusions, however, come with inherent risks...

raw: the invention discloses a power supply system provided with a power supply battery and a super capacitor and a power supply method. the power supply system comprises the power supply battery, a super capacitor, a load and a control system, wherein the power supply battery and the super capacitor are respectively connected to the control system; the power supply battery is connected to the super capacitor; when the system supplies power to the load, the control system allocates the output power of the power supply battery and the super capacitor according to the power demand of the load and the work condition of the super capacitor, and when the system is standby, the control system controls the power supply battery to charge the super capacitor. the super capacitor can be applicable to the system with the high power and large capacity power demand, and by adopting the power supply method for the power supply system provided with the power supply battery and the super capacitor, the power demand for the load under various work conditions can be met.
rewritten: the invention reveals a power supply system and method that incorporate both a battery and a super capacitor. the system consists of a power supply battery, a super capacitor, a load, and a control system. both the battery and the super capacitor are connected to the control system, and the battery is also linked to the super capacitor. when powering the load, the control system allocates the energy from both the battery and the super capacitor based on the load's requirements and the current state of the super capacitor. conversely, when the system is on standby, the control system directs the battery to charge the super capacitor. this design ensures that the system can handle high-power and high-capacity demands, effectively meeting the load's needs under various operating conditions.

raw: gpcrs are the most successful pharmaceutical targets in history. nevertheless, the pharmacology of many gpcrs remains inaccessible as their endogenous or exogenous modulators have not been discovered. tools that explore the physiological functions and pharmacological potential of these 'orphan' gpcrs, whether they are endogenous and/or surrogate ligands, are therefore of paramount importance. rates of receptor deorphanization determined by traditional reverse pharmacology methods have slowed, indicating a need for the development of more sophisticated and efficient ligand screening approaches. here, we discuss the use of structure-based ligand discovery approaches to identify small molecule modulators for exploring the function of orphan gpcrs. these studies have been buoyed by the growing number of gpcr crystal structures solved in the past decade, providing a broad range of template structures for homology modelling of orphans. this review discusses the methods used to establish the appropriate signalling assays to test orphan receptor activity and provides current examples of structure-based methods used to identify ligands of orphan gpcrs. linked articles this article is part of a themed section on molecular pharmacology of g protein-coupled receptors. to view the other articles in this section visit http://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/bph.v173.20/issuetoc.
rewritten: g protein-coupled receptors (gpcrs) have been the most successful targets in pharmaceutical history. however, the pharmacology of many gpcrs remains largely elusive because their natural or external modulators have yet to be identified. therefore, it is crucial to develop tools that can investigate the physiological roles and pharmacological potential of these 'orphan' gpcrs, whether by using their endogenous factors or surrogate ligands. the slowdown in receptor deorphanization using traditional reverse pharmacology methods highlights the need for more refined and efficient ligand screening techniques. in this article, we examine structure-based ligand discovery strategies aimed at finding small molecule modulators to probe the functions of orphan gpcrs. these efforts have been supported by the increasing number of gpcr crystal structures solved over the past decade, which provide a wealth of templates for homology modeling of orphan receptors. this review covers the methods used to develop suitable signaling assays for assessing orphan receptor activity and presents current examples of structure-based approaches for identifying ligands targeting orphan gpcrs. this article is part of a themed section on the molecular pharmacology of g protein-coupled receptors; to view other articles in this series, please visit the provided wiley online library link.

raw: the classical axis of renin-angiotensin system (ras), angiotensin (ang)-converting enzyme (ace)/ang ii/at1, contributes to the development of non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (nafld). however, the role of bypass axis of ras (angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (ace2)/ang-(1-7)/mas) in hepatic steatosis is still unclear. here we showed that deletion of ace2 aggravates liver steatosis, which is correlated with the increased expression of hepatic lipogenic genes and the decreased expression of fatty acid oxidation-related genes in the liver of ace2 knockout (ace2(-/y)) mice. meanwhile, oxidative stress and inflammation were also aggravated in ace2(-/y) mice. on the contrary, overexpression of ace2 improved fatty liver in db/db mice, and the mrna levels of fatty acid oxidation-related genes were up-regulated. in vitro, ang-(1-7)/ace2 ameliorated hepatic steatosis, oxidative stress and inflammation in free fatty acid (ffa)-induced hepg2 cells, and what's more, akt inhibitors reduced ace2-mediated lipid metabolism. furthermore, ace2-mediated akt activation could be attenuated by blockade of atp/p2 receptor/calmodulin (cam) pathway. these results indicated that ang-(1-7)/ace2/mas axis may reduce liver lipid accumulation partly by regulating lipid-metabolizing genes through atp/p2 receptor/cam signaling pathway. our findings support the potential role of ace2/ang-(1-7)/mas axis in prevention and treatment of hepatic lipid metabolism.
rewritten: the classical renin-angiotensin system (ras) axis—comprising the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ace), angiotensin ii, and the at1 receptor—has been shown to play a role in developing non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (nafld). in contrast, the role of the alternative ras axis, which includes angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (ace2), angiotensin-(1-7), and the mas receptor, in hepatic steatosis remains unclear. in our study, we found that deletion of ace2 exacerbates liver steatosis. this worsening condition is accompanied by higher expression of hepatic lipogenic genes and lower expression of genes related to fatty acid oxidation in the livers of ace2 knockout (ace2(–/y)) mice. additionally, these mice exhibited increased oxidative stress and inflammation. on the other hand, overexpressing ace2 improved fatty liver symptoms in db/db mice and was associated with an up-regulation of mrna levels for genes involved in fatty acid oxidation. furthermore, in vitro experiments revealed that the ang-(1-7)/ace2 pathway reduced hepatic steatosis, oxidative stress, and inflammation in hepg2 cells induced by free fatty acids (ffa). interestingly, the beneficial effects of ace2 on lipid metabolism were lessened when akt inhibitors were applied. this suggests that ace2 activates akt, a process that can be dampened by blocking the atp/p2 receptor/calmodulin (cam) pathway. these results indicate that the ang-(1-7)/ace2/mas axis may lessen liver lipid accumulation by partially regulating lipid-metabolizing genes via the atp/p2 receptor/cam signaling pathway. our findings support the potential for targeting the ace2/ang-(1-7)/mas axis in the prevention and treatment of hepatic lipid metabolism disorders.

raw: disclosed herein are methods for treating or preventing a disease comprising administering a pharmaceutical composition comprising a therapeutically-effective amount of nitazoxanide to a subject having the disease, wherein the disease is causally linked to infection by one or more pathogens selected from the group consisting of members of the genus ebolavirus , members of the genus marburgvirus , members of the species dengue virus, human immunodeficiency viruses 1 and 2, and members of the species mycobacterium tuberculosis.
rewritten: the disclosure presents methods for treating or preventing a disease, which involve administering a pharmaceutical composition that includes a therapeutically effective amount of nitazoxanide to a subject afflicted with the disease. in this context, the disease is linked causally to an infection by one or more pathogens selected from the group consisting of members of the genus ebolavirus, members of the genus marburgvirus, the dengue virus, human immunodeficiency viruses 1 and 2, and members of the species mycobacterium tuberculosis.

raw: on the basis of the finite-element method, we propose a mathematical model of frictional heating of a disk in the process of multiple braking. we obtain the numerical solution of the axisymmetric initial boundary-value problem of heat conduction and the boundary-value problem of quasistatic thermoelasticity for a disk periodically heated on the friction surface by a heat flow whose intensity is proportional to the specific power of friction. the numerical analyses of temperature and temperature stresses in the process of multiple braking are performed for a disk made of cast iron and a cermet pad.
rewritten: using the finite-element method as a foundation, we develop a mathematical model that describes the frictional heating of a disk undergoing multiple braking events. we then derive a numerical solution for the axisymmetric initial boundary-value problem of heat conduction, along with the boundary-value problem of quasistatic thermoelasticity for a disk that is periodically heated on its friction surface by a heat flux proportional to the specific friction power. moreover, we conduct numerical analyses of temperature and thermal stresses during repeated braking for a disk composed of cast iron paired with a cermet pad.

raw: this multitrait multimethod twin study examined the structure and sources of individual differences in creativity. according to different theoretical and metrological perspectives, as well as suggestions based on previous research, we expected 2 aspects of individual differences, which can be described as perceived creativity and creative test performance. we hypothesized that perceived creativity, reflecting typical creative thinking and behavior, should be linked to specific personality traits, whereas test creativity, reflecting maximum task-related creative performance, should show specific associations with cognitive abilities. moreover, we tested whether genetic variance in intelligence and personality traits account for the genetic component of creativity. multiple-rater and multimethod data (self- and peer reports, observer ratings, and test scores) from 2 german twin studies-the bielefeld longitudinal study of adult twins and the german observational study of adult twins-were analyzed. confirmatory factor analyses yielded the expected 2 correlated aspects of creativity. perceived creativity showed links to openness to experience and extraversion, whereas tested figural creativity was associated with intelligence and also with openness. multivariate behavioral genetic analyses indicated that the heritability of tested figural creativity could be accounted for by the genetic component of intelligence and openness, whereas a substantial genetic component in perceived creativity could not be explained. a primary source of individual differences in creativity was due to environmental influences, even after controlling for random error and method variance. the findings are discussed in terms of the multifaceted nature and construct validity of creativity as an individual characteristic. (psycinfo database record
rewritten: this twin study employing a multitrait, multimethod approach investigated the structure and origins of differences in creativity among individuals. drawing on diverse theoretical, metrological perspectives, and prior research, we anticipated finding two distinct facets: perceived creativity and creative test performance. we proposed that perceived creativity, which reflects everyday creative thinking and behavior, would be linked to certain personality traits, while creative test performance, which measures peak task-specific creative ability, would be tied to cognitive capacities. additionally, we examined whether genetic variations in intelligence and personality traits could explain the genetic influences on creativity. we analyzed data drawn from multiple sources (self-reports, peer ratings, observer evaluations, and test scores) across two german twin studies—the bielefeld longitudinal study of adult twins and the german observational study of adult twins. confirmatory factor analysis supported our expectation of two correlated dimensions of creativity. specifically, perceived creativity was related to openness to experience and extraversion, whereas tested figural creativity was linked to both intelligence and openness. further multivariate behavioral genetic analyses revealed that the heritability of tested figural creativity could be largely attributed to the genetic influences tied to intelligence and openness, while a significant portion of the genetic component underlying perceived creativity remained unexplained. overall, environmental factors emerged as a major source of individual differences in creativity, even after accounting for measurement error and method variance. these findings are discussed in relation to the multifaceted nature of creativity and its construct validity as an individual characteristic.

raw: the invention relates to a diagnosis and treatment table, in particular to a tcm (traditional chinese medicine) diagnosis and treatment table. the tcm diagnosis and treatment table comprises a table top, and is characterized in that a groove is formed in the table top, a thermostat water bath kettle is placed in the groove, the upper surface of a water tank of the thermostat water bath kettle is downwards provided with a concave part, the concave part is provided with a plurality of through holes, the concave part is internally provided with a pulse-feeling pillow made of a flexible waterproof material, a flexible material or liquid is contained in the pulse-feeling pillow, the upper surface is hinged with a kettle cover, and a gap used for accommodating the wrist of a patient and being convenient for a doctor to take the pulse is reserved on the kettle cover, and a thermal insulation material is arranged in the kettle cover. a medicine bag is arranged on the edge of the groove, and an atomization spray head is arranged on the medicine bag. the side of the groove in the table top, facing the patient, is provided with an arm support. the diagnosis and treatment table is convenient, clean and sanitary to use, the cross infection can be avoided, and the patient feels comfortable.
rewritten: the invention relates to a diagnosis and treatment table, specifically designed for traditional chinese medicine (tcm) applications. this tcm diagnosis and treatment table features a table top with an integrated groove designed to house a thermostat water bath kettle. the upper water tank surface of the kettle includes a concave section equipped with multiple through holes. inside this concave section, there is a pulse-sensing pillow constructed from a flexible, waterproof material that encloses either a flexible substance or a liquid. a kettle cover, which is hinged on the upper surface, incorporates a gap intended to accommodate a patient’s wrist for convenient pulse taking by a doctor, and it is lined with thermal insulation material. additionally, a medicine bag is mounted along the edge of the groove, with an atomization spray head attached to it. an arm support is provided on the patient-facing side of the groove in the table top. this design ensures that the diagnosis and treatment table is convenient and sanitary to use, prevents cross-infection, and enhances patient comfort.

raw: the utility model provides an edible fungus preserves uses intelligent drying -machine, includes stoving case, resistance wire, stoving case the inside is provided with the heat preservation, the heat preservation side is provided with the thermometer, stoving case the inside is provided with the charging tray frame, be provided with the material dish on the charging tray frame, be provided with the light above the charging tray frame, the light next door is provided with the draught fan, be provided with the motor on the draught fan, stoving case next door is provided with the heating cabinet, the heating cabinet the inside is provided with the temperature controller, be provided with the drain pipe below the temperature controller, be provided with above the temperature controller the resistance wire, the heating cabinet surface is provided with control panel, stoving case side is provided with the intake pipe, the intake pipe next door is provided with the blast pipe. through circulating hot air in inclosed environment, the energy resource consumption has been reduced for drying efficiency.
rewritten: the utility model describes an intelligent drying machine designed for preserving edible fungi. this apparatus includes a stoving case that houses a resistance wire and an internal heat preservation unit, which is monitored by a thermometer mounted on its side. within the stoving case is a charging tray frame supporting a material dish, and above this tray, a light is installed. adjacent to the light, a draught fan—with an attached motor—is positioned. additionally, a heating cabinet is provided next to the stoving case; it contains a temperature controller, with a drain pipe located beneath the controller and a resistance wire installed above it. the surface of the heating cabinet features a control panel. furthermore, an intake pipe is arranged on the side of the stoving case, next to a blast pipe. this design circulates hot air within a confined space, thereby reducing energy consumption and enhancing the drying efficiency.

raw: disclosed are a small flying vehicle without a camera and a moving method of the small flying vehicle, capable of preventing collision when a small flying vehicle takes off, lands, and flies in case of transporting objects. the small flying vehicle flies to a first descending point positioned in a vertical sky of a landing point in a flying altitude. thereafter, the small flying vehicle, while lowering to the landing point from the first descending point, determines whether an external object is sensed through a distance sensor. when an external object is sensed, the small flying vehicle returns to the first descending point and returns a path to a second descending point away by a predetermined distance from the first descending point in the flying altitude. the small flying vehicle then vertically descends from the second descending point to move to a point having the same altitude as that of the landing point. thereafter, the small flying vehicle moves to the landing point from the point having the same altitude as that of the landing point.
rewritten: the disclosure relates to a small flying vehicle without a camera and a method for its movement that prevents collisions during takeoff, landing, and flight when transporting objects. the process begins with the vehicle flying to a first descending point positioned vertically above the landing area at a specified altitude. as it descends from the first descending point toward the landing zone, a distance sensor detects if any external object is present. should an object be detected, the vehicle returns to the first descending point and then follows a path to a second descending point, which is located at a predetermined distance from the first descending point within the same flight altitude. from this second point, the vehicle descends vertically until it reaches an altitude matching that of the landing area, after which it proceeds horizontally to the landing point.

raw: abstract : computer network defense (cnd) analysts serve an increasingly vital role in the defense of our nations computing infrastructure. an important component of their work is the monitoring of suspicious activity identified by an intrusion detection system (ids). while analysts are trained to quickly recognize abnormal patterns in textual log files, humans are generally not well suited for such processing in any large quantity. many authors have proposed the use of visualization techniques to aid the cyber security analysts search activities; however, such techniques are not widely used by analysts. this report describes an evaluation of 2 graphical displays (a parallel coordinates display and a node-link display) compared against a traditional tabular arrangement with the goal of better understanding analyst performance and obtaining subjective feedback on the graphical alternatives. both expert analysts and novices (students) participated in the study. results show that analysts generally preferred familiar tools but were able to use some graphical alternatives (node-link) to achieve similar performance in less time. students were not found to be effective surrogates for experienced analysts for research/validation of techniques. this report describes the development and design of the displays and the experiment, and provides insight into analyst needs and evidence on effective methods for validating cyber defense visualization tools based on results obtained.
rewritten: abstract: computer network defense (cnd) analysts are playing an ever more crucial role in safeguarding our nation's computing infrastructure. a key aspect of their responsibility involves monitoring suspicious activities flagged by an intrusion detection system (ids). although analysts are trained to swiftly detect anomalies in textual log files, human capabilities are limited when it comes to processing large volumes of such data. while many researchers have suggested using visualization techniques to support cybersecurity analysts’ investigative tasks, these methods have yet to gain widespread adoption. this report presents an evaluation of two graphical displays—a parallel coordinates display and a node-link display—compared with a conventional tabular format. the goal is to better understand analyst performance and gather subjective feedback on these graphical alternatives. both experienced analysts and novices (students) took part in the study. the findings indicate that while analysts generally favored familiar tools, they could use certain graphical alternatives (specifically the node-link display) to achieve comparable performance in a shorter time. additionally, the study revealed that students do not serve as effective substitutes for seasoned analysts when researching or validating techniques. this report outlines the development and design of the displays and the experiment, offering insight into analysts' requirements and evidence on effective methods for validating cybersecurity visualization tools.

raw: the invention relates to a seawater bacteria detection system based on multi-wavelength laser inducing bacteria inherent fluorescence. the system includes an outer shell, a first convex lens, a multi-wavelength laser excitation device, an optical fiber body, a fluorescent spectrum instrument, and a data process and analysis system. the first convex lens is arranged in the outer shell and divides the outer shell into a seawater chamber and a light collection chamber. a window glass is arranged on an inner wall, except the position that the first convex lens is arranged, of the seawater chamber. the multi-wavelength laser excitation device is disposed between the window glass and the outer shell. a water inlet and a water outlet are arranged on the outer shell and are communicated with the seawater chamber. the light collection chamber is provided with a light collection port. the centers of the first convex lens, the light collection port and the optical fiber body are located in the same straight line. the optical fiber body receives a fluorescent signal from the light collection port. the data process and analysis system is used for processing and analyzing the fluorescent signal that is sent from the optical fiber body and is collected by the fluorescent spectrum instrument. the system can be used for on-situ detection of abundance and diversity of bacteria in seawater in ocean, is effectively improved in detection efficiency and is convenient and quick to use.
rewritten: the invention discloses a system for detecting seawater bacteria using inherent bacterial fluorescence induced by multi-wavelength lasers. the system comprises an outer shell, a primary convex lens, a multi-wavelength laser excitation device, an optical fiber assembly, a fluorescence spectrometer, and a data processing and analysis module. the primary convex lens is positioned within the outer shell, which is thereby divided into a seawater chamber and a light collection chamber. within the seawater chamber, a window glass is mounted on an inner wall, excluding the area occupied by the convex lens. the multi-wavelength laser excitation device is installed between the window glass and the outer shell. the outer shell also features a water inlet and a water outlet that communicate with the seawater chamber. the light collection chamber contains an opening for light collection such that the centers of the primary convex lens, the light collection port, and the optical fiber assembly align on a single straight line. the optical fiber assembly then captures the fluorescence signal from the light collection port. this signal is transmitted to the data processing and analysis module after being gathered by the fluorescence spectrometer. the system is designed for on-site detection of bacterial abundance and diversity in seawater, improving detection efficiency and offering a convenient and rapid method for analysis in marine environments.

raw: in this paper, firstly, we introduce the q_{l}-generating matrix for the bi-periodic lucas numbers. then, by taking into account this matrix representation, we obtain some properties for the bi-periodic fibonacci and lucas numbers.
rewritten: in this paper, we first present the qₗ-generating matrix for the bi-periodic lucas numbers. then, using this matrix representation, we derive several properties of the bi-periodic fibonacci and lucas numbers.

raw: the utility model relates to a horizontal processing frock of foundry goods, its include flat base, vertical fixation at the stand of base right -hand member, fix at the crossbeam on stand top and set up at the tight subassembly in the top of base left end, the crossbeam right -hand member is fixed on the stand top, and the left end hangs through two lift bolts and sets up a last briquetting, and the lower terminal surface middle part of going up the briquetting is provided with arched recess, and the base middle part is provided with the lower cushion of rectangle, and lower cushion lies in under the last briquetting, the tight subassembly in top includes reference column and the ejector pin of suit on the reference column that the vertical fixation set up, and the reference column top is provided with the gland nut who is used for injecing the ejector pin, and the left side vertical fixation of reference column is provided with rear support column, and the rear support column top is provided with the support nut that is used for the support top rod afterbody. the utility model discloses simple structure, simple manufacture is with low costs, and convenient to use has fine centre gripping fixed action to the part to can not cause the damage to the foundry goods, the convenience is processed the foundry goods simultaneously.
rewritten: the utility model relates to a horizontal processing frock for foundry goods. it comprises a flat base; a vertical fixing mechanism on the right-hand member of the base; a crossbeam mounted at the top of the stand; and a secured assembly positioned at the top of the base’s left end. in detail, the right-hand part of the crossbeam is fixed on the stand top, while its left end is suspended by two lift bolts to support a final briquetting. this briquetting has an arched recess in the middle of its lower terminal surface, and a rectangular lower cushion is provided in the center of the base below the final briquetting. the top secured assembly includes a reference column with an ejector pin fitted into it vertically. the top of the reference column features a gland nut which is used to drive the ejector pin. additionally, a rear support column is attached to the left side of the reference column, with a support nut at its top intended to support the end of the top rod. this design is simple in structure, easy to manufacture at low cost, and convenient to use. it also offers secure, fine center gripping of the parts without damaging the foundry goods, all while allowing simultaneous processing of the goods.

raw: social media analytics play a major role in e-commerce for extracting the useful information of a product or service. opinion mining has become the key process of social media analytics. twitter is a big online social activity platform where millions of people share their opinions. in this paper two clustering techniques, k-means and dbscan, are applied to an annotated twitter dataset in order to evaluate use of clustering for detecting different types of sentiment. results are very encouraging for dbscan but less useful for k-means.
rewritten: social media analytics are crucial in e-commerce for extracting valuable insights about products or services. opinion mining, a key aspect of these analytics, helps reveal consumer sentiments. twitter, a vast online platform where millions share their views, serves as an important source of such data. in this study, we apply two clustering methods—k-means and dbscan—to an annotated twitter dataset to assess their ability to detect different sentiment types. the findings are highly promising for dbscan, while k-means shows less favorable results.

raw: currently contact probes are widely used to determine the vital signs. as the medical technology develops, non-invasive method becomes more feasible. we are using a new non-contact method of determining pulse rate (pr) commonly known as video plethysmography technique. in this method, face video is captured using a web camera and from the captured video frames, photoplethysmogram (ppg) signals are obtained from different regions of the face based on the pixel intensity variation. this ppg signals are preprocessed by filtering and noise compensated using dynamic weight factoring based on power spectral density and from this finally obtained ppg signal, we are determining the pulse rate by converting it into fast fourier transform (fft). in this paper, we present an algorithm for pulse rate determination which can be easily implemented on portable device or on standard pc. this method of noncontact detection and monitoring of cardiac pulse is more useful in both hospitals and telemedicine.
rewritten: today, contact probes are commonly used to measure vital signs. however, as medical technology advances, non-invasive approaches are becoming increasingly viable. we are employing a novel, non-contact method for determining pulse rate (pr), known as the video plethysmography technique. in this approach, a face video is captured using a webcam, and photoplethysmogram (ppg) signals are extracted from various regions of the face based on pixel intensity fluctuations. these ppg signals are then preprocessed by filtering and are further refined through noise compensation using dynamic weight factoring based on power spectral density. finally, the pulse rate is determined by applying a fast fourier transform (fft) to the processed signal. in this paper, we introduce an algorithm for pulse rate determination that can be easily implemented on a portable device or a standard pc. this method of non-contact cardiac pulse detection and monitoring is beneficial in both hospital environments and telemedicine applications.

raw: human hepatocellular carcinoma (hcc) is one of the most common types of cancer and has a very poor prognosis; thus, the development of effective therapies for the treatment of advanced hcc is of high clinical priority. in the present study, the anti-oncogenic effect of combined knockdown of c-myc expression and ectopic restoration of deleted in liver cancer 1 (dlc1) expression was investigated in human liver cancer cells. expression of c-myc in human hcc cells was knocked down by stable transfection with a myc-specific short hairpin (sh) rna vector. dlc1 expression in huh7 cells was restored by adenovirus transduction, and the effects of dlc1 expression and c-myc knockdown on ras homolog gene family, member a (rhoa) levels, cell proliferation, soft agar colony formation and cell invasion were measured. downregulation of c-myc or re-expression of dlc1 led to a marked reduction in rhoa levels, which was associated with decreases in cell proliferation, soft agar colony formation and invasiveness; this inhibitory effect was augmented with a combination of dlc1 transduction and c-myc suppression. to determine whether liver cell-specific delivery of dlc1 was able to enhance the inhibitory effect of c-myc knockdown on tumor growth in vivo, dlc1 vector dna complexed with galactosylated polyethylene glycol-linear polyethyleneimine was administered by tail vein injection to mice bearing subcutaneous xenografts of huh7 cells transfected with shmyc or control shrna. a cooperative inhibitory effect of dlc1 expression and c-myc knockdown on the growth of huh7-derived tumors was observed, suggesting that targeted liver cell delivery of dlc1 and c-myc shrna may serve as a possible gene therapy modality for the treatment of human hcc.
rewritten: human hepatocellular carcinoma (hcc) is one of the most prevalent cancers and is associated with a very poor prognosis, making the development of effective treatments for advanced hcc a high clinical priority. in this study, we examined the anti-cancer effects of simultaneously reducing c-myc expression and restoring the expression of deleted in liver cancer 1 (dlc1) in human liver cancer cells. we achieved c-myc knockdown in human hcc cells by stably transfecting them with a myc-specific short hairpin rna (shrna) vector. in huh7 cells, dlc1 expression was reinstated via adenovirus transduction. we then assessed how dlc1 expression and c-myc suppression affected levels of ras homolog gene family, member a (rhoa), as well as cell proliferation, soft agar colony formation, and cell invasiveness. both downregulation of c-myc and re-expression of dlc1 led to a significant decrease in rhoa levels, accompanied by reductions in cell proliferation, colony formation in soft agar, and invasiveness; notably, the combined treatment of dlc1 transduction and c-myc knockdown produced an enhanced inhibitory effect. to determine whether targeted delivery of dlc1 to liver cells could further potentiate the tumor growth inhibition seen with c-myc knockdown in vivo, we administered dlc1 vector dna—complexed with galactosylated polyethylene glycol-linear polyethyleneimine—via tail vein injection into mice bearing subcutaneous xenografts of huh7 cells that had been transfected with either shmyc or a control shrna. the results revealed that the combination of dlc1 expression and c-myc knockdown cooperatively inhibited the growth of huh7-derived tumors, suggesting that targeted liver cell delivery of dlc1 along with c-myc shrna may represent a promising gene therapy approach for treating human hcc.

raw: problem to be solved: to allow a quantum dot nanowire having a high-quality superlattice structure which can be used for a light emitting element to be formed.solution: a quantum dot nanowire manufacturing method comprises a step of forming a lower nanowire part 103 on a substrate 101 where fine particles 102 are arranged. the lower nanowire part 103 has a superlattice structure in which first crystal parts 131 and second crystal parts 132 are alternately laminated one by one. in this case, a first material gas group and a second material gas group which are composed of organic metal are alternately supplied, and the first crystal parts 131 each composed of a frist compound semiconductor obtained by crystallization of the first material gas group and the second material gas group by using the fine particles 102 as a catalyst, and the second crystal parts 132 each composed of a second compound semiconductor obtained by crystallization of the first material gas group and the second material gas group by using the fine particles 102 as a catalyst are alternately laminated one by one. in growth of the second crystal part 132, a material gas of the second material gas group, which is not included in the first material gas group is supplied for one second and under in a pulsed manner.selected drawing: figure 1b
rewritten: challenge: enable the formation of a quantum dot nanowire with a high-quality superlattice structure that can serve as a light-emitting component. solution: a method for manufacturing the quantum dot nanowire involves first creating a lower nanowire section (103) on a substrate (101) where fine particles (102) have been arranged. this lower section (103) features a superlattice structure in which first crystal parts (131) and second crystal parts (132) are alternately stacked. in the process, a first material gas group and a second material gas group—both comprising organic metals—are alternately introduced. each first crystal part (131) consists of a first compound semiconductor formed by crystallizing the first and second material gas groups using the fine particles (102) as catalysts. correspondingly, each second crystal part (132) is made up of a second compound semiconductor produced through a similar crystallization process, again utilizing the fine particles (102) as catalysts, resulting in an alternating layered structure. notably, during the growth of the second crystal part (132), a material gas from the second material gas group that does not include the first material gas group is supplied in a pulsed manner for a duration of one second. (reference: figure 1b)

raw: within this book chapter, technologies for data mining, data processing and data interpreting are introduced, evaluated and compared. especially, traditional high performance computing, and the newly emerging fields high performance data analytics and cognitive computing are put into context in order to understand their strengths and weaknesses. however, the technologies have not been evaluated solely, but also the missing links between them have been identified and described.
rewritten: this chapter presents, assesses, and contrasts various technologies involved in data mining, processing, and interpretation. it focuses particularly on traditional high performance computing alongside the emerging fields of high performance data analytics and cognitive computing, examining both their strengths and limitations. moreover, the chapter not only evaluates these technologies individually but also identifies and discusses the gaps that exist between them.

raw: background: garlic and zataria multiflora boiss (zmb) are herbal medicines used traditionally in the treatment of infections, including candidal vaginitis. objectives: this study aimed to examine the effect of garlic and zmb vaginal cream 2%, in the treatment and recurrence of candidal vaginitis. methods: this randomized, double-blinded clinical trial was conducted on 240 married women with candidal vaginitis. the patients used 5 gr garlic, zmb or clotrimazole vaginal cream 2% daily, for 7 days. complaints of the patients, clinical observations and laboratory parameters were recorded before treatment, on days 7 and 30, after treatment. the data were analyzed by spss v. 21 through chi-square, fisher, anova with repeated measures, mcnemar and kruskal-wallis tests. a p 0.05). mean scores of candidal symptoms reduced significantly in all groups at on 7 and 30, after treatment (p < 0.001). conclusions: garlic and zmb vaginal cream 2% can be used as an effective treatment option in cases of drug resistance and, also, by the people who are interested in treating with herbal medicines.
rewritten: background: garlic and zataria multiflora boiss (zmb) have long been used as herbal treatments for infections, including candidal vaginitis. objective: this study aimed to assess the effect of 2% garlic and 2% zmb vaginal creams on the treatment and recurrence of candidal vaginitis. methods: in this randomized, double-blind clinical trial, 240 married women with candidal vaginitis were recruited. they applied 5 g of either garlic, zmb, or clotrimazole 2% vaginal cream daily for 7 days. patient complaints, clinical observations, and laboratory results were recorded before treatment, as well as on days 7 and 30 after treatment. data were analyzed using spss version 21 with chi-square, fisher, repeated measures anova, mcnemar, and kruskal-wallis tests, considering a p-value of <0.05 as significant. mean candidal symptom scores significantly decreased in all groups at both 7 and 30 days after treatment (p < 0.001). conclusion: the results indicate that 2% garlic and zmb vaginal creams can be effective treatment options, particularly for patients with drug-resistant cases or those preferring herbal therapies.

raw: problem to be solved: to efficiently reconfigurate the needed number of logical configuration data to wiring of an internal interface signal between a previously secured logical block and a logical block in accordance with a job.solution: an information processing device for executing job processing determines whether the number of logical blocks that are not used in first storage means is larger than the number of logical configuration data associated with a job that is reconfigured in the logical block in accordance with the request of the job. here, in the case that the number of logical blocks that are not used in the first storage means is larger than the number of logical configuration data associated with the job that is reconfigured in the logical block, and that the unused blocks exist at positions close to each other, a cpu 101 instructs a configuration controller 130 to reconfigurate the logical configuration data corresponding to the requested job.selected drawing: figure 5
rewritten: issue at hand: the goal is to efficiently reassign the required logical configuration data to the wiring of an internal interface signal that connects a previously secured logical block with another logical block as specified by a task. solution: an information processing device, while executing task processing, first verifies if the number of logical blocks that are unused in the primary storage exceeds the number of logical configuration data items required for reconfiguring the logical block as per the job request. if there are indeed more available logical blocks than needed, and these unused blocks are located near each other, then the cpu (101) directs the configuration controller (130) to reassign the logical configuration data corresponding to the requested task. illustration: refer to figure 5.
