raw: the invention discloses a wireless charging led cosmetic mirror which comprises a base and a cosmetic mirror, and also comprises a power box. an led lamp is arranged at the periphery of the cosmetic mirror; the upper end of the power box is fixedly connected with the cosmetic mirror; a lithium battery is arranged inside the power box and is a power supply of the led lamp; the lower end of the power box is in plug connection with a slot at the upper part of the base; a charging jack is formed in one side of the base and is matched with a charger; and when the lower end of the power box is inserted into the slot, the lithium battery inside the power box is charged by the charger through the charging jack in the base. the cosmetic mirror disclosed by the invention can be arranged on the base to be used and also can be unplugged from the base to be singly used, is not limited by distance and is very convenient to use, the power box is inserted into the slot while charging, trouble brought by the operation of placing a transformer inside or outside the cosmetic mirror is avoided, the led can be adjusted by a button, illumination of different brightness is realized, and the wireless charging led cosmetic mirror can adapt to environments of different light intensities.
rewritten: the invention discloses a wireless charging led cosmetic mirror that includes a base, a mirror, and a power box. an led lamp is positioned around the edge of the cosmetic mirror, and the mirror is securely attached to the top of the power box. inside the power box, there is a lithium battery that powers the led lamp. the bottom of the power box plugs into a slot located at the top of the base. additionally, a charging jack on one side of the base connects to a charger. when the power box is inserted into the slot, the lithium battery is charged through the charging jack. this design allows the cosmetic mirror to be used either while attached to the base or on its own, providing convenient, wireless operation without distance limitations. by integrating the charging mechanism into the base, the need to position a transformer inside or outside the cosmetic mirror is eliminated. furthermore, a button enables adjustment of the led brightness to suit different lighting conditions.

raw: problem to be solved: to provide a monitoring and reporting system for reporting the monitoring information quickly to a terminal not having a dialup function of a smart phone, or the like, by using the public line, even if a place where a monitoring and reporting terminal is installed has such line conditions that only the public line is available, in the monitoring and reporting system in a field of water and sewerage apparatus management device.solution: in a monitoring and reporting system using a public line, monitoring information is provided by installing an application for performing data transmission by audio signals. furthermore, quick reporting to a reporting destination is implemented, by preparing a function for sharing the monitoring information between cellular phones or smart phones.selected drawing: figure 1
rewritten: problem: develop a monitoring and reporting system that can quickly relay monitoring data over a public telephone line to a terminal lacking a dial-up function—such as a smartphone—particularly in locations where only public lines are accessible, like in areas equipped with water and sewerage management apparatus. solution: in a system that utilizes a public line, an application is installed to transmit monitoring data using audio signals. additionally, the system is designed to rapidly forward reports by incorporating a feature that shares monitoring information between cellular phones or smartphones. selected drawing: figure 1

raw: problem to be solved: to provide an information processing device capable of reducing burdens on a person in charge engaged in a contract renewal job of terminal contract laborers, and to provide an information processing method, and a program.solution: the information processing device according to one embodiment of this invention includes an acquisition unit, a determination unit, and a generation unit. the acquisition unit acquires a contract history of a prescribed terminal employee corresponding to a terminal contract laborer among a plurality of employees from a database storing the contract information of each of the plurality of employees. the determination unit calculates the total contract period of the prescribed terminal employee on the basis of the acquired contract history, and determines whether the calculated total contract period exceeds a prescribed threshold. the generation unit generates a contract period image including at least the calculated total contract period on the basis of a result of the determination by the determination unit.selected drawing: figure 19
rewritten: problem to be solved: to develop an information processing device that alleviates the workload of personnel handling contract renewals for terminal contract laborers; additionally, to provide an information processing method and a corresponding program. solution: in one embodiment of this invention, the information processing device comprises an acquisition unit, a determination unit, and a generation unit. the acquisition unit retrieves the contract history of a specific terminal employee (from among multiple employees) from a database that stores the contract details of each employee. the determination unit then calculates the total duration of the contract for that terminal employee based on the acquired history and determines whether this total period exceeds a predetermined threshold. following this, the generation unit creates an image depicting the contract period, which includes at least the computed total duration, based on the determination unit’s results. selected drawing: figure 19

raw: the utility model provides an intelligent mobile robot with anticollision function, its anticollision mechanism are located the robot's front side, and it includes crashproof backplate, two guidance axiss and the elastomeric element who sets up with the guidance axis one -to -one, the anticollision backplate transversely sets up, and thereafter about the side both ends articulate respectively in the front end of two guidance axiss, insert the rear end slidable of guidance axis and establish inside the robot, and the rear end tip has and prevents that the guidance axis breaks away from the robot's spacing portion, the elastomeric element cover is established on its corresponding guidance axis and is had the pretightning force, the inside limit switch that is equipped with of the robot, limit switch is connected with intelligent mobile robot's the control unit electricity. the utility model discloses simple structure, the anticollision function is reliable, and is applicable to any load conditions's intelligent mobile robot homoenergetic.
rewritten: the utility model presents an intelligent mobile robot equipped with an anticollision function. this mechanism is installed on the front side of the robot and includes a crash-resistant backplate, two guidance axes, and elastomeric elements that correspond one-to-one with each guidance axis. the crashproof backplate is arranged transversely, with both of its ends connected to the front ends of the two guidance axes. the rear ends of the guidance axes are inserted in a slidable manner into the robot, and each rear tip is designed to prevent the guidance axis from detaching from the spacing portion of the robot. additionally, each guidance axis is covered by an elastomeric element that is preloaded with a tightening force. an internal limit switch is also included, which is electrically connected to the robot’s control unit. the disclosed utility model features a simple structure, delivers a reliable anticollision function, and is suitable for intelligent mobile robots under various load conditions.

raw: lidar datasets now commonly reach billions of points and are very dense. using these point cloud becomes challenging, as the high number of points is intractable for most applications and for this http url this work we propose a new paradigm to easily get a portable geometric level of details (lod) inside a point cloud server.the main idea is to not store the lod information in an external additional file, but instead to store it implicitly by exploiting the order of the points.the point cloud is divided into groups (patches). these patches are ordered so that their order gradually provides more and more details on the patch. we demonstrate the interest of our method with several classical uses of lod, such as visualisation of massive point cloud, algorithm acceleration, fast density peak detection and correction.
rewritten: modern lidar datasets often contain billions of points with very high density, making them difficult to handle since the sheer volume of data can overwhelm most applications. in this work (available at this http url), we introduce a novel approach to easily obtain a portable geometric level of detail (lod) within a point cloud server. our key idea is to avoid keeping lod information in a separate external file; instead, we implicitly encode it by leveraging the order of the points. specifically, the point cloud is segmented into groups, or patches, which are then arranged in such a way that their sequence progressively reveals increasing detail about each patch. we illustrate the advantages of our method across several common lod applications, including visualization of massive point clouds, acceleration of algorithms, rapid detection of density peaks, and their subsequent correction.

raw: the present invention relates to biomass and polysaccharide accumulation through spirulina culture, specifically to spirulina culture using the control of the addition of a nutritional salt so as to concurrently accumulate biomass and polysaccharides. according to the present invention, spirulina cells cultured to achieve a exponential growth phase are transferred into a nutrition limiting culture medium, natural illumination or artificial illumination is performed, culture is performed to achieve a stable phase, and the spirulina cells are harvested, wherein the biomass yield is 1-4 times the biomass yield of the culture under the rich nutrition condition, the polysaccharides yield is increased by 0.8-20 times compared to the culture under the rich nutrition condition, and the polysaccharide content achieves 45-80% of the spirulina dry weight, and is increased by 2-6 times compared to the culture under the rich nutrition condition; and the contradiction that the spirulina biomass and the polysaccharide cannot be concurrently accumulated is solved, the rapid and efficient spirulina polysaccharide production is achieved, the advantages of low nutrition salt consumption, low production cost, high polysaccharide content and the like are provided, the downstream processing operations are easily simplified, and the industrial production of the spirulina polysaccharide can be promoted.
rewritten: this invention concerns a method for accumulating both biomass and polysaccharides from spirulina culture by controlling the addition of a nutritional salt. in this process, spirulina cells are first cultured to reach an exponential growth phase and then transferred to a nutrient-limited medium. under either natural or artificial illumination, the culture is allowed to progress until it reaches a stable phase, after which the spirulina cells are harvested. as a result, the biomass yield is 1 to 4 times higher than that achieved under nutrient-rich conditions, the polysaccharide yield increases by 0.8 to 20 times compared to nutrient-rich cultures, and the polysaccharide content accounts for 45 to 80% of the spirulina's dry weight—representing a 2- to 6-fold increase over cultures grown in nutrient-rich environments. this innovative approach overcomes the previously conflicting challenge of simultaneously accumulating spirulina biomass and polysaccharides. it enables rapid and efficient production of spirulina polysaccharides, while also offering advantages such as reduced nutritional salt consumption, lower production costs, higher polysaccharide content, simplified downstream processing, and enhanced feasibility for industrial-scale production.

raw: problem to be solved: to make it easy for a user to confirm an image of a local portion or the like which the user particularly wants to watch.solution: an image sensor 11 generates an image signal, and a control unit 14 generates a set value used to set a range for cutting an image based on the image signal on the basis of a first instruction input from a user. the generated set value is stored in a set value storage unit 15. further, if a second instruction is input from the user, the set value is read out from the set value storage unit 15 such that an image conversion unit 13 cuts out a region specified by the set value from the image and enlarges the same. then, an output unit 18 converts the image signal cut out and enlarged by the image conversion unit 13 into an image signal in a predetermined format to output the converted image signal.selected drawing: figure 1
rewritten: problem to be solved: how can a user easily verify a specific local area or similar portion they want to view? solution: an image sensor (11) captures an image signal. based on this signal and a first instruction from the user, a control unit (14) generates a set value that defines the range for cropping the image. this set value is then stored in a set value storage unit (15). when a second instruction is received from the user, the set value is retrieved from the storage unit (15), allowing an image conversion unit (13) to crop the specified region from the image and enlarge it. finally, an output unit (18) converts the cropped and enlarged image into a predetermined format for display. selected drawing: figure 1

raw: the invention relates to a simple synthetic method for dicyclopentadiene ethoxy methacrylate. the synthetic method is characterized in that in the presence of an appropriate catalyst and polymerization inhibitor, addition reaction of hydroxyethyl methacrylate and dicyclopentadiene is used for synthesizing dicyclopentadiene ethoxy methacrylate. the catalyst is strong acid or super acid and includes boron trifluoride diethyl etherate, chlorosulfonic acid, fluorosulfuric acid, trifluoro sulfoacid, polyphosphoric acid, phosphotungstic acid and silicotungstic acid. the polymerization inhibitor is one of or a combination of phenothiazine, hydroquinone, p-benzoquinone, p-hydroxyanisole, 2-tert-butyl-hydroquinone, 2,5-di-tert-butylhydroquinone, dibutyl dithiocarbamic acid and 2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-hydroxy-piperidine nitroxide free radical, wherein the combination is formed by two components mentioned above.
rewritten: this invention discloses a straightforward method for synthesizing dicyclopentadiene ethoxy methacrylate. the process involves an addition reaction between hydroxyethyl methacrylate and dicyclopentadiene, performed in the presence of a suitable catalyst and a polymerization inhibitor. the catalyst used is a strong or super acid, which may include agents such as boron trifluoride diethyl etherate, chlorosulfonic acid, fluorosulfuric acid, trifluorosulfoacid, polyphosphoric acid, phosphotungstic acid, or silicotungstic acid. the polymerization inhibitor is either a single component or a combination of components chosen from phenothiazine, hydroquinone, p-benzoquinone, p-hydroxyanisole, 2-tert-butyl-hydroquinone, 2,5-di-tert-butylhydroquinone, dibutyl dithiocarbamic acid, and a 2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-4-hydroxy-piperidine nitroxide free radical, with any combination being comprised of two of these agents.

raw: tuberculous ulcer of the anterior chest wall as a result of direct extension from underlying pleural and pulmonary tuberculosis is a very rare entity. its clinical presentation may resemble a tumor or abscess. isolated chest wall tuberculous ulcer without bone involvement is even rarer. an illustrated case report is presented.
rewritten: tuberculous ulcers on the anterior chest wall, resulting from the direct spread of pleural and pulmonary tuberculosis, are extremely uncommon. they can present clinically in a way that resembles a tumor or an abscess. even more rare is the occurrence of an isolated chest wall tuberculous ulcer without any involvement of the bone. this report includes an illustrated case example.

raw: the present invention relates to lactic acid bacteria having effects of lowering the cholesterol level of antimicrobial substances and a host, and to a probiotic composition containing the same. a pediococcus pentosaceus kid7 strain (pediococcus pentosaceus kid7, kctc no. 18308p) derived from naturally fermented finger millet (eleusine coracana) porridge is separated and is identified in the present invention. in the present invention, provided are various intake methods of the probiotic composition using the same. the composition through the intake reduces 20% of total cholesterol in blood in the case of being orally administered to hyperlipidemia-induced mouse, and reduces ldl-cholesterol in blood by 30%, thereby being able to be used in preventing and treating hyperlipidemia and preventing and treating bowel diseases by preventing harmful bacteria.
rewritten: this invention concerns lactic acid bacteria that both lower cholesterol levels and act as antimicrobial agents in a host, as well as a probiotic composition containing these bacteria. more specifically, a particular strain, pediococcus pentosaceus kid7 (kctc no. 18308p), which is isolated from naturally fermented finger millet (eleusine coracana) porridge, is identified herein. the invention also outlines various methods for ingesting the probiotic composition that includes this strain. when orally administered to mice with hyperlipidemia, the composition reduces total blood cholesterol levels by 20% and lowers ldl-cholesterol by 30%, suggesting its potential use for preventing and treating hyperlipidemia as well as bowel diseases by inhibiting harmful bacteria.

raw: theoretically, the everolimus-eluting bioresorbable vascular scaffold (bvs) could eliminate stent thrombosis and improve outcomes in patients having percutaneous coronary intervention.to estimate the incidence of stent thrombosis after bvs implantation and to compare the efficacy and safety of bvss versus everolimus-eluting metallic stents (eess) in adults having percutaneous coronary intervention.pubmed, embase, cochrane central register of controlled trials, conference proceedings, and relevant web sites from inception through 20 january 2016.6 randomized, controlled trials and 38 observational studies, each involving at least 40 patients with bvs implantation.two reviewers independently extracted study data and evaluated study risk of bias.the pooled incidence of definite or probable stent thrombosis after bvs implantation was 1.5 events per 100 patient-years (pys) (95% ci, 1.2 to 2.0 events per 100 pys) (126 events during 8508 pys). six randomized trials that directly compared bvss with eess showed a non-statistically significant increased risk for stent thrombosis (odds ratio [or], 2.05 [ci, 0.95 to 4.43]; p = 0.067) and myocardial infarction (or, 1.38 [ci, 0.98 to 1.95]; p = 0.064) with bvss. the 6 observational studies that compared bvss with eess showed increased risk for stent thrombosis (or, 2.32 [ci, 1.06 to 5.07]; p = 0.035) and myocardial infarction (or, 2.09 [ci, 1.23 to 3.55]; p = 0.007) with bvss. the relative rates of all-cause and cardiac death, revascularization, and target lesion failure were similar for bvss and eess.scarce comparative data, no published data from large trials with long-term follow-up, and limited quality and incomplete reporting of observational studies.compared with eess, bvss do not eliminate and might increase risks for stent thrombosis and myocardial infarction in adults having percutaneous coronary intervention. results of large trials with long-term follow-up are critically needed to establish the safety or at least the noninferiority of bvss compared with eess.none.
rewritten: in theory, the everolimus-eluting bioresorbable vascular scaffold (bvs) might prevent stent thrombosis and improve outcomes for patients undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention. the study aimed to assess the incidence of stent thrombosis after bvs implantation and to compare the efficacy and safety of bvs with that of everolimus-eluting metallic stents (ees) in adult patients receiving percutaneous coronary interventions. searches were performed on pubmed, embase, the cochrane central register of controlled trials, conference proceedings, and other relevant websites, covering records from inception through january 20, 2016. the analysis included six randomized controlled trials and 38 observational studies, each with at least 40 patients undergoing bvs implantation. two reviewers independently extracted data and evaluated the risk of bias for each study. the overall incidence of definite or probable stent thrombosis after bvs implantation was found to be 1.5 events per 100 patient-years (95% confidence interval: 1.2 to 2.0 events per 100 patient-years), corresponding to 126 events over 8,508 patient-years. in the six randomized trials that directly compared bvs with ees, there was a non-statistically significant increase in the risk of stent thrombosis (odds ratio [or] 2.05, 95% ci: 0.95 to 4.43; p = 0.067) and myocardial infarction (or 1.38, 95% ci: 0.98 to 1.95; p = 0.064) associated with bvs. similarly, the six observational studies that compared the two devices reported a higher risk of stent thrombosis (or 2.32, 95% ci: 1.06 to 5.07; p = 0.035) and myocardial infarction (or 2.09, 95% ci: 1.23 to 3.55; p = 0.007) with bvs. the rates of all-cause death, cardiac death, revascularization, and target lesion failure were similar between bvs and ees. however, due to the limited comparative data, the absence of published data from large trials with long-term follow-up, and the variable quality and incomplete reporting of the observational studies, the available evidence suggests that compared with ees, bvs do not eliminate—and might even increase—the risks of stent thrombosis and myocardial infarction in adults undergoing percutaneous coronary intervention. therefore, large trials with extended follow-up are critically needed to establish the safety or at least the noninferiority of bvs compared with ees. none.

raw: the invention discloses a current transformer of a high-power and medium-high frequency power supply. the current transformer comprises a primary magnetic ring and a secondary magnetic ring. a primary magnetic ring secondary winding is wound onto the annular magnet of the primary magnetic ring. a secondary magnetic ring secondary winding is wound onto the annular magnet of the secondary magnetic ring. the two ends of a wire for winding the secondary magnetic ring secondary winding are provided with a load-side outgoing line of the current transformer of the high-power and medium-high frequency power supply. a wire for winding the primary magnetic ring secondary winding is wound onto the annular magnet of the secondary magnetic ring at a specified number of turns to form a secondary magnetic ring primary winding. the number of turns of the secondary magnetic ring primary winding is smaller than the number of turns of the primary magnetic ring secondary winding and the number of turns of the secondary magnetic ring secondary winding. the invention also provides a winding method for the current transformer of the high-power and medium-high frequency power supply.
rewritten: the invention relates to a current transformer designed for use in high-power, medium-high frequency power supplies. this transformer includes both a primary magnetic ring and a secondary magnetic ring. a secondary winding for the primary magnetic ring is wound around its annular magnet, while another secondary winding for the secondary magnetic ring is similarly wound on its annular magnet, with both ends of this winding serving as the transformer’s load-side output connection. additionally, a winding wire is wrapped around the annular magnet of the secondary magnetic ring—performed for a predetermined number of turns—to create what is termed the primary winding of the secondary magnetic ring. notably, the number of turns in this secondary magnetic ring primary winding is less than that of either the primary magnetic ring secondary winding or the secondary magnetic ring secondary winding. the invention further discloses a method for winding this current transformer in a high-power, medium-high frequency power supply.
